The_Boy_in_the_Iceberg.action: Katara and Sokka go out fishing in a small canoe
Sokka prepares to spear a fish
Katara bends the water around a fish and lifts it above the canoe in a bubble
Sokka readies his spear to strike, ignoring Katara’s pleas to look at her catch
Sokka accidentally hits the water bubble with the back of his weapon, knocking the fish back into the ocean and drenching himself
Sokka berates Katara, complaining he always ends up soaked when she plays with "magic water"
Katara corrects Sokka, saying it is waterbending, not magic
Sokka interrupts Katara and says if he had "weird powers" he would keep his "weirdness" to himself
Katara and Sokka start to bicker
Katara and Sokka start to bicker
The sea currents suddenly pick up speed
Sokka tries to steer the canoe to avoid collision with floating ice chunks
Katara frantically shouts to "go left"
The boat is crushed between three ice floes, leaving Sokka and Katara stranded on the ice in the middle of the sea
Katara blames Sokka for failing to "go left" as she instructed
Sokka counters by saying Katara should have waterbent them to safety if she didn't like his steering
Sokka concludes that it could be "left to a girl to screw things up"
Katara loses her temper and angrily yells at Sokka, calling him sexist, immature, and "nut-brained"
Katara rages about all the chores she does for Sokka
Katara involuntarily waterbends, cracking a large iceberg and causing it to break and plunge into the ocean
Katara and Sokka are pushed back on their ice float
Another iceberg, glowing blue and containing two forms, rises to the surface
Katara deduces one of the forms is a living human
Katara grabs Sokka's club and heads to the iceberg, despite Sokka's warning to stay back
Sokka orders Katara to stay back
Katara starts chopping at the iceberg
Katara is blown back by a gust of air escaping from the hole she made
Katara and Sokka watch in astonishment as the ice breaks and a bright column of light shoots into the sky
The iceberg cracks in two and a bright column of light shoots high into the sky
Zuko sees the bright light from his ship
Iroh dismisses the event as completely natural
Zuko orders his crew to head toward the light
Zuko insists that the light was emitted by the Avatar
Katara breaks through the ice and finds a young boy with arrow-shaped marks on his head and hands
Sokka points his spear at the boy
The boy collapses into Katara's arms
Katara lays the boy down and tries to help him
Sokka pokes the boy in the head with the blunt side of his spear
Katara shoos Sokka away, annoyed
The boy drowsily opens his eyes to see Katara staring at him
The boy abruptly and cheerfully asks Katara if she wants to go penguin sledding
Katara confusedly agrees
The boy rises to his feet swiftly, surprising Sokka
Sokka becomes defensive with his spear
The boy, surprised by Sokka's aggressiveness, inquires what is happening
Sokka angrily demands to know how Aang became trapped in the ice
The boy says he does not know and casually nudges Sokka's spear aside
The boy hears a growling noise from within the crater of the hollowed iceberg
The boy climbs back over the rim to find a large, furry, six-legged creature lying motionless
The boy enthusiastically rouses the creature
Sokka asks what the creature is
The boy replies that it is Appa, his flying bison
Sokka expresses disbelief that the bison can fly
Appa sneezes on Sokka, covering him with bison snot
Sokka tries frantically to rub the bison snot off himself onto the snow
The boy assures Sokka that it washes out
The boy looks around and asks if they live nearby
Sokka angrily tells Katara not to answer, convinced the boy is a Fire Nation spy
Katara sarcastically rejects Sokka’s notion
Katara introduces herself and asks the boy his name
The boy tries to answer but sneezes, launching himself into the air
The boy drifts back to the ground, rubs his nose, and tells them his name is Aang
Katara, astonished, muses that Aang must be an airbender
Aang confirms that he is an airbender
Sokka decides aloud to go home and leave behind the nonsensical world
Sokka takes three steps before realizing he is stranded on an iceberg in the middle of the ocean
Aang offers to fly Sokka and Katara back to Wolf Cove
Katara happily agrees to Aang's offer
Sokka refuses at first, then agrees after realizing he has no other choice
The group gets on Appa's saddle
Sokka grumpily refuses to believe that Appa can fly
Aang says "Yip-yip!"
Appa leaps into the air, then crashes back down into the water and starts swimming
Sokka crows sarcastically about Appa's inability to fly
Aang decides Appa is still too tired to fly
Aang turns around and stares at Katara with a huge smile
Katara, after a few awkward seconds, asks Aang why he is smiling at her like that
Aang replies, "Oh, I was smiling?"
Sokka lifts his head back, groaning
Katara, at first smiling at Aang's response, frowns back at Sokka
Zuko stands on the deck of the ship looking out over the water alone
Iroh tries to convince Zuko to sleep
Iroh tells Zuko he will fail to capture the Avatar even if they find him, like his ancestors
Zuko refuses to accept Iroh's words
Zuko believes he will succeed because he seeks to regain his honor, a trait his ancestors did not share
Katara asks Aang if he knows the fate of the Avatar
Katara notes that the Avatar was supposed to be an Air Nomad
Aang awkwardly states that he knew people who knew the Avatar but did not know the Avatar himself
Katara drops the subject, disappointed
Aang looks guilty
Aang falls asleep on Appa's back
Aang has a dream recalling his last moments before being encased in ice
Aang and Appa fly through a terrible storm near the South Pole
Aang and Appa are buffeted by the fury of the gale, struggling to escape
Aang and Appa are forced under the waves
Aang begins to lose consciousness
Aang's eyes and tattoos begin to glow
Aang creates a bubble around himself and Appa, which he freezes solid
The ice thickens around Aang and Appa
Aang is jolted awake by Katara
Aang finds himself in a tent in Wolf Cove
Katara is eager to introduce Aang to the rest of the village
Aang dresses quickly with Katara watching
Katara notices the arrow markings on Aang's body and gasps
Katara does not say anything about the markings
Aang finishes dressing
Katara pulls Aang along and introduces him to the entire population
The population is composed of a small number of women and children
Sokka watches from a short distance away
Aang bows to the villagers respectfully
Villagers respond with fear and hurriedly take a few steps away from Aang
Aang and Katara are unhappily surprised by the villagers' reaction
Aang and Katara are unhappily surprised by the villagers' reaction
Aang asks Katara in a hushed voice why the villagers are looking at him this way
Aang wonders if Appa has sneezed on him and checks his clothes for bison snot
An elderly woman steps forward and addresses Aang, explaining the villagers believed airbenders to be extinct
Aang repeats the word "extinct" in disbelief
Katara introduces the elderly woman as her grandmother
The elderly woman insists on being called "Gran-Gran"
Sokka abruptly interrupts the conversation, redoubles his interrogation of Aang, snatches Aang's staff, and examines it closely
Sokka assumes the staff is a weapon and derisively deems it a failure for having blunt ends
Aang snatches the staff back from Sokka with a burst of airbending
Aang tells Sokka that the staff's purpose is airbending, not stabbing
Aang opens the wings on the staff
Sokka reels back in terror from the staff
a young girl asks Aang to repeat the "magic trick"
Aang reminds the girl that it is airbending, not magic
Aang moves the staff around himself, mimicking flight and explaining airbending
Sokka responds with sarcastic skepticism about humans flying
Aang replies "Check again" calmly and confidently
Aang launches himself into the air, raising a gust of wind
villagers cover their faces from the gust of wind
Aang flies circles around the villagers
villagers are astounded and show adulation
Aang attempts to fly with his eyes closed
Aang collides head-on with a watchtower made of snow and ice
the watchtower collapses
Katara and a few of the children rush to Aang's aid
Sokka rushes to the base of the fallen structure and tries vainly to save it
a mass of snow buries Sokka
Sokka grumbles aloud about Aang and Katara being time-wasting benders and storms off
Aang is thrilled to learn that Katara is a waterbender
Katara tells Aang she is only "sort of" a waterbender before her grandmother calls her away for chores
Katara's grandmother calls Katara away, telling her she has chores to do
Katara ecstatically tells Gran-Gran she believes she has found a bender who can teach her waterbending
Gran-Gran warns Katara not to place too much stock in a person she has only just met
Katara brushes aside Gran-Gran's request and says she believes Aang is "filled with much wisdom" as she strikes a meditative pose
Katara and Gran-Gran glance over at Aang
Aang shows the village toddlers his ability to stick his tongue to his staff, amusing them deeply
Zuko practices his firebending techniques with Iroh as the instructor by sparring against two soldiers
Iroh instructs Zuko during the sparring session
Zuko and the two soldiers exchange firebending attacks
Iroh stands up with a sigh and stops the fight, correcting Zuko's mistake
Zuko becomes impatient and demands to be taught the next, more advanced set of moves
Iroh brushes off Zuko's demand, insisting he must master basic moves first
Zuko flies into a rage and attacks one of the soldiers with a blast of fire, knocking him off his feet
Zuko repeats his demand to Iroh, reasoning that the Avatar cannot be beaten with basic firebending
Iroh considers Zuko's words and concedes, but only if he can finish eating his roast duck
Iroh eats his meal placidly in front of a dismayed Zuko
Sokka gives the six young boys of the tribe a stern lecture on their military practices
Sokka emphasizes that they are the "warriors" of the tribe
One boy raises his hand and says he needs to pee, interrupting Sokka
Sokka attempts to get his point across
Sokka unhappily asks how many of them need to pee
All the boys raise their hands
The kids leave
Sokka facepalms in annoyance
Katara walks up, looking for Aang
Aang leaves the outhouse as Katara talks to Sokka
The boys arrive at the outhouse
Aang enthusiastically declares, "Wow! Everything freezes in there!"
The boys laugh
Sokka becomes angry and demands that Aang leave, as the lesson is for "warriors only"
Sokka hears more laughing children
Sokka discovers some boys and Aang playing on Appa, sliding down his tail
The boys use Sokka's spear to measure the distance they have flown
Sokka rushes over and demands that they stop
Sokka berates the boys and rails on Aang, accusing him of distracting his "warriors" with fun and games
Aang slides down from Appa's back and reveals he has no knowledge of the war
Sokka, disbelieving, asks Aang if he is kidding
Aang opens his mouth to reply, but is distracted by the sudden appearance of an otter penguin
An otter penguin appears behind the siblings
Aang races off toward the otter penguin, raising a cloud of blowing snow with airbending
Sokka, dumbfounded, asks Katara if Aang is kidding
Sokka and Katara exchange looks of uncertainty and confusion
Sokka and Katara exchange looks of uncertainty and confusion
Katara tracks Aang to an icy waste populated by otter penguins
Aang tries to catch an otter penguin, but the creatures are too fast and slippery for him
Katara approaches Aang and agrees to teach him how to catch a penguin, on the condition that he teach her waterbending
Aang points out that he knows nothing about waterbending, being an airbender
Aang asks if there are any other waterbenders from whom Katara could learn
Katara responds despondently that she is the only waterbender in the South Pole
Aang is struck by the injustice of the situation and suggests the Northern Water Tribe as an option
Aang offers to fly Katara to the North Pole himself
Katara expresses uncertainty at the thought of leaving her tribe
Aang suggests Katara consider the matter later and asks her to teach him to catch penguins
Katara gives Aang a pensive speech about catching penguins being "an ancient and secret art"
Katara pulls a fish from her sleeve and tosses it to Aang
Aang laughs as he is mobbed by otter penguins
Aang and Katara go penguin sledding
Aang and Katara race through varied terrain
Aang and Katara enter a tunnel in the ice
Aang and Katara emerge on a wide, icy plain with an old abandoned shipwreck
Aang asks Katara what the ship is
Katara tells Aang it is a Fire Navy ship, source of a dark episode in the tribe's history
Aang makes as though to investigate the ship
Katara expresses reluctance to investigate the ship
Aang tells Katara that being a bender involves letting go of fear
Katara joins Aang and they walk toward the ship
Aang and Katara make their way inside the ship
Katara explains the ship is as old as her grandmother and was part of the Fire Nation's first assaults
Aang and Katara investigate a room full of spears
Aang tells Katara he has friends all over the world, including the Fire Nation, and never saw any war
Katara asks Aang how long he was in the iceberg
Aang guesses it could have been a few days
Katara tells Aang it must be around a hundred years
Aang objects to Katara's suggestion
Katara clarifies that Aang must have been trapped for nearly a hundred years
Aang collapses to the metal floor, horrified
Katara tries to comfort Aang, telling him there might be a bright side
Aang perks up and says he got to meet Katara
Aang takes Katara's hand and they walk back to the hallway
Aang investigates another room and accidentally triggers a tripwire
The booby trap imprisons them in the room with metal bars and fires a flare into the sky
Aang notices a hole in the ceiling, grabs Katara, and tells her to hold on tight
Aang launches them with airbending through the opening
Zuko sees the flare from the deck of his ship
Zuko watches through a spyglass as two small figures jump down the side of the ship
Zuko realizes he has found the last airbender
Zuko comments that the bender is "quite agile for his old age"
Zuko yells to a soldier to wake his uncle and tell him he has found the Avatar
Zuko looks through the spyglass again and tracks the two figures
Zuko scouts ahead in the direction they are running and spots a tiny village in the distance
Zuko lowers the spyglass and squints into the distance
Zuko quietly adds that he has also found the Avatar's hiding place

The_Avatar_Returns.action: Aang and Katara return from the site of the shipwreck
Aang and Katara are greeted by many angry faces in the village
Aang and Katara accidentally trigger a flare on the ship, warning the Fire Nation of their presence
The safety of the village is compromised
A bitter argument ensues in the village
Sokka decides to banish Aang from the Southern Water Tribe for his perceived betrayal
Kanna agrees with Sokka’s decision to banish Aang
Katara is outraged by the decision and initially resolves to leave with Aang
Aang offers to take Katara to the Northern Water Tribe to find a master waterbender
Sokka asks Katara if she would actually choose Aang over her own family
Aang states that he does not want to come between Katara and her family
Katara rethinks her decision and sadly decides to stay
Aang and Appa depart from the village, setting course for the Southern Air Temple
Katara, frustrated that they have banished Aang, snaps angrily at her grandmother
Kanna is left looking saddened
Sokka hurriedly readies the young children for imminent combat with the Fire Nation
Aang and Appa rest on a wind-carved ice formation
Aang acts as if Appa can talk to him
Aang tells Appa, somewhat dejectedly, that he liked Katara
Aang spots Zuko's ship approaching the village
Aang realizes the severity of their predicament
Aang quickly decides to help defend his new friends
Aang leaves a dozing Appa behind
Zuko dons a traditional Fire Nation soldier's uniform and helmet
Sokka readies his weapons
Sokka applies warpaint to his face
Zuko's ship arrives at Wolf Cove
The ship's approach collapses Sokka's makeshift walls and towers
Sokka continues to stand bravely
Sokka balks to avoid the ramp descending from the ship
Zuko quickly descends from the ship, accompanied by a few soldiers
Sokka charges at Zuko
Zuko counters Sokka's attack with his bare hands, dismissing Sokka's presence
Zuko demands the location of the Avatar from the villagers, expecting to find an old man
Zuko grabs Kanna threateningly
Sokka attacks Zuko again
Zuko defeats Sokka with ease
Sokka lands a lucky hit when his boomerang strikes the back of Zuko's helmet
Zuko becomes enraged at being caught off guard
Zuko prepares to deal a vicious strike with his firebending
Aang arrives riding on the back of an otter penguin and knocks Zuko off his feet
Aang dismounts the otter penguin
Aang uses airbending and his staff to send a spray of snow at Zuko and his men
Aang stands and faces Zuko, asking if he is looking for him
Zuko realizes and is shocked that the Avatar is a twelve-year-old boy
Zuko voices his realization, revealing Aang's true identity to the entire village
Villagers learn Aang is the Avatar
Zuko regains his focus and launches a series of fire blasts at Aang
Aang defends himself with airbending
Aang realizes Zuko's attacks could strike the villagers
Aang asks Zuko if his surrender will ensure the villagers' safety
Zuko relaxes his stance and nods
Katara begs Aang not to go with the firebenders
Zuko's soldiers take Aang aboard as a prisoner
Zuko and his soldiers depart for the Fire Nation with Aang
Katara tries to convince Sokka that they need to rescue Aang
Sokka tries to interrupt Katara briefly
Sokka yells at Katara, asking if she is coming with him as he motions toward a small canoe
Katara realizes Sokka is on her side, runs to him, and gives him a hug
Kanna walks up behind them, catching them off-guard and sternly asks what they plan on doing
Katara and Sokka have no answer
Katara and Sokka have no answer
Kanna holds out two sleeping bags to them, saying they would need those
Kanna tells them they have a long journey ahead and thanks Katara for restoring her hope
Kanna tells Sokka to be nice to his sister
Kanna states that since her grandchildren found Aang, their destinies are now tied to his
Katara and Sokka look uncertain
Katara and Sokka look uncertain
Katara remarks that the canoe is not going to work if they want to catch the ship
Appa lumbers over a nearby hill, growling loudly
Katara runs excitedly toward Appa
Sokka mutters quietly about being taken out of his comfort zone
Zuko examines Aang's staff and muses that it would make a nice gift for his father
Zuko commands the guards to take Aang to the prison hold
Zuko commands his uncle to take the staff to the prince's personal quarters
Zuko thrusts the staff onto his uncle
Iroh passes on the order to another guard
Iroh retreats to his own quarters
Aang makes conversation with the two guards escorting him
Aang asserts he could defeat both guards in a fight with his hands tied behind his back
The guard in front orders Aang to stop talking as they arrive at the cell door
Aang takes a deep breath and blows, creating a powerful gust of wind
The gust of wind throws the guard in front into the heavy metal door
The gust sends Aang flying backward into the guard behind him, propelling them into the stairs at the opposite end of the corridor
Both soldiers are dazed by the attack
Aang quickly jumps onto the deck and forces his way into the interior corridors of the ship with airbending
As Aang runs through the hallway, one of the guards emerges from the hold and shouts to a guard on the crow's nest that the Avatar has escaped
Katara and Sokka slowly pursue Zuko's ship on Appa, who swims through the water
Katara sits on Appa's head, trying to encourage him to fly
Sokka reclines in the saddle and blandly calls out commands such as "fly", "elevate", and "up" at Appa
Sokka tries to remember what phrase Aang used to get Appa to fly
Sokka eventually says "Yip-yip"
Appa grunts and lifts itself out of the water and into the sky, moving at a much more rapid pace
Sokka becomes excited, forgetting his cynical façade and grumpiness
Sokka's excitement is momentarily dimmed when he sees Katara smirking smugly at him
Katara smirks smugly at Sokka
Aang runs through the ship's hallways, displaying tremendous speed and agility
Aang encounters several soldiers with drawn swords and asks if they have seen his staff
Soldiers try to rush Aang
Aang uses airbending to spiral through the corridor, running on the walls and ceiling to evade the soldiers
Aang meets another soldier, somersaults over him, and uses the sharp point of the soldier's helmet to cut the bonds on his wrists, knocking the guard down
Aang looks through several rooms, including one with a sleeping Iroh
Iroh sleeps in one of the rooms
Aang discovers Zuko's personal quarters and sees his staff leaning against the far wall
Aang runs into the room to claim his staff
Zuko slams the door shut to cut off Aang's escape route, forcing a confrontation
Zuko attacks Aang in an attempt to incapacitate him
Aang avoids Zuko's flames, with some difficulty
Aang wraps Zuko in a tapestry hanging from the wall
Aang retrieves his staff after a short exchange
Aang uses the staff to airbend, picks up a mattress, and slams it into Zuko, crushing him against the wall and ceiling
Zuko and the mattress fall back to the floor, stunned
Aang escapes the room
Zuko looks up after a few seconds, left enraged by his defeat
Aang reaches the bridge of the ship
Aang jumps over several stunned soldiers
Aang rushes to the outer deck
Aang throws his glider, wings open, into the empty air beyond the railing
Aang jumps to the glider and suspends himself underneath it
Zuko leaps off the deck after Aang and grabs him by the ankle
Aang and Zuko fall to the deck below, unable to compensate for the extra weight or break free
Aang and Zuko prepare to face off a third time
Aang and Zuko prepare to face off a third time
Appa appears suddenly, carrying Sokka and Katara
Zuko is stunned briefly as he looks at Appa
Zuko attacks Aang almost immediately
Aang airbends with his staff, accidentally lifting himself into the air
Zuko fires several fire blasts, pressing Aang to the edge of the deck and knocking his staff away
Zuko fires another two blasts, throwing Aang, unharmed but unconscious, over the side of the ship into the frigid ocean
Katara cries out in dismay as Aang begins to sink
Aang's eyes and tattoos flash open, glowing white as his expression becomes stern and angry
Aang, in the Avatar State, waterbends the sea around him, rising out of the ocean on a towering, whirling column of water
Zuko, Katara, and Sokka are shocked by Aang's immense display of bending power
Zuko, Katara, and Sokka are shocked by Aang's immense display of bending power
Zuko, Katara, and Sokka are shocked by Aang's immense display of bending power
Aang lowers himself to the deck of the ship, bending his liquid platform into a spinning wall of water
Aang washes Zuko and all of the guards on deck away with the spinning wall of water
Zuko tumbles overboard
Appa lands on the deck
Aang, apparently dazed after the massive display of power, collapses
Katara and Sokka rush to Aang's aid and assure he is all right
Sokka prepares to leave and goes to retrieve Aang's staff
Zuko climbs back onto the deck with Aang's staff
Sokka jabs Zuko in the forehead with the staff three times, stunning him and sending him overboard
Zuko grasps the anchor chain to prevent himself from falling into the water
Some soldiers recover and begin to approach Katara and Aang
Katara freezes the guards in place to stop their approach
Katara, Sokka, and Aang take off on Appa just as Iroh arrives
Iroh helps Zuko on board
Iroh and Zuko launch a combined fire blast at the escaping Appa
Aang recovers and uses a blast of wind to deflect the fireball into a wall of ice
The resulting avalanche of ice collapses onto the front half of Zuko's ship, immobilizing it
Appa flies into the distance as his passengers laugh happily
Zuko vows not to underestimate the Avatar again
Zuko instructs his crew to dig the ship out and pursue the Avatar and his friends as soon as possible
The majority of Zuko's crew is either frozen solid or engaged with de-icing their compatriots
Appa flies into the sunset
Katara excitedly asks Aang how he was able to execute powerful waterbending techniques
Aang is unable to understand how he performed the techniques
Katara questions Aang about why he denied knowledge of the Avatar
Aang sadly replies that he never wanted to be the Avatar
Katara reminds Aang of the world's need for the Avatar and states he must master water, earth, and fire
Katara muses they might find a waterbending teacher at the North Pole
Aang brightens up and suggests they could both learn waterbending
Sokka decides to come along to defeat firebenders
The trio decides to travel to the Northern Tribe
Aang lists a few places he wants to stop along the way

The_Southern_Air_Temple.action: Katara and Aang pack up their campsite
Aang excitedly tells Katara about the beauty of the Southern Air Temple and his eagerness to return
Sokka remains in his sleeping bag
Aang tries to convince Sokka to get up
Sokka ignores Aang’s request
Aang picks up a stick and slides it across Sokka’s sleeping bag
Aang tells Sokka there is a prickle snake in his sleeping bag in a frantic tone
Sokka, terrified and screaming, leaps to his feet still in the sleeping bag
Sokka topples back to the ground face-first and realizes he has been tricked
Katara suppresses a laugh
Aang declares they can leave since Sokka is now wide awake
Zuko docks at Ketu Harbor with his damaged ship
Zuko wants repairs done quickly to avoid losing the Avatar's trail
Zuko warns Iroh not to mention the Avatar during their stay
Zhao walks up to Iroh and Zuko, welcoming them to his harbor and informing them of his promotion
Zhao inquires about the cause of the ship's damage
Zuko and Iroh invent a story about a collision with an Earth Kingdom ship
Zuko and Iroh invent a story about a collision with an Earth Kingdom ship
Zhao, unconvinced, invites Zuko and Iroh for a drink
Zuko attempts to reject Zhao's offer
Iroh berates Zuko and accepts the invite on his behalf, telling him he owes Zhao respect
Sokka discovers that Aang used all the blubbered seal jerky to start the campfire
Aang uses all the blubbered seal jerky to start the campfire
Sokka remarks that it was "no wonder the flames smelled so good"
Katara tries to keep Aang from getting too excited about their visit to the air temple
Katara tells Aang to expect the possibility that his people have been killed by the Fire Nation
Aang refuses to entertain the possibility that his people have been killed
Aang believes reaching an air temple is impossible without a flying bison
Aang maintains that the Fire Nation could not have reached the temple without a flying bison
The group approaches the top of the mountain, which pierces the clouds below
They look down on the Southern Air Temple
Commander Zhao outlines the Fire Nation's plan for conquering the Earth Kingdom to Zuko and Iroh over tea in his tent
Zuko voices his bitter skepticism of the plan, calling his father a fool for believing the Earth Kingdom will capitulate quickly
Commander Zhao inquires about Zuko's search for the Avatar
Zuko replies that he has been unsuccessful in finding the Avatar
Commander Zhao remarks he did not expect anything different and persists in his pursuit of information from Zuko
Zuko and Commander Zhao have a brief, heated exchange
Zuko rises and attempts to leave
Zhao's guards detain Zuko at the entrance to the tent
One of Zhao's soldiers reports that he interrogated Zuko's crew and found out that Zuko had the Avatar but let him escape
Commander Zhao, smiling smugly, asks Zuko to explain how his ship was damaged again
Sokka is berated for thinking only about food at the airbender temple
Aang starts to give Katara and Sokka a tour of the temple
Aang becomes disheartened after failing to see any other airbenders
Sokka volunteers to play a game of airball with Aang to take his mind off it
Aang defeats Sokka in airball by using airbending
The airball hits Sokka in the stomach and sends him flying into a pile of snow
Sokka, lying on the ground, finds an archaic Fire Nation soldier's helmet
Sokka points out the helmet to a shocked Katara
Katara and Sokka agree to show the helmet to Aang
Katara changes her mind and waterbends snow to cover the helmet and Sokka
Katara tells Aang she wanted to show him her snowbending
Aang believes Katara and compliments her snowbending
Aang resumes the tour, wishing to show the rest of the temple
Sokka tells Katara that shielding Aang from the truth is a mistake
Katara replies sadly that Aang would be devastated if he knew firebenders invaded his home
Aang, Katara, and Sokka come to a tall statue of an airbender monk
Aang bows respectfully and introduces the statue as Monk Gyatso
Gyatso teaches Aang the secrets of making fruit pies
Aang is disinterested in the lesson
Aang tells Gyatso he thinks the monks may have erroneously identified him as the Avatar
Gyatso tells Aang the only mistake the monks made was telling him his true identity before age sixteen
Gyatso reassures Aang and tells him to focus on the present
Gyatso tells Aang that once he is old enough to enter the air temple sanctuary, he will meet someone who will help him become a better Avatar
Aang feels reassured and happier
Aang assists Gyatso in airbending the freshly baked fruit pies
The fruit pies soar through the air and land on the other elder monks' heads while they are meditating
Winged lemurs pounce on the monks and slurp the pie off their heads
Gyatso and Aang laugh at their prank
Gyatso congratulates Aang on his perfected aim
Aang presses onward toward the air temple sanctuary, recalling Gyatso's words about the mysterious entity within
Aang tells his friends that there is someone he is ready to meet
Katara expresses doubt that anyone could have survived in the temple for a hundred years unaided
Aang offers his own survival as a rationale for the possibility
Aang wonders if the person inside can help him understand and teach him about being the Avatar
Sokka offers a hopeful conjecture that the person within may have food, particularly cured meats
Sokka runs at the doors, becomes irrational in his desire for food, and slams himself into them, failing to open them even after several more tries
Aang bends two focused currents of air into the large doors' ornate locking mechanism, causing the locks to disengage and the doors to open slowly
Aang walks into the darkened temple as Katara and Sokka follow him
Katara and Sokka follow Aang into the temple
Katara and Sokka follow Aang into the temple
Zhao expresses appall at a twelve-year-old boy besting Zuko and his crew, calling Zuko "more pathetic than he thought"
Zuko furiously states that he underestimated his opponent once and tries to convince Zhao it won't happen again
Zhao disregards Zuko's plea and declares he will take over the hunt for the Avatar, dismissing Zuko as a mere teenager
Zuko leaps at Zhao in rage and is restrained by the guards
Zhao orders for Zuko and Iroh to be kept in his tent and leaves
Zuko kicks the table in anger
Iroh placidly asks for more tea
The air temple sanctuary is filled with hundreds of statues
Sokka is disappointed at the lack of food
Katara and Aang notice a pattern in the line of the statues: they are arranged in the Avatar Cycle
Katara realizes the statues are of previous Avatars, Aang's past lives
Aang singles out the last statue in the line and gazes into its eyes, going into a trance
Katara gently shakes Aang, breaking him out of the trance
Aang recognizes the statue as Avatar Roku, the Avatar immediately preceding him
Sokka recognizes Roku as a firebender by his robes and top-knot, citing this as the reason he did not trust Aang when they first met
The group hears a humming noise from the entrance, indicating someone else is nearby
The noise causes the group to take cover behind the statues
Sokka prepares to fight a firebender and jumps out, trying to catch the intruder off-guard
Aang and Katara peer out from behind their statues
Aang and Katara peer out from behind their statues
Aang and Katara find themselves looking down on a solitary lemur
Aang and Katara find themselves looking down on a solitary lemur
Sokka wants to eat the lemur for dinner
Aang wants to keep the lemur as a pet
Sokka and Aang race to see who can catch the lemur first, each trying to trip up the other
Sokka and Aang race to see who can catch the lemur first, each trying to trip up the other
Katara stays behind in the temple sanctuary
The lemur flies out from the window
Aang jumps off the temple balcony after the lemur, determined to catch it
Commander Zhao re-enters his tent and tells Zuko and Iroh that he and his search party are almost ready to embark on their search for the Avatar
Zhao says he plans to have his guards release Zuko and Iroh once his ship has left
Zuko, still infuriated, reiterates his belief that he will capture the Avatar before Zhao can
Zhao laughs at the thought of a banished prince competing with himself, who has hundreds of warships
Zhao demeans Zuko, saying that if the Fire Lord truly loved him, he would let him come home without the Avatar
Zhao states that all Ozai sees in Zuko is failure, citing Zuko's scar as proof
Zuko, goaded by rage and disgust, challenges Zhao to an Agni Kai at sunset
Zhao accepts the challenge
Zhao leaves the tent, telling Zuko to prepare to be humiliated
Iroh reminds Zuko of his last duel with a firebending master
Zuko says he will "never forget" that fight
Aang pursues the lemur toward the tattered ruin of a tent
Aang pokes his head inside the tent
Aang finds Fire Nation soldiers' bodies scattered in the snow
Aang walks farther inside the tent
Aang finds the skeleton of Monk Gyatso
Aang breaks down, devastated, and falls to his knees in tears
Sokka, still searching for the lemur, catches up to Aang and playfully asks if he caught the lemur
Sokka says he did not actually intend on eating the animal
Sokka notices the skeletons and understands why Aang is upset
Sokka tries to comfort Aang
Aang, overcome by emotion, enters the Avatar State
Aang's tattoos and eyes glow suddenly
Sokka gasps and reels away from Aang in fright
Katara looks at the statue of Roku within the temple sanctuary
The eyes of Roku's statue begin to glow white
Katara watches in shock as the eyes of all the Avatar statues begin to glow
A string of lights spirals into the darkness above Katara
Katara realizes Aang must be the cause
Katara races out of the sanctuary to find Aang
Avatar temples in the other three nations experience similar events, with their Avatar statues and murals glowing
The Fire Sages in the Crescent Island Fire Temple prepare to inform the Fire Lord of the return of the Avatar
Aang begins to bend a windstorm around himself while in the Avatar State
Sokka is blown backward off his feet as the gale intensifies
The tattered tent is obliterated by the blast of wind
Katara walks with an arm protecting her face as she struggles against the wind
Katara asks Sokka what happened, yelling over the wind
Sokka, sheltering behind an outcropping of rock, yells back that Aang found out firebenders killed Gyatso
Katara realizes Aang triggered the Avatar State and resolves to try to calm him down
Aang’s rage escalates and the sphere of air containing him begins to float upward, halting a short distance off the ground
Katara begins making her way through the blinding whirlwind toward Aang
The sun sets over the Fire Nation port
Zhao and Zuko are in a large courtyard, ready to duel
Zhao and Zuko crouch back-to-back with some distance between them
Zhao and Zuko shed their shoulder garments and turn to face each other
Iroh counsels Zuko to remember his basics, calling them his greatest assets
Zuko ignores Iroh's advice and states he will not allow himself to lose
Zuko assumes his stance
Zhao assumes his stance and taunts Zuko, saying "This will be over quickly."
A soldier on the courtyard wall sounds a gong
The match commences
Zuko and Zhao stare fiercely into each other's eyes, waiting for the other to strike
Zuko begins the duel with a series of fire blasts from his hands and feet as he moves along his side of the courtyard
Zhao effortlessly avoids and nullifies all of Zuko's fire blasts
Zuko catches his breath
Iroh advises Zuko to remember his basics
Zhao throws his own volley of fire blasts
Zuko blocks each attack but is slowly forced back with every parry
Zhao sends a ball of fire with both fists that connects with Zuko, knocking him to the ground
Zhao leaps into the air, covers the distance, and prepares a finishing blast aimed at Zuko's face
Zuko rolls out of the way an instant before contact, rises with a kicking flourish, and knocks Zhao out of his stance
Zuko releases a series of low attacks that cause Zhao to retreat
Zuko finishes Zhao with a jet of fire from a full body kick
Zhao expects Zuko to deliver a final, devastating blow
Zuko's fire blast lands beside Zhao's face instead
Zhao insults Zuko for what he perceives as cowardice
Zuko promises not to show restraint if Zhao gets in his way in the future
Zuko walks away
Zhao, beaten and furious, sends a jet of fire at Zuko's back
Iroh intervenes, stopping the attack with his bare hand and throwing Zhao to the ground
Iroh stands over Zhao
Zuko tries to attack Zhao again
Iroh tells Zuko not to taint his victory by retaliating
Iroh lectures Zhao about the dishonor he has brought upon himself
Iroh states that Zuko, even in exile, has proved himself more honorable
Iroh politely thanks Zhao for sharing his tea and turns away
Zhao, defeated, picks himself up and groans as he watches them go
Zuko and Iroh leave the arena
Zuko asks Iroh if he truly meant what he said
Iroh responds, "Of course. I told you ginseng tea is my favorite."
Zuko and Iroh walk back toward the ship
A faint smile crosses Zuko's lips
Katara attempts to console Aang by relating to him that she lost her mother and stating that he has found a new family with her and Sokka
Katara’s words seem to reach Aang
The wind slowly dies and Aang descends back to the ground, though his eyes continue to glow
Sokka joins Katara and reinforces her statement, promising that neither of them will let anything happen to Aang
Katara takes Aang’s hand
Aang leaves the Avatar State, looking visibly sad for a brief moment before collapsing into Katara’s arms
Aang apologizes for his outburst
Katara tells Aang there is no need to be sorry
Aang admits Katara was right about the firebenders’ invasion of the temple
Aang concludes with great sadness that if one temple was attacked, the others must have fallen as well
Aang realizes that he truly is the last of the airbenders
Aang, Katara, and Sokka stand in the sanctuary
Aang stares at Roku's statue
Katara asks Aang if he is ready to leave
Aang wonders aloud to Katara about how a past life could help him
Katara replies, "Maybe you'll find a way."
The lemur reappears with a variety of fruits and lays them in front of Sokka
Sokka devours the fruits eagerly
The lemur climbs onto Aang's shoulder
Katara and Sokka finish packing behind Aang
Aang stands overlooking the silent remnants of the deserted temple with Appa and the lemur
Aang tells Appa and the lemur that the three of them are now the temple's only living remnants and that they have to stay together
Aang calls out to Katara and Sokka and introduces the lemur as the newest member of their group
Katara approaches Aang and asks what the lemur's name will be
The lemur leaps off Aang's shoulder, steals the moon peach Sokka was eating, and returns with it to Aang
Aang names the lemur Momo
Aang and Katara laugh at Sokka
Sokka, unaware his food is gone, takes a bite of nothing but air
the gang departs from the temple on Appa as night falls
Aang leans on the pack at the back of Appa's saddle, still sad
Aang takes a final, wistful gaze at what was once his home
the home is concealed by swirling clouds

The_Warriors_of_Kyoshi.action: Zuko’s ship drifts at sea
Zuko meditates
Iroh enters with information regarding the Avatar
Iroh begs Zuko not to become angry
Zuko calmly replies that he can control his temper
Iroh states he does not have any information about the Avatar's current location
Zuko becomes angry and flares the flames of his meditation candles
Iroh takes out a fan to cool himself off
Iroh explains there have been multiple sightings of Aang, but he is impossible to track, and shows Zuko a detailed map
Zuko takes the map from Iroh's hands
Zuko asks Iroh how he will be able to track down the Avatar
Aang, Katara, and Sokka fly on Appa over the South Sea
Sokka indicates Aang's total lack of navigational skills
Katara sews Sokka's trousers
Aang tries to get Katara's attention and impress her with his new airbending trick involving spinning three marbles
Katara ignores Aang, being preoccupied
Sokka makes a sexist comment
Katara angrily throws Sokka's trousers, which are not completely re-sewn, back at him
Sokka quickly regrets what he said
Sokka tells Katara he cannot wear his pants as they still have a large hole in them
Aang lands Appa on a small Earth Kingdom island
Sokka informs Aang that they have taken too many detours and need to reach the North Pole
A large fish springs out of the water
Aang proclaims the fish is why they came, then strips down to his underwear
Aang tries to show off to Katara by riding on the back of an elephant koi
The unagi attacks Aang
Aang, terrified, runs back to land and redresses into his airbender attire
The group is ambushed by a mysterious group of warriors
Katara, Sokka, and Aang are kidnapped and tied to a pillar
The warriors reveal themselves as the group's ambushers
Sokka, upon seeing the warriors are all female, remarks with misogynistic and sarcastic disbelief
One of the warriors threatens to throw Sokka to the unagi
Katara intercedes, apologizing and asking the warriors not to hurt Sokka, saying he can be an idiot sometimes
The islanders demand to know Aang and his friends' reason for being on the island
Aang says they came to ride elephant koi
Oyaji is skeptical and suspects they may be Fire Nation spies
Oyaji states that Kyoshi Island has stayed out of the Hundred Year War and intends to keep it that way
Aang responds that the island is named after Kyoshi and reveals that he knows her
Suki states that it is impossible for Aang to know Kyoshi
Oyaji elaborates that Avatar Kyoshi died over four hundred years ago
Aang tells them he is the new Avatar
The people of Kyoshi believe Aang is lying and want to throw him to the unagi
Aang airbends his way out of his predicament to prove he is the Avatar
The villagers are convinced and look on in awe
The news of the Avatar's return travels from person to person
News reaches Zuko and Iroh
Zuko jumps up in surprise
Zuko readies to depart to Kyoshi Island
Aang and Katara enjoy a delicious meal of desserts and treats, charitably given from the town
Aang and Katara enjoy a delicious meal of desserts and treats, charitably given from the town
Sokka lies on the floor, still somewhat beside himself at being defeated by girls
The town's people clean up the statue of Avatar Kyoshi in Aang's honor
Aang shows off to the young women of Kyoshi as they chase him around
Young women of Kyoshi chase Aang around
Katara is annoyed by Aang showing off
Sokka sees the Kyoshi Warriors practicing
Sokka asks where he might get a good workout
Suki asks if Sokka wants to show off some of his 'moves'
Sokka tries to demonstrate his techniques
Suki brings Sokka down
Sokka stammers that he was going easy on her
Sokka attempts to demonstrate again and fails
Suki ties Sokka arm-to-leg and swings him to the ground
Sokka leaves the dojo in embarrassment
Katara asks Aang if he can help her carry some supplies for their journey
Aang declines to help Katara
Aang asks Katara if she would like to go with him and the pack of young village girls on a trip on Appa
Katara rejects Aang's invitation
Aang accuses Katara of being jealous of him being in the company of the girls
Katara angrily storms off
Sokka returns to the training area and bows before Suki, admitting he would be honored to be trained by her
Suki makes sure Sokka will not care about being trained by a girl
Sokka admits he said some inappropriate things before and apologizes
Suki agrees to train Sokka on the condition that he undergo all their traditions, including adopting the Kyoshi Warrior uniform
Sokka complains about being in a dress
Suki points out the symbols of honor and bravery that make up the uniform
Sokka begins feeling proud to wear the uniform
Aang walks by giggling and compliments Sokka on his "nice dress"
Suki begins training Sokka in the art of Kyoshi style fighting
Sokka believes he is starting to understand the form after a little training
Sokka chucks his weapon out the door moments later
Suki laughs and explains it is not about strength, but about using an opponent's force and waiting for the right moment to strike
Sokka manages to knock down Suki
Suki, embarrassed, claims it was on purpose
Suki admits she was knocked down and asks Sokka to try again
Aang informs Katara that he is going to locate and ride the unagi
Aang and Katara have a heated exchange
Aang and Katara have a heated exchange
Aang runs off to ride the beast
Aang waits in the water while young ladies on the beach wait impatiently for the unagi's arrival
Young ladies on the beach wait impatiently for the unagi's arrival
The girls decide to leave after waiting
Katara shows up
Aang and Katara apologize to each other for their behavior
Aang and Katara apologize to each other for their behavior
Aang and Katara start to make fun of their previous behavior
Aang and Katara start to make fun of their previous behavior
The unagi appears, thrashing around and throwing Aang violently into the water
Aang falls unconscious
The unagi swims toward Aang to finish the attack
Katara rapidly swims to rescue Aang
Katara grabs hold of Aang
Katara uses her waterbending to propel herself and Aang toward land
Katara comes ashore after a fast escape
Katara hides behind some rocks
Katara spots Fire Nation troops approaching
Fire Nation troops pass by
Katara waterbends water out of Aang's throat
Aang coughs and wakes up
Aang tells Katara to not ride the unagi
Oyaji runs to Sokka, Suki, and the rest of the Kyoshi Warriors, urging them for help
Sokka, Suki, and the Kyoshi Warriors run toward the commotion
A battle ensues
The Kyoshi Warriors demonstrate their abilities and skills, taking out many firebenders despite being outnumbered
Fire Nation troops, led by Zuko, burn the town as they search for the Avatar
The Kyoshi Warriors attack the Fire Nation troops, knocking Zuko and several of his men from their rhinos
Suki and several Kyoshi Warriors surround Zuko
Zuko uses firebending to escape
Zuko calls out to Aang, sarcastically complimenting him on his use of the Kyoshi Warriors as an advance defensive force
Zuko sees Aang, and they engage in a short battle, causing further damage to the town
Aang engages in a short battle with Zuko, resulting in further damage to the town
Aang, distressed by the destruction, flees to find Katara
Aang wishes he could have prevented the destruction to the town
Katara tells Aang that if they flee, the firebenders will follow them
Aang, with no other options, agrees
Suki urges Sokka to hurry and leave
Sokka says he will not leave Suki, at least not without an apology
Suki asks Sokka why he wants an apology
Sokka explains he treated Suki like a girl instead of a warrior
Suki kisses Sokka on the cheek
Suki tells Sokka that while she is a warrior, she is a girl as well
Sokka blushes
Suki returns to battle
Suki gives Aang and his companions a chance to escape
Aang, Katara, and Sokka leave as the town is being set on fire
Zuko and his followers pursue Aang, Katara, and Sokka again
Katara tries to assure Aang that he did the right thing
Aang leaps down from Appa's back into the waters of the bay
The unagi bursts out of the water with Aang clinging onto its whiskers
Aang struggles and gains footing on the unagi's head, directing it toward the burning village
The unagi spews a blast of water from its mouth, extinguishing the fires
Aang leaps into the air and is caught by Appa
Aang, Katara, and Sokka fly away into the sunset as the unagi swims underneath them

The_King_of_Omashu.action: Aang and his friends arrive in the Earth Kingdom city of Omashu
Sokka and Katara are amazed at the sight of Omashu
Sokka and Katara note that they do not have any cities like Omashu at the South Pole, including buildings that do not melt in the heat
Aang wants to get to the city as soon as possible
Katara stops Aang, pointing out it could be dangerous if people find out he is the Avatar
Katara suggests Aang dons a disguise for the time being
Aang uses Appa’s shed fur to put on a makeshift wig and a fake mustache
Aang adopts an old man voice and uses his staff as a walking stick
Aang complains about the fur being itchy
Sokka and Katara are pleased with Aang’s disguise
Sokka says Aang looks just like his and Katara’s grandfather
Katara notes that Aang is technically 112-years-old and points out the disguise is fitting
Aang remarks that Omashu is one of the friendliest places in the world
guards at the city wall give a cabbage merchant trouble for trying to bring rotten cabbages into the city
guards earthbend the cabbage merchant’s vegetable-filled cart into the valley below
the cabbage merchant screams, "No! My cabbages!"
Aang tells Sokka and Katara to keep smiling
Aang, Sokka, and Katara approach the guards
guards immediately halt Aang and the siblings by levitating a boulder over Aang’s head
a guard demands Aang state their business
Aang, using his old man-voice, angrily claims that his business is his own and threatens to bend the soldier over his knee
The guard tells Aang to calm down and asks for his name
Aang introduces himself as "Bonzu Pippinpaddleopsicopolis the Third"
Aang claims that Sokka and Katara are his grandchildren
Katara introduces herself as "June Pippinpaddleopsicopolis"
The guard eyes them suspiciously for a moment
The guard tasks "June" with keeping her "grandfather" out of trouble in Omashu and lets them pass
Sokka walks by the guard
The guard captain stops Sokka, momentarily alarming Team Avatar
The guard berates Sokka for not carrying his grandfather's bag
Aang tosses the bag he was carrying to Sokka
They reach the wall, which is bent open, shocking the siblings with the immensity of the city
Team Avatar walks through the gates, which close behind them
Momo, hiding in Aang's wig, pokes his ears out, surprising the guard captain
The gate closes, hiding Team Avatar from sight before the guard captain can do anything
Aang shows them the Omashu delivery system, which consists of miles of tubes and chutes that send packages throughout the city via earthbending
Aang tells them that, one hundred years in the past, he and his eccentric childhood friend Bumi would slide down the Omashu delivery system for fun
Bumi tells Aang to always open his brain to the possibilities in life
Aang affectionately calls Bumi a "mad genius"
Aang promises Katara they will go to the North Pole after getting a ride down the chutes
Aang and Katara go to the highest point in the city and slide down
Aang and Katara are almost killed by a package of spears following behind them as they slide
Aang derails them from the chute
Their bin scrapes across the rooftops of several houses
Aang and Katara panic and cannot stop
Aang and Katara crash into the cabbage merchant's cart
Cabbage merchant screams in dismay for his ruined cabbages
Guards surround Aang and Katara and take them captive to be punished by the King of Omashu
The king looks at Aang for a while and, instead of punishment, orders a feast to be thrown for them
guards and Team Avatar are surprised by the king's decision
At the feast, the king inquires about where Aang came from
Aang lies that he came from Kangaroo Island
The king cracks a joke about Kangaroo Island being "really hoppin'"
Sokka laughs at the king's joke
The king feigns his departure from the table
The king suddenly throws a chicken drumstick at Aang
Aang catches the drumstick with airbending out of reflex, revealing he is the Avatar
Aang and the others try to leave
guards detain Aang and the others
the king declares that the Avatar shall face three deadly challenges the next day to win freedom for himself and his friends
the king locks Aang and the others in a recently refurbished chamber
Aang wakes and finds Sokka and Katara are missing
The king holds Sokka and Katara hostage and promises to return them once the challenges are completed
Aang threatens the king and demands he return his friends immediately
The king brings forth Sokka and Katara and has rings of jennamite placed on their fingers
The crystals (jennamite) begin to grow, threatening to cover Sokka and Katara’s bodies
Aang agrees to undertake the king’s challenges for the safety of his friends
Aang attempts to retrieve a "lunchbox" key hanging from a chain within a powerful, cascading waterfall
The force of the water prevents Aang from diving in or climbing the ladder
Aang tries to use airbending to get to the key, but fails
The crystal creeps up to cover Katara and Sokka's arms
Aang breaks the tip off a stalagmite
Aang uses airbending to shoot the stalagmite tip like a dart into the waterfall
The stalagmite breaks the chain and carries the key to the king
The king watches from a balcony
Aang must return the king's lost pet Flopsie to him as the second challenge
Aang sees a rabbit and chases after it, assuming it to be the King's pet
A large goat gorilla appears and begins to chase Aang
The rabbit escapes
The monster closes in on Aang
Aang suddenly has an idea and turns to face the monster
Aang realizes the creature is the real Flopsie
Flopsie picks Aang up tenderly and gives him a huge lick before dropping him
Flopsie runs to the king, who rubs the monster's belly
Katara and Sokka are covered from head to ankle in crystal
Aang demands the final challenge begin
Aang faces his final challenge in a large arena, where he must battle an opponent in a one-on-one duel of his own choosing
Aang is offered two options: a nasty-looking assassin with a war scythe and a hulking masked gladiator with an axe
Aang asks for clarification that he can fight whoever he points at
The king warns Aang to choose carefully
Aang chooses the king as his opponent
The king declares Aang made the wrong choice and removes his robes, revealing a muscular physique
Aang recoils, realizing his mistake
The king claims he is the most powerful earthbender Aang will ever meet and begins the battle
Aang asks to reconsider and choose the guy with the axe instead
The king refuses any “take-backsies” in his kingdom
The king launches several boulders at Aang
Aang dodges the boulders
The king taunts Aang for using a predictable airbender tactic
The king continues to taunt Aang for his unwillingness to fight back
Aang begins to counter the king
The king tears a massive chunk of earth out of the ground and holds it over his head
Aang screams in terror and begins to run in circles, airbending a tornado
The king throws the massive mound at Aang
Aang’s tornado redirects the mound back at the king
The king bisects the mound neatly in two
Aang appears out of the dust and pins the king with his staff
The king concedes defeat but requires the Avatar to figure out his name before releasing his friends
Aang recalls the point of all the challenges
Aang realizes the challenges forced him to think differently than usual
Aang realizes who the king is and calls him Bumi, his old childhood friend
Bumi, the old Monarch of Omashu, is revealed to be the king and a mad genius
Aang and Bumi embrace
Bumi declares Aang has not changed a bit, literally
Bumi shatters the crystal encasing Sokka and Katara
Bumi eats a piece of the crystal, revealing that jennamite is made from rock candy
Sokka and Katara are surprised to learn that the king is actually Aang's old friend
Sokka and Katara question Bumi on why he made Aang do all those challenges instead of just telling him who he really was
Bumi explains that, aside from finding it fun to mess with people, Aang has a difficult task to restore balance to the world by defeating Fire Lord Ozai
Bumi says that with how much the world has changed in the hundred years Aang was gone, Aang still has much to learn
Bumi hopes that when Aang learns, he will think "like a mad genius"
Aang thanks Bumi for all he has done
Aang challenges the king in return
Aang and Bumi slide down the Omashu delivery system like they used to a century prior
Aang and Bumi crash into the cabbage merchant's cart
The cabbage merchant's cart is crashed into

Imprisoned.action: Sokka arrives at camp with dinner, which is just some nuts
Team Avatar discusses their dinner options
Team Avatar discusses their dinner options
Team Avatar discusses their dinner options
Aang, Katara, and Sokka are startled by loud noises in the distance
Aang and Katara set out to investigate the noises
Aang and Katara set out to investigate the noises
Sokka warily follows Aang and Katara
Aang, Katara, and Sokka discover a young earthbender practicing bending
Katara introduces herself to the young earthbender
The young earthbender races off fearfully
Aang suggests following the young earthbender to find a town for dinner
Aang, Katara, and Sokka end up in a small settlement swarming with Fire Nation soldiers
They find the same young man and follow him into a store
They see the young man speaking with his mother
They inquire why the young man fled earlier
Haru and his mother explain that since the Fire Nation took control of the town, any earthbender discovered is arrested and sent to a prison rig far offshore
Haru's mother tells them that his father was one of those sent to the rig
Haru and Katara take a walk together, sharing stories and forming a strong bond
Haru and Katara come across an old man trapped by a cave-in
Haru reluctantly uses his earthbending to save the old man
Fire Nation soldiers and the old man whom Haru saved come and arrest Haru for earthbending during the night
Katara encounters Haru's mother the next morning
Haru's mother tells Katara that Haru was arrested and sent to the prison rig because the old man turned him in
Katara decides to take action and find a way onto the rig to free Haru
Katara concludes that the only way is to trick the Fire Nation into thinking she is an earthbender
Katara, with the help of Aang and some ventilation pipes, poses as an earthbender while Fire Nation soldiers are watching
Katara is apprehended and sent to the rig
Katara and several other captives meet the prison warden, a self-important and cruel taskmaster
The warden states that the earthbenders are helpless since the entire rig is made of metal and they have nothing to control
Katara inquires Haru and Tyro about their escape plan
Tyro says escape is not an option and survival depends on obeying and hoping for the war to end in their favor
Katara delivers an inspirational speech to lift the spirits of the other benders
The other benders ignore Katara's speech
Katara meets up with Aang and Sokka at night to devise a way for the prisoners to regain confidence and rebel
Aang notices smokestacks in the distance
Aang states that the smokestacks are burning coal, meaning there is earth on the rig for the prisoners to bend
Sokka instructs Aang to close all the vents in the vicinity where coal is being burned, except for one leading to the outer vent where Katara and Sokka are
Aang closes all the vents except for one
The warden discovers Katara and Sokka and threatens them to surrender or die
Coal bursts from the vent and lands before Haru and the others
Katara states this is their chance to stand up and fight
No one seems to have the will to revolt
The warden claims the prisoners' spirits were broken long before Katara's arrival and walks away in triumph
As the warden departs, a chunk of coal strikes him on the back of the head
The warden turns to see Haru, who bears a look of rage
The warden attacks Haru
Tyro and two other benders block the warden's assault
The prisoners begin to regain hope and join in battling the Fire Nation soldiers
The prisoners and Team Avatar defeat the Fire Nation soldiers after a dramatic struggle
Tyro prepares to throw the soldiers and the warden overboard
The warden pleads that he cannot swim
Tyro responds that he heard cowards float and drops them all in the water
Team Avatar and the earthbenders sail away in stolen Fire Nation ships
Tyro and Haru express their gratitude to Katara for renewing their people's hope
Katara is touched by Haru's words
Katara notices that her mother's necklace is no longer around her neck
Katara feels distressed about losing her mother's necklace
Zuko finds the necklace with its fabric strap torn
Zuko takes the necklace
Zuko looks out toward the horizon with a blank look

Winter_Solstice,_Part_1:_The_Spirit_Worl.action: The group arrives at an Earth Kingdom forest and sees a huge black scar in the woods
They land and discover the forest was burned down by Fire Nation soldiers
Aang feels guilty and blames himself for the incident, stating it is the Avatar's duty to protect nature
Katara assures Aang the forest will grow back and shows him the abundance of acorns on the ground
The party is startled when an old man comes asking the Avatar for help
The old man leads them to Senlin Village, where many buildings are in ruins
Aang learns the village is being terrorized by a spirit known as Hei Bai
The villagers ask Aang for help
Aang agrees to help, though he is skeptical of what he can do to save the village
Iroh relaxes in a self-heated pool of water, turning it into his own personal jacuzzi
Zuko becomes impatient and demands that Iroh get ready to leave so they can continue searching for the Avatar
Iroh agrees to leave
Iroh stands up and reveals his naked body
Zuko, disgusted, shields his eyes and tells Iroh he can take a few more minutes
Zuko warns Iroh that if he is not ready within a half-hour, he will be left behind
Iroh slumps back into the pool and quickly falls asleep
He is awoken some time later by the sound of a meadow vole, initially thinking it to be an ambush
He overcomes his disorientation and notices the sound came from the meadow vole, then relaxes
He gently takes the meadow vole into his hands and begins talking to it
Iroh realizes he has missed Zuko's time limit but does not seem to care, saying he had "a very sweet nap"
Earth Kingdom soldiers suddenly ambush Iroh, recognizing his position as a former Fire Nation general
Aang goes out to confront the spirit at sunset
Aang waits for the spirit’s appearance
Aang begins to walk away when the spirit fails to appear
The massive form of the spirit appears behind Aang
Aang tries to talk to the spirit
The spirit ignores Aang and destroys houses with huge energy blasts from its mouth
Sokka runs out to help Aang
Sokka is captured and taken away by the spirit
Aang chases after Sokka and the spirit
Aang finds himself unexpectedly in the Spirit World
Zuko arrives with two soldiers
Zuko finds that the pool of water where Iroh rested has a landslide-like formation
Zuko deduces that Iroh was captured by earthbenders
Zuko sets out to find his uncle's attackers
Earth Kingdom soldiers travel with Iroh chained up on ostrich horses
Earth Kingdom soldiers plan to bring Iroh back to Ba Sing Se
Iroh acknowledges his defeat, stating he and his men were tired and that he still is
Iroh feigns falling asleep
Iroh secretly leaves his stinky sandal for Zuko to find
Aang is confronted by a dragon
The dragon, revealed to be Avatar Roku's dragon Fang, takes Aang to the temple on a crescent-shaped island
Aang is brought to see Avatar Roku's statue
Aang finds out that Avatar Roku wants to speak to him about a comet
Aang learns that the only time they can talk is during the winter solstice
Aang’s journey with Fang is spotted by Iroh
Iroh uses the opportunity to trick his captors by asking them to tighten his chains
The captain orders a soldier to tighten Iroh’s chains
Iroh stealthily snorts onto the cuffs, heating them up
The soldier touches the cuffs, burns his hand, and screams in agony
Iroh quickly attempts to escape, but ultimately fails
Iroh is caught by the soldiers
The soldiers debate among themselves about what to do
The dragon takes Aang back to his body
Aang learns why the spirit is terrorizing the village: the spirit was distraught over the destruction of its home, the forest
Aang returns to the village
Katara is relieved at Aang's return
Aang reports that he has not found Sokka, but is now ready to face the spirit
earthbenders decide to crush Iroh's hands by dropping a large rock on them
Zuko appears and kicks the rock aside
Zuko lifts his leg and breaks the chains binding Iroh in half with one swift movement
Iroh praises Zuko, saying "Excellent form, Prince Zuko."
Zuko smiles and replies, "You taught me well."
Zuko and Iroh defeat the soldiers without much difficulty
Zuko tells Iroh, "Now would you please put on some clothes?"

Winter_Solstice,_Part_2:_Avatar_Roku.action: Aang packs his things on Appa
Aang attempts to convince Appa to fly
Appa refuses to move, acting stubborn
Aang tells Appa that Katara and Sokka are not going with them
Aang decides to make the journey to the temple alone
Aang pulls hard on the reins and is thrown to the ground
Katara, Sokka, and villagers from Senlin Village stop Aang, believing the trip is unnecessary
Aang insists that Avatar Roku can tell him the meaning of his vision of a comet
Katara and Sokka decide to let Aang go on the condition that they accompany him
The village leader gives Aang supplies and tells him to leave immediately to reach the temple before the solstice ends
Aang tries to thank the leader for his kindness
The leader cuts Aang off mid-sentence, yelling at him to leave instantly
Aang leaves immediately
Later, there is a knock at the leader’s door
The leader opens the door to find Prince Zuko
Zuko forces the leader back through his door, demanding to know where the Avatar went
Appa flies through the sky
Katara spots Zuko's ship trailing them
Iroh scolds Zuko for entering Fire Nation waters in pursuit of the Avatar
Iroh reminds Zuko that he was banished and risks arrest if he continues
Zuko tells Iroh that his father will understand because he is chasing the Avatar
Iroh responds that Zuko is giving Ozai too much credit and that Ozai is not understanding
Katara and Sokka watch as a catapult is readied on Zuko's ship
Katara and Sokka watch as a catapult is readied on Zuko's ship
The catapult's missile is set on fire and hurled at Appa
Appa barely dodges the shot
Zuko orders the catapult to be reloaded
Zuko sees a blockade of Fire Nation ships in the distance
Iroh tells Zuko there is still time to stop since they are in Earth Kingdom waters
Zuko refuses to turn away and orders to "run the blockade"
Zhao commands the blockade and looks through a spyglass, recognizing both Appa and Zuko's ship
Zhao orders his ships to fire on the Avatar
One of Zhao's men informs him that there is a Fire Nation vessel in their line of fire
Zhao states that the Fire Nation vessel belongs to a traitor and gives the attack order again
Dozens of catapults fire shots toward Appa, creating a firestorm
Aang prepares to dodge the barrage of attacks as the first few shots whiz by Appa
One of the shots connects with Zuko's ship, causing it to smoke heavily
One of Zuko's men yells that the engines are damaged and they will need to stop to repair them
Zuko refuses to relent and continues to order the ship forward
Aang dodges a swarm of shots
The force of a blast created by the collision of two fireballs knocks Sokka from Appa's saddle
Sokka plummets toward the water
Appa dives down and catches Sokka mere feet above the water
Aang directs Appa to charge right down the middle toward the blockade
Zhao takes a final point-blank shot at Aang
Aang throws himself off Appa's head directly at the fireball, destroying it with airbending
Aang, Katara, and Sokka soar past Zhao and his blockade
Aang expresses his excitement over reaching the Fire Nation
Katara and Sokka barely speak, having been thoroughly shaken by their encounter with Zhao
Zhao is displeased that the Avatar broke through the blockade and knows he has to follow him, but does not know where he is going
Zuko's ship approaches the blockade
Zhao allows Zuko to pass through the blockade untouched, realizing he can follow the ship to the Avatar
Appa spends hours flying through the air
Appa arrives at the crescent-shaped island where Fang took Aang
Appa lands and takes a well-deserved rest
The others enter the temple
The group finds the first room of the temple empty
Five men dressed in red robes suddenly appear behind the group
The men reveal themselves as Fire Sages, guardians of the Avatar's temple
Aang reveals himself as the Avatar, thinking the sages will help him
The sages unexpectedly attack Aang
Aang blocks the attacks
The group runs from the five sages, going down many different halls
The group reaches a dead end
The group turns around and finds one of the sages blocking their escape
Aang prepares to fight the sage
The sage explains he is on their side
The group is skeptical at first
The sage hears the others approach and opens a secret passageway
The group escapes from the other sages through the secret passageway
Zuko is confused as to why Zhao let him pass
Iroh states that Zhao wants to follow Zuko, knowing Zuko will lead him to the Avatar
Zuko believes his uncle to be right
Zuko muses that if Zhao wants to follow their trail of smoke, he can do just that
the friendly sage tells them his name is Shyu
Shyu explains that when Avatar Roku died, the other sages, including his grandfather, waited for the new Avatar to appear, but that never happened
Shyu says that when Fire Lord Sozin started the Hundred Year War, he forced the sages to serve him
Shyu says only his family remained loyal to the Avatar
Shyu explains that when the sages found out Aang was alive, he knew he would have to betray the others to help Aang
they arrive on a higher level of the temple where a huge door is located
Shyu tells them the door must have recently been closed and only a fully realized Avatar or five fire blasts from five different firebenders can open it
none of them know how to open the door
Sokka begins to formulate a plan
Zuko embarks a smaller vessel
Zuko orders his uncle to continue their northern course
Zuko explains that Zhao will follow the smoke trail, allowing him to use it as cover to sneak away
Zhao keeps track of Zuko's ship via a telescope from a distance
Sokka reveals his plan to the group
Sokka makes five small oil-based bombs
Sokka places one bomb in each of the five holes in the door
Shyu lights the fuse
The group stands back and takes cover
The explosion of the grenades rips through the room
Shyu warns Aang to be quick, as the explosion will warn the other sages
Aang rushes to the door
Aang finds the door has not opened and assaults it with waves of air in frustration
Sokka expresses confusion as to why the door did not open
Katara states that even though the plan did not work, it looked as though it did
The group is confused by the cryptic nature of Katara’s statement
Shyu yells to the other sages to come to the door
Shyu states that the Avatar has entered the room
The sages do not know how the Avatar entered
Shyu points out burn marks on the door and a moving shadow from the other side
The sages combine their firebending and open the door
The sages discover the shadow was Momo, who had crawled through the pipes
Each of the four sages are grabbed by Shyu, Katara, Sokka, and Momo
Shyu yells for Aang to enter the chamber
There is no answer from Aang
Zuko, restraining Aang, appears from behind a pillar, stunning Team Avatar
The Fire Sages break free and restrain their captors
Zuko tells the sages to close the door immediately
Aang turns to see the others being tied to a pillar
Aang catches Zuko off guard, struggles free, and prepares to rescue Katara and Sokka
Katara yells at Aang to leave them
Aang vaults over the heads of two attacking sages and through the closing doors of Roku's sanctuary
The doors seal
A bright light emanates from the room and dies away
Aang, now sealed in the chamber, looks up at the statue of Avatar Roku and wonders what to do next
Zuko and the four sages attempt vainly to open the doors from outside
The four sages attempt vainly to open the doors from outside
One of the sages states that Avatar Roku must have sealed the door, not wanting them to enter
Aang, becoming upset, calls out to Roku for help
Sunlight from above the door spreads over the statue’s face; its eyes glow white and a gust of cloud and smoke spirals around Aang
When the clouds dissipate, Aang finds himself on a desolate mountaintop before the apparition of Roku
Roku greets Aang with a smile and asks what took him so long
Back outside, Zuko asks Shyu why he helped Aang
Shyu says it is the Fire Sages’ duty to help the Avatar
Zhao and a group of soldiers suddenly appear, surprising Zuko and his captive
Zhao orders his troops to arrest Zuko
Zuko angrily taunts Zhao that Aang is locked away from him in the sanctum
Zhao tells Zuko he will simply wait for the Avatar to come out
Roku begins to tell Aang why they had to meet and elaborates on the significance of the comet
Roku states that about a hundred years prior, a comet appeared in the sky, greatly amplifying firebenders' abilities
Fire Lord Sozin uses the comet's power to start the Hundred Year War, dealing a deadly first strike against the other nations
Roku tells Aang that by the end of summer, the comet will return and Fire Lord Ozai will use its power to conquer the world if left unchecked
Roku adds that Aang must master water, earth, and fire before summer's end to restore balance to the world
Aang is shocked by Roku's revelation, doubting his ability to master the elements in such a short time
Roku expresses confidence in Aang, telling him he has done so in the past
As the solstice nears its end, Aang points out he will not be able to return to the Fire Temple if he has more questions
Roku reminds Aang that, as an Avatar, he is a part of him and promises Aang will find a way to talk to him if needed
Roku states that a great danger is waiting for Aang at the temple and offers his help if Aang thinks he is ready
Aang, speaking with the voices of his past lives, his eyes and tattoos blazing white, replies that he is ready
Zhao and his soldiers prepare to attack Aang when he emerges from the chamber
The door opens with a flash of light and a billow of smoke
Zhao and his soldiers firebend at the figure behind the door
Zhao and his soldiers are shocked and horrified to see Avatar Roku emerge instead of Aang
Avatar Roku takes control of their fire and sends it back at them with great force, knocking his attackers backward
Avatar Roku melts the chains holding Katara, Sokka, Shyu, and Zuko, releasing them
Zuko escapes immediately
Zhao and his soldiers escape
Avatar Roku turns his glowing eyes to the treacherous Fire Sages
The Fire Sages flee the scene rather than face Roku's wrath
Shyu tells Katara and Sokka to escape before Roku destroys the temple
Katara and Sokka refuse to leave without Aang
Katara and Sokka refuse to leave without Aang
Avatar Roku splits the ground, bending up magma from the volcano below and bringing it into the temple
Zhao and his men retreat to their ships as the temple begins to crumble
The temple begins to crumble
The sun sets, marking the end of the solstice
The ray of sunlight moves away from the face of Roku's statue and the light from its eyes fades
Roku exhales, his work complete, and is wrapped in a whirling cloud of smoke
The dust clears, revealing an utterly spent Aang
Aang collapses to the floor
Katara and Sokka pick up Aang
Katara and Sokka pick up Aang
The temple falls apart around them
The group realizes they have no way to escape, as all exits except a hole in the wall are blocked by lava and debris
Appa and Momo, now wearing the Great Sage's hat, appear
The group jumps through the opening into Appa's saddle
The group jumps through the opening into Appa's saddle
The group jumps through the opening into Appa's saddle
They fly away from the temple
The temple collapses into the lava
Zhao looks up from the deck of his ship and sees the Avatar flying away
Zhao becomes enraged at his failure to capture both Zuko and the Avatar
Zhao angrily states that all he has are five traitors in his custody, referring to the five Fire Sages
The Great Sage tries to convince Zhao that only Shyu helped the Avatar
Zhao ignores the Great Sage's plea
Zhao orders that all five Fire Sages be thrown into the prison hold
Zuko watches as the Avatar flies away from his own ship
Katara kneels around Aang on Appa, comforting him
Sokka kneels around Aang on Appa, comforting him

The_Waterbending_Scroll.action: Aang receives Avatar Roku's warning of the impending comet
Aang becomes anxious and worries about the short time remaining to master the three remaining bending arts
Katara offers to teach Aang what she knows of waterbending
Aang graciously accepts Katara's offer
Zuko spars with Lieutenant Jee
Iroh diverts the course of the ship to find a port
Iroh seeks to replace his lost white lotus tile for his Pai Sho game
Zuko becomes furious about the detour
Zuko vents his rage in a cloud of smoke and flame through his nose and mouth
Smoke suffuses the bridge
Iroh calmly declares he is lucky to have such an understanding nephew
Katara begins to teach Aang her knowledge of waterbending at a waterfall
Aang immediately masters the lessons with little to no effort
Katara is displeased by Aang's quick mastery
Katara tries to hide her feelings
Katara begins to grow jealous of Aang's natural talent
Aang overdoes a waterbending move and accidentally washes away their supplies
The trio ventures into a nearby town to buy replacements
Aang purchases a bison whistle on an impulse
The trio comes upon a ship docked with a barker trying to attract business
The ship's crew sells items acquired by "high risk trading"
Katara searches the wares and discovers a scroll featuring several waterbending techniques
The pirates reserve the scroll for a wealthy client in the Earth Kingdom
The captain offers to sell the scroll for two hundred gold pieces if available immediately
Aang offers one copper piece for the scroll, stating pirates love to haggle
The pirates laugh and reject Aang's offer
Aang ups his offer to two coppers, angering the captain
Katara pleads with Aang to stop trying, saying they are drawing too much attention
Aang complies with a pirate-style "Aye, we be castin' off now" and they leave the ship
Aang and Sokka, out of earshot of the pirates, raise objections to leaving so soon
Sokka raises objections to leaving so soon
Katara states she does not feel comfortable
The pirate crew begins to pursue Aang and his friends, brandishing weapons
Aang initially believes the pirates have mellowed to his haggling
Aang and his friends are forced to flee
Aang airbends the cabbage merchant's cart toward some of the incoming pirates, destroying it
The cabbage merchant's cart is destroyed
Aang becomes cornered by the pirates
Aang uses his glider to escape, flying away as Katara and Sokka hang on
