Katara and Sokka hang on to Aang as he escapes
Katara and Sokka hang on to Aang as he escapes
Aang, Katara, and Sokka return to their camp by the waterfall
Aang, Katara, and Sokka return to their camp by the waterfall
Aang, Katara, and Sokka return to their camp by the waterfall
Aang and Sokka find that Katara has stolen the waterbending scroll
Aang and Sokka find that Katara has stolen the waterbending scroll
The pirates were seeking to retrieve the waterbending scroll
Sokka objects to Katara's theft
Katara regurgitates the pirates' nomenclature for theft at Sokka
Sokka remains displeased
Aang laughs and says, "What's done is done; we might as well learn from it."
Zuko and Iroh arrive at the same port visited by Team Avatar earlier that day
Iroh fails to find a replacement white lotus tile, to Zuko's irritation
Iroh purchases numerous trinkets and instruments for music night on the ship
Three of their troops carry the trinkets and instruments
Zuko overhears a conversation between two pirates
Zuko discovers the pirates were tracking a bald-headed monk and a Water Tribe girl
Zuko whirls on the pirates and demands to know if the boy had an arrow on his head
Katara and Aang begin to practice waterbending at the waterfall
Katara and Aang begin to practice waterbending at the waterfall
Katara and Aang try the water whip technique
Katara and Aang try the water whip technique
Katara attempts the water whip first and accidentally strikes herself and Momo
Katara becomes embarrassed after striking herself and Momo
Aang explains to Katara how to shift through the stances
Aang succeeds in his first attempt at the water whip
Katara grows frustrated and offended by Aang's talent
Katara launches into an angry rant, reprimanding and insulting Aang
Katara sarcastically claims they should dispose of the scroll
Sokka gives Katara a quick glance of disapproval
Katara realizes her outburst has driven Aang to the verge of tears
Katara apologizes to Aang for her rant and to Momo for striking him
Katara gives the scroll to Aang, saying she wants nothing to do with it
Katara succumbs to temptation and slips away with the scroll to study the technique again while Sokka and Aang sleep
Zuko joins forces with the pirates to hunt down Aang
a pirate asks Zuko if they need to search the woods
Zuko replies that, since they stole a waterbending scroll, they will be near a source of water
Zuko and the pirates search for the group
Zuko and the pirates find Katara practicing the water whip
Zuko and the pirates ambush Katara
Zuko ties Katara to a tree
Zuko threatens to hurt Katara and Sokka unless Katara divulges the whereabouts of the Avatar
Katara initially refuses to give up Aang’s location
Zuko reveals Katara’s mother’s lost betrothal necklace to her
Zuko attempts to convince Katara to exchange Aang’s freedom for her memento
Katara firmly refuses a second time
the pirates demand the scroll, seeing the interrogation as a waste of time
Zuko uses the scroll as collateral and threatens to destroy it by lighting a fire underneath it
Zuko forces the pirates to go search for Aang
the pirates begrudgingly comply
Sokka wakes up at the campsite and notices Katara is missing
Sokka looks through his bag and realizes the scroll is gone
Sokka tells a waking Aang that Katara ran off with the scroll
Sokka comments that trouble will follow them because of Katara's actions
The pirates ambush Sokka and Aang
Sokka evades immediate capture
Aang fails to airbend a net away and is quickly captured
Aang is dragged away by the pirates
The pirate Sokka was battling follows the Avatar
Sokka calls after them, asking if he is not good enough to be kidnapped
Sokka is captured by the pirates
Sokka and Aang are taken to the river's shore, bound by their hands and, in Aang's case, arms
The pirates regroup with Zuko and his soldiers, who had been guarding Katara while she was bound to a tree
Zuko comments on the pirates' success and approaches them with the waterbending scroll
Katara tries to apologize to Aang, claiming responsibility for their situation
Aang brushes off Katara's apology
Zuko and the pirates attempt to make a trade: scroll for Avatar, but neither side wants to exchange their collateral first
Sokka asks the pirates if they really are going to hand over the Avatar for a "piece of parchment"
The pirates are surprised by the revelation that Aang is the Avatar
Sokka suggests the pirates could make more money trading Aang on the black market or handing him over to the Fire Lord
The pirate captain is convinced, calls off the deal, and tells Zuko to keep the scroll as he turns and walks away
Zuko is outraged and, along with his soldiers, attacks the pirates
Aang, Katara, and Sokka escape, taking advantage of the distraction from the fighting
Katara and Aang use waterbending to get the pirates' boat into the water and sail away
Iroh points out the escape in the midst of the fighting
Zuko laughs at Iroh's observation
The pirates, except for their leader, steal Zuko's smaller ship and start chasing after Team Avatar, infuriating Zuko
Team Avatar battles the pirates aboard the pirate ship
Katara successfully performs the water whip upon re-attempting it
The ship approaches a towering waterfall
Aang and Katara use waterbending to stop the ship
The pirates ram into the ship with Zuko's boat, sending them falling over the waterfall
Appa appears, having heard Aang's bison whistle, and catches Team Avatar, saving them from plummeting to their deaths
Iroh discovers that his "lost" white lotus tile was tucked away in his sleeve the entire time
Iroh produces the white lotus tile for Zuko with a great smile on his face
Zuko becomes enraged
Zuko snatches the tile from Iroh's hand
Zuko hurls the tile over the waterfall
The tile strikes one of the pirates far below on the head
Katara gives Aang a full apology
Sokka reveals he was able to regain the scroll during the chaos
Katara tries to take the scroll from Sokka excitedly
Sokka asks Katara what she has learned from their encounter
Katara replies that she learned to never steal
Katara reclaims the scroll from Sokka
Katara adds, "Unless it's from pirates!"
Appa flies away
Aang and Katara laugh
Aang and Katara laugh

Jet_(episode).action: Momo stumbles into a hunter's trap and is left caged, hanging from a tree branch
Aang, Katara, and Sokka notice Momo is missing and set out to find him
Aang, Katara, and Sokka notice Momo is missing and set out to find him
Aang, Katara, and Sokka notice Momo is missing and set out to find him
They locate Momo suspended in a tree
Aang uses airbending to move toward the trap and free Momo
Aang discovers hog monkeys have also been trapped
Aang readies himself to scale the tree again to free the hog monkeys
Sokka uses his boomerang to cut the cords and free the animals in one go
Sokka examines the traps and determines they were constructed by the Fire Nation
Sokka advises the group to leave quickly
Sokka decides they should travel on foot, as Appa is too easy to spot
Katara protests, opposing Sokka's notion that he is the leader and suggests Aang should be the leader
Sokka counters that Aang cannot be the leader since he is just a "goofy kid"
Aang agrees with Sokka's statement
Katara teases Sokka, saying he would not be so bossy if he kissed a girl
Sokka claims he already has kissed someone that Katara does not know
Katara smugly notes that she has met their grandmother
Sokka quickly changes the subject and reiterates they should walk to stay unnoticed
Aang and Katara relent to Sokka's plan
Aang and Katara relent to Sokka's plan
Aang grows frustrated over their slow progress
Aang and Katara start to tease Sokka
Aang and Katara start to tease Sokka
Their banter is interrupted when they accidentally stumble into the middle of a Fire Nation campsite
The trio panics, sheds their bags, and tries to run away
A firebender sets the bushes behind them ablaze, cutting off their escape route
Soldiers corner the trio
Sokka tries to bluff, promising not to hurt the soldiers if they let them pass safely
A soldier with an eye patch laughs at Sokka’s bluff
The eye-patch soldier suddenly falls to the ground, unconscious
Aang compliments Sokka, asking how he did that
Sokka responds, "instinct," quizzically
Katara points out a young man standing atop a tree branch, solving the mystery
The young man readies his dual hook swords and jumps down, landing atop two soldiers and knocking them out
The young man sweeps the feet out from underneath two other soldiers with his weapons
The young man fights off an incoming soldier
A small child drops down on the shoulders of another soldier, blinding him by pushing his helmet over his eyes
An archer disarms five more soldiers
More children and teenagers engage the remaining soldiers in battle
Aang and Katara use their respective bending arts to join the fight
Katara uses her bending arts to join the fight
Each time Sokka prepares to engage a soldier, the children's leader knocks out the soldier first
After the troops are defeated, the leader introduces himself as Jet and his allies as the Freedom Fighters: Sneers, Longshot, Smellerbee, The Duke, and Pipsqueak
Aang, thinking The Duke is named Pipsqueak, comments that it is a funny name
Pipsqueak bends down menacingly and asks if Aang truly thinks his name is funny
Aang happily notes that it is hilarious
The trio laughs, and Pipsqueak smacks Aang jovially on the back, knocking him over
Katara develops a large crush on Jet and frequently flatters him
Katara and Jet chat, indirectly insulting Sokka's instinctual behavior
The Duke and Pipsqueak raid the abandoned Fire Nation campsite, finding crates of jelly candy and barrels of blasting jelly
Jet asks The Duke and Pipsqueak to keep the jelly candy and blasting jelly separate
The Freedom Fighters mention bringing their score back to their hideout
Aang expresses interest in seeing the hideout
Jet invites Aang, Katara, and Sokka to come with him
Katara eagerly accepts Jet's invitation on behalf of the group
The group reaches the site of the Freedom Fighters' hideout, but nothing is in sight
Sokka notes in disbelief that there is nothing in sight
Jet offers Sokka a rope dangling from the branches above
Sokka takes the rope and is yanked upward through the canopy, screaming
Aang uses airbending to jump into the trees
Jet holds Katara as he is pulled up into the trees
Katara blushes deeply at her close contact with Jet during the ascent
Upon reaching the hideout, Jet explains the Freedom Fighters' mission to Katara
The Fire Nation takes over the Earth Kingdom village, Gaipan
The Freedom Fighters ambush Fire Nation troops and attempt to cut off the town's supply lines
The Freedom Fighters aim to force the Fire Nation from the town permanently
Katara expresses infatuation with Jet and praises his bravery
Sokka makes a sarcastic comment in response to Katara's praise
Jet shows no ill-will in response to Sokka's remark
Jet explains to Katara that all the Freedom Fighters live in their treetop base and have no families
Longshot's village is burned to the ground
The Duke is found on his own
Jet reveals he lost his parents to the Fire Nation at age eight
Katara feels great sympathy for Jet, having also lost her mother to the Fire Nation
Jet gives a speech to all of the Freedom Fighters, congratulating them on their victory over the Fire Nation
Jet privately compliments Aang and Katara for their role in the battle and their bending proficiency
Katara blushes and diverts attention from herself by revealing that Aang is the Avatar
Jet learns Aang is the Avatar and states he hopes to have Aang and Katara help in their battle with the Fire Nation
Sokka quickly interjects, stating that they plan to leave that very night
Jet replies with feigned disappointment, saying he hoped Sokka would assist him in a mission the following morning
Sokka hesitantly accepts Jet's request
Sokka waits in the trees overlooking a path with Jet
Sokka uses a trick with his machete to amplify vibrations and alerts Jet when an old Fire Nation civilian approaches
Jet and two other Freedom Fighters, Smellerbee and Pipsqueak, descend toward the path
Sokka states that the man approaching is just feeble and old
Jet and his Freedom Fighters bully the old man, eventually knocking him prone onto the ground
Jet ruthlessly ignores the man's pleas for mercy
Sokka stops Jet from striking the man further and tells him to stop his actions
Jet orders the man be searched, further angering Sokka
Sokka insists the man is harmless and innocent
Jet justifies his actions by stating the man is with the Fire Nation
Jet and the Freedom Fighters leave with the man's belongings
Sokka reluctantly follows, regret evident in his expression
Aang finds Sokka deep in thought, leaning against a tree
Aang enjoys hanging out with The Duke
Katara makes Jet a hat from the leaves of the trees
Sokka dismisses this and announces that they are leaving
Aang and Katara object to leaving
Aang and Katara object to leaving
Sokka reveals Jet's previous bullying actions
Katara demands to hear Jet's side of the story
Jet reiterates that the man was with the Fire Nation and produces a knife with a compartment for poison in the hilt
Jet claims the man was an assassin sent to kill him
Sokka doubts Jet's story and states he never saw a knife in the man's belongings
Katara is convinced by Jet's explanation
Aang is convinced by Jet's explanation
Sokka storms off, stating he will prepare to leave
Jet pleads for Katara and Aang to stay, stating the Fire Nation plans to burn down the forest and he needs their waterbending to fill the reservoir
Katara approaches Sokka with Aang and adamantly refuses to leave until they have helped Jet
Katara accuses Sokka of acting out of jealousy and refuses to listen to his reasons
Sokka overhears Jet and the core of the Freedom Fighters sneaking out of their base
Sokka decides to follow Jet and the Freedom Fighters
Jet and the Freedom Fighters bring barrels of blasting jelly on a cart
Jet reviews their plan to blow up the barrels beneath the dam of the river
Jet explains the plan will flood the village and wipe out the Fire Nation soldiers
The Duke expresses concern for the innocent Earth Kingdom citizens
Jet replies that the sacrifice must be made
Sokka overhears the conversation
Smellerbee and Pipsqueak catch Sokka
Sokka is dragged out to face Jet at dawn
Sokka confronts Jet about the plan
Jet attempts to persuade Sokka to persist in the plan
Jet realizes Sokka will not change his decision
Jet has Pipsqueak and Smellerbee walk Sokka into the forest to prevent him from warning Aang and Katara
Jet leads Aang and Katara to an extremely shallow river leading into the reservoir
Katara apologizes for Sokka's crude behavior
Jet lies that Sokka has already apologized and is out scouting with Smellerbee and Pipsqueak
Aang and Katara find it strange that Sokka would apologize quickly, but do not question Jet's word
Jet covers himself by saying Katara talked to Sokka to make him relax
a steam geyser goes off directly under Aang's feet, launching him into the air
Jet states they have reached their destination as Aang floats safely back down
Jet explains to Aang and Katara their role in the mission: to bend water beneath the vents
Katara doubts her ability to bend unseen water
Jet encourages Katara, placing his hands on her shoulders
Katara and Aang successfully release water from one of the vents
Jet leaves Aang and Katara to their work, saying he will be at the reservoir and meet them at the hideout
Jet underestimates the time Aang and Katara need to release enough geysers
Aang and Katara quickly raise the river to a high enough level and decide to go meet Jet at the reservoir
Sokka, with his hands bound, is marched through the forest by Smellerbee and Pipsqueak
Smellerbee and Pipsqueak march Sokka through the forest
Sokka attempts to convince Smellerbee and Pipsqueak to reconsider Jet's plan
Smellerbee and Pipsqueak refuse to listen, remaining loyal to Jet
Sokka gives up trying to reason with them
Sokka spots Fire Nation traps in the forest
Sokka breaks away from his escorts
Sokka tricks Smellerbee and Pipsqueak into pursuing him into the traps
Smellerbee and Pipsqueak are caught and caged, suspended from the branches above
Sokka frees his hands
Sokka quickly moves to stop Jet's plan
Aang and Katara come to the edge of a cliff overlooking the dam
Aang and Katara spot four Freedom Fighters setting up barrels of blasting jelly
Aang realizes Jet's plan to blow the dam and flood the village
Aang moves to fly down on his glider to stop the plan
Katara is in denial about Jet's plan
Jet jumps forward and snags Aang's glider with his hook swords, preventing Aang from flying off
Jet confesses his true plan to Katara
Jet attempts to convince Katara that blowing the dam is necessary
Jet argues that he and Katara must be merciless to prevent the Fire Nation from hurting anyone again
Katara is nonplussed by Jet's argument
Jet states he hoped Katara and Sokka would understand his reasons
Katara demands to know where Sokka is and begins to cry
Jet places a comforting hand on Katara's cheek
Katara draws water from her water skin and violently throws Jet to the ground
Aang goes for his glider to prevent the explosion
Jet reaches the glider first, snatching it with his hook swords and placing it on his back
Jet engages Aang in combat
Jet adapts an aggressive fighting style, swinging his swords dangerously
Aang avoids almost all of Jet's attacks using agility and airbending
Aang is reluctant to fight Jet and fails to reclaim his glider for some time
Aang blows Jet back with a powerful airbending move, sending Jet plummeting to the ground
Jet catches a branch with his swords
Aang's glider drops from Jet's back
Aang and Jet race to the ground after the glider
Aang and Jet race to the ground after the glider
Jet kicks Aang from behind in mid-air, causing Aang to crash into a branch and fall, dazed
Aang crashes into a branch and falls to the ground, extremely dazed
Katara arrives and bends water from a nearby stream to attack Jet, pushing him back and freezing him to a tree
Katara berates Jet for betraying her trust
Katara is startled by a bird call from the distance
Jet responds with his own call, signaling to set off the explosion
Jet reveals he has delayed Aang and Katara long enough for his Freedom Fighters to set up the blasting jelly
Aang tries to fly on his glider, but it fails due to a torn main sail
Aang and Katara wait and hope that Sokka succeeds where they have failed
Aang and Katara wait and hope that Sokka succeeds where they have failed
Longshot, perched in a tree, fires a flaming arrow, igniting the jelly and wrecking the dam
The entire village is inundated in seconds
Katara, enraged, turns on Jet and resumes insulting him for his actions
Jet does not back down, stating this act is a victory against the Fire Nation
Sokka arrives, riding Appa and accompanied by Momo
Jet is infuriated to learn Sokka warned the villagers and the town was evacuated
Jet explains that Sokka's warnings were reinforced by the elderly man from the previous day
Everyone is safely escorted out of the village to safety
Jet calls Sokka a fool, believing he ruined the people's chance for freedom
Sokka responds that there would be no one left alive to appreciate the freedom
Jet declares Sokka a traitor
Sokka comments that Jet became the traitor when he stopped protecting the innocent
Katara turns her back on Jet
Katara, Aang, and Sokka fly off on Appa, leaving Jet frozen to the tree
Aang and Katara apologize for not believing Sokka
Aang and Katara apologize for not believing Sokka
Sokka, while steering Appa, says to follow instincts, as they are usually right
Aang points out Sokka is flying in the wrong direction
Sokka admits sometimes instincts are wrong and changes direction

The_Great_Divide.action: Katara and Sokka argue over how to set up their campsite
Katara and Sokka argue over how to set up their campsite
Aang ends the fight and gloats about his ability to solve problems as the Avatar
Aang, Katara, and Sokka travel to the Great Divide
Aang, Katara, and Sokka travel to the Great Divide
Aang, Katara, and Sokka travel to the Great Divide
Katara expresses awe at the sight of the Great Divide
Sokka is bored by the sight and expresses intent to fly over it on Appa
A man runs up and warns them not to leave without a guide to cross the canyon
The man claims he is holding a spot for the rest of his tribe, the Gan Jin
The Zhang tribe arrives
The man claims the Zhang tribe has been an enemy of his tribe for a hundred years
The Gan Jin tribe appears neat, well-mannered, and clean, dressed in white fabric
The Zhang tribe appears unkempt, aggressive, and primitive, dressed in animal skins
The earthbending canyon guide arrives
Both tribes begin arguing over which should be escorted first
The Zhangs claim some of their members are sick
The Gan Jins assert their elderly are weary from travel
Katara wonders if Aang is ready to test his role as a mediator
Aang expresses reservations about his possible success
Katara alerts the tribes of Aang's status as the Avatar
Aang proposes that both tribes travel together
The tribes dismiss the suggestion and resume their argument
Aang ends the verbal fight by shouting that Appa will carry the sick and elderly while both tribes cross together
Both tribes agree to Aang's conditions
The canyon guide warns the group not to take any food into the canyon
The two tribes, the gang, and the guide begin their descent to the canyon floor
The guide breaks a rock shelf to prevent potential Fire Nation soldiers from following
A canyon crawler assaults the group
Aang and his friends fend off the canyon crawler
The canyon crawler attacks the guide, breaking both of his arms and rendering him unable to earthbend
Aang realizes they are now imprisoned in the canyon with no other earthbenders
Both tribes begin to argue again and decide to split up
The tribes blame each other for the attack
Aang furiously orders the tribes to separate and travel in two groups
Aang asks Katara to accompany the Gan Jins
Aang asks Sokka to accompany the Zhangs
Aang asks Katara and Sokka to try to discover the reason for the feud
The Zhangs discuss the use of tarps and feel it unnecessary to use them as rain protection during the dry season
The Gan Jins dutifully hang tarps over their tents, reasoning you could never be too careful
Katara sympathizes with the group she is accompanying
Sokka sympathizes with the group he is accompanying
Both tribes reveal that they brought food into the canyon against the orders of the guide
Both tribes justify bringing food by stating they should not go hungry while the other tribe eats
Both tribes tell their guests their version of the story behind their feud
The Gan Jins tell Katara about their tribal forefather, Jin Wei, and the redemption ritual involving the sacred crystal orb
Jin Wei is tasked with taking the sacred crystal orb from a gate in the east to a gate in the west as part of a redemption ritual
Jin Wei is assaulted by a thief, a Zhang named Wei Jin, who steals the orb out of greed (according to the Gan Jins)
The Zhangs tell Sokka their version of the story about their ancestor, Wei Jin
Wei Jin finds Jin Wei passed out on the ground near the western gate (according to the Zhangs)
Wei Jin stops to help Jin Wei and is told about the redemption ritual and the orb's importance
Jin Wei rejects assistance and asks Wei Jin to take the orb back to his tribe
Wei Jin takes the orb back to the Gan Jin tribe
Jin Wei's tribe arrests Wei Jin for stealing the orb and imprisons him for twenty years
Both sides claim they will never forget the terrible injustice dealt to them by the other
Aang spends the night with Momo and the guide, away from the tribes
Aang sadly states that getting the two factions to cooperate is virtually impossible and that escaping the canyon is the most important issue
The guide wonders if the two problems are connected
The two tribes reunite and reach the end of the canyon
Aang asks Katara and Sokka if the tribes might work together
Katara and Sokka begin to argue, each siding with their assigned tribe
Katara and Sokka begin to argue, each siding with their assigned tribe
The tribes begin arguing again
Aang tries to pacify the tribes, saying "harsh words won't solve problems, action will"
Both tribe leaders agree with Aang but misinterpret his meaning as an excuse to duel to the death
The two leaders are about to stab each other with their weapons
Aang uses his airbending to separate the leaders
Aang reveals each tribe's hidden cache of food
Aang scolds the tribes for disobeying the canyon guide's warning not to bring food, calling them awful people
Aang is distracted by an egg custard tart since he has not eaten in a day
A horde of canyon crawlers, attracted to the abundance of food, approach the refugees
The tribes and Aang have an initial skirmish with the canyon crawlers
Aang realizes the crawlers want the food more than to attack the tribes
Aang convinces the tribes to cooperate
Everyone works together, throwing bags over the crawlers' heads, mounting the beasts, and riding them up the wall and out of the canyon
The two tribes compliment each other's ability to take on the crawlers
The two tribes claim they are not different from each other after all
The two tribes refuse to let go of their grudges and prepare to duel again
Aang hears the names of the two tribes' ancestors, Jin Wei and Wei Jin
Aang pipes up that he knew Jin Wei and Wei Jin personally long ago
Aang begins to tell everyone that he met them a hundred years prior
Aang notes that a lot of confusion rose regarding the details of the story
Aang explains that Jin Wei and Wei Jin were not enemies, but eight-year-old twin brothers who played a game called "Redemption"
Aang clarifies that the sacred orb was just a regular ball and the gates were the goals
Aang describes Jin Wei falling and fumbling the ball
Aang describes Wei Jin recovering the ball, running to the other end, but stepping out of bounds and being put in the penalty box for two minutes
Aang points out that Wei Jin was "kind of a slob" and Jin Wei was "a little stuffy," but they respected each other's differences
The tribes realize their feud started over a misunderstanding
The tribes make amends and continue their journey to Ba Sing Se as one tribe
The guide chooses to follow them, expressing his intention to never work as the Great Divide's tour guide again
Sokka tells Aang how convenient it was that he knew the twins
Aang reveals to Katara and Sokka that the story was a complete fabrication
Katara and Sokka are shocked by Aang's revelation
Katara and Sokka are shocked by Aang's revelation
Aang asks where the egg custard tart is
Aang, Katara, and Sokka make their way to Appa to continue their journey
Aang, Katara, and Sokka make their way to Appa to continue their journey
Aang, Katara, and Sokka make their way to Appa to continue their journey

The_Storm.action: Aang dreams that he, Katara, and Sokka fly through clear, sunny skies
Sokka uses Aang's glider
Aang rides on Appa
Katara rides a larger version of Momo
Katara says, "We need you, Aang"
Aang responds, "I need you, too."
The dream takes a dark turn as the sky blackens
Aang panics and finds himself alone on Appa
Monk Gyatso appears before Aang and asks why he disappeared

Gyatso's body dissolves into dust
The voices of Katara and Gyatso repeat "We need you, Aang"
Aang and Appa are swallowed by a storm, causing them to crash into the sea
Aang and Appa sink into darkness

Aang gasps for air and jolts awake, causing his companions to stir
Aang apologizes for the disturbance and explains he had a nightmare, without elaborating
Aang sees the perfectly clear skies above
Aang remarks about how smooth their flying should be
Katara informs Aang that there is no food left and they will have to fly to the closest market
Sokka dramatically tells Aang and Katara that he had a dream the previous night in which "food eats people"
Sokka claims that his dream means they should avoid the market
Aang and Katara are annoyed by Sokka's claim
Aang and Katara are annoyed by Sokka's claim
Iroh predicts that there will be a terrible storm and advises altering their course for the sake of the crew
Zuko shouts that the safety of the crew does not matter when the Avatar is at stake
Lieutenant Jee overhears Zuko's statement

fruit vendor snaps at Katara that the watermelon she is holding is "perfect"
Katara is not convinced due to a swishing noise made by the fruit when shaken

Katara, Aang, and Sokka contemplate what to do next to get food and money
they overhear an argument between an elderly fisherman and his wife

the elderly couple argues, the wife refuses to accompany her husband, and the fisherman declares he will find someone else and pay double

Sokka reiterates the promise of a double wager, but the fisherman acts like he never said that
Zuko's ship approaches ominous storm clouds at sea

Jee sarcastically comments about Iroh being right about the weather

Jee declares Zuko only cares about himself and calls him a "spoiled prince"
Zuko answers Jee's comment with an Agni Kai challenge
Zuko and Jee lock arms, smoke erupting from Zuko's hand
Iroh breaks up the fight, trying to defuse the situation
Iroh offers them a bowl of noodles
Zuko, Jee, and the crew separate and retreat inside

Iroh places a comforting hand on Zuko's shoulder
Zuko shrugs off Iroh's hand and walks to the front of the bow to gaze out over the stormy waters

The fisherman's wife states that the "boy with the tattoos" is right
The fisherman realizes that Aang is the Avatar
Aang and Katara smile at the recognition
Aang and Katara smile at the recognition
The fisherman scolds Aang, accusing him of turning his back on the world by disappearing for a hundred years
Katara comes to Aang's defense, proclaiming that he would never turn his back on anyone and is the bravest person she knows
Aang is shaken by the accusation and backs away

Katara jumps on Appa and commands the flying bison to follow Aang

Sokka launches into a rant about previous impolite gestures by his friends
The fisherman interrupts Sokka and orders him to go below deck
Rain pours down
Katara finds Aang in a small cave
Katara enters the cave
Aang apologizes for having run away
Katara accepts Aang's apology and declares the fisherman was out of line
Aang sadly notes that the fisherman was right to say what he said
Katara wonders why Aang would say that
Aang declares he does not want to talk about it
Katara deduces it has to do with Aang's dream and urges him to open up
Katara gets a fire going
Aang begins to tell Katara the story of his childhood
Aang recalls the day the monks told him he was the Avatar
Aang shows his friends how to use his air scooter technique at the Southern Air Temple
Gyatso and the other elder monks tell Aang to follow them
Gyatso and the elder monks meet with Aang in their meeting room
The monks tell Aang that he is the Avatar
The monks show Aang the four toys he picked as a child, revealing them to be Avatar relics
Aang notes that he chose the toys because they seemed fun
Tashi says Aang picked the toys because they seemed familiar to him
Gyatso admits they would have waited until Aang was sixteen to tell him, but signs of war forced them to reveal it early
Jee sits around a small fire with several crew members, ranting about Zuko
Jee rhetorically asks who Zuko thinks he is

Younger, scarless Zuko makes his way to the Fire Lord's War Room in a flashback

Other children tell Aang they made up a game to play with the air scooters

Other monks dismount and tell Aang that being the Avatar would give him an unfair advantage in any team
Aang argues that nothing has changed
Other monks do not permit Aang to play with them
Aang, saddened, retreats inside the temple
Aang plays a game of Pai Sho with Monk Gyatso, though his heart is not in it
Gyatso uses airbending to pull Aang’s robe over his head and switches two tiles on the board
Aang discovers Gyatso’s cheating, and they are both amused
Tashi enters the room, scolding Gyatso for playing games with Aang, saying the Avatar should be training
Aang stands to follow Tashi to be tested on high-level airbending techniques
Gyatso stops Aang, saying as his guardian, he will decide when Aang trains and plays

Iroh recalls how the Fire Lord became angry with Zuko for his outburst
Fire Lord declares Zuko's challenge of the general to be an act of "complete disrespect"
Fire Lord states the disrespect must be solved via an Agni Kai
Zuko accepts the challenge
Zuko prepares to fight in front of a large, gathered crowd
Zuko discovers his opponent is his father, not the old general
Aang confides in Katara that just when he started to feel better, something worse happened
Tashi, annoyed over having been told off by Gyatso, takes the matter to Monk Pasang
Gyatso argues that Aang needs to grow up as a normal child
Pasang decides that Aang will be sent to the Eastern Air Temple to complete his training, away from Gyatso
Aang perches on the roof of the room and overhears everything
Katara sympathizes with Aang
Aang turns away from Katara, raging about the monks' decision
Aang involuntarily enters the Avatar State, sending powerful currents of air through the cave
Powerful currents of air move through the cave
Aang instantly calms down and apologizes for his outburst
Katara notes that Aang has the right to be angry after the monks sent him away
Aang sadly continues his story, noting that events occurred differently
Aang admits to being afraid and confused, so he decided to run away
Aang flees the temple and braces a terrible storm on Appa
Aang notes that he never saw Gyatso again, as he was thrown into the waters
Aang freezes himself in the iceberg from which Katara rescued him a hundred years later
The flashback ends
Aang continues that the Fire Nation attacked the temple afterward, displaying guilt over not being there to protect his people
Aang concludes that the fisherman was right for saying he turned his back on the world
Katara tries to console Aang, stating he is being too hard on himself

Katara emphasizes that the world needs Aang now and that he gives people hope
Iroh reveals to the crew that Zuko begged for mercy and refused to fight his father during the duel
Zuko tries to convince Ozai that he only had the Fire Nation's best interests at heart and apologizes for speaking out of term
Ozai demands that Zuko fights for his honor
Zuko falls to his knees, emphasizing his loyalty as a son
Ozai orders Zuko to rise and fight
Zuko prostrates himself more and declares he will not fight
Ozai, unfazed, states that Zuko will learn respect and suffering will be his teacher
Zuko takes in Ozai's words with tears in his eyes
Iroh admits he looked away while Ozai scarred Zuko's face
Jee reveals he thought Zuko had been in a training accident
