Iroh says only by capturing the Avatar can Zuko return home with honor
Jee finally understands Zuko's obsession with capturing the Avatar
Iroh adds that the Avatar is Zuko's source of hope
Zuko meditates in front of four candles, which rise and fall with his breathing
Brief flashes of Zuko's youth appear
Zuko and Azula run over a meadow with Ozai behind them
Ozai places a hand on Zuko's shoulder
Zuko, without his scar, looks out over the sea with his father's arm around him and a smile on his face
The face of the happy child changes to that of a scowling teenage Zuko
Zuko suddenly looks up as lightning strikes his ship
Katara and Aang are startled by the fisherman's wife's cries for help
Katara rushes to the fisherman's wife's side and says she is safe
The fisherman's wife declares that her husband is not safe, since he and Sokka are still out at sea, caught in the storm
Aang declares he will find them
Katara immediately resolves to accompany Aang
The elderly woman makes herself comfortable in the cave, noting that she will remain there
Everyone makes it to the bow of Zuko's ship
Zuko wonders where they were hit
Iroh directs attention to the crow's nest, where the helmsman is hanging on
Zuko and Jee climb the ladder to save the helmsman
The ship is dangerously rocked back and forth
Another lightning bolt snakes down toward the metal ship
Iroh intercepts the lightning and redirects it into the water, leaving him smoking
The helmsman is unable to hold on and plummets down, screaming
Zuko grabs the helmsman's hand as he falls and lowers him to Jee
Jee supports the helmsman to safety
Zuko and Jee look at each other with a small smile of respect
Aang and Katara face the storm, searching for Sokka and the fisherman
Aang and Katara face the storm, searching for Sokka and the fisherman
A large wave threatens to swallow them
Aang uses his staff to spin the water out of the way so Appa can fly through unharmed
Aang and Katara spot the fishing boat
Aang and Katara spot the fishing boat
Aang and Katara fly past Zuko's vessel
Aang and Katara fly past Zuko's vessel
Zuko spots Aang and Katara
Jee asks Zuko what he wants to do
Zuko orders his men to let Aang go and set course for safer waters
Iroh advises heading to the eye of the storm
Iroh smiles proudly at Zuko
The fishing boat bobs and weaves on the rough waves
Sokka and the fisherman hold on to the ropes for their lives
Sokka laments that he is too young to die
The fisherman remarks that he is not too young to die but still does not want to die
Aang jumps aboard the boat
Lightning strikes the boat and causes the mast to fall down
Aang uses waterbending to sever the beam in two, saving Sokka and the fisherman
Aang helps Sokka and the fisherman up onto Appa's saddle
They are overtaken by another large wave and pulled below the surface
Aang sees Sokka, Katara, and the fisherman all holding on to Appa's saddle unconsciously
Aang starts slipping away but grabs hold of Appa's reins with renewed vigor
Aang's tattoos start to glow
Aang pulls himself back atop Appa's head
Aang smashes his fists together and forms an air pocket around them all
Appa flies through the water
Zuko's ship reaches the calmness inside the eye of the storm
Zuko apologizes to his uncle
Zuko and his crew keep their course
Appa and his passengers burst through the surface in front of the ship, rapidly rising in the air
Aang looks over his shoulder and notices Zuko staring after them
Zuko stares after Aang and his group, but leaves them to fly out of the storm
Aang and his group rise above the clouds
The fisherman reunites with his wife at the cave
The fisherman's wife orders her husband to apologize to Aang
Aang notes that an apology is not necessary
The fisherman circumvents his wife's wishes by offering Aang and the group a free fish instead
Aang announces that he does not eat meat
The fisherman claims that fish is not meat
Sokka approaches the fisherman to claim his pay for working on the boat
The fisherman gives Sokka a fish as payment
Sokka is horrified to receive a fish as payment
The elderly couple resumes arguing
Aang approaches Katara and says she had been right before
Aang declares he is done dwelling on the past and will make the best of the present
Katara is happy to hear Aang's decision
Katara adds that she does not think Aang will be plagued by nightmares anymore
The fisherman approaches Aang and thanks him for saving his life
They notice that the storm has cleared
They walk outside and stare upward at the brightening sky
Appa shakes the water from his fur, sending a final cascade of water splashing onto them

The_Blue_Spirit.action: A messenger hawk approaches Pohuai Stronghold, a Fire Nation fortress
Highly proficient archers practice their skill in the courtyard
Commander Zhao and Colonel Shinu observe the archers and argue on a parapet
Colonel Shinu denies Zhao's request to use the Yuyan Archers to capture the Avatar
Colonel Shinu identifies the archers as the Yuyan Archers
Colonel Shinu calls Zhao's quest a "vanity project" rather than part of the Hundred Year War
A hawk arrives with news from Fire Lord Ozai that Zhao has been promoted to admiral
Zhao informs Colonel Shinu that his request is now an order due to his promotion
Colonel Shinu bows in submission and leaves
A figure in dark clothes and a blue mask perches on the roof above Zhao and overhears the conversation
The masked figure silently retreats into the shadows and disappears
Katara cares for an ill and delirious Sokka in the ruins of Taku
Sokka talks nonsense, convinced that Appa can talk and that he can earthbend
Katara explains Sokka’s illness is from exposure to a recent storm
Aang returns from an unsuccessful hunt for ginger root, but brings a map to a herbalist institute on a nearby mountain
Katara states Sokka is not in any condition to go to the institute and declares he just needs rest
Katara begins to cough herself
Katara protests that she is fine
Aang realizes Katara has the same illness as Sokka and decides to travel to the herbalist alone
Lightning flashes across the sky
Aang decides to go by foot instead of using his glider and begins running quickly with the aid of his airbending
Lieutenant Jee goes over a map with Prince Zuko to make plans to continue the search for the Avatar
The ship is overshadowed by a large Fire Nation Naval ship
Zuko wonders what the Fire Nation ship wants
Iroh, in the middle of a Pai Sho game, jokingly speculates that they want to play Pai Sho
It is revealed that the Fire Nation ship is there to inform Zuko and his crew that all information regarding the Avatar is to be reported to Admiral Zhao
It is revealed to all present crew members that Zhao has been promoted and is now in charge of the search for the Avatar
Zuko informs the Fire Nation soldier that he has no information to provide and orders them to get off his ship and let them pass
Zuko and his crew are informed that Zhao is not permitting the passage of ships in or out of the area
Zuko again demands that the soldiers get off his ship
Iroh declares enthusiastically that he has won the pot in his Pai Sho game
Iroh encourages his opponents to play again
Fire Nation lookouts hide in a concealed blind-like structure in the forest
One guard reads the wanted poster for the Avatar and comments that it is "pretty amazing" the Avatar can create tornadoes and run faster than the wind
Another soldier peers through a telescope and dismisses the claims as Fire Lord propaganda
Aang speeds by, unknowingly wreaking havoc on the outpost
The soldier blows a signal horn to alert others of Aang's presence
Sokka, while sick, implores his sister to get him some water
Katara, weakened by sickness, instructs Momo to take her water skin to the river and fill it with water
Momo does not understand Katara, hearing only unintelligible noise
Momo departs with the water skin
Aang reaches the herbalist institute
Aang relays his problem to the elderly herbalist
The elderly herbalist gathers ingredients
Aang’s explanation becomes panicked
The herbalist calmly tells Aang to settle down and assures him his friends will be fine
The herbalist begins to tell Aang her life story
The herbalist reveals she has lived atop the mountain for forty years
The herbalist explains the institute used to be lively, but now only she and her cat Miyuki remain
Aang reacts nonplussed to the herbalist’s story
The herbalist continues, revealing wounded Earth Kingdom soldiers still visit and recover thanks to her remedies
Aang, uninterested, asks if the concoction is almost finished
The herbalist tells Aang it only needs one more ingredient
The herbalist starts to peruse her vast garden at a leisurely pace
Aang grows impatient
Zuko angrily practices his firebending on the deck of his ship
Iroh approaches Zuko and asks if he is all right, noting he has not given the crew an order in over an hour
Zuko declares his indifference about the matter
Iroh tries to instill hope in Zuko, saying he could still capture the Avatar before Zhao
Zuko questions how that could happen given Zhao's man-power and voices his despair over losing his honor, throne, and country
The herbalist finds the final ingredient for her concoction
Aang feels relieved
The herbalist drops the plum blossom in the bowl
Aang grabs the bowl expectantly and thanks the herbalist for the potion
The herbalist swats Aang away with a wooden spoon
The herbalist angrily asks Aang why he did that
Aang reveals his intention to bring the cure to his friends
The herbalist laughs and explains that what she made is dinner for Miyuki, not a cure
The herbalist tells Aang his friends need to suck on frozen wood frogs from the swamp below
The herbalist explains the frogs' skin has medicinal properties only when frozen
Aang deems the herbalist insane
The herbalist cheerfully confirms her insanity
Aang departs for the swamp below
Two arrows pin the cuffs of Aang's boots to the ground
Aang deflects several other arrows with airbending
Aang sees the Yuyan Archers, who have tracked him down
Aang frees himself
Aang makes a mad dash for the swamp with the archers in pursuit
Aang avoids several arrows
Aang makes it to the swamp
Aang collects frozen frogs
The archers pin both of Aang's arms to a tree and capture him
Aang is brought back to Pohuai Stronghold
Aang is kept secured in a large room, shackled to two posts to prevent bending
Four men guard the room
Zhao enters the room
Zhao tells Aang his century-long game of hide and seek is over
Aang, angered, challenges Zhao to a duel
Zhao declines the duel and taunts Aang about being the only airbender left and missing his people
Zhao ensures Aang he will not be killed, but kept alive "just barely"
Zhao turns his back and walks away
Aang airbends with his mouth, sending a firebender flying off his feet
Zhao slams into a wall and falls to the floor
Zhao gets to his feet and declares Aang's situation is futile, with no escape or rescue
Sokka, feverish, asks Katara for water again
Katara, also ill, assures Sokka that Momo should return soon
Momo returns but does not fill the water skin
Momo brings a dead mouse and drops it on Katara's chest
Katara is disgusted
Katara pleads with Momo again for water
Momo departs
Katara wonders what is keeping Aang
The figure with the blue mask waits patiently, hidden in the shrubbery outside the Fire Nation fortress
A supply cart passes by the masked figure
The masked figure nimbly rolls underneath the cart and grabs hold, letting himself be transported to the stronghold
The cart is halted at a checkpoint
A Fire Nation guard inspects the interior of the vehicle
The guard moves to look underneath the cart
The masked figure moves to the other side of the wagon and hides inside
The masked figure gains access to the heavily guarded fortress
The cart stops to unload
The masked figure stealthily sneaks out and makes his way up a nearby staircase
Zhao addresses his troops, informing them he has captured the Avatar and describing the Avatar as the Fire Nation's final hurdle
Zhao predicts the Fire Nation will take Ba Sing Se that year and burn it to the ground with the strength of Sozin's Comet
The majority of the troops gather in the courtyard to listen to Zhao's speech
The masked figure sneaks past the gathered troops
The masked figure scales a wall to reach the inner ring of the fortress
The masked figure continues to the main tower via an underground sewer
Aang struggles to free himself in his cell, but fails
The frozen frogs Aang collected start to thaw out, wiggle out of his clothes, and leave the room
Aang is dismayed as the frogs leave
The guards outside notice the frogs coming out under the door
A Fire Nation soldier’s helmet rolls down the hallway toward the guards
One guard investigates alone
The other three guards see a flame erupt from the hallway and hear muffled combat and chains being strung up
Two more guards follow after the first and find their companion strung up with his hands to the ceiling
The masked figure, clinging to the ceiling, wraps a chain around a hand of each guard and drops down, pulling the guards up
The last guard in front of Aang’s cell hears the scuffle and takes his horn to sound the alarm
Before the guard can blow the horn, a well-aimed knife knocks it out of his hands
The guard notices a figure running toward him and firebends
The masked man extinguishes the fire by throwing water
The masked man sweeps the legs from underneath the guard with the bucket
Aang hears the scuffle and eyes the door with apprehension as the lock is being turned
Aang gasps as a masked figure enters and unsheathes his dual broadswords
The masked figure swings his weapons and advances on Aang
Aang screams
The masked figure slices through Aang’s bonds, freeing him
The masked figure retreats without saying a word
Aang asks the figure who he is, what is going on, and wonders if the man is there to rescue him
The masked figure does not respond, opens the door, and signals for Aang to follow him
Aang believes the figure means him no harm and follows him
Aang passes by the tied up guard and notices his partially frozen frogs making their escape through another hallway
Aang tries to gather the frogs
The masked stranger pulls Aang away
Aang protests loudly that his friends need to suck on the animals
Momo brings many items to Katara, ranging from fans to pottery to jewelry
Momo places a tiara on Katara's head
Katara, with a weak voice from her illness, reiterates that they desire water
Katara gives up on Momo
Katara rolls around and pleads for Aang's swift return
Sokka, completely delusional, asks Katara who the "Aang kid" is
Sokka refers to Katara as "Your Highness"
Katara is annoyed by Sokka's comment
The masked stranger and Aang sneak out of the tower via the sewer system of the fortress
The masked stranger surveys the courtyard
The masked stranger gestures for Aang to follow him out
The guards have their backs turned
Zhao walks through the hallways, dictating to his assistant that he wants his speech and testimonies from all high-ranking officers present at its delivery to be sent to the Fire Lord
Zhao's train of thought is interrupted by the muffled moans of the overpowered guards strung from the ceiling
Zhao quickly makes his way to Aang's cell
Zhao finds Aang's cell completely deserted except for a thawed wood frog that croaks at him
Zhao becomes furious and runs off
Zhao's assistant trails after him, asking if he should postpone sending the message to the Fire Lord
Aang and the masked figure scale the wall via a rope
The alarm is set off, drawing attention to their attempt
A guard atop the wall cuts the rope, sending them tumbling down
Aang breaks their fall with airbending, securing a safe landing
The masked figure unsheathes his swords and points to the open gates
Aang and the masked figure make a run for the gates
Zhao appears on the balcony, bellowing that the Avatar has escaped and orders all the gates to be closed
Aang tells the masked figure to remain close to him
Aang uses airbending to clear away several guards blocking their path
Aang runs through the closing doors
The masked figure is forced to stop and defend himself as several guards surround and assault him
Aang realizes the masked figure is no longer following him
Aang takes the spear from a guard attacking him, breaks off the spearhead, and uses the stick as a staff
Aang charges the soldiers attacking the masked figure and uses airbending to incapacitate them
Aang hurls the masked figure atop the first of the fortress walls, where more soldiers immediately swarm him
Aang propels himself in the air by twirling the stick in a helicopter fashion
Aang grabs hold of the masked figure with his legs
The masked figure defends them against all the spears thrown at them
Aang is unable to sustain the twirling motion and they crash atop the next fortress wall
Aang loses his staff in the process
More guards come up to attack the duo
The masked figure throws one of the soldiers over the wall
Aang blows the other guards away with airbending
More troops use bamboo ladders to scale the wall
Aang blows the troops off the ladders
Aang takes the ladders and calls to the masked figure to get on his back
Aang and the masked figure use the three ladders like stilts to go from the second wall to the third and final wall
A soldier lights the last ladder on fire
Aang and the masked figure jump off the burning ladder in hopes of making it over the wall
Aang and the masked figure lose their grip and fall to the ground just in front of the main gate
The duo is confronted by firebenders
Zhao halts the firebenders' attack, saying the Avatar must be captured alive
The masked figure suddenly holds Aang hostage with his broadswords against Aang's neck
Zhao orders the gate to be opened and the pair to be let out
The masked figure takes Aang with him, swords still around Aang's neck, facing toward the compound
Zhao goes onto higher ground and has an archer knock out the masked figure with a single shot
Zhao plans to deliver both the masked figure and Aang to the Fire Lord
The masked figure falls to the ground
Aang sees a small part of the face below the mask
Aang creates a dust cloud as cover
Zhao sends guards out to capture them
Aang removes the mask to reveal Zuko
Aang is shocked and afraid, begins to run
Aang stops to look back at Zuko
The dust clears as guards approach the area
Aang and Zuko escape
Aang and Zuko escape
Zhao is frustrated and infuriated
Zuko regains consciousness
Aang tells Zuko a story about Kuzon, someone he befriended in the Fire Nation one hundred years earlier
Aang wonders if he and Zuko could have been friends before the war
Zuko attacks Aang with firebending
Aang easily avoids Zuko's attack by hopping from tree to tree
Aang leaves Zuko alone in the forest
Aang goes back to the swamp to collect frozen frogs
Zuko heads back to his ship
Zuko’s uncle asks him where he has been
Zuko replies that he is going to bed and asks for no disturbances
Aang returns to his friends
Aang puts the frogs into his friends’ mouths
Aang sits down on Appa’s tail to rest
Sokka asks Aang if he made any new friends
Aang responds that he does not think he did and turns on his side
Zuko looks at the Fire Nation flag and turns away to his side
Sokka and Katara begin to feel better
Sokka and Katara realize they are sucking on frogs and quickly spit them out, disgusted and confused
Sokka and Katara realize they are sucking on frogs and quickly spit them out, disgusted and confused

The_Fortuneteller.action: Katara spots a fish jumping out of the water while camping beside a river
Sokka becomes convinced the fish is taunting him
Sokka grabs his fishing rod to catch the fish
Sokka realizes the fishing line is gone
Aang confesses he used Sokka's fishing line to craft a necklace for Katara to replace her lost necklace
Sokka becomes annoyed at Aang's confession
Sokka attempts to catch the fish by throwing the fishing rod at it
Sokka takes out his knife and runs into the water after the fish when the rod throw is unsuccessful
Katara puts the necklace on
Aang sees Katara in a completely new way, realizing his growing crush on her
Sokka teases Aang by saying, "Smoochy! Smoochy! Someone's in love!"
Sokka makes kissing faces at the fish he finally caught
The fish slaps Sokka with its tail and lands back in the water
Katara remains ignorant of Aang's crush, commenting that Aang is only a good friend
Katara compares Aang to Momo
Aang feels dismayed
The trio hears loud noises nearby
The trio witnesses an old man being attacked by a huge platypus bear
The old man calmly dodges the platypus bear's attacks while commenting on the pleasant, sunny weather
The trio yells advice to the old man to escape the platypus bear
The old man ignores their advice
Aang and Appa confront the platypus bear head-on
Appa rears up onto his hind legs and bellows
The platypus bear lays a spotted egg out of fear and flees
Sokka takes the egg hungrily
The man explains his calmness is due to Aunt Wu's prediction of a safe journey
Sokka objects, saying the man was almost killed by the creature
The man replies cheerfully, "But I wasn't!"
The man hands a wrapped umbrella to the group, saying Aunt Wu told him to give it to travelers
Aang opens the umbrella
It begins to rain as soon as the umbrella is opened
The egg slips from Sokka's grasp and falls on his head, shattering
Sokka remains skeptical of fortunetelling
Sokka falsely claims the sky had been cloudy all day and makes his own prediction: "It's going to keep drizzling."
The rain stops immediately and the sun shines down on them
The trio arrives in Makapu Village
The trio meets a man keeping the door of a large building
The man motions them inside, saying Aunt Wu is expecting them
Aang meets Meng, the fortuneteller's assistant
Meng instantly develops a crush on Aang, demonstrated by a romanticized shot from her point-of-view
Meng asks if they would like any bean curd puffs, aiming the question primarily at Aang
Meng makes remarks about the similarity of their names, Aang and Meng
Meng comments on the large size of Aang's ears
Sokka sarcastically reinforces Meng's comment about Aang's ears
Meng leaves to retrieve the puffs
Katara and Sokka have a brief discussion
Katara states that some things in the world cannot be understood and that knowing a taste of the future would be nice
Meng returns with the curd puffs
a young woman emerges from the inner room, ecstatic with joy
the young woman tells Meng Aunt Wu's prediction: her true love will give her a rare panda lily
Meng glances over her shoulder at Aang and wonders aloud if her own true love will do the same
the young woman asks if Aang is the "big-eared guy" Aunt Wu predicted Meng would marry
Meng becomes embarrassed and jittery
Meng delivers the puffs to a somewhat disinterested Aang
Meng trips and almost spills the food
Aang catches the food for Meng, holding her hands for a brief moment
Meng and Aang slowly raise their heads and look into each other's eyes
Meng blushes, embarrassed further
Aang is only slightly surprised
Meng leaves the room awkwardly
Aunt Wu emerges from her sanctum and asks the trio who would like to go next
Sokka looks away
Aang looks to Katara
Katara answers, "I guess that's me."
Aang becomes agitated about what Aunt Wu and Katara will talk about
Aang nervously consults Sokka, asking what he thinks they will discuss
Sokka says they will talk about boring stuff like love, marriage, and babies
Sokka confirms Aang’s suspicions that they are talking about love
Aang becomes curious about his role in Katara’s future
Aang excuses himself, saying he has to go to the bathroom
Aang tiptoes down the outside of the walled sanctum to eavesdrop out of sight
Katara talks briefly about cosmetics
Katara asks Aunt Wu whom she will marry
Aunt Wu says she can see "a great romance" for Katara and that the man is "a very powerful bender"
Aang is delighted to hear Aunt Wu's prediction
Aang jumps and dances in silent jubilation
Aunt Wu predicts that Sokka's future will be full of "struggle and anguish, most of it self-inflicted" without taking him inside her room
Sokka objects, saying Aunt Wu needs to read his fortune in the room
Aunt Wu tells Sokka it is unnecessary and beckons Aang into the sanctum
Aunt Wu chooses a fortunetelling method involving throwing a bone into a small fire and observing the cracks
Aang picks a bone and throws it into the fire
The bone explodes in the fire
Aunt Wu explains hysterically that Aang will be involved in a great battle between the forces of good and evil which will determine the fate of the whole world
Aang, already aware of that, dismisses her prediction nonchalantly
Aang inquires as to whether or not there is love in his future
Aang is downcast when Aunt Wu tells him she did not see anything of that nature in the bones
Aunt Wu notices Aang's disappointment
Aunt Wu grabs a piece of shattered bone and tells Aang she missed a crack on it
Aunt Wu explains to Aang its meaning: if he trusts his heart, he will be with the one he loves
Sokka complains about fortunetelling, believing it to be a hoax
Sokka rants that his life will be joyful, not full of self-inflicted pain as Aunt Wu predicted
Sokka kicks a pebble
The pebble ricochets off a sign and hits Sokka in the head
The group notices a crowd of people gathering in the town square
The group learns it is the day of the village's annual cloud reading ritual
Aunt Wu predicts the fate of the villagers by studying cloud formations
Aunt Wu reads the clouds, predicting a good harvest
Aunt Wu predicts it will be a good year for twins
Aunt Wu predicts Mt. Makapu will not destroy the village that year
The villagers cheer at their good fortunes
Katara becomes obsessed with Aunt Wu's predictions and repeatedly asks for more readings
Sokka remains very skeptical of fortunetelling
Aang, discouraged that Katara seems to have no special feelings for him, asks Sokka for advice
Sokka, assuming the girl is Meng, tells Aang to act aloof to get a girl
Aang acts indifferent when he next encounters Katara, who is eating a papaya on Aunt Wu's suggestion
Katara remains oblivious to Aang's feelings
Aang abandons the aloof approach and decides to make his intentions clear by taking a panda lily from the rim of the volcano
Aang and Sokka climb to the rim of the volcano
Aang and Sokka discover the volcano is on the verge of erupting, contrary to Aunt Wu's prediction
Aang and Sokka rush back down to the town to warn everyone of the imminent danger
The townspeople do not believe Aang and Sokka because of their unshakable faith in Aunt Wu
Aang decides to steal Aunt Wu's cloud reading book
Aang breaks into Aunt Wu's house
Meng appears behind Aang and tells him she knows he doesn't feel the same attraction
Aang reassures Meng that she will find her love in the future
Meng presents Aang with the cloud-reading book, admitting she was stalking him
Katara and Aang manipulate the clouds with waterbending and airbending to create the symbol for "volcanic destruction"
Katara and Aang manipulate the clouds with waterbending and airbending to create the symbol for "volcanic destruction"
Sokka convinces Aunt Wu to make a second reading
Aunt Wu makes a second reading, which tells her the volcano is going to erupt
Aang and Sokka organize the village's earthbenders and anyone capable of digging to make a giant trench around the village
Aang and Sokka organize the village's earthbenders and anyone capable of digging to make a giant trench around the village
Lava surges over the trench, hanging in space over the village
Aang unleashes a massive gust of wind to rapidly cool the lava, solidifying it into a giant wall
Sokka, awestruck, admits he sometimes forgets how powerful a bender Aang is
Katara has an epiphany, remembering Aunt Wu's love fortune
Aang sheepishly hands back the guide for cloud-reading to Aunt Wu
Aunt Wu praises Aang instead of reprimanding him
Aang tells Aunt Wu he suspects she lied to him about the love in his future and made something up to please him
Aunt Wu responds that, just as Aang molded the clouds to his own design, it is his responsibility to actively shape his own destiny
Sokka happily tells the villagers that Aunt Wu was wrong
a man explains that Aunt Wu did not claim the volcano would not erupt, only that it would not destroy the village
Sokka retorts with an aggravated "I hate you"
Katara drags Sokka away
Aang, Katara, and Sokka leave the village to continue their northern journey
Meng and Katara wish each other goodbye
Appa flies away
Meng softly says "floozy" as a parting shot, unheard by the others

Bato_of_the_Water_Tribe.action: Aang finds a whale's tooth scimitar in a field near a body of water
Sokka quickly identifies the scimitar as a Water Tribe weapon
The discovery prompts Team Avatar to search for other traces of Water Tribe warriors in the area
Sokka finds a burned arrow and further evidence of a fight between firebenders and Water Tribe warriors
Their search leads the trio to the shore
Katara finds a beached boat
Katara and Sokka recognize the vessel as part of their father's fleet
Zuko and his uncle Iroh drink tea on Zuko's ship
Iroh remarks to Zuko that a moment of quiet is good for mental well-being
The ship rocks suddenly, causing Zuko to spill his tea
Zuko angrily makes his way to the deck
Iroh follows Zuko to the deck
Zuko and Iroh find June, a bounty hunter, mounted on a shirshu
Zuko's soldiers have a brief altercation with June
June states she is looking for a stowaway, despite Zuko's denial
The shirshu tears a hunk of metal from the deck with its teeth
The shirshu follows the stowaway's scent and finds its prey
The fugitive tries to escape
The shirshu stuns the fugitive with its whip-like tongue, paralyzing him temporarily
June prepares to mount her shirshu with the fugitive in custody
Zuko inquires how June knew of the man's hideout
June replies that the shirshu smelled him
Iroh is impressed with both the animal and its owner
Sokka remembers the day Hakoda left the Southern Water Tribe, leaving him to take care of his little sister
Bato appears at the group's campsite next to the boat
Bato explains that he was injured and left behind
Bato takes the party to his current home in an abbey of nuns who make perfumes and cures
Sokka and Katara share stories about their tribe, excited over the reunion
Sokka and Katara share stories about their tribe, excited over the reunion
Sokka and Katara unintentionally leave Aang out, causing him to feel excluded
Sokka and Katara unintentionally leave Aang out, causing him to feel excluded
Aang feels excluded
June arm wrestles a muscular man in a rowdy bar as the crowd cheers
Zuko walks into the bar, pushing people out of his way and shouting
Iroh follows Zuko, apologizing to his nephew's victims
Zuko pulls up next to June and demands to speak with her
June briefly acknowledges Zuko and Iroh, calling them "angry boy" and "uncle lazy"
June beats her opponent in the arm wrestling match and starts collecting money from the table
Zuko declares that June's beast trashed his ship and demands reimbursement
June claims she is "short on money" despite just collecting money, then buys drinks for the entire bar
Zuko grabs June's hand and gets in her face, claiming he was not requesting money
Zuko hands June Katara's necklace outside and states he needs her to find someone
June sarcastically asks if Zuko is looking for his girlfriend
Zuko ignores the jab and states he is after the bald monk traveling with the girl
Zuko offers to consider the ship damage paid for if June finds them
June is not interested and starts to walk away
Iroh offers to pay June her weight in gold
June accepts under the condition the price is changed to Iroh's weight in gold
Iroh laughs and agrees
Bato states that a message is arriving soon about Katara and Sokka's father's whereabouts
Bato says Katara and Sokka might be able to see their father
Aang runs off, feeling hurt that Katara and Sokka might leave him to go find Hakoda
Aang does not hear the last part of the conversation
Katara and Sokka reject the offer to go see their father, saying they must travel to the North Pole with Aang
Katara and Sokka reject the offer to go see their father, saying they must travel to the North Pole with Aang
Aang contemplates their possible departure
a messenger arrives and gives Aang a rendezvous map from Hakoda
Aang hides the map instead of giving it to Katara and Sokka
Aang returns to Bato's home with an air of guilty awkwardness
Zuko, June, and Iroh search for Aang in the ruins of Taku
the herbalist worries, thinking her cat Miyuki got into trouble with the Fire Nation again
Aang hides the footprints of the messenger's ostrich horse
Bato takes Sokka, Katara, and Aang on a coming-of-age ritual called ice dodging
They are forced to navigate through jagged rocks instead of ice due to the region
Zuko continues his search and arrives at Aunt Wu's place in Makapu Village
With Katara and Aang's bending under Sokka's conduct, they pass the ice dodging test
Bato gives Sokka the ceremonial mark for wisdom
Bato gives Katara the ceremonial mark for bravery
Bato gives Aang the ceremonial mark for trust
Aang rejects his mark, believing himself unworthy of trust, and reveals the rendezvous map
Sokka angrily yells at Aang
Katara expresses disappointment in Aang
Sokka and Katara leave Aang to find their father
Sokka and Katara leave Aang to find their father
the shirshu arrives at the abbey, closing in on its prey
Katara travels with Sokka and Bato to find Hakoda
Katara hears a wolf howling
Sokka suggests that the wolf is hurt
Bato tells them the wolf has been separated from its pack
Bato explains he can relate, sharing his experience of being left in the abbey to heal after being wounded in battle
Sokka is reminded of the day Hakoda left to fight abroad
Sokka and Katara realize their mistake in leaving Aang and turn around to find and apologize to him
Sokka and Katara realize their mistake in leaving Aang and turn around to find and apologize to him
Aang prepares to leave the abbey
Mother Superior warns Aang of Zuko’s approach
Zuko, Iroh, and June find Katara and Sokka and realize the Avatar is not with them
The shirshu paralyzes Katara and Sokka as they try to escape
The shirshu finds the map with Aang's scent
They follow the scent and return to the abbey
They find Aang waiting for them at the abbey
Aang flies on his glider and takes the shirshu down
June quickly rises and, once on her shirshu, tries to attack Aang
Appa stops June and the shirshu
Aang and Zuko start fighting, creating a large explosion
Nyla attempts to paralyze Appa with his tongue
Zuko recovers from the explosion and starts firebending at Aang
Aang is chased by the shirshu
Appa tries to aid Aang by firing air blasts at the shirshu, but is unsuccessful
Zuko resumes his duel with Aang
Aang notices Katara's necklace in Zuko's fist
Aang dodges Zuko's fire whips atop a well and manages to recover the necklace
Appa tries to reach Zuko, who is shooting fire at Aang
June and the shirshu paralyze Appa after numerous attacks
Zuko and June corner Aang against a wall
Sokka devises a plan and directs the nuns to pour out all their vats of perfumes
Katara waterbends the perfume into a thick mist
The excess of different scents acts as a smokescreen, overwhelming the shirshu
The shirshu becomes blinded and panics, lashing out haphazardly with its tongue
The shirshu strikes and paralyzes Zuko and June
Iroh pretends to be paralyzed to get close to June, holding her in his arms as he lies on the ground
Aang, Katara, and Sokka use the opportunity to escape
Aang, Katara, and Sokka use the opportunity to escape
Aang, Katara, and Sokka use the opportunity to escape
Aang asks the group about their current plans
The group informs Aang that they will travel to the North Pole and that he has become part of their family
Aang returns Katara's necklace to her, saying that Zuko asked him to do so
Katara understands the joke and kisses Aang on the cheek
Aang blushes and twiddles his thumbs
They fly off into the sunset

The_Deserter.action: Aang and his friends decide to attend a "Fire Days" festival in a Fire Nation town
Sokka points out they are wanted criminals
Aang, Katara, and Sokka disguise themselves and acquire festival masks
Aang, Katara, and Sokka enjoy the festivities
Aang, Katara, and Sokka witness a puppet show where Fire Lord Ozai is presented as a hero and Earth Kingdom soldiers as evil
Aang, Katara, and Sokka stumble upon a firebending show
The audience is dazzled by the firebending show
Malu, the performer, asks for volunteers and calls Katara up to the stage
Malu has Katara play the part of a damsel in distress and ties her to a chair
Malu creates a dragon of fire and makes a show of it, but loses control
The fire dragon flies directly toward Katara
Aang leaps onto the stage to shield Katara and uses airbending to stop the fire dragon
It is revealed to the crowd that Aang is the Avatar
Fire Nation soldiers give chase to Aang, Katara, and Sokka
Aang, Katara, and Sokka escape from the Fire Nation soldiers
Chey aids the trio's escape
Chey explains he serves a firebending master named Jeong Jeong, who has abandoned the Fire Nation
Chey says Jeong Jeong is the first to leave the Fire Nation Army and live, while he himself is the second
Chey admits that being the second does not make him famous
Chey leads the trio to Jeong Jeong's camp within a forest
Jeong Jeong's followers, led by Lin Yee, intercept the group
Chey greets Jeong Jeong's followers as friends
Jeong Jeong's followers are indifferent
The group arrives at Jeong Jeong's camp
Aang attempts to persuade Jeong Jeong to instruct him
Admiral Zhao arrives at the town
Zhao learns Aang and his team passed through the town
Zhao decides to pursue Aang and his team himself
Zhao orders his men to prepare river boats to search the woods where Aang fled
Jeong Jeong refuses to see Aang
Aang barges into Jeong Jeong's hut to persuade him to teach firebending
Jeong Jeong tells Aang to leave
Aang pushes forward and continues to ask Jeong Jeong to teach him
Jeong Jeong tells Aang a metaphor involving a fish and a river
Jeong Jeong says Aang must first master waterbending and earthbending
Jeong Jeong describes water as "cool and soothing," earth as "steady and stable," and fire as destructive
Jeong Jeong deems Aang "too weak" to learn firebending
A vision of Avatar Roku appears to Jeong Jeong
Avatar Roku asks Jeong Jeong if he thinks Aang is "too weak"
Avatar Roku says he has mastered the elements a thousand times in a thousand lifetimes
Avatar Roku instructs Jeong Jeong to teach Aang firebending
Jeong Jeong begrudgingly agrees to teach Aang firebending
Jeong Jeong teaches Aang the basics, focusing exclusively on breathing exercises
Aang desires to learn more advanced firebending techniques
Aang spends hours working on squats, performing breathing practices, and feeling the sun's energy
Aang grows impatient and accuses Jeong Jeong of wasting his time
Jeong Jeong becomes greatly angered by Aang's lack of discipline
Jeong Jeong tells Aang that without control, firebending brings unnecessary destruction, using a former student as an example
Aang quickly apologizes and says he is ready to learn Jeong Jeong's way
Jeong Jeong instructs Aang in actual firebending
Aang feels enthusiastic about learning firebending
Jeong Jeong assigns Aang the task of preventing a leaf from being completely burned
Aang’s eagerness fades due to the simple task
Jeong Jeong leaves to take care of other matters
Aang takes his exercise to the next level and creates fire
Aang plays around with fire
Katara insists that Aang be careful
Aang ignores Katara’s pleas and continues to firebend
Aang accidentally burns Katara’s hands while imitating a move from the festival
Katara cries in pain and runs away
Sokka tackles Aang furiously
Sokka tells Jeong Jeong that this is all his fault
Jeong Jeong agrees with Sokka’s accusation
Jeong Jeong orders Team Avatar to pack up and leave immediately
Katara places her burned hands in the water to ease the pain
Katara is shocked when her hands heal completely
Jeong Jeong explains that Katara possesses vitakinetic healing powers, with water as the catalyst
Jeong Jeong confesses his desire to be a waterbender because fire only destroys
Admiral Zhao and his men locate the camp
Admiral Zhao, revealed as Jeong Jeong’s former student, and his men battle Jeong Jeong
Jeong Jeong’s attempt to battle is half-hearted
Aang hides away in Jeong Jeong's hut to reflect over his error
Aang vows never to firebend again
Katara reveals she has made a full recovery
Katara's recovery restores Aang's battle spirit
Jeong Jeong and his men make their escape into the woods with a fiery display
Aang confronts Zhao
Aang recalls Jeong Jeong's lesson of control
Aang provokes Zhao, goading him into destroying his own ships
Zhao destroys his own ships in anger
Zhao watches his ships burn as Aang escapes
Aang escapes
The three resume their journey and leave the forest after Jeong Jeong is gone
The three reflect on a hard-learned lesson of fire's destructive power
Chey returns to the campsite
Chey finds the campsite deserted
Katara notices Aang had burned his arm while fighting Zhao
Katara heals Aang's injury, demonstrating her new ability
Katara tells Aang and Sokka that she must have always possessed her healing abilities
Sokka rants about the many times in the past when Katara failed to use her abilities to aid him

The_Northern_Air_Temple.action: Team Avatar sits around a campfire with a larger group, listening to a man tell a story
Team Avatar sits around a campfire with a larger group, listening to a man tell a story
Team Avatar sits around a campfire with a larger group, listening to a man tell a story
a man tells a story about a reclusive band of "air walkers" who laugh at gravity
the man finishes his story and goes around the group taking tips
Katara and Aang talk briefly about airbenders
Katara and Aang talk briefly about airbenders
Sokka searches unsuccessfully for money to tip the storyteller
Aang, Katara, and Sokka believe the story is an ancient tale about airbenders
Aang, Katara, and Sokka believe the story is an ancient tale about airbenders
Aang, Katara, and Sokka believe the story is an ancient tale about airbenders
Aang mentions that the storyteller's great-grandfather must have heard the tale a hundred years ago
the storyteller replies that his great-grandfather saw the "air walkers" only a week before
Aang, Katara, and Sokka are shocked by the allegation that a living group of airbenders exists
Aang, Katara, and Sokka are shocked by the allegation that a living group of airbenders exists
Aang, Katara, and Sokka are shocked by the allegation that a living group of airbenders exists
They approach the Northern Air Temple and discover a cluster of people flying around the temple
Aang initially believes the people are airbenders
Aang looks closer and realizes they are not airbenders, disappointed
Aang states he can tell they are not airbenders by the way they move without spirit
A boy appears, sitting in a glider
Aang, eager to prove himself, takes off on his own glider
Appa becomes swarmed by a mob of airborne children
Sokka directs Appa to land in the temple courtyard
Aang and the boy on the glider engage in a talent contest
Aang airbends himself across the side of a wall on an air scooter as his glider flies alongside him and the other kid
The boy uses a canister of smoke on his glider to create an image of Aang's irked face in the sky
The watching crowd enthusiastically applauds
The boy lands and is revealed to be Teo, a paraplegic
Sokka excitedly compliments Teo on his glider
Teo responds, saying his dad designed even better things
Teo shows the gang into a room of the temple decorated by ancient murals, mostly hidden by pipes and machinery
Teo explains that the operation of the repurposed monastery is his father's idea and that all the machinery runs on hot air, powering everything in the temple
Sokka is enthralled by the machines
Aang is not excited by the machines
Aang describes the modifications to the temple as "unbelievable"
Teo says he thinks the changes are "great"
Aang rejects Teo's opinion, reiterating his previous assertion in a monotone
Aang tells Katara that the images on the mural-covered wall represented the history of the Air Nomads
The fountain in the courtyard, now green with algae, has been converted into an exhaust vent
The gang and Teo find a seemingly untouched room
Teo's father, the mechanist, destroys the statue in the room with a crane to make room for a bathhouse
Aang hurls the crane over the cliff with airbending, infuriated by the mechanist's destruction of history
The mechanist reacts quizzically to Aang's claim of knowing the temple's original monks
Teo tells the mechanist that Aang is the Avatar
Aang asks the mechanist what right he has to the temple
The mechanist tells Aang his story about losing his wife, Teo becoming an invalid, and finding the abandoned temple
The mechanist finds images of airbenders on gliders in the temple, inspiring him to create gliders for his son
The mechanist tells Aang he is improving on what already existed, like nature
Aang counters that "nature knows where to stop"
The mechanist acquiesces and rushes off with Sokka to tend to his duties
Teo tries to console Aang over his loss by showing him the animals living within the temple
Teo shows Aang the untouched entrance to the air temple's sanctuary
Teo asks Aang to open the sanctuary entrance, expressing his curiosity
Aang refuses to open the entrance, wanting to preserve the last unadulterated piece of the temple
Aang, Katara, and Teo take a short flight around the temple, with Katara using a glider
Aang changes his mind and agrees to open the gate with airbending
Sokka is given a tour of the inner workings of the temple by the mechanist
The mechanist takes Sokka into a dark cavern deep below the foundations of the temple
The mechanist explains they cannot use lanterns because the passage is filled with natural gas
The mechanist tells Sokka he inadvertently discovered the gas while holding a lit torch and nearly killed himself
The mechanist quips he thought his eyebrows would never grow back
The mechanist explains that gas occasionally leaks from the rock and filters upward to the temple
The mechanist admits he cannot detect the gas leaks and has not solved the problem
Sokka helps the mechanist with his duties
Sokka drops a rotten hard-boiled egg and other things
The mechanist gets the idea to use rotten eggs to give the gas a smell, making leaks detectable
A bell rings on the mechanist’s desk
The mechanist panics and hurries out of the room with Sokka in tow
Aang opens the door to the sanctuary
Aang discovers the sanctuary is filled with weapons and tools, including self-propelled tanks and a prototype hot air balloon bearing the Fire Nation insignia
The mechanist arrives quickly
Aang and Teo berate the mechanist about the situation
Aang and Teo demand an explanation from the mechanist
The mechanist reluctantly supplies an explanation
The mechanist explains that the Fire Nation discovered the settlement and threatened to destroy it
The mechanist made a deal with War Minister Qin to spare the settlement
The mechanist agreed to supply weapons to the Fire Nation on a monthly basis in exchange for the villagers’ safety
The mechanist leaves and goes to his study
The mechanist studies a small prototype for a hot air balloon
Aang and Teo interrupt the mechanist
Aang tells the mechanist he cannot continue supplying weapons to the Fire Nation
The mechanist insists there is no other alternative, fearing dire repercussions
A bell rings on the mechanist's desk
The mechanist orders Aang and Teo to leave
Aang and Teo refuse to leave
The mechanist tells Aang and Teo to hide
Aang and Teo hide
The mechanist pulls a rope
War Minister Qin rises up through the floor on an elevator
Qin demands the weapons he expects to receive
The mechanist is about to comply
Aang reveals himself by airbending the door shut, cornering Qin, and announcing the deal is off
Qin threatens to destroy the temple
Aang slaps Qin in the face with airbending
Qin announces the Avatar will be blamed for the destruction of the temple
Qin disappears via the elevator
Aang forcefully slams the trapdoor shut behind Qin
Teo, Sokka, and Katara express their concerns about Aang's decision to invite the Fire Nation military to attack the temple
Aang tells them the Fire Nation will be forced to attack from the ground
Aang explains the people of the temple can fight back from the sky, giving them an advantage
The mechanist approaches and offers his services
The mechanist and Sokka perfect the design of the war balloon
The mechanist and Sokka explain they have four types of bombs: smoke, slime, fire, and stink
The mechanist enthusiastically notes, "Never underestimate the power of stink!"
Fire Nation forces arrive and march along the narrow mountain path with grappling tanks
Assembled forces in the temple take flight against the advancing hordes
Aang accompanies the temple forces
Aang flips the tanks over repeatedly with airbending
The cockpits of the flipped tanks right themselves
Aang finds himself unable to beat the tanks
Teo states that the tanks have a water balancing system
Katara uses waterbending to fight the tanks
Katara disables a few tanks
The number of tanks proves to be overwhelming
Appa arrives, knocks all the tanks away, and retrieves Aang and Katara from the battlefield
Villagers run out of explosives
The war balloon appears in the sky with the mechanist and Sokka at its helm
Fire Nation does not attack the war balloon upon seeing its emblem
Sokka drops several slime bombs on the unaware army below
The aerial bombing does not stop the advance of the army
Sokka and the mechanist smell the pungent odor of rotten eggs used to detect natural gas leaks
Sokka realizes the damage a gas explosion could do to the tanks below and notices the balloon is out of bombs
Sokka detaches and throws the balloon's hot air engine into the crack
The explosion of the engine ignites the methane and decimates the invaders
Sokka and the mechanist are rescued by Aang
The derelict balloon, deprived of power, descends into the forest below
Aang tells Teo he is no longer upset by the changes made to the air temple
Aang expresses happiness that they have made the temple their own and asks them to continue protecting it
Qin and his soldiers find the war balloon, damaged but mostly intact
Qin's soldiers attempt to inflate the balloon
Qin mutters, "This defeat is the gateway to many victories."

Sokka complains that Appa is flying too slowly
Aang replies irritably that Sokka should fly them to the North Pole

Sokka notices a young woman on a boat and jumps onto Appa's tail to get another glimpse of her

Sokka murmurs, "Yeah... she is..."

Iroh invites Zuko for a nightly stroll to clear his head

Sokka suggests that Aang teach Katara everything he has learned during the day at night

the water Katara is bending begins to writhe around her in a complex stream

Katara expresses surprise, saying she is not manipulating the water

Aang and Katara look up to see Master Pakku on the bridge above them

Aang hastily apologizes

Iroh takes Admiral Zhao's offer of serving under him as general of the fleet
Katara goes to wait outside for Master Pakku
Master Pakku strolls past Katara, making patronizing comments

The fight begins between Katara and Master Pakku
Katara demonstrates powerful waterbending techniques and advanced forms
Katara and Master Pakku manipulate water and snow with basic and advanced waterbending moves
Katara and Master Pakku manipulate water and snow with basic and advanced waterbending moves
Master Pakku grins and smirks as he attacks Katara

Katara launches an ice disc at Master Pakku
Katara gains the upper hand by toppling ice-obelisks over Master Pakku

Master Pakku picks up the necklace after walking away
Master Pakku recognizes the necklace as the one he made for Kanna sixty years earlier
General Iroh walks past a Fire Nation soldier on one of Zhao's ships, stopping to speak quietly
Iroh tells the soldier their plan is working perfectly and that Zhao does not suspect a thing
The soldier takes off his faceplate, revealing he is an injured Prince Zuko
Zuko thanks his uncle for his help and returns his faceplate
Iroh tells Zuko to stay hidden until they reach the North Pole, where he will have a chance to capture the Avatar before Zhao
Iroh wishes Zuko luck
Iroh and Zuko continue on
Master Pakku tells Katara that her grandmother was arranged to marry him sixty years prior, but left for the South Pole before they could
Katara understands that Pakku loved Kanna, but she did not feel the same way
Katara explains that her grandmother refused to let the Northern tribe's sexist customs rule her life, and that is why Pakku lost her
Princess Yue bursts into tears and runs away
Sokka follows Yue and finds her on the bridge where they met earlier
Yue weeps softly
Sokka tells Yue he wants nothing but to tell her she is beautiful and that he never thought she would notice a guy like him
Yue expresses that Sokka does not understand
Sokka says he believes he does: she is a princess and he is just a Southern Water Tribe peasant
Sokka turns to leave, but Yue kisses him
Sokka is happy but extremely confused
Yue explains she has fallen for Sokka, but they cannot be together, and not for the reason Sokka thinks
Yue pulls down the furs about her neck to reveal a betrothal necklace, confessing that she is engaged
Yue apologizes and leaves Sokka alone on the bridge
Aang practices a form with the waterbending master
The waterbending master sarcastically remarks about Aang's ability
Katara shows up
Pakku encourages Katara to come train, accepting her as a student
Admiral Zhao's enormous invasion fleet mobilizes and sets a course for the Northern Water Tribe

The_Siege_of_the_North,_Part_1.action: a sparring match is fought between an anxious young man and a confident Katara
Katara swiftly defeats her opponent by encasing him in a tall ice formation
Master Pakku unceremoniously releases the student from the ice
Master Pakku asks the other students if any of them want to spar with Katara again
the other students do not respond
Master Pakku tells Katara she has progressed faster than the others and praises her hard work and determination

Sokka walks on the ledge of an ice bridge

Sokka comes down from the ledge
Yue touches Sokka's shoulder and laughs at his statement

Sokka and Yue converse as they climb into Appa's saddle
Sokka directs Appa into the sky with a dramatic "Yip yip"
Yue is amazed and wonders if it is always cold when flying
Yue moves closer to Sokka
Sokka softly replies, "Not when you're with someone."
Sokka and Yue look into each other's eyes and move toward a kiss
Sokka and Yue realize what is about to happen, blush, and quickly try to change the subject
Sokka looks around in embarrassment
Sokka notices gray snow beginning to fall from the sky and becomes concerned

gray snow begins to fall in Agna Qel'a
Aang rolls in the snow around Momo
Momo eats one of the gray particles and coughs it out
Momo's coughing attracts Aang's attention
Water Tribe citizens look on in wonder as the snow falls
Sokka and Yue land on a nearby glacier
Sokka examines the snow and tells Yue he recognizes it as soot from Fire Nation ships mixing with snow
Sokka tells Yue the Fire Nation has closed in on the North Pole with a massive fleet
a huge gray mushroom cloud looms on the horizon
Several Fire Nation ships sail through the water
Zhao tells Iroh they are in the process of writing history by destroying the last of the Water Tribe civilization

Zhao condescendingly assures Iroh this will not be like Iroh's failure at Ba Sing Se

Iroh departs
Iroh finds Zuko disguised as a guard in the bowels of the ship
Iroh tells Zuko they will be landing soon and inquires if Zuko has a plan
Zuko replies that he is working on something
A large drum adorned with the Water Tribe's insignia is beaten
citizens rush to the main gathering hall
Sokka and Yue head up the steps
Yue abruptly stops to talk to Sokka
Yue tells Sokka they cannot see each other because being around him makes her too confused, as she is engaged but likes him as more than a friend

Chief Arnook calls upon the Ocean and Moon Spirits to be with them as they fight for their existence
Chief Arnook asks for volunteers to engage in a dangerous mission

Katara objects to Sokka volunteering
Yue looks on, fighting back tears in her eyes
Chief Arnook paints a mark of commitment on Sokka's forehead

Aang admits he was not with his people when they were attacked by the Fire Nation
Aang vows to make a difference for the people of the Water Tribe
Water Tribe warriors look on as the first fireball is launched
first fireball strikes the center of the Northern Water Tribe emblem on the outer wall, throwing many soldiers off their feet
second fireball lands in the heart of the city
third fireball approaches
Aang mounts Appa and flies out to face the Fire Nation Navy
Several fireballs are launched by the lead ship
Aang approaches the lead ship
Aang deflects a fireball into the nearby ice cap
Aang leaps off Appa, expands his glider, and maneuvers between soldiers' fire blasts as he lands on the deck
Aang sweeps away those attacking him

Aang leaps aboard Appa to head to the next ship

Hahn presents himself in an eighty-five year old uniform
Sokka laughs at Hahn
Sokka sarcastically mocks Hahn's uniform
Sokka points out differences between the modern and old uniforms
Chief Arnook declares their first objective is to identify the fleet's commanding officer

Chief Arnook instructs Hahn to show Sokka respect, commenting on Sokka being his future son-in-law
Sokka is shocked by the revelation and insincerely congratulates Hahn
Iroh advises Zhao to cease the attack as waterbenders draw strength from the moon
The moon nears its full phase
Zhao states he is aware of the problem and is working on a solution
Zhao agrees to halt the attack until morning
Aang returns as the ships cease firing, exhausted and frustrated
Katara and Yue run to Aang
Aang tells Katara and Yue he was able to take out about a dozen ships
Aang expresses doubt about his ability to help the Water Tribe hold off the Fire Nation attack, saying he is "just one kid" against an entire fleet
Zuko removes his Fire Nation uniform and prepares for a solo journey into Agna Qel'a to capture the Avatar
Iroh expresses concern for Zuko, explaining he nags because he sees Zuko as his own son after losing his own
Zuko is touched by Iroh's statement and acknowledges it
Zuko says he will meet Iroh again after he has the Avatar
Hahn arrogantly explains to Sokka the perks of marrying Yue
Hahn insults Sokka by insinuating he lacks the ability to understand the Northern Water Tribe's way of functioning
Sokka becomes enraged
Sokka attacks Hahn, calling him a "jerk without a soul"
Sokka and Hahn fight
Sokka and Hahn fight
Chief Arnook pulls Sokka and Hahn apart
Sokka is removed from the mission
Zuko maneuvers his kayak to the shores of the ice cap near the base of the Water Tribe outer wall
Zuko notices turtle seals diving into an opening in the ice
turtle seals dive into an opening in the ice
Zuko deduces the creatures must come up for air somewhere
Zuko takes a deep breath and dives in after the turtle seals
The nearly full moon hangs in the night sky
Yue tells Aang and Katara that the moon taught waterbending to the Water Tribes by its push and pull on the ocean
Aang is hit with inspiration and realizes the spirits might be able to help him save the Water Tribe
Katara voices skepticism about how Aang can reach the spirits
Yue leads Aang and Katara to the Spirit Oasis, the most spiritual place in the North Pole
The Spirit Oasis is revealed as a verdant garden surrounded by towering walls of ice
Aang is ecstatic to see grass again
Katara is amazed by the warmness of the air
Aang senses the tranquility of the oasis and begins to meditate at the base of the oasis pool
The oasis pool contains a black and a white koi fish encircling each other
Zuko finds an underground cave, which serves as the turtle seals' air pocket
Zuko uses his "breath of fire" technique to ward off the chilling effects of the water
Zuko begins to work his way up an opening with water rushing through
Sokka sharpens his boomerang in the now-empty barracks
Chief Arnook approaches Sokka
Chief Arnook clarifies that he removed Sokka from the mission in hopes that Sokka would accept to guard Princess Yue
Sokka humbly agrees to guard Princess Yue
Zuko ascends to an air pocket in the freezing water and catches his breath
Zuko spots another passage under the water and heads through
Zuko is met with an ice barrier
Zuko uses his firebending to melt the ice
Zuko breaks through into a passage within the city
Zuko stops for a moment to rest
Aang tries to meditate into the Spirit World
Katara and Yue's conversation distracts Aang
Aang shouts at Katara and Yue to be quiet
Katara and Yue comply and become quiet
Aang focuses on the koi fish
Aang enters the Spirit World after noticing the similarity of the two fish to yin and yang
Yue sees Aang's eyes and tattoos glow and asks Katara if they should get help
Katara says she can protect Aang
Zuko suddenly walks in
Yue leaves to get help
Katara and Zuko duel
Zuko realizes Katara's skill level has greatly advanced
Zuko is encased in ice
The sun rises, increasing Zuko's firebending strength
Zuko melts his way out of Katara's trap
Zuko overpowers Katara and renders her unconscious by throwing her against a gate post with a fire blast
Zuko stands over Katara's motionless form and notes that he draws strength from the sun
Zuko takes Aang's body and escapes from the oasis
Fire Nation ships break through the Water Tribe's outer wall
Fire Nation soldiers rush through the breaches to attack
Zhao states the city will fall today despite any efforts to fight off the inevitable
Katara regains consciousness and discovers that Aang is gone
Sokka and Yue approach on Appa
Katara tells Sokka and Yue that Zuko has Aang
Sokka and Katara wonder where Zuko might have gone
Zuko treks across the polar ice surrounding Agna Qel'a, carrying a limp Aang over his shoulder
Aang remains in the Spirit World, limp
snow flutters from an ominous, gray sky

The_Siege_of_the_North,_Part_2.action: Sokka, Katara, and Princess Yue set out on Appa in search of Aang
Fire Nation continues its attack on the Northern Water Tribe
Zuko carries Aang across the polar ice cap
Aang's spirit is in the middle of a swamp in the Spirit World
Aang approaches a meditating baboon spirit for help finding the Moon and Ocean Spirits
Baboon spirit grows annoyed and tells Aang to leave him alone
Aang ignores the baboon spirit's request
A glowing speck flies by
Baboon spirit suggests Aang chase the glowing speck
Aang chases the glowing speck through the Spirit World
Aang grasps the glowing speck
The branch Aang is standing on disappears
Aang falls into the water
Avatar Roku's image appears as a reflection in the water and greets Aang
The ice on the frozen waste begins to give way under Zuko
Zuko carries Aang over his shoulder
Zuko dashes frantically across the fracturing surface, avoiding falling through the ice
Zuko catches his breath
Zuko sees a cave a short distance away
Zuko drags Aang's body into the shelter of the cave
Zuko binds Aang
Aang’s spirit tells Roku that the Water Tribe is under attack and he needs help locating the Moon and Ocean Spirits
Roku ascends from the water and materializes in front of Aang
Roku tells Aang that the Moon and Ocean Spirits crossed over into the mortal world near the beginning of time
Roku guides Aang toward Koh, knowing only Koh is old enough to help
Roku warns Aang to be careful, as Koh is dangerous and will steal his face if he shows emotion
Aang is horrified by the possibility, but nods in understanding
Sokka, Katara, and Yue continue to look for Aang in the blizzard
Sokka, Katara, and Yue continue to look for Aang in the blizzard
Sokka, Katara, and Yue continue to look for Aang in the blizzard
Zuko talks to an unconscious Aang
Zuko laments over the irony of capturing the Avatar but being prevented from returning to the Fire Nation by the blizzard
Zuko muses that there is always something preventing his success
Zuko compares Aang to his sister, noting everything came easy to her
Zuko bitterly recalls his father's statement that his sister was born lucky, while he was lucky to be born
Zuko denounces his need for luck, claiming his struggles have made him strong
Fire Nation troops move further into the Northern Water Tribe despite the waterbender warriors' efforts
Iroh reminds Zhao that if the Water Tribe is not subdued by the rise of the full moon, the waterbenders will be undefeatable
Zhao claims he is in control of the situation and plans to remove the moon from the battle
Hahn interrupts Zhao and Iroh, and attacks Zhao
Zhao nonchalantly throws Hahn overboard
Zhao continues his conversation with Iroh as if nothing happened
Zhao explains that years earlier, as a young officer in the Earth Kingdom, he discovered the secret of the Moon and Ocean Spirits' mortal forms in an underground library
Zhao declares it is his destiny to kill the spirits
Iroh angrily warns Zhao that the spirits are not to be trifled with
Zhao condescendingly tells Iroh he has heard tales of Iroh's journey into the Spirit World
Zhao assures Iroh that the Moon and Ocean Spirits will face the consequences of giving up their immortality
Aang approaches a large leafless tree
Aang notices another spirit in the form of a monkey
Aang approaches and speaks to the monkey spirit
The monkey spirit turns around, revealing it has no face
Aang, terrified but determined, takes a deep breath
Aang repeats Roku's instructions to himself
Aang enters the hollow tree, calling out to Koh
Koh, with a large centipede-like body and a face similar to a Japanese Noh mask, appears in front of Aang
Koh switches faces
Koh explains to Aang that this is not their first encounter
Koh states that one of Aang's previous incarnations tried to kill him over the stealing of a loved one's face
Koh continues to state he is no longer concerned for what Aang did in his past life
Aang takes a breath in to gather strength and remain composed as Koh tries to manipulate him
Aang’s body takes a deep breath
Zuko looks on as Aang breathes
Zuko says they will be in the shelter for a while
Yue tries to comfort Katara by saying Zuko and Aang could not have escaped
Katara states her worry is not that Zuko will escape, but that he will fail and jeopardize Aang’s life
Sokka assures Katara that Zuko will not give up, and that both he and Aang are alive and will be found
Koh asks, "So. How may I help you?" menacingly
Aang tells Koh of his need to find the Moon and Ocean Spirits
Koh tells Aang the spirits' names: Tui and La
Aang explains that an entire culture may be destroyed if he does not get the spirits' help
Koh replies that the spirits are in need of Aang's help, as someone is planning to kill one of them
Aang asks how he can find the spirits to protect them
Koh tells Aang that he has already met them
Aang realizes the spirits' mortal identities upon hearing Koh's reference to yin and yang, and his face lights up
Koh hears the uplift in Aang's tone, whirls around to face him, and finds that Aang has caught himself, narrowly avoiding losing his face
Aang leaves a somewhat disappointed Koh behind in his tree
Aang explains to Roku's reflection that the spirits are in trouble and he needs to get back to the mortal world
Roku tells Aang that an old friend will help him back
Hei Bai appears; Aang climbs on Hei Bai's back, and they rush away into the wild
Night falls and the full moon rises at Agna Qel'a, enhancing the power of the waterbending warriors
Waterbending warriors battle the soldiers of the Fire Nation
Waterbenders, with enhanced bending, demolish tundra tanks and infantry easily
Zhao and his search party depart his ship in search of the Spirit Oasis
Zhao declares that when they get to the Spirit Oasis, "We're going fishing."
Hei Bai takes Aang back to the gate at which he entered the Spirit World
Hei Bai breathes out a light toward Aang, sending his spirit to the mortal world
Aang crosses out of the Spirit World
The baboon spirit, still meditating nearby, comments, "Good riddance"
Hei Bai temporarily changes into his monstrous, angered form
Hei Bai, seemingly upset by the comment, blasts the baboon spirit off his rock
Hei Bai returns to his normal appearance
Aang's spirit returns to the oasis in the Northern Water Tribe and finds his body is not there
Aang's spirit is pulled through the sky toward his body as a streak of light
Katara, on Appa's back, searches for Aang and sees the streak of light
Katara realizes the light is Aang's spirit and turns Appa to follow it
The light reaches the cave where Zuko is sheltering with Aang's body
Aang's spirit rejoins his body; his tattoos fade to blue and he sits up
Aang has a short, curt exchange with Zuko
Aang airbends a large gust of air, throwing Zuko against the side of the shelter and propelling himself out of the cave and into the snow
Zuko catches the still-bound Aang
Appa lands next to them and Katara jumps down
Zuko inquires if Katara came for a rematch
Katara replies confidently and quickly defeats Zuko by forcing him high into the air with a column of snow and smashing him to the ground, knocking him out
Sokka, Katara, Aang, and Yue prepare to leave
Aang hesitates, refusing to leave Zuko to freeze to death
Aang lifts Zuko's limp body into the saddle before they depart
Zhao removes the white koi from its pond
Zhao hoists the koi over his head in triumph
The full moon transforms from white to blood-red
Waterbending warriors lose their bending abilities
Fire Nation troops begin to push forward
Yue, on Appa's back, begins to feel faint
Sokka takes notice of Yue's condition
Yue tells the others about her condition
Aang concurs, saying he feels it as well
Yue explains that the Moon Spirit restored her life as a baby and that her name means "moon"
Zhao arrogantly applauds his own efforts and speculates about his legacy
Momo jumps onto Zhao's head, pulls at his hair, and flies over to Aang
Aang arrives with the others and stands opposite Zhao
Aang pleads with Zhao to consider his actions, warning of harm to all
Iroh appears, affirms Aang's words, and promises Zhao retribution
Iroh orders Zhao to release the fish and prepares to fight
Zhao begrudgingly releases the spirit back into the water
Zhao attacks the spirit with fire, killing it and destroying the moon
The moon vanishes completely from the sky, plunging the world in darkness
Iroh unleashes his firebending on Zhao and his guards, easily defeating the soldiers
Zhao escapes, unwilling to take on Iroh
Iroh lifts the burned white fish from the water sorrowfully
Yue mournfully states, "There's no hope now. It's over."
Aang goes into the Avatar State, offering a response: "No. It's not over."
Aang steps into the oasis
Katara tries to follow Aang
Iroh gently motions Katara to stay back
Aang stands in the oasis as the living fish begins to circle him
Aang is abruptly pulled under the surface and merges with the Ocean Spirit
Aang and the Ocean Spirit leave the oasis, following the conduits of water into Agna Qel'a
Aang and the Ocean Spirit take the form of a massive glowing koi fish
Water Tribe citizens fall to their knees and bow respectfully
Fire Nation troops raise their weapons at the new apparition
The great koi leaves the Water Tribe citizens unharmed and unleashes its wrath upon the invaders, wiping them out and clearing the city of enemy soldiers
The koi makes its way toward the sea
Zhao slips through the deserted streets, trying to escape
Zuko, newly freed and infuriated, suddenly attacks Zhao
Zhao is shocked that Zuko is alive
Zhao antagonizes Zuko by confirming he tried to have Zuko killed
Zhao reveals he knew Zuko was the Blue Spirit who stole the Avatar
Zuko counters that he had no choice
Zhao tells Zuko he should have accepted his fate as a failure and a disgrace
Zhao and Zuko begin to fight again
Iroh notices that Yue has been touched by the Moon Spirit and that some of its life force is within her
Yue affirms Iroh's conjecture
Yue decides to try to restore Tui to life by giving hers to it
Sokka protests Yue's decision, saying, "Your father told me to protect you!"
Yue calmly replies, "I have to do this,"
Yue places her hands on the dead fish
Yue's spirit leaves her body as she closes her eyes, exhales one last time, and collapses into Sokka's arms, dead
Yue's body disappears moments later
The fish, suddenly filled with life, glows as Iroh places it gently in the oasis
The fish begins to swim
The oasis glows as well
Yue appears above the water as a spirit, clothed in a flowing white dress
Yue tells Sokka that she will always be with him, kisses him one last time, and disappears
The moon reappears in the sky, restoring the waterbenders' abilities
Aang and La lay waste to the Fire Nation's soldiers and navy
Aang and La make their way to the ocean, destroying ships as they go
The moon reappears
The Ocean Spirit acknowledges the moon's restoration
The Ocean Spirit ends its violent vendetta
The Ocean Spirit places Aang atop the outer wall as it moves back through Agna Qel'a
Zuko and Zhao continue dueling
Zhao sees that the moon has returned and shouts, "It can't be!"
The Ocean Spirit, in the form of a giant hand, seizes Zhao and begins to pick him up
Zuko forgets the duel and tries to help Zhao, reaching out a hand to him
Zhao stubbornly refuses to take Zuko's hand
Zhao is pulled beneath the water and disappears from view
Master Pakku announces plans to head to the Southern Water Tribe with other tribe members to help rebuild their sister tribe
Katara asks Pakku who will oversee Aang's training
Pakku tells Katara that Aang will have to get used to calling her 'Master Katara'
Chief Arnook quietly tells Sokka about a vision the spirits gave him when Yue was born: a young woman giving her life to become the moon
Sokka states that Chief Arnook must be proud
Chief Arnook replies that he is proud, but also deeply saddened by Yue's death
Iroh slyly says he is surprised that Zuko is not absorbed with capturing the Avatar
Zuko replies that he is tired
Iroh softly tells Zuko that a man needs his rest
Zuko lays flat on his back and closes his eyes
Katara meets Aang on an overlook in Agna Qel'a, and they embrace
Aang embraces Katara
Momo joins the group hug
Sokka places his hand reassuringly on Aang's shoulder
Appa flies in midair nearby
They look out over the city, toward the ocean and the moon beyond
Fire Lord Ozai imparts the knowledge of Iroh's treasonous behavior and Zuko's failure to Azula
Fire Lord Ozai entrusts Azula with a special task
Azula looks up at Ozai with a smirk on her face

The_Avatar_State.action: Aang wanders the ruin of the Southern Air Temple in a dream
Aang finds the statue of Monk Gyatso
The statue morphs into a doppelganger of Aang in the Avatar State
Aang sees Sokka and Katara huddling behind a rock
Aang's doppelganger attacks him, throwing him backward
Aang finds himself in the Fire Nation Avatar Temple
The doors to the inner sanctum open
The doppelganger appears within the inner sanctum
The doppelganger splits the floor, causing Aang to fall through the crack
Aang lands on the deck of a Fire Nation ship
Aang looks up and finds himself face to face with the doppelganger, now fused with La as a giant koi fish
The monstrous creature slashes at Aang
Aang wakes up, horrified and gasping
Aang realizes he was having a nightmare
Aang quietly leaves the hammock in which he was sleeping
Aang wakes Katara while leaving the hammock
Aang moves to the deck of the Water Tribe ship and stands by the side of the ship
Katara follows Aang and stands next to him, asking if he wants to talk
Aang confides his nightmare to Katara and tells her the Avatar State terrifies him
Pakku gives the group parting gifts for their journey to the Earth Kingdom base
Katara receives an amulet filled with water from the Spirit Oasis
Aang receives waterbending scrolls
Sokka hopes for a gift
Pakku gives Sokka a pat on the shoulder and says "Take care, son."
Iroh relaxes and gets a massage at a beautiful spa resort
Zuko is unhappy on the three-year anniversary of his banishment from the Fire Nation
Zuko reiterates to Iroh his desire to reclaim his honor, the throne, and his father's respect
Iroh conjectures that the Fire Lord does care, explaining that he otherwise would not have banished Zuko
Zuko angrily leaves
Iroh asks the masseurs if what he said had come out wrong
Fire Nation ship carrying Princess Azula nears the Earth Kingdom
Azula tells her crew that Zuko and Iroh have disgraced the Fire Lord and brought shame on them all
Azula warns her crew that she will not hesitate to punish them if they show restraint in attacking the royal family
Azula dismisses her crew
The captain informs Azula that the tides will not allow them to enter the port on time
Azula threatens to have the captain thrown overboard if he does not bring the ship in
The captain complies with Azula's demand
Aang and his friends arrive at the Earth Kingdom base and are greeted with honor
General Fong describes Aang's single-handed destruction of a Fire Nation fleet
Fong expresses belief that Aang is ready to face the Fire Lord and end the Hundred Year War
Aang panics and insists he is not ready
Aang explains he could only do what he did because of the Avatar State, which he cannot enter willingly
Fong shows Aang the earth army infirmary, housing soldiers hurt by the War
Fong asks Aang to consider his offer to help Aang gain control of the Avatar State
Azula practices lightning generation, a highly advanced form of firebending, on her ship
Lo and Li watch Azula's progress and remark she is close, but not perfect
Azula angrily remarks that "almost isn't good enough"
Azula continues practicing and fires a bolt of lightning into the sky
thunder is heard from some distance away
Iroh wakes and looks around, concerned
Aang goes to meet Fong and accepts his offer
Aang returns to the room where Katara and Sokka are sleeping and informs them of his choice
Sokka agrees with Aang, seeing it as a simple, obvious shortcut
Katara disagrees, believing Aang needs to master the elements in the proper manner
Aang tells Katara to be realistic, saying he doesn't have time to learn all the elements "the right way"
Katara becomes upset and leaves the room, refusing to be a part of the plan
Fong begins testing Aang the next day, attempting to trigger the Avatar State
Fong serves Aang a special herbal tea to increase his energy, but it only gives Aang an energy rush
Sokka tries to scare Aang into the Avatar State, but it does not work
Fong organizes a ritual where Aang wears clothing from all four nations and is bathed in all four elements, but it only makes Aang sneeze
Azula finds Iroh and Zuko at the resort and pays them a friendly visit
Iroh and Zuko are immediately suspicious of Azula's intentions
Azula tells them the Fire Lord regrets banishing Zuko due to rumors of treachery and subversion in the Fire Nation
Azula says the Fire Lord wants Zuko to come home, as family is the only one he can trust
Zuko is dumbstruck by the news
Azula departs, requesting that they make their choice and inform her by the next morning
Katara finds Aang on a balcony at sunset
Katara tells Aang that watching him in the Avatar State is frightening for those who care about him
Aang acknowledges Katara's concern but stands by his decision, believing Fong is right
