Aang faults himself for being unable to stop the Hundred Year War and feels he must try to do something about it
Katara, saddened, leaves and tells Aang she will no longer watch their attempts to induce the Avatar State
Zuko is overjoyed by the news that his father wants him to return home
Iroh is suspicious of the news, having never known the Fire Lord to be regretful of anything
Iroh warns Zuko that things are never what they seem in their family
Zuko becomes upset by Iroh's assertion
Zuko angrily insults his uncle, accusing him of being jealous of his younger brother
Aang has another nightmare
Aang's Avatar State doppelganger rises out of the ocean on a column of water on Zuko's ship
Avatar State doppelganger descends to the deck and hurls the real Aang off the deck with waterbending
Avatar State doppelganger assaults Zuko
Aang awakens, terrified
Aang wakes a sleeping Sokka
Aang tells Sokka that trying to force the Avatar State out is a bad idea
Aang asks Sokka if General Fong will be upset by his change of heart
Sokka casually replies that there is nothing Fong could say since Aang is the Avatar and the expert
Zuko makes his way to the port
Iroh joins Zuko, having changed his mind
Iroh tells Zuko that family sticks together
Zuko smiles happily
Zuko and Iroh head for the ship
Iroh glances at the ship warily
Aang explains to Fong that he is done testing himself and believes the Avatar State can only be triggered in genuine danger
Fong assaults Aang with earthbending
Fong orders his soldiers to attack Aang
Aang evades the soldiers' attacks, unwilling to fight them
Aang unsuccessfully tries to persuade the soldiers to stop attacking
Sokka breaks free from two guards attempting to restrain him
Azula warmly welcomes her brother and uncle at the port
Azula, Zuko, and Iroh board the ship
Azula tells the captain to set a course for home
The ship's captain absentmindedly orders the crew to raise the anchors, saying they are "taking the prisoners home"
Iroh and Zuko catch the captain's slip and realize Azula's true intentions
Zuko attacks the captain and throws him overboard
Zuko moves to confront Azula
Iroh fights off the ship's guards
Aang runs from the earthbenders
Katara hears noise outside while lying on her bed
Katara hears another loud rumble and grows worried
Katara decides to check on Aang's progress
Katara runs down a staircase and sees General Fong and his troops attacking Aang
Katara asks Sokka what is going on
Sokka comes down the opposite staircase
Sokka briefly explains that Fong is trying to force Aang into the Avatar State
Katara and Sokka rush to help Aang
Katara and Sokka rush to help Aang
General Fong earthbends the ground beneath Katara, causing her to sink
Katara is slowly swallowed by the earth
Aang begs Fong to release Katara
General Fong refuses to relent and lets the ground completely swallow Katara
Katara's scream is cut off as she is completely buried
Aang becomes enraged and enters the Avatar State
General Fong is initially delighted
Aang devastates everything around himself indiscriminately
Zuko fights Azula
Azula easily avoids Zuko's attacks
Azula taunts Zuko about their father's opinion and insults him
Zuko becomes furious and renews his attacks
Azula throws Zuko harshly to the bottom of the stairs
Azula prepares to kill a defenseless and disoriented Zuko with lightning
Iroh grabs Azula's hand to redirect the lightning blast into a nearby cliffside
Iroh throws Azula overboard
Iroh and Zuko escape
Fong releases an unharmed Katara, revealing her disappearance was a trick to trigger the Avatar State
Fong admits the trick, but fails to calm Aang
Aang continues to destroy the base atop his earth vortex
Avatar Roku appears to Aang
Roku takes a projection of Aang's soul to explain the Avatar State
Roku informs Aang that the Avatar State is a defense mechanism providing the skills and knowledge of all past Avatars
Roku warns Aang that if the Avatar is killed in the Avatar State, the reincarnation cycle will be broken and the Avatar will cease to exist
Aang returns to his own body and leaves the Avatar State, distressed by the destruction he has caused
General Fong is gleeful over having succeeded in inducing the Avatar State
Aang tells Fong he is insane
Fong ignores Aang and says they can figure out how to control the Avatar State on the way to the Fire Nation
Sokka rides up to Fong and knocks him unconscious with his club
Sokka asks the remaining soldiers if they have any qualms about his actions
None of the soldiers raises an objection
Team Avatar departs on Appa for Omashu by themselves, rejecting the escort offered by Fong's shell-shocked soldiers
Azula holds up a wanted poster depicting Zuko and Iroh's faces
Azula warns the gathered villagers that anybody harboring Zuko and Iroh will face the Fire Lord's wrath
a group of villagers gathers to listen to Azula
Zuko and Iroh escape from Azula's guards
Zuko and Iroh stop to rest near a small creek
Zuko and Iroh cut off their top-knots as a symbol of severance from their family and the Fire Nation
Zuko and Iroh place their top-knots in the creek
Zuko and Iroh watch as the current slowly washes the top-knots out of sight
The current slowly washes the top-knots out of sight

The_Cave_of_Two_Lovers.action: Aang and Katara practice waterbending
Sokka relaxes in the middle of a lake
A group of Earth Kingdom nomads appears, displaying carefree, nature-loving characteristics
Team Avatar reveals their plan to travel to Omashu
Chong, the lead nomad, speaks of a shortcut through a tunnel under a mountain
Sokka initially distrusts the nomads
Team Avatar attempts to fly over the mountains but is assaulted by Fire Nation soldiers
Team Avatar agrees to follow the nomads through the tunnels
Zuko and Iroh try to survive on their own
Zuko cannot find food
Iroh makes tea using the dangerous white jade bush, mistaking it for the white dragon bush
Iroh procures a branch of berries that could either cure or blind him
Zuko swipes the branch away, refusing to take more chances with strange plants
Zuko states they need to find help
Zuko and Iroh weigh their options about where to go for safety
Zuko and Iroh decide to travel to an Earth Kingdom village for medicine and food
Chong mentions that the tunnels form a labyrinth protected by a curse
Chong explains that only those who put their trust in love can navigate the maze; others will be forever lost
Aang, Katara, and Sokka enter the cave
Fire Nation soldiers in tundra tanks destroy the entrance to the tunnels, trapping everyone inside
Sokka creates a map to mark their progress while traveling through the labyrinth
Sokka's technique proves useless as the tunnels continually change around them
Zuko and Iroh meet a friendly girl named Song in the nearby town
Song inquires about their identities
Zuko states his name is Lee and Iroh's name is Mushi
Iroh, displeased, claims Zuko was named after his father and calls him Junior
Zuko is dismayed by being called Junior
Song invites Zuko and Iroh to her house for dinner
Zuko and Iroh accept the invitation when Song mentions her mother would be cooking roast duck
During the meal, Song tells Zuko and Iroh that the Fire Nation captured her father during a raid and she has not seen him since
Song asks Zuko if his father is fighting in the Hundred Year War
Zuko affirms that his father is fighting, but does not disclose his father's identity as the Fire Lord
Appa panics at being underground, causing a cave-in
a wolfbat attacks, exacerbating the situation
a torch is carelessly wielded, worsening the cave-in
Aang acts quickly to protect everyone from being crushed by falling rocks
the group ends up divided by the cave-in
Aang, Katara, and Appa wind up together
Momo and Sokka are forced to travel through the tunnels with the musical nomads
Sokka is dismayed by the situation
Chong assures Sokka not to be let down by the cave-in
Zuko sits on the front porch of Song's house
Song joins Zuko on the porch
Song observes that the Fire Nation has hurt Zuko
Song tries to tenderly touch Zuko's scar
Zuko rebuffs Song's attempt to touch his scar
Song sympathizes with Zuko and shows him a burn scar on her leg
Zuko expresses surprise upon seeing Song's injury
Aang and Katara travel for a while
Aang and Katara discover the Tomb of the Two Lovers spoken of in lore
Aang and Katara read script engraved on the walls
Aang and Katara learn the true story of the two lovers
A man and a woman from feuding towns meet atop a mountain
The loving couple continue their relationship in secret despite dangers
The couple learn earthbending by observing badgermoles
The couple create a labyrinth as a secret meeting place
One day, the man does not come because he is killed in the war
The woman expresses her fury in a display of earthbending prowess
The woman declares an end to the conflict instead of destroying the towns
The two villages create a city to honor the couple's love
The settlement is named Omashu, from the names Oma and Shu
Aang and Katara turn around to see a statue of the lovers
The statue is marked with the slogan: "Love is brightest in the dark."
Katara suggests that by kissing, she and Aang could find their way out of the labyrinth
Aang finds the idea intriguing due to his affection for Katara
Aang attempts to reassure an embarrassed Katara that he did not want to kiss her
Katara misinterprets Aang’s statement, thinking he was implying she was undesirable
Aang assures Katara that, given the choice between certain death and kissing her, he would choose to kiss her, insisting it was a compliment
Katara becomes angered and storms off
Aang laments his poor choice of words
Chong expresses his belief that the way out of the cave is to play a love song
Chong proceeds to guide the group
Sokka follows, feeling irked
Zuko and Iroh depart from Song's residence
Iroh expresses gratitude to Song's family for their excellent meal
Song tells Zuko not to lose hope because the Avatar has returned
Zuko responds that he is well aware the Avatar has returned
Zuko steals the family's ostrich horse for easier travel
Iroh tries to convince Zuko not to steal the ostrich horse
Iroh mounts the ostrich horse after Zuko insists
Song witnesses Zuko and Iroh stealing the ostrich horse but does not stop them
Song closes her door, seemingly disappointed, as Zuko and Iroh leave
the torch burns out
Aang and Katara share a meaningful look
Aang and Katara lean toward each other, close their eyes, and share a kiss in the darkness
Aang and Katara discover that the two lovers followed a path of glowing crystals, which only shone in the dark, illuminating a pathway to the tunnel's exit
Aang, Katara, and Appa find their way out of the tunnel
Appa is delighted to see sunlight
Sokka and the nomads continue to incur dead-ends in their quest for the exit
Sokka and the nomads are confronted by a pair of enormous badgermoles who corner the group
Sokka tries to escape and accidentally strums a dropped guitar, producing music
The angry badgermole pauses, intrigued by the music
Sokka tries to play a song to pacify the beast
The nomads realize the effect of music and contribute their talents
The group successfully pacifies the badgermoles
The badgermoles transport the group to the exit of the labyrinth
The group is reunited with Aang, Katara, and Appa
The badgermoles depart
Katara notices a big red spot on Sokka's forehead
Sokka smacks his forehead whenever the nomads act imprudently
Chong states his belief that the one with tattoos is the Avatar
Sokka hits himself again
Aang asks Moku if they will be accompanying them to Omashu
Moku abruptly responds that they will not accompany them
Aang thanks Moku and bids the singing nomads farewell
Aang, Sokka, and Katara arrive at Omashu
Aang, Sokka, and Katara mount a ridge which hides the city from view
Aang, Sokka, and Katara are devastated to discover that the Fire Nation has taken control of the city
The Fire Nation has taken control of Omashu

Return_to_Omashu.action: Aang insists on entering Omashu to search for Bumi, despite Sokka and Katara's reservations
Sokka and Katara express reservations about entering the city
Sokka and Katara express reservations about entering the city
Aang, Sokka, and Katara enter the city through the sewers to remain unnoticed by the guards
Aang, Sokka, and Katara enter the city through the sewers to remain unnoticed by the guards
Aang, Sokka, and Katara enter the city through the sewers to remain unnoticed by the guards
Sokka finds purple pentapi attached to his face
Sokka removes the pentapi, leaving prominent red marks on his skin
Aang removes the last pentapus from Sokka's face
Fire Nation soldiers approach, accusing them of breaking curfew
Guards notice the blotches and inquire how Sokka received them
Katara invents a cover story, claiming the marks are from a highly contagious and deadly disease called pentapox
Fire Nation soldiers believe the story and flee, not wanting to catch the disease
Azula seeks the advice of her trainers, Lo and Li, about capturing her brother and uncle
Lo and Li advise Azula that traveling with the Royal Procession may no longer be wise if she hopes to keep the element of surprise
Azula takes their advice
Azula decides to put together a small, elite team to track her brother and uncle
Aang, Katara, and Sokka decide that wherever Bumi is being held prisoner must be made entirely of metal to prevent him from earthbending
The governor's wife and their daughter, Mai, enjoy an evening stroll
Mai complains about how boring it is in Omashu
Two earthbenders from the resistance send large boulders plummeting toward Mai and the governor's wife
Aang sees the rocks heading toward the people below
Aang airbends the rocks out of the way
The governor's wife misunderstands the situation and assumes Aang and his friends are members of the resistance
Mai and the guards escorting her give chase to Aang and his friends
Mai demonstrates a natural talent for throwing knives during the pursuit
As Mai comes close to catching up, the ground opens up underneath Team Avatar
Aang, Katara, and Sokka fall into the hole as it closes over them
Mai abandons the chase
Aang, Katara, and Sokka discover they have been saved by the resistance
Azula tracks down Ty Lee, the daughter of a nobleman
Azula finds Ty Lee at the circus
Azula informs Ty Lee that she wants her help in tracking down her "fuddy-duddy" uncle, neglecting to mention Zuko
Ty Lee declines Azula’s offer, saying she is happy with her life at the circus
Azula seemingly accepts Ty Lee’s decision
Azula informs Ty Lee that she is staying to watch her show, instilling nervousness in Ty Lee
Aang is informed that King Bumi surrendered the city before the fighting began
Aang decides the best thing to do is get everyone out of the city
The resistance agrees to Aang's plan against the leader's protests
Sokka comes up with the idea to use pentapi to fake "pentapox" so the guards will avoid them
The citizens re-create pentapox spots on their faces
Aang goes in search of Bumi while the guards are distracted
The governor and his family watch as the "sick" people roam the streets
The governor orders the guards to drive the "sick" people out of the city
The governor and his family lose track of the governor's infant son, who wanders off
Aang comes across Flopsie during his search
Flopsie rushes forward, but is held back by a chain
Aang freezes the chain with ice to make it brittle and smashes it, freeing Flopsie
Aang and Flopsie go off in search of Bumi
Momo finds some berries inside a room after wandering off
The young infant is intrigued by Momo's tail and quickly grasps it
Momo is spooked and attempts to escape the infant's clutches
Momo inadvertently half flies, half drags the infant across the city
The boy finally falls off Momo's tail and lands on the ground
The boy notices the mob of people leaving the city
The boy wanders off further toward the open gates
Shuzumu addresses the audience, declaring the presence of the Fire Lord's daughter an honor
Shuzumu takes a seat beside the Fire Lord's daughter
Shuzumu asks the Fire Lord's daughter how he can make the show more to her liking
The Fire Lord's daughter accepts his offer with a smirk
Ty Lee performs a feat of balance on the high wire
Azula asks the ringmaster if he believes Ty Lee will fall
The ringmaster confidently replies, "Of course not."
Azula asks the ringmaster to have the net below Ty Lee removed
Azula decides it would be more interesting if Ty Lee performed without the safety net
The ringmaster tries to object
Azula relents for a moment, then decides to have the net set on fire
The ringmaster, unwilling to refuse a direct order, sets the net ablaze
The fire grows, making Ty Lee more anxious
The ringmaster is clearly worried for Ty Lee's safety
Azula orders the ringmaster to release all of his dangerous animals inside the tent
The ringmaster fearfully complies and releases the animals
The gang and the resistance leader, Yung, successfully and safely escape the city
Yung informs the gang that they have one more person than they should – Tom-Tom, the infant
The governor and his family in Omashu become aware that Tom-Tom is missing
The governor believes the resistance has kidnapped Tom-Tom
The governor ponders how to get Tom-Tom back
Azula congratulates her friend on the show
Azula wonders aloud how she will supersede her act the next day
Ty Lee tells Azula there will be no performance, as she is leaving the circus
Ty Lee states that the universe is giving her strong hints to go with Azula
Katara plays with Tom-Tom
Yung offers the opinion that Tom-Tom will not be "so cute" when he is older and believes the child will grow up to be a killer
a messenger hawk arrives from the city
the messenger hawk delivers a message that the governor is willing to trade King Bumi for his son
they decide to accept the offer
the next day, Aang, Katara, and Sokka take Tom-Tom back to the city on Appa
Sokka believes they are walking into a trap
Aang says he has a good feeling about this
Azula enters Omashu on her throne with Ty Lee and approaches Mai
Mai jokingly asks if Azula has come to kill her
Azula and Mai embrace
Azula tells Mai about her mission
Mai accepts Azula’s mission immediately, thrilled to help and eager to leave Omashu
Azula confronts the governor, who pleads for forgiveness
Azula tells the governor he has mishandled the situation
Azula corrects the governor, declaring there is no more "Omashu"
Azula renames the city New Ozai in honor of her father
Azula places Mai in charge of handling the trade, excluding the governor completely
Aang, Katara, and Sokka approach Azula, Ty Lee, and Mai, who are ready to trade
Azula, Ty Lee, and Mai prepare for the trade
They lower Bumi, who is trapped in midair in a metal coffin on the end of a long chain
Azula comments that trading a powerful earthbending king for a two-year-old is not a fair exchange
Mai agrees and calls off the deal
They hoist Bumi upward
Aang, angered by the double-cross, jumps into the air after Bumi as Azula attacks him
Azula attacks Aang
Aang opens his glider, causing the turban covering his head to slip off
Azula sees Aang’s arrow and realizes he is the Avatar
Azula abandons the original plan and rushes for Aang
Aang jumps onto Bumi’s coffin and starts trying to freeze the chain as Azula closes in on him
Katara prepares to defend herself from Ty Lee and Mai
Sokka calls Appa
Ty Lee rushes for Sokka
Katara blocks an attack from Mai
Katara gives Sokka cover so he can move Tom-Tom to safety
Sokka moves Tom-Tom to safety
Aang nearly reaches the top of the statue and almost completely freezes the chain
Bumi tells Aang to stop
Azula emerges from the top and shoots a blast of fire at Aang and Bumi, breaking the chain
Aang and Bumi plummet toward the mail system
Azula jumps into a cart and rides after Aang and Bumi
Aang happily tells Bumi "It's just like old times"
Bumi, oddly serious, says he needs to talk to Aang
Azula fires at Aang and Bumi before Aang can reply
Katara and Mai exchange blows on the platform
Ty Lee strikes Katara in three different pressure points, rendering her unable to bend
Mai pulls out a three-pronged knife and taunts Katara's defenselessness
Sokka throws his boomerang from Appa's back and knocks Mai's weapon from her hand
Appa smacks his tail to the ground, knocking Ty Lee and Mai off the platform
Katara and Sokka find Aang being chased by Azula
Azula chases Aang
Aang shoots himself and Bumi off the track once close enough to Appa, but overshoots and lands back on the track with Azula
Azula continues blasting at Aang
Bumi, still trapped inside his coffin, earthbends a barrier in the track using his uncovered face
Azula barely avoids the obstacle and is forced to come to a stop
Aang expresses his surprise that Bumi is still able to earthbend
Bumi replies that they did not cover his face
Aang angrily asks why Bumi did not defend himself and why he surrendered Omashu
Bumi begins to explain the concept of jing to Aang
Aang states that he is already aware of positive and negative jing
Bumi notes a third form of jing: neutral jing, and explains it consists of doing neither and waiting for the opportune moment to strike
Bumi explains that this is why he surrendered the city and why he must go back to his captors
Bumi tells Aang he must learn from a master who has mastered neutral jing
Bumi tells Aang he will see him again when the time is right
Bumi topples himself over and begins pushing himself up the chute with earthbending, laughing as he goes
Azula rides in her palanquin with Mai and Ty Lee beside her
Ty Lee asks Mai if it will be interesting seeing Zuko again
Mai harbors a smile
Azula ignores Ty Lee's comment
Azula tells Mai and Ty Lee that they now have a third target to pursue: the Avatar
The governor and his wife stand on their porch
Aang, unseen by them, lands behind the governor and his wife with Tom-Tom
Aang sets Tom-Tom on the ground
Aang jumps to the roof
The governor and his wife turn around and joyously reunite with their son
Aang smiles
Aang turns away and disappears into the night

The_Swamp.action: Zuko and Iroh beg for money in the street of a village
Zuko refuses to grovel, trying to keep his dignity
Iroh accepts their new situation and does what he must to get by
A swordsman approaches Iroh and has him perform for a gold coin
Iroh begins to sing
The swordsman swings his blades at Iroh's feet to make him dance
The swordsman leaves
Zuko is angered and humiliated by his uncle's self-degradation
Aang, Katara, and Sokka search for an earthbending teacher for Aang
They fly over a swamp on Appa
Appa begins to inexplicably lose altitude
Aang tells his friends he feels as if the swamp is calling to him
Aang agrees to leave due to his friends' reluctance to land
A tornado suddenly appears
The tornado overwhelms Appa and hurls everyone into the swamp
Aang uses airbending to make a sphere of air as a shield around Appa
The tornado shears the air sphere apart with ease
The tornado separates humans and animals into two separate groups
The human group begins looking for Momo and Appa
Aang is wary of the environment, sensing something ominous
Katara is wary of the environment, sensing something ominous
Sokka uses his machete to haphazardly hack through the swamp vegetation, trying to clear a path
Appa, with Momo on the saddle, looks for the rest of Team Avatar
Appa trudges through the swamp
Appa's path is blocked by a large root
Appa drops to the ground to rest
Momo wants to keep going
Momo digs out Aang's bison whistle and blows on it as hard as he can
Appa roars in pain
Momo whistles a second time
Appa arches his tail and slams it down onto his back, knocking Momo out cold and stopping the noise
Swamp natives find Appa's tracks and begin hunting them for dinner
Sokka becomes annoyed at his sister and friend's warnings
Sokka states there is nothing out of the ordinary about the place
a scream echoes through the swamp, startling the trio
the trio realizes a small, white bird was responsible for the sounds
Sokka decides to build a fire
Momo hunts a fly for dinner
Momo ends up on the back of a catgator
Momo becomes alarmed and hurries back to Appa with the catgator in pursuit
Appa opens his mouth and catches the surprised catgator
Appa holds the struggling catgator momentarily
Appa spits out the catgator
The catgator and Momo hiss angrily at each other
The catgator leaves
Appa chastises Momo for his behavior
Aang, Katara, and Sokka make camp for the night
The group falls asleep
A swamp monster attacks the group
The group becomes separated further
Katara experiences a vision of her mother in the swamp
Katara runs joyfully to meet her mother
Katara’s vision is revealed to be an ordinary stump
Katara gasps in disbelief and falls to her knees in tears
Sokka envisions Yue in spirit form, who blames him for not protecting her
Sokka refuses to believe the vision and clings to a logical explanation
Aang spots a young laughing girl and a flying boar in the swamp
Aang calls out to the girl
The girl runs away
Aang chases after the girl
Tho, Due, and their brethren follow Appa and Momo in canoes
Tho attempts to coax Appa and Momo toward him
Tho mentions the animals will become food once captured, unaware they understand his speech
Appa and Momo flee
Due shouts angrily at Tho for his ignorance
Tho defends himself, asking how he was to know about the animals' intelligence
Tho begins bending the water around his canoe to speed forward rapidly
Aang chases the girl
Aang thinks he corners the girl, but the vision disappears and Katara is in her place
Sokka drops in and joins Aang and Katara
Aang, Katara, and Sokka confess their encounters
Aang, Katara, and Sokka confess their encounters
Aang, Katara, and Sokka confess their encounters
Aang and Katara wonder about the mysterious workings of the swamp
Aang and Katara wonder about the mysterious workings of the swamp
Sokka insists nothing is out of the ordinary
a giant swamp monster attacks the group
the swamp monster grabs Sokka and swings and slams him against the river
Aang frees Sokka by sending an air blade toward the swamp monster
the swamp monster attacks Aang
the swamp monster grabs Sokka again and tries to surf away
Katara damages the swamp monster’s arm with waterbending
the swamp monster holds onto Sokka even after Katara pushes it away with a large wave
Katara pushes the swamp monster away with a large wave
Katara is pushed back along with Aang, who had come back
Aang is pushed back along with Katara
Aang twists and tangles the swamp monster’s body with a tornado
Katara creates a big hole in the swamp monster with a water drill
Katara slings an array of water blades at the swamp monster
the swamp monster replaces its armor of vines after each attack
the group sees a man inside the swamp monster with each cut
the group sees a man inside the swamp monster with each cut
the group sees a man inside the swamp monster with each cut
Katara sends a water blade through the monster’s head, forcing the man to give up
They demand to know who the waterbender is
The mysterious figure asks why the three are in the swamp
Sokka reveals that Aang is the Avatar and that strange things happen to them often
The bender understands that the swamp chose them
The bender reveals his name is Huu
Huu explains he attacked them to protect the swamp from Sokka’s harm
Huu takes Aang, Katara, and Sokka to the roots of an enormous tree, the heart of the swamp
Huu reveals he achieved enlightenment under the tree and saw the swamp’s roots spread for miles
Huu states that everything is connected, and time and death are illusions
Huu explains the swamp manifests visions of deceased loved ones to show spiritual presence
Aang asks about the girl he saw, saying he didn’t know her
With Huu’s encouragement, Aang realizes the girl is someone he will meet in the future
Aang touches the tree, connecting to the swamp
Aang sees Appa and Momo being chased far away
Aang, Katara, and Sokka rush to help Appa and Momo
Aang and Katara use waterbending against Due and Tho
Katara uses waterbending against Due and Tho
The swamp-dwellers use waterbending themselves
The fighting stops upon the discovery that both groups can waterbend
Tho and Due unite with Huu, who had followed the group
It is shown that the waterbenders know each other
Aang, Katara, and Sokka sit around a fire in the swamp-dwellers' camp
Aang and Katara wonder about the swamp
Sokka insists there is nothing out of the ordinary
a white screeching dodo lands on a branch and lets out a screechy call
a sentient vine immediately bats the dodo away
The swordsman stands in a dark alleyway
The swordsman senses someone nearby and pulls out his blades
The swordsman demands that the person show himself
Two hands grab the swordsman's wrist, making him release the blades
The swordsman is thrown into a stack of crates, knocking him out
The attacker walks up and retrieves the dropped swords
The camera pans up to reveal the Blue Spirit

Avatar_Day.action: Momo spots a spider that has made a web in Sokka's mouth while he is sleeping with his mouth wide open
Momo attempts to grab the spider
Momo wakes Sokka
Sokka yells at Momo in response
Sokka wakes Katara and Aang
Sokka's outburst attracts the attention of the Rough Rhinos, a mounted Fire Nation military unit
Rough Rhinos surround them
The trio realizes they each left a precious possession behind at the camp: Katara's waterbending scroll, Aang's staff, and Sokka's boomerang
Katara uses her bending abilities to retrieve her waterbending scroll
Aang uses his bending abilities to retrieve his staff
Sokka is unable to retrieve his boomerang
Sokka sarcastically and upsetly asks Katara and Aang if they only had enough time to get their possessions and not his
Katara affirms that they only got their own possessions
Aang, Sokka, and Katara learn of a festival called "Avatar Day" while purchasing supplies
Aang, Sokka, and Katara become curious and excited about the festival
Aang, Sokka, and Katara go to the festival in the Earth Kingdom village
The trio find a parade with giant floats made to look like Avatars Kyoshi, Roku, and Aang
Aang, Sokka, and Katara initially believe the festival is honoring the Avatar
Aang is very satisfied with the apparent honor
A man appears and torches the three floats
Villagers loudly approve and chant "Down with the Avatar!"
Aang, Sokka, and Katara are shocked by the villagers' reaction
Katara promptly extinguishes the burning effigies
festival-goers protest
Aang reveals himself as the Avatar
villagers cower from Aang, believing he will destroy them
Mayor Tong tells Aang to leave the village as he is an unwanted visitor
Katara states the villagers have no right to question Aang's honor, since he helps people
Tong scoffs at Katara's suggestion and explains that Kyoshi, Aang's past life, is believed to have killed Chin the Great
Aang agrees to stand trial in an attempt to clear his name
Aang is imprisoned as the town does not accept Water Tribe currency for bail
Aang tasks Sokka and Katara with investigating the townspeople's accusations
Mayor Tong describes Chin's statue and recounts Kyoshi emerging from the temple to strike down the emperor
Katara realizes Kyoshi would never have set foot on the island if the temple and statue were carved from the same stone and the statue was built after Chin's death
They discover a footprint at the end of the island, which would prove to be evidence later
Sokka and Katara return to Kyoshi Island to find evidence to prove Aang's innocence
Sokka and Katara are welcomed with cheers from the islanders, including the foaming mouth guy
The villagers become disappointed when they realize Aang is not one of the visitors
Katara and Sokka state their business to Oyaji
Oyaji takes Sokka and Katara to the Kyoshi Shrine
Sokka and Katara examine relics and a painting, "The Birth of Kyoshi," inside the shrine
Katara discovers that Kyoshi's feet were very large by looking at Kyoshi's boots
Katara begins to voice her conclusion about the footprint
Sokka interrupts Katara and points to his detective getup
Katara sarcastically bows and allows Sokka to voice the conclusion
Sokka states there was nothing linking Kyoshi to Chin's murder, as the small footprint did not match Kyoshi's large one
Katara sarcastically congratulates Sokka on his "brilliant" discovery
They return to Chin Village and discover their gathered evidence will be rendered useless by the structuring of the trials
The opposing sides only tell what they believe happened during the trial
The jury decides who is innocent and who is guilty using only the two stories
The village mayor serves as both prosecution and jury for the case
Aang, still in his jail cell, becomes somewhat friendly with the other prisoners
One of the prisoners offers Aang advice about girls, specifically Katara
The entire population of the village gathers in the amphitheater near the statue of Chin
The mayor briefly tells those present about the murder
The mayor gives the floor to Aang
Aang listens to Sokka's tips during the trial after Tong's story
Aang mixes all clues by mentioning his big feet
Aang declares his innocence at the end
Aang is declared guilty
Katara and Sokka notice that they need to save Aang from the situation
Sokka notices that they need to save Aang from the situation
Aang dresses up in Kyoshi's old uniform as a witness invited by Katara
Katara invites Aang as a witness
Tong disagrees and says he would not allow the mockery of the court
Tong begins to read out the verdict
Kyoshi manifests through Aang's body
Kyoshi announces to the village that she killed Chin the Great
Kyoshi explains Chin was conquering neighboring lands and planning to conquer her village
Kyoshi confronts Chin's army alone
Kyoshi comes face to face with Chin
Kyoshi uses the Avatar State to separate the peninsula from the mainland, forming Kyoshi Island
Kyoshi's airbending scatters and decimates Chin's army
Chin stands on a weakening overhang at the end of the cliff
Chin cries out in frustration
Chin falls to his death when the ground breaks off from under his feet
Aang, groggy, asks Katara what happened after Kyoshi disappears
Katara tells Aang that he confessed to the murder
The village brings out a wheel for Aang to spin to determine his punishment
Each villager hopes for a painful punishment
Katara begs for community service as Aang's punishment
Aang spins the wheel and it lands on being boiled in oil
The Rough Rhinos arrive and attempt to take over the village before the sentence can be carried out
The mayor, fearing for his safety, begs Aang to stop the invasion
Aang reminds the mayor of his punishment
The mayor quickly changes the punishment to community service and pleads with Aang to defeat the warriors
Aang, Sokka, and Katara engage the Rough Rhinos in battle
Aang, Sokka, and Katara are successful in driving the Rough Rhinos away
Sokka regains his boomerang during the battle and exclaims, "Boomerang! You do always come back!"
The mayor decides that in the future, Avatar Day will honor the day Avatar Aang saved the town from the Rough Rhinos
The celebration involves eating effigies of the Avatar made from unfried dough, since Aang was not boiled in oil
Zuko disguises himself as the Blue Spirit to steal from the wealthy
Zuko runs on the roofs of houses
Zuko attacks a man and a woman to steal their baskets with provisions
Zuko hides the mask and swords
Zuko returns to the cave to his uncle Iroh, where they have temporarily settled
Iroh comments on how he does not like that Zuko has been hiding the source of their provisions
Iroh eats the food anyway
Zuko robs a tax collector in a coach, defeats the guards, and steals the money
Zuko uses the stolen money to decorate their cave
Iroh tells Zuko not to be ashamed of poverty
Zuko mentions the capture of the Avatar
Iroh tells Zuko that capturing the Avatar will not solve all their problems and warns him not to fall into despair
Iroh tells Zuko, "in the darkest times, hope is something you give yourself. That is the meaning of inner strength."
Zuko tells Iroh that he has to find his way in life
Iroh understands and decides not to stop Zuko when he goes out of the cave
Zuko, after thinking about what his uncle said, decides to travel alone

The_Blind_Bandit.action: Sokka contemplates purchasing an expensive bag in a shop in Gaoling
Sokka’s indecisiveness begins to bore Aang and Katara, despite their attempts to be supportive
Sokka’s indecisiveness begins to bore Aang and Katara, despite their attempts to be supportive
Aang and Katara leave the store
Aang and Katara leave the store
Sokka finally makes his purchase
A man approaches Aang and Katara, handing out flyers for a local earthbending academy
Katara convinces Aang to check out the earthbending academy since he needs to find a teacher
Aang uses the flyer's coupon to receive a free "lesson" at Master Yu's Earthbending Academy
Aang is clobbered by boulders during the lesson
Master Yu attempts to sell Aang a year's worth of lessons
Aang declines Master Yu's offer
Aang tells Katara and Sokka that Yu is "not the one."
The trio departs and overhears two young men discussing Earth Rumble VI, an underground earthbending tournament
Aang asks the young men where they might find the tournament
The young men sarcastically reply, "It's on the island of None'ya ... None'ya business!"
The two young men walk away, laughing at their own joke
Katara follows the young men around a corner
Katara forces the information out of the young men by freezing them to the wall with waterbending
Katara returns to Aang and Sokka
Aang and Sokka ask Katara how she got the information
Aang and Sokka ask Katara how she got the information
Katara replies casually, "Oh, a girl has her ways."
A hidden tunnel in a rock face leads down into the earth
The trio finds seats in the front row area, left vacant due to the threat of haphazardly launched boulders
The arena is illuminated by a ceiling of glowing crystals
Xin Fu, the host, explains the rules from the center of the ring: each contestant must knock the opponent out of the ring with earthbending to advance
The Boulder battles The Hippo and defeats him
Aang is unimpressed with The Boulder and comments that Bumi said he needs a teacher who listens to the earth, not just his muscles
Sokka becomes The Boulder’s newest fan
Fire Nation Man enters as the next challenger, plays the role of a heel, and sings the Fire Nation national anthem
Fire Nation Man is swiftly defeated
The Boulder defeats all challengers and earns a chance to take on the Earth Rumble champion, the Blind Bandit
The Blind Bandit is escorted to the ring
The crowd cheers
Aang and his friends realize the champion is a little blind girl
The Boulder states his reservations about fighting her
The Blind Bandit taunts The Boulder, saying it sounds as if he is scared
The Boulder's reservations vanish and he prepares to attack her
The Blind Bandit taunts The Boulder again, calling him "The Pebble" and laughs to herself
Aang realizes she is the laughing girl from his vision in the swamp
The Boulder is about to attack
The Blind Bandit senses the vibrations The Boulder’s steps create in the ground
The Blind Bandit makes her move
The Blind Bandit disrupts The Boulder’s first step, causing him to slip into a leg split
The Boulder screams in pain
The Blind Bandit quickly and easily knocks The Boulder out of the ring
Sokka is devastated
Katara is amazed and wonders how The Blind Bandit did it
Aang explains to Katara, "She waited and listened!"
Xin Fu offers a bag of gold pieces to any spectator who can defeat the Blind Bandit
Aang jumps into the ring
The Blind Bandit taunts Aang, calling him a little girl
Aang tries to explain that he does not want to fight and wants to talk
Sokka boos Aang
Katara hits and chastises Sokka
The Blind Bandit begins to fight anyway
Aang uses airbending to drift above the floor and evade the Blind Bandit
The Blind Bandit is unsettled by her inability to track Aang
Aang speaks to the Blind Bandit
The Blind Bandit finds Aang’s position and throws a large rock at him
Aang deflects the rock with a blast of airbending
Aang accidentally pushes the Blind Bandit off the ring, winning the match
Aang rushes to catch the Blind Bandit and tells her he thinks she is supposed to be his earthbending teacher
The Blind Bandit, upset by her loss, departs from the stadium and tells Aang to leave her alone
Aang collects the gold pieces and belt, disappointed
The trio heads to Master Yu's Earthbending Academy in search of the Blind Bandit
Katara tries her intimidation tactics again on the young men she froze the day before
The young men fail to provide the information Katara wants
Aang realizes they are asking about the wrong person
Aang describes the girl from his vision as wearing a white dress and having a pet flying boar
The young men do not recognize the description
The young men mention a local family whose symbol is a flying boar – the Beifong family
The trio leaves to investigate
The Boulder tells Xin Fu that there was no earthbending involved in the defeat of the Blind Bandit
Xin Fu is led to believe the Blind Bandit intentionally lost to split the money with the challenger
Xin Fu becomes enraged, believing he has been swindled
Xin Fu decides to hunt the Blind Bandit down
The trio arrives at the Beifong estate, but it is heavily guarded
The trio heads around back and climbs over the outer wall into a gardened area
The earth is pushed up from underneath, thrusting the trio into the air
The Blind Bandit, dressed in an elegant dress, confronts Aang and asks why he has come
Aang and his friends explain that he is the Avatar and needs an earthbending teacher
The Blind Bandit calls the guards after they refuse to leave her alone
The guards arrive and escort the Blind Bandit inside, revealing her name to be Toph
Aang sneakily smiles to himself as he forms an idea of what to do next
Toph's parents meet with Master Yu to confirm that Toph is not trying anything dangerous in her earthbending lessons
Master Yu assures Toph's parents that he has kept Toph at the basic level, avoiding anything too dangerous
A servant comes in to tell them a visitor has arrived
The servant announces that the visitor is the Avatar
Toph becomes agitated upon hearing the Avatar has arrived
Aang, Katara, Sokka, and Master Yu join Toph and her family for dinner
Toph is served hot soup
Toph's father tells her to blow on her soup
Aang volunteers to use his powers to cool Toph's soup
Those in attendance clap for Aang
Toph's father asks Aang how long he thinks the Hundred Year War will continue
Aang explains he hopes to defeat the Fire Lord by summer and says he still needs an earthbending teacher
Toph's father suggests Master Yu as a candidate for Aang's teacher
Aang tries to bring up Toph as his teacher
Toph hits Aang's foot with earthbending under the table to keep him quiet
Aang tries to reveal Toph's secret identity
Toph earthbends Aang's chair forward, causing his face to smash into his soup
Aang airbends a sneeze, throwing Toph's soup onto her, Master Yu, and Toph's mother
Toph and Aang start to fight
Toph's mother asks if they should move to the living room for dessert
Sokka, Katara, and Aang get ready for bed as guests of Toph's family
Aang is surprised by the sudden appearance of Toph in his room
Aang jumps into a defensive stance
Toph calls a truce
Toph and Aang walk the grounds of her family's estate
Toph explains to Aang that she "sees" with her feet using earthbending, despite being born blind
Aang is amazed by Toph's explanation
Toph claims her parents do not understand her and treat her as hopeless
Toph expresses envy of Aang's carefree life but says she has commitments to her family
Toph suddenly warns that they are being ambushed by Xin Fu and his wrestlers
Xin Fu and his wrestlers ambush Aang and Toph
Cages are dropped on Aang and Toph, capturing them
A ransom note is left demanding five hundred gold pieces for Toph's safe return
Toph's father enlists Master Yu's help
Sokka and Katara come along to help
They arrive at the arena with the ransom, and Toph is released
Katara asks about Aang
Xin Fu states he plans to turn the Avatar over to the Fire Nation for a reward
Xin Fu is flanked by all the wrestlers, who threaten them as they leave
Katara runs after Toph, asking for her assistance
Toph's father protests, saying Toph is blind, tiny, helpless, and fragile
Toph rejects her father and decides to assist them
Toph heads back into the arena and creates a spike in front of the wrestlers who are trying to leave, demanding Aang's release
The earthbenders begin to attack
Toph tells Sokka and Katara to leave the earthbenders to her
Toph defeats each opponent almost effortlessly
Lao and Master Yu are amazed and shocked by Toph's abilities
Toph faces off against Xin Fu and sends him flying into the wall
Toph tries to explain her secret identity to her parents, hoping it will not upset them
Toph's parents jump to the conclusion that the freedom they gave her allowed her to put herself into danger
Toph's parents decide Toph has had too much freedom and must be confined and guarded from now on
Aang, Katara, and Sokka are forced out of the house
Aang, Katara, and Sokka are forced out of the house
Aang, Katara, and Sokka are forced out of the house
Toph sheds tears for losing the possibility of gaining friends and what little freedom she had left
Toph comes running up, telling them her father has changed his mind
Toph knocks Aang into the air as payback for knocking her off the stage after the championship battle
Aang lands in a tree
Toph requests the championship belt back
Sokka tosses the belt down to Toph, forgetting that she is blind
The belt hits Toph on the head, and she falls to the ground
Sokka sheepishly apologizes
Aang falls out of the tree
Toph's father brings a small chest to a table in front of Master Yu and Xin Fu
Toph's father says he knows Master Yu and Xin Fu are different but have a similar interest
Toph's father opens the chest to reveal a large amount of gold
Toph's father offers the gold to Master Yu and Xin Fu for the safe return of his daughter
Toph's father states that his daughter was kidnapped by the Avatar, proving Toph's story to be fabricated
Team Avatar, now a quartet, flies away on Appa
A contented smile breaks across Toph's face

Zuko_Alone.action: Zuko travels alone through semi-arid terrain on his ostrich horse
Zuko nearly falls through a bridge, but maneuvers his mount back into place
Zuko, appearing gaunt and hungry, stops at the smell of food
Zuko sees a man cooking a large piece of meat over an open flame
Zuko puts a hand over his gurgling stomach and moves his hand to his sword hilt
Zuko sees the man is traveling with a pregnant woman and decides to continue on
Zuko becomes more tired and dehydrated, struggling to maintain consciousness
Zuko’s vision becomes blurred as he presses on and he closes his eyes
A woman with a hood appears, walking down a dark hallway
Zuko opens his eyes again but keeps his head down
Zuko arrives in a village
Zuko passes some Earth Kingdom soldiers and locks eyes with them
Zuko dismounts his ostrich horse
Zuko walks over to a merchant and asks to purchase water, a hot meal, and a bag of feed
Zuko reveals a small number of coins to the merchant
The merchant tells Zuko he does not have enough for a hot meal, but can give him two bags of feed
The merchant retrieves Zuko's feed
Two boys hiding beside the counter throw an egg at the soldiers behind Zuko and run for cover
The soldiers angrily confront and interrogate Zuko, thinking he threw the egg
Zuko covers for the boys, saying he saw nothing and suggests a chicken may have flown over their heads
The vendor brings out Zuko's feed
Gow, the leader of the soldiers, takes Zuko's feed and thanks him for his "contribution"
The vendor tells Zuko that Gow and his men are supposed to protect them from the Fire Nation but are just a group of criminals
Zuko remounts his ostrich horse
The boy who threw the egg pops up from behind the ostrich horse and thanks Zuko for covering for him
Zuko does not reply
Zuko begins to leave on his mount
The boy follows Zuko, offering to take him to his farm and feed the ostrich horse in return for not ratting him out
Zuko accepts the offer
Zuko allows the boy, Lee, to lead him
Zuko and Lee approach a large farm full of several kinds of hybrid pigs that oink loudly
The hybrid pigs oink loudly as Zuko and Lee approach the house
Lee remarks that the noise is good because no one can ever sneak up on them
Lee goes to put Zuko's ostrich horse in the barn
Gansu approaches Zuko
Gansu inquires if Zuko is a friend of Lee
Lee recounts Zuko's small confrontation with the soldiers
Sela comes and asks for the newcomer's name
Zuko stutters, not wanting to use his typical alias of "Lee"
Gansu intervenes, saying Zuko has no obligation to say who he is
Gansu welcomes Zuko to his home because of Zuko's opposition to the Earth Kingdom soldiers
The family explains that real soldiers like Lee's older brother, Sensu, are fighting in the War while thugs stay to "protect" the village from the Fire Nation
Sela asks Zuko if he wishes to stay for dinner
Zuko declines Sela's offer to stay for dinner
Sela, understanding Zuko is too proud to accept help, says he can help Lee's father fix the roof of the barn and that later they will eat
Zuko accepts Sela's offer to help fix the roof and eat later
Zuko sets to work, producing a plethora of bent nails due to his inexperience
Lee asks Zuko random personal questions
Lee's father silences Lee
Lee persists and asks Zuko how he got his scar
Zuko accidentally hammers his own thumb, momentarily distracted
Lee's father tells Lee it is not polite to ask so many personal questions and that Zuko's past is not his business
Zuko continues hammering and recalls a distant memory
Zuko and his mother sit by a small pond in a garden on the palace grounds, feeding turtle ducks
Zuko offers to show his mother how Azula feeds the turtle ducks
Zuko throws a large piece of bread at a turtle duck
Zuko's mother berates him for throwing bread at the turtle duck
The mother turtle duck comes forward and bites down hard on Zuko's ankle
Zuko yells in pain
Zuko's mother removes the small creature from Zuko's ankle
Zuko angrily asks why the duck bit him
Ursa replies that mothers are protective and will "bite back" when their young are in danger, and pretends to bite Zuko
Zuko and his mother laugh briefly
Zuko and his mother return their gaze back to the pond
Zuko and his mother walk back through a courtyard
Princess Azula attempts a cartwheel but falls to the ground
Ty Lee runs forward and performs the cartwheel with ease, topping it off with several somersaults
Azula, jealous, pushes Ty Lee over and laughs
Mai sits uninterested against a tree, watching them
Mai turns, sees Zuko walking through the garden, and diverts her gaze, blushing and smiling to herself
Azula notices Mai’s reaction and whispers to Ty Lee, "Watch this."
Ty Lee giggles
Azula runs up to Zuko and his mother, asking if Zuko would come play with them, stating it is important for siblings to spend time together
Zuko and Azula have a small argument
Their mother makes Zuko go and play with the girls
Azula explains the tactic of the game is to knock an apple off the other person's head
Azula sets an apple on the head of a bored-looking Mai and sets it ablaze
Zuko rushes forward trying to knock the burning apple off Mai's head
Zuko trips, crashing into Mai and causing them to fall into the fountain
Zuko and Mai land on top of each other in the fountain
Ty Lee comments on how cute Zuko and Mai are together
Mai is angered
Zuko, embarrassed, storms inside to dry off, passing his mother and shouting, "Girls are crazy!"
Zuko's mother informs Zuko that she has received a letter from his uncle in Ba Sing Se
Iroh writes in the letter about the magnificence of Ba Sing Se and its wall
Iroh expresses hope that Zuko and his family can visit Ba Sing Se one day, unless he burns it down first
Zuko and Azula burst out in laughter at Iroh's joke
Iroh sends gifts for his niece and nephew
Zuko receives a pearl dagger that belonged to the general who surrendered when Iroh broke through the Outer Wall
The inscription on the dagger reads "Never give up without a fight"
Azula receives an Earth Kingdom doll, much to her displeasure
Azula comments that if Iroh did not make it back from the siege, her father would be next in line to be Fire Lord
Azula's mother scolds her for saying such a thing
Zuko asks Azula how she would feel if Iroh's son, Lu Ten, wanted their dad to die
Azula retorts that she thinks their dad would make a much better Fire Lord than Iroh
Azula sets her new doll ablaze
Zuko sleeps soundly on a pile of hay in the family's barn
Lee sneaks in and takes Zuko's swords
Lee silently slips out of the barn
Zuko's eyes open as Lee leaves
Lee takes the swords outside and hacks wildly at a field of sunflowers, pretending to fight an enemy
Zuko appears behind Lee and tells him he is holding the swords the wrong way
Zuko explains that Lee has to think of the swords as one whole rather than two separate blades
Lee heeds Zuko's comments and his motions become more fluid
Lee and Zuko walk back to the farm in the moonlight
Lee states that Zuko would have liked Sensu, as Sensu used to share things with him just as Zuko was doing
Zuko mounts his ostrich horse and prepares to set off
Lee's mother gives Zuko some food for his journey
dust kicks up in the distance
soldiers from the village arrive with grave news
soldiers inform that Sensu's battalion has been captured
Gow jokes with his men about the Fire Nation army dressing up Earth Kingdom prisoners as Fire Nation soldiers and deploying them unarmed
Gansu tells Gow to watch his mouth, infuriated by the remark
Gow prepares to strike Gansu for his outburst
Zuko blocks Gow's advance by positioning his mount between the two parties
Gow insults the family as unworthy and leaves with his company
Zuko is brought back to a memory of his cousin's death
Zuko and Azula play together in the garden
Zuko and Azula play together in the garden
Their mother sits near the pond
a servant delivers a message to their mother
Their mother reads the message, looks up, and a tear drips down her face
Their mother informs her children that their cousin did not survive the battle
Lee's father declares he is going to the front to look for Sensu
Lee asks if Zuko will stay in his father's absence
Zuko declines to stay
Zuko gives Lee the pearl dagger his uncle had given him
Zuko rides off
Zuko is thrown into another memory
Zuko practices with his knife while Azula watches from a chair
Azula comments that Zuko is not particularly skilled and wastes his time playing with knives
Zuko replies with anger
Azula ignores Zuko's outburst and informs him that Iroh is coming home, calling him "a quitter and a loser"
Ursa enters the room and tells Zuko and Azula their father has requested an audience with their grandfather, Fire Lord Azulon
Azula disrespectfully inquires why Ursa does not just refer to Fire Lord Azulon as "grandfather" and comments that he isn't exactly the powerful Fire Lord he used to be
Azula continues, saying someone will probably take Azulon's place soon
Ursa scolds Azula for her brazen and callous speech
Ursa wonders to herself what is wrong with her daughter
Ozai asks Zuko and Azula how their great-grandfather Sozin won the Battle of Han Tui in the Fire Lord's throne room
Zuko stammers for an answer
Azula decisively answers correctly
Ozai, pleased with Azula, asks her to show her grandfather the new moves she demonstrated for him
Azula gracefully performs her firebending forms
Ozai smiles as he watches Azula
Ozai states that Azula is a true prodigy, just like her grandfather
Azula resumes her seat beside Zuko
Azula whispers to Zuko that he will never catch up
Zuko rises to demonstrate what he has been learning
Ozai’s smile turns into a frown as he watches Zuko
Zuko stumbles with the same form
Azula smirks viciously
Zuko falls to the ground after trying again
Zuko sadly says that he failed
Ursa comforts Zuko, saying she loved watching him perform and that he always keeps fighting
Azulon dismisses Ursa, Zuko, and Azula, and asks Ozai to tell him what he wants
Ursa, Zuko, and Azula make for the door
Azula grabs Zuko's hand and leads him behind a curtain to eavesdrop
Zuko follows Azula behind the curtain
Azula and Zuko eavesdrop on Ozai and Azulon's meeting
Ozai comments that with Lu Ten gone, Iroh's bloodline has ended and no one knows when he will return
Ozai points out that he is present and his children are alive, leading up to his request to cheat on Iroh's birthright
Azulon tells Ozai that Iroh has suffered enough, but Ozai's punishment has only just begun
The Fire Lord's flames rise
Zuko runs away, frightened
Zuko hears the punishment Azulon would give Ozai
Azula remains behind with a sadistic smile
Zuko lies on his bed, frightened by what he has seen
Azula enters Zuko's room with an evil smirk, singing that their father is going to kill Zuko
Azula reveals that Ozai is to sacrifice Zuko so he may know the pain of losing a firstborn son
Zuko refuses to believe Azula
Zuko remarks that their dad would never do that to him
Ursa enters the room demanding to know what is going on
Ursa sees Azula's smug face and pulls her away angrily, saying it is time for a talk
Zuko remains in bed chanting "Azula always lies" to himself
Zuko lies on the grass, eyes closed, repeating words
Lee's mother pulls up in a cart and informs Zuko that Lee pulled a knife on the soldiers when they demanded food
The soldiers take Lee away, deciding he is old enough to join the army
Lee's mother, in tears, begs Zuko for help
Zuko agrees to help
Zuko rides up to the soldiers
Lee, tied to a pole in the town square, is extremely happy to see Zuko
At Zuko's arrival, the soldiers all stand up, ready to face him
Zuko dismounts and removes his straw hat
Zuko orders the soldiers to release Lee
The leader bursts out laughing
The leader harshly inquires who Zuko is
Zuko calmly replies that his identity does not matter
Zuko denounces the soldiers as bullies abusing their power and cowards for messing with a family who already lost a child to the war
The leader asks the other soldiers if they are going to let Zuko insult them without responding, implying they should attack him
One soldier rushes forward
Zuko knocks the soldier off his feet with a quick hit from his sword hilt, without removing the sword from its sheath
The next soldier runs at Zuko
Zuko ducks under the spear and pushes the soldier backward onto the ground with his hand on the man's face
The third soldier runs at Zuko
Zuko performs a front kick, breaking the man's spear and scaring him into running away
The leader pulls out two large hammers
Zuko pulls out his swords, mirroring the leader's motion
The leader smacks the ground with his hammers and sends a rock pelting toward Zuko
Zuko breaks the rock easily with his swords
Zuko is hit in the stomach by a rock but does not give up
Zuko charges the leader
an elderly spectator cheers for Zuko
Zuko’s advance is halted by three quick boulders coming his way
Zuko barely blocks the rocks flying toward him
Zuko is hit with an earth pillar that knocks him airborne and back about ten feet
Zuko hits the ground hard and is thrown into another memory
Zuko is sound asleep in his room
Zuko’s mother gently wakes him
Zuko’s mother tells him that everything she has done has been to protect him, and that he must never forget who he is
Zuko’s mother hugs him a last time and disappears down a dark corridor
Zuko lies on the ground, seemingly passed out
The soldier moves forward for the final blow
Zuko opens his eyes and unleashes a blast of firebending upon the leader
Zuko uses his swords to guide his flames and creates a furious ring of fire around him as he jumps up
Lee and the villagers look at Zuko, terrified
Zuko charges the soldier, sending blasts of fire toward him as he swings the blades
The soldier makes a feeble attempt at earthbending a wall to block the attack
The soldier is knocked back against a house
The defeated soldier asks Zuko who he is
Zuko tells everyone his full title and royal heritage
The crowd erupts in nervous whispers, horrified by Zuko's revelation
The man who had been cheering Zuko accuses him of being a liar, claiming Zuko was an outcast burned and disowned by his father
Zuko ignores the man
Zuko retrieves his dagger from the pocket of the defeated soldier
Sela unties Lee
Zuko walks toward Lee
Lee's mother steps between Zuko and Lee, telling Zuko not to come closer
Zuko obeys and kneels, offering the dagger
Lee rejects the offer and tells Zuko he hates him
Zuko wakes suddenly from his bed yelling for his mother
Zuko runs down the hall
Azula plays with Zuko's knife
Zuko asks Azula about their mother
Azula replies that nobody knows and adds that their grandfather passed away in the night
Zuko tells Azula that she is sick
Zuko takes back his knife
Zuko runs to find his mother
Zuko finds his dad at the pond, standing silently and staring into the distance
Zuko demands to know where his mother is
Ozai does not reply
Zuko bows his head
The Fire Sages hold a special funeral ceremony for the late Fire Lord
The Fire Sages retell the late Fire Lord's past accomplishments and family ties as a eulogy
Ozai, Zuko, and Azula are present at the ceremony, all donned in white robes
The sages cremate Azulon's body
The Head Sage coronates Ozai as the new Fire Lord, claiming it was Azulon's dying wish
Zuko rides away from the village on his ostrich horse, wearing his round hat
villagers line either side of the street as Zuko rides away
An Earth Kingdom flag flies in the breeze on the left of the frame
Some villagers, including Lee, glare at Zuko with bitter hatred
Zuko rides away into a burning sunset

The_Chase.action: Aang, Katara, Sokka, and Toph land in a clearing for the night and begin to unpack
Toph comments on their superb campsite finding abilities, based on the feeling of "soft grass"
Sokka tells Toph that what she is feeling is actually Appa's shedded fur
Katara reacts negatively, believing the fur is gross
Aang and Sokka play with the fur, making hats and mustaches out of it
Aang and Sokka play with the fur, making hats and mustaches out of it
Katara expresses happiness at having another girl in the group to counter the boys' immaturity
Toph joins in by stuffing hair into her armpits
Aang and Sokka laugh at Toph's antics
Aang and Sokka laugh at Toph's antics
Aang sneezes because of the hair
Katara giggles at Aang's sneeze
Night falls as the group sets up camp
Aang pitches a tent
Sokka unravels sleeping bags
Katara fills a pot with water
Toph lounges lazily against a rock
Katara comes over and explains the group's normal system of setting up camp
Toph tells Katara she has already collected her food and water and claims she can carry her own weight
Katara gets slightly frustrated and tries re-explaining the system
Toph becomes aggravated and snaps at Katara
Katara angrily storms off
Aang and Sokka set up the last of camp as they unsaddle Appa
Aang and Sokka set up the last of camp as they unsaddle Appa
Katara comes over to apologize to Toph
Toph agitates Katara by turning the apology around on her
Toph sleeps silently in her earthen tent
Toph is awakened by a vibration in the earth
Toph wakes the others
Toph tries to identify the source of the vibration, saying "it feels like an avalanche, but also not an avalanche"
Sokka responds sarcastically, "Your powers of perception are frightening."
They decide leaving the campsite is the safest choice
They get a slight glimpse of a large, tank-like war machine following them across the desert terrain
The group's exhaustion is discernible on their faces after hours of flying
They each have dark circles under their eyes from lack of sleep
They settle on a spot and land again
Toph makes her way to where she plans to sleep
Katara asks Toph to help
Toph declines to help
Katara is displeased by Toph's refusal
Katara decides enough is enough and confronts Toph about her lack of assistance
Toph argues back before "slamming the door" in Katara's face by earthbending a tent; the door rises right in front of Katara
Aang decides to play peacemaker and asks Toph and Katara to calm down
Katara snaps wildly at Aang due to lack of sleep, screaming "I'm completely calm!"
Aang backs off awkwardly
Aang and Sokka are seemingly fast asleep as Katara stares angrily at the night sky
Sokka is fast asleep
Katara stares angrily at the night sky
Katara loudly comments on how beautiful the stars are and how Toph will never be able to see them
Toph earthbends Katara a few feet into the air
Katara lands on Sokka
Sokka snaps due to emotions and sleep deprivation as he wakes up
Toph informs the group that the machine is back
The group flies away on Appa
While in the air, the group debates about the machine and how it might be tracking them
The group finds a spot that seems out of the machine's range
The group decides not to set up camp and to sleep instead
The group debates for a minute about what the machine is or who is chasing them
Katara brings up the possibility that it is Zuko, since they have not seen him since they were up north
Toph, unaware of who Zuko is, inquires about him
Katara mocks Sokka, asking "What's wrong with ponytails, ponytail?"
Sokka sternly responds that his is a warrior's wolf tail
Momo senses the machine's approach
Toph senses the machine's approach
They see the machine moving forward in the distance, kicking up dirt behind it
Aang believes they should face their pursuers and find out who they are
The tank comes to a halt in front of them, and doors open on the sides
Three mongoose lizards emerge from the tank
Ty Lee, Mai, and Azula are revealed as the riders and cause of the tension
Toph believes they can take them and calculates the odds: three on three
Sokka corrects Toph, reminding her there are actually four of them
Toph recalculates: three on three plus Sokka, as he has no bending powers
Sokka is infuriated
Azula's group draws closer
Toph sends the first strike by earthbending boulders toward them
The lizards easily climb over the boulders
The group decides it is safer if they leave
Aang, Katara, and Sokka head back for Appa
Toph tries to buy time by creating a large rock wall separating Azula's team from them
Azula sends a bolt of lightning at the rock wall, blasting a gaping hole in it
The team barely escapes as Azula sends a jet of fire at them
Appa rapidly closes and opens his eyes, trying desperately to stay awake
The group debates on what to do
Katara reveals her fear of Ty Lee taking her bending away again
The sun rises over a mountain in the distance, signaling they have been up all night
Sokka is horrified by the sunrise
The group decides to keep flying for as long as possible
Aang warns them that they cannot keep flying forever
Azula's machine races to catch up with Team Avatar as the sun rises
Zuko follows the machine's tracks not far behind
Appa has difficulty staying awake and finally falls asleep
Appa and Team Avatar plummet toward the ground
Aang tries desperately to wake Appa as they race toward the ground
Appa awakens and stops just above the trees
Appa, due to exhaustion, is unable to keep airborne and crash-lands in the woods
Another fight breaks out between Katara and Toph
Toph blames Appa's shedding for their predicament
Aang joins the fight and berates Toph for blaming Appa
Toph, aggravated by Aang's objections, gives up and decides to leave
Azula's machine picks up speed after seeing Appa's fur floating through the air
Aang laments that he cannot believe he yelled at his earthbending teacher
Katara sympathizes with Aang and notes that she had been mean to Toph
Sokka affirms that they were jerks, offering little comfort
They decide to find Toph and apologize
They wash Appa
Appa is now clean and shed fur-less
Aang deems Appa ready to fly as long as his saddle and everything else is left off
Aang decides to try giving Azula the slip and take what is left of the shed fur to lead the tank off course
Appa flies off in one direction, hitting trees on take-off
Aang goes in another direction
Toph walks for some time and gains much distance
Toph stops suddenly upon feeling a vibration
Toph hits the ground, knocking the unknown person off their feet
Iroh is revealed as the unknown person
Azula, Mai, and Ty Lee reach the spot of Appa's bath and find wet fur in the stream
Ty Lee debates with herself about whether the "wads" are "bundles" or "bunches" of fur, noting the "uh" sound
Mai helps Ty Lee by deeming them "clumps"
Ty Lee excitedly hugs Mai before Mai can block the action
Mai pushes Ty Lee off
Mai tells Azula the trail heads off to the right
Azula notices the broken trees from Appa's take-off and becomes suspicious of Aang's plan
Azula tells Mai and Ty Lee to head in the direction of the broken trees
Azula decides to follow the fur trail herself
Aang lands in a small abandoned Earth Kingdom town, exhausted
Aang slumps to the end of the town's main street
Aang dumps the remaining fur
Aang sits and waits for Azula
Momo lets out a screech signaling the return of Mai and Ty Lee
Mai attacks with darts
Appa's passengers duck out of the way of Mai's darts in time
Katara desperately tries to get Appa to move faster
Appa crashes on the other side of the river
Sokka and Katara believe they are safe
Sokka and Katara's hopes are shattered when the mongoose lizards reveal their ability to run on water
Ty Lee heads for Katara
Ty Lee darts her hands to pressure points
Katara dodges Ty Lee's attacks due to past experience with chi-blocking
Katara and Ty Lee have a small battle
Mai pins Katara to a tree by the hem of her wristbands with expert knife-throwing
Ty Lee strikes Sokka, almost completely paralyzing him
Appa smacks his tail to the ground, blasting a gust of wind at Mai and Ty Lee
Mai and Ty Lee are thrown into the river by the gust of wind
Sokka and Katara leave
Ty Lee comments that she thought Sokka was "cute"
Mai gives Ty Lee a look of disgust
Azula reaches the end of the trail and finds Aang waiting for her
Azula dismounts her lizard
Aang, exhausted, tries to find a diplomatic resolution and asks who Azula is and what she wants
Azula expresses surprise that Aang has not guessed her identity
Azula gives Aang a hint by covering the left side of her face, deepening her voice to mimic Zuko, and says, "I must find the Avatar to restore my honor!"
Aang does not respond to Azula's attempt at humor
Azula softly berates Aang
Azula tells Aang he cannot elude her any longer, no matter how much he tries
Aang rises to his feet and replies, "I'm not running."
Toph and Iroh share a cup of tea on a cliff overlooking a farming village
Iroh finds similarities between Toph and his nephew
Toph asks Iroh where his nephew is and if he is lost
Iroh responds that his nephew is "a little lost" and explains his nephew's life has recently changed and he is trying to discover who he is
Iroh advises Toph not to resist help from those who care about her
Toph takes Iroh's words into consideration
Toph bids Iroh farewell and goes to search for her friends
Azula and Aang stand facing each other, neither moving
Azula asks Aang if he really wants to fight her
Zuko bursts through an old building and lands between Aang and Azula
Zuko says, "Yes, I really do."
Zuko holds his position between Aang and Azula, keeping his battle stance poised at both of them
Azula fires the first shot, hitting Zuko hard and knocking him back
Azula and Zuko engage in an intense battle
Azula corners Aang in a collapsed and burning building
Katara interrupts Azula before she can deliver a deadly injury to Aang
Sokka joins Katara
Iroh joins Katara and Sokka
Toph joins Katara, Sokka, and Iroh
The group (Katara, Aang, Sokka, Toph, Zuko, and Iroh) creates a perfect perimeter and corners Azula
Azula surrenders
Iroh relaxes his guard and turns to see Toph standing with Aang
Azula fires at Iroh, taking advantage of his distraction
Iroh screams in pain and crumples to the ground
Zuko lets out a horrified cry
Aang, Katara, Zuko, and Toph unleash their power on Azula
Sokka throws his boomerang at Azula
The combined attack causes a large explosion
Azula disappears by the time the smoke clears
Zuko rushes to Iroh's side in anguish
Zuko senses the others behind him
Zuko orders the others to leave
Katara comes forward and offers to help
Zuko lashes out at the team with a burst of fire, angrily demanding they leave
The team finally obeys and leaves
Appa lands in the mountains later that night
The team falls asleep

Bitter_Work.action: Aang awakes from his sleep, excited to begin earthbending training with Toph
Aang wakes up a weary Katara and a cranky Sokka
Toph awakens, bursting from her stone tent
Aang greets Toph as his sifu
Katara feels jealous because Aang has never called her sifu
Aang enthusiastically asks what high-level techniques he will learn first
Toph replies that they will start by learning how to move a rock
Toph imparts to Aang that the key to earthbending is a firm, steady stance
Toph explains that one must be "like a rock" to move rock
Toph demonstrates the motion by sending a rock into a wall
Aang attempts to emulate Toph
Aang blasts the rock with air and propels himself backward into Appa
Sokka remarks that "rock beats airbender"
Iroh runs past a tree on a grassy hill, being chased by his son Lu Ten
Lu Ten imitates a firebending motion with his hands
Iroh playfully pretends to get hit by Lu Ten's firebending and falls to the ground
Lu Ten falls on top of Iroh and they share a warm laugh
Years later, Iroh kneels in the rain before Lu Ten's grave under the same tree
Iroh tells his son that he will see him again
Iroh awakens from his dream
Zuko watches over Iroh within a partially destroyed home near a deserted town
Zuko explains to Iroh that he was knocked unconscious after a surprise attack by Azula
Iroh serves Zuko some tea
Zuko states he has contemplated the situation and realizes it is only a matter of time before they encounter Azula again
Zuko expresses the need to learn more advanced firebending techniques to stand a chance against his sister
Iroh agrees with Zuko and decides it is time to resume their training
Zuko is surprised by his uncle's agreement
Aang and Katara are perplexed as to why Aang was not able to move the boulder, despite copying Toph’s motion
Aang suggests trying to find an alternate solution, such as coming at the rock from a different angle
Toph explains that Aang’s airbender thinking is the problem, since earthbending requires a head-on approach
Aang shows signs of being unsure of himself
Katara takes Toph aside to explain that Aang responds well to positive teaching methods
Katara explains that encouragement, praise, kind words, and gentle nudges help Aang learn
Toph thanks Katara for her advice
Toph engages Aang in rigorous earthbending activities, including carrying a heavy boulder, punching solid rock, and balancing on stone columns, while yelling at him to be more "rock-like"
Aang initially fails at the training activities
Aang gradually becomes successful with the different training activities
The others oversee Aang's training
Sokka goes hunting alone in a small forest near the quarry
Sokka hides in a tree
Sokka spots a small, cute, unidentifiable creature
Sokka states that the creature is cute but made of meat
Sokka jumps down from the tree to deliver a killing blow
Sokka falls through a crevice straight into the ground, becoming buried up to his waist
Sokka tries attacking the creature again
Sokka sinks further into the ground
Sokka becomes buried up to his neck and deprived of the use of his arms
Sokka is unable to do anything but wait
Iroh explains to his nephew the essence of lightning and how a firebender can engender it
Iroh describes lightning as "cold-blooded fire," a pure expression of the elemental art, without aggression
Iroh contrasts lightning with other forms of firebending
Iroh notes that, like Azula, lightning is precise and deadly
Iroh explains that performing lightning generation requires peace of mind
Iroh continues his lecture outside the decrepit building, explaining the separation of positive (yang) and negative (yin) energy creates imbalance
Iroh explains only a select few firebenders can separate the energies
Iroh explains that when the energies recombine, they create lightning
Iroh explains the firebender’s role is to provide release and guidance, not command
Iroh demonstrates by releasing a massive stream of lightning into the distance
Zuko, taken aback, states he is ready to try it
Zuko mimics Iroh’s motions
Zuko creates a fiery explosion instead of lightning, which throws him backward
Toph decides to take a change of pace in Aang's training
Toph informs Aang that he will stop a moving rock instead of moving a stationary one
Toph blindfolds Aang
Toph rolls a huge boulder down a steep slope toward Aang
The boulder rolls closer and closer to Aang
Aang panics at the last second and jumps over the rock
Toph berates Aang for his passiveness and calls him a jelly-boned wimp
Katara tries to console a disappointed Aang by offering to help him with waterbending
Zuko becomes enraged and frustrated over being unsuccessful with lightning
Zuko comments that lightning keeps blowing up in his face, like everything always does
Iroh concludes that Zuko's inner turmoil is hampering him from creating lightning
Iroh says Zuko will never master lightning until he lets go of his feelings of shame
Zuko denounces the notion and tells Iroh he is as proud as ever
Iroh corrects Zuko, noting that pride is not the opposite of shame, but rather its source
Iroh offers to teach Zuko a firebending move of his own invention instead of continuing lightning lessons
Sokka scornfully converses with the moose lion while stuck in the crack
Sokka explains he has nothing against the moose lion; hunting and eating meat is part of his nature
Sokka states he will lead a "karmically correct" vegetarian existence if he survives
Sokka remains trapped inside the crevice
Aang and Katara practice moving a small mass of water around and between each other
Katara reminds Aang that the block can only be temporary and tries to persuade him to face the issue instead of avoiding it
Aang feels too dejected to talk about his problem or face it
Aang expresses frustration and confusion about why he cannot seem to do it
Katara explains that working with his opposite element, earth, is especially hard for Aang
Katara expresses her belief that Aang will figure it out in the end
Katara abruptly breaks off and hurls a reed at Aang
Aang uses a wave of water to slice the reed into two halves before it hits him
Katara congratulates Aang for having the reflexes of a waterbending master
Aang bows and thanks Katara, calling her "Sifu Katara"
Katara, touched, bows back to Aang
Iroh gives further detail about the four nations to Zuko
Iroh says the Fire Nation symbolizes power and desire
Iroh says the Earth Kingdom symbolizes endurance and diversity
Iroh says the Air Nomads symbolize freedom from worldly concerns and had good senses of humor
Iroh says the Water Tribes symbolize love and community, especially in the face of change
Iroh explains that people can gain wisdom by understanding each other
Iroh says that if wisdom is drawn from only one place, it becomes rigid and stale
Zuko interjects that this is beginning to allude toward the Avatar
Iroh claims that the combination of the four elements in one person makes the Avatar powerful, and that it can do the same for Zuko
Iroh reveals he created his special technique by studying the waterbenders
Toph taunts Aang by abusing his staff, using it as a nutcracker
