Toph walks away, unopposed by Aang
Katara arrives, worried that Sokka has not yet returned
Aang and Katara split up to search for Sokka
Aang and Katara split up to search for Sokka
Sokka prepares to give up meat and sarcasm to escape from the crack and change his karma
Sokka abruptly abandons all ideas of reformation when Aang finds him
Sokka immediately asks Aang if he has any meat
Aang attempts to use airbending to free Sokka, but it is ineffective
Sokka asks Aang to use earthbending to free him
Aang says he cannot use earthbending
Sokka requests Aang to bring Toph to help him
Aang describes the notion as uncomfortable
Aang shares his feelings about learning earthbending and the pressure he feels
Sokka interjects with dry, deeply sarcastic responses
Aang misses Sokka’s sarcasm
The critter, whom Sokka introduces as Foo Foo Cuddlypoops, returns
Aang identifies the critter as a saber-tooth moose lion cub
Aang asks the cub if he had lost his mother
The fully grown mother of the cub arrives, furious, assuming the boys stole her cub
Iroh begins to teach Zuko how to redirect lightning
Iroh explains that lightning goes through one arm, into the stomach, and out through the other arm
Iroh and Zuko practice the hand motion to feel the pathway
Zuko thinks he has mastered the technique
Zuko demands that Uncle Iroh strike him with lightning
Iroh refuses to strike Zuko, fearing he might hurt his nephew
Iroh states that if Zuko is lucky, he will never have to use the technique
Zuko gazes at the dark clouds approaching
Zuko rides off
The mother saber-tooth moose lion roars and charges at Aang and Sokka
Aang tries to avoid the beast and distract it away from Sokka
Sokka is left open for attack
Aang stands his ground before the crack as the mother charges again
Aang faces the mother's charge head-on
Aang uses a strong gust of air to blow back the beast
The beast loses interest and walks away
Toph claps and half-heartedly congratulates Aang
Toph continues cracking nuts with Aang's staff
Aang forcefully demands that Toph return his staff
Toph says Aang is thinking like an earthbender by standing his ground
Toph orders Aang to move the rock again
Aang successfully moves the rock
Toph deems Aang to be a true earthbender
Aang offers to remove Sokka from the crack
Toph drags Sokka out of the hole herself
Aang, Toph, and Sokka return to the campsite and reunite with Katara, Appa, and Momo
Katara is relieved that her brother Sokka is safe
Sokka tells the group that being trapped in the fissure gave him time to think and he came to a realization
Aang interrupts Sokka to show off his new skill to Katara
Aang demonstrates his newly acquired earthbending ability
Katara takes Toph aside and questions if she tried positive reinforcement
Toph replies that she did and says it "worked wonders"
Aang tells Appa that he can earthbend now and that the key is to remain unmovable
Aang tries to show Appa how unmovable he is
Appa moves Aang with ease, amusing everyone else
Zuko stands alone at the top of a mountain
Zuko yells defiantly into the raging thunderstorm as lightning crackles overhead
Zuko challenges the storm to strike him, yelling that he can take whatever it throws at him and that it has "never held back before"
The storm does not strike Zuko
Zuko falls to his knees in tears, crying out in anguish

The_Library.action: Aang sits on the ground, looking over a vast savanna landscape
The gang stands behind Aang, who is holding a flute
Sokka asks, "What's out here?"
Toph bends down to touch the ground
Toph responds, "A lot actually."
Aang interrupts Toph, telling her not to ruin the surprise
Aang plays a musical note on the flute
A groundhog-like creature emerges and repeats the same note
Aang plays several more notes on the flute
Sokka plugs his finger into the end of the flute in frustration
Sokka states that they need to make plans
Toph points out that they are making mini-vacation plans
Sokka argues they do not have time for vacations
Aang points out he is working hard with Toph and Katara to master the elements
Katara agrees there is nothing wrong with enjoying themselves during downtime
Sokka expresses concern about lacking inside information about the Fire Nation and being unable to locate the Fire Lord
Sokka states they need intelligence to win the war
Katara says they will finish their vacations and then look for Sokka's "intelligence"
Katara chooses the Misty Palms Oasis for her mini-vacation
The gang discovers the Misty Palms Oasis' reputation has declined over the past hundred years
The gang enters a bar-like establishment
An employee prepares a mango
A customer accidentally bumps into Aang, spilling some of his drink onto him
Aang uses airbending to dry himself
The customer, excited to meet an airbender, introduces himself as Professor Zei of Ba Sing Se University
Professor Zei quizzes Aang as though he is a science specimen
Sokka inquires if the professor has a more recent map
Professor Zei unrolls a map showing a vast desert with marked routes
Sokka dramatically announces his choice of the library as the next mini-vacation location
Professor Zei states it is impossible to cross the desert on foot
The gang introduces Professor Zei to Appa
Sandbenders attempt to steal Appa as the team leaves the cantina
Professor Zei shoos the sandbenders away
The professor admires Appa, having never seen a flying bison before
The group heads out in search of the enormous library
Toph inquires if the library exists
The professor claims that some believe the library does not exist, annoying Toph
Some time passes
Toph claims that she has found the library
Everyone looks, only to see desert sand, then looks back at Toph
Toph reminds them that she is blind
Katara notes it should not be hard to find a giant, ornate building from the air
After a long search, Sokka spots a tower-like structure sticking out of the sand
The group investigates the tower and concludes it is not the library
A fox carrying a scroll races up the side of the tower and enters via a window at the top
Sokka realizes this is the library and that most of it is underground
The professor, devastated, drops to the ground and begins to excavate
Toph places her hand on the tower and announces the library is intact under the sand
Everyone enters the library via the open window, except for Appa and Toph
Appa does not fit through the opening
Toph declines to enter due to not being able to read
The group enters the library
They hear something approaching and hide behind ornate columns
A huge black, white-faced owl walks forward
The owl observes his surroundings for a few moments
The owl speaks, telling the group he is aware of their presence
Professor Zei comes out excitedly and introduces himself
The owl tells Professor Zei to leave or be stuffed like an animal trophy
The others come out from hiding
Sokka asks if the owl is the spirit who brought the library to the physical world
The owl confirms he is the spirit
Wan Shi Tong informs them that humans are not permitted in the library anymore because they abuse knowledge
Wan Shi Tong asks why they have come
Sokka poorly lies to Wan Shi Tong
Aang promises they will not abuse the library's knowledge
Wan Shi Tong relents but only after each provides some knowledge for his collection
Professor Zei offers a first edition book
Katara offers a waterbending scroll
Aang offers his wanted poster
Sokka offers a specially-tied knot
Wan Shi Tong accepts the donations, though less than impressed with the knot
Wan Shi Tong descends into the lower levels of the library, returning to work
The group is allowed to look around the library
Toph attempts to make small talk with Appa outside
Toph explains to Appa that her seismic sense is impaired because of the looseness of the sand
Toph describes her vision as "fuzzy"
The group looks around inside the library
It is learned that Aang was once left-handed in a previous life
Sokka finds a parchment stating the darkest day of the Fire Nation marked with a date
The group seeks out a Fire Nation wing of the library
They discover that everything in the Fire Nation wing has been destroyed by firebenders
The group is shocked by the destruction
A fox appears and indicates it will lead them to another source of information
The fox opens the door to a planetarium and shows them how to use a mechanism to operate it
Katara suggests entering the date from the parchment and running the machine
The group enters the date and runs the machine
They realize a solar eclipse will incapacitate the firebenders
Sokka gloats about the defeat of the firebenders
The Knowledge Spirit appears, angry at the visitors for betraying his trust
Sokka tries to make an excuse, explaining that the Fire Nation are the bad guys and damaged the library
Wan Shi Tong replies that many others have visited seeking greater power
Wan Shi Tong flaps his wings and begins sinking the library
The group starts to flee with the owl chasing them
Toph realizes the library is sinking
Toph attempts to prevent the library from sinking, but fails and begins to sink into the sand herself
Toph twists sand around her feet into sandstone
Toph uses her fists to slow the library's descent
Wan Shi Tong pursues the team
Sokka persuades Aang to return to the planetarium to discover if or when the next solar eclipse will happen before the return of Sozin's Comet
Aang and Sokka head back to the planetarium
Wan Shi Tong continues to chase Katara and the professor
Toph continues to hold up the library
sandbenders arrive on scene, intent on capturing Appa
Toph is preoccupied with preventing the library from sinking
Toph is unable to use her seismic sense effectively
Toph is unable to determine the sandbenders' exact identity or location
Aang begins checking dates from that day to the arrival of Sozin's Comet
Sokka begins checking dates from that day to the arrival of Sozin's Comet
Aang and Sokka find the date of the next solar eclipse, which is a few months away
Aang and Sokka find the date of the next solar eclipse, which is a few months away
Toph alternates between holding up the library and firing misaimed blasts at the sandbenders
Toph realizes she must choose between protecting Appa or preventing her friends' deaths
Toph plants her fists in the library again
Toph hears Appa's calls of distress as he is pulled away
Toph sadly apologizes for being unable to help Appa
The Knowledge Spirit catches up to Katara and warns her that he is versed in all types of waterbending styles, rendering her attacks worthless
Sokka drops down on the owl, knocking him unconscious and saying, "That's called 'Sokka Style'. Learn it!"
Sokka and Katara begin to ascend the rope through which they entered
Sokka calls to the professor, who refuses to leave
The spirit regains consciousness and shakes Sokka and Katara off the rope
Aang catches Sokka and Katara and flies them out of the library
Toph releases her grip on the library, and it vanishes into the sand
The group is initially happy at the discovery of the Fire Nation's weakness
The mood changes once they realize Appa is gone
A tear runs down Aang's face

The_Desert.action: Team Avatar is stuck in the middle of the desert after escaping the library
Aang, Sokka, Katara, and Momo narrowly escape the library before it vanishes under the sand
All but Toph look in different directions for signs of Appa and a way out
Aang scolds Toph for letting the sandbenders escape and accuses her of intentionally letting Appa be captured
Katara attempts to calm Aang down and points out that Toph saved their lives
Sokka states that they are still in need of rescuing, being stranded in the middle of nowhere
Aang lashes out at everyone, calling them selfish
Katara tries to calm Aang down again
Aang heads out alone with his glider to search for a sign of Appa
Katara takes charge and decides the rest need to start walking to get out of the desert and reach Ba Sing Se
The group walks away from the crater left by the sunken library
Sokka sarcastically wonders if digging up the "giant owl" might get them a ride
Iroh utters several fake-moans, pretending to be in pain to get Zuko to stop for a break
Zuko stops the ostrich-horse and makes camp with Iroh
Five adversaries on rhinos surround Iroh and Zuko
Iroh identifies the group as Colonel Mongke and his Rough Rhinos
Iroh greets the Rough Rhinos in a friendly manner, while taking note of their positions
Colonel Mongke states they are there to capture fugitives
The Rough Rhinos attack Iroh and Zuko
Iroh and Zuko maneuver an escape
Zuko asks Iroh if he has any old friends that do not want to attack them
Iroh gets an idea from Zuko’s question
Katara, Sokka, Toph, and Momo stagger tiredly through the desert
Sokka halts and uses Momo's wings for shade
Sokka causes Toph to crash into him
Sokka begins to say "Can't you watch where you're ...?" but stops, remembering Toph cannot see
Katara encourages the group to stick together
Katara shares some of her bending water to help keep them hydrated
Sokka criticizes Katara for using water that was used on the swamp people
Sokka spots a cactus and slices it open, revealing liquid inside
Sokka and Momo drink the cactus liquid happily, despite Katara's warning
Katara warns against eating strange plants
Sokka's eyes dilate and he begins to hallucinate
Momo begins to hallucinate
Toph asks if she can try some cactus juice
Katara decides nobody should drink any more cactus juice
The group continues looking for Aang
Sokka deliriously asks how they came to be in the middle of the ocean
Aang glides over the sand dunes of Si Wong Desert, looking for Appa
Aang blows his bison whistle and yells
Aang lands and, overcome by rage and grief, slams his staff on the ground
The impact creates a huge mushroom cloud
The others see the mushroom cloud from a distance
The others feel the current of air created by the impact
The others unknowingly decide to keep walking away from Aang
Sokka and Momo, hallucinating, notice the sand cloud and believe it to be a giant mushroom
Sokka and Momo begin to worship the "mushroom," thinking it could be friendly
Xin Fu and Master Yu learn that Aang and Toph have been at the Misty Palms Oasis recently, but headed into the desert and may never come back
Xin Fu and Master Yu debate if Toph is wanted dead or alive
Xin Fu notices Fire Nation wanted posters
Iroh and Zuko arrive at the oasis and enter the local tavern
It nears sunset
Aang rejoins the group without Appa
Aang feels discouraged and seems willing to give up
Katara pulls the group together again and urges them to continue on
buzzard wasps fly overhead
Katara announces that they should stop for the night
The others gladly fall to the ground exhausted
Toph asks for water
Katara begins to ration out the last of her water
Momo jumps into the water, causing it to sink into the ground
Katara uses her waterbending to recover most of the water
Katara asks Sokka if she can look at some things he got from the library
Sokka, still under the effects of the cactus, becomes defensive and claims he did not steal anything
Sokka rounds on Momo, angrily accusing the lemur of ratting him out
Momo starts to sob
Katara tells Sokka she was at the library with him and takes the maps from him
Katara unfurls one of the maps
Aang, still in defeatist mode, remarks that none of the maps will help them find Appa
Katara points out that the maps will help them head to Ba Sing Se by following the stars
Katara says they should start up again in a few hours to take advantage of the cool nights
Iroh spots someone at the Pai Sho table and believes this may be the friend they are looking for
Iroh and Zuko head over to the Pai Sho table
Xin Fu wants to go after Iroh and Zuko
Master Yu restrains Xin Fu and tells him to be patient, pointing out the place is full of people who would do anything for easy money
Iroh approaches the Pai Sho table and asks if he may have this game
Iroh places the lotus tile in the center of the board
Fung and Iroh begin a ritualistic Pai Sho game, eventually creating a lotus formation with the tiles
Xin Fu loses his patience and goes for Iroh and Zuko
Fung states that Iroh and Zuko are wanted criminals with giant bounties on their heads
Fung tells Xin Fu he thinks he will capture Iroh and Zuko and collect all that gold
The word "gold" catches the attention of the other patrons
A brawl starts among the patrons, enabling Zuko and Iroh to escape
Zuko and Iroh escape during the brawl
Katara rouses everyone, wanting to take advantage of the cool night
Aang mistakes a cloud for Appa and brightens for a second
Aang realizes his error and his mood deteriorates further
Katara asks Aang to waterbend the cloud into her water skin
Aang angrily snatches the pouch from Katara and glides off
Katara comments on how little water there was from the cloud
Aang blows up at Katara, asking what she is doing to help
Katara quietly states she is trying to keep everyone together
Toph stubs her foot against what she claims is a boat
Aang uncovers the object with airbending, revealing a sand-sailer
The group finds a compass aboard the sand-sailer and decides to use the vehicle to try to escape the desert
Iroh and Zuko are led into a flower shop by their friend
The friend tells Iroh what an honor it is to meet such a high-ranking member of the Order of the White Lotus
Zuko makes a derisive statement about flower arranging, still unconvinced how playing Pai Sho will help them
Iroh apologizes to Fung about Zuko's lack of appreciation for the cryptic arts
Iroh and Fung have a cryptic exchange through a speakeasy-type opening
Iroh and Fung are let into a back room
Zuko is shut out and made to wait in the flower shop, as the room is for "members only"
Aang uses his airbending to propel the sand-sailer over the sand
Katara mentions she does not think the needle on the compass is pointing north
Katara sees a huge rock formation ahead and understands the compass is pointing toward it, the magnetic center of the desert
Toph is ecstatic to learn they are headed toward a rock
Aang appears to be vindictively hoping to find some sandbenders
The sun starts to break through at the top of the huge rock
Toph falls back to make a rock angel in the solid ground
The crew explores one of the odd caves at the top
Sokka breathes deeply and says his head is starting to clear up from the cactus juice
Sokka and Momo eat a yellow, sap-like, gooey substance from the walls
Momo eats a yellow, sap-like, gooey substance from the walls
Sokka and Momo feel woozy from eating the substance
Katara is dismayed by Sokka and Momo’s actions
Toph realizes they are not walking down a natural cave
Toph feels something buzzing and that it is coming toward them
Huge buzzard wasps swarm out of the cave, which appears to be their nest
Toph's aim is hampered by the fact the creatures are flying
Katara is completely out of water to bend
Momo is snatched by one of the buzzing creatures in the confusion
Aang declares he will not lose another friend to the desert and gives chase
The others work their way down the rock
Aang successfully frees Momo
Aang uses his airbending to knock the fleeing buzzard wasp down, showing his sinister and vengeful side, shocking Momo
Katara helps Toph fight off the buzzard wasps
Huge sandblasts shoo Katara and Toph back to the top of the rock
The sand clears to reveal several sandbenders
Aang returns and lands in between the two parties, looking angry
Iroh emerges from the back room, startling Zuko awake
Zuko wakes up, having been sleeping standing up
Iroh tells Zuko they will head to Ba Sing Se to hide as refugees
Xin Fu and Master Yu continue searching for Iroh and Zuko
The sandbenders ask the group why they have one of their vessels and accuse them of theft
Katara explains they found the vessel and that their bison was stolen
The younger son shows anger at being accused of theft
The father claims no one was accusing them and suggests offering the Avatar hospitality
Toph recognizes one of the voices as belonging to the one who took Appa
Aang steps forward, enraged, and demands to know Appa's whereabouts
The sandbenders challenge Aang's accusation
Aang destroys one of their sand-sailers and repeats his demand
Xin Fu and Master Yu head into the flower shop looking for Zuko and Iroh
Xin Fu knocks down the door to the back room
Xin Fu and Master Yu do not find Zuko and Iroh
Xin Fu and Master Yu decide to abandon the search for the firebenders and go back to hunting for Toph
Two potted plants are carted out of the oasis
Zuko and Iroh briefly peer out from under the lids of the potted plants
Aang demands to know where Appa is, destroying another sand-sailer
The sandbender leader turns to his son, Ghashiun, and asks what he did to cause the violence
Ghashiun denies wrongdoing and insists Team Avatar are the thieves
Toph states she heard Ghashiun say to put a muzzle on Appa
Aang, enraged by the revelation, enters the Avatar State and destroys the remaining sand-sailers
Ghashiun, frightened, confesses he took Appa without knowing it was the Avatar's and apologizes
Aang, in the Avatar voice, demands to know Appa's location
Ghashiun tells Aang he traded Appa to merchants and that Appa is probably in Ba Sing Se
Aang, still enraged, begins to form a sphere of air, creating the beginnings of a sandstorm
The sandbenders, frightened, beg for mercy and offer safe passage out of the desert
Aang ignores their pleas
Sokka pushes Toph forward and shouts to the sandbenders to run
The sandbenders quickly run
Katara does not run from the sandstorm
As Aang rises in the air, Katara approaches, grabs his wrist, and embraces him tightly
Aang, with tears streaming down his face, lets himself be calmed
The whirlwind dissipates
Aang exits the Avatar State
Aang breaks down to tears in Katara's embrace
Katara embraces Aang

The_Serpent%27s_Pass.action: Aang, Toph, Momo, and Katara enjoy a break in a pool at the base of a waterfall
Sokka reviews the maps acquired from the Spirit Library
Katara accidentally soaks the maps with a waterbending bomb
Katara uses waterbending to extract the water from the maps
The group huddles around the map to discuss their direction
The group concludes that the Serpent's Pass is the most direct route
A group of three Earth Kingdom refugees (Than, Ying, and Ying's sister) approaches and greets the gang
Ying describes the Serpent's Pass as a "deadly" route for only the desperate
The Earth Kingdom trio convinces Aang's group to go to Full Moon Bay instead to take a ferry to Ba Sing Se
Katara arrives at the hidden Full Moon Bay ferry station and is amazed to see how many refugees are awaiting transportation
Iroh and Zuko, disguised as Mushi and Lee, board a departing ferry
Jet, Smellerbee, and Longshot, three former Freedom Fighters, are also on the ferry
Iroh contemplates the irony of returning to his greatest failure
Zuko complains about how bad the food tastes
Jet overhears Zuko's complaints and approaches Zuko and Iroh
Jet tells Zuko and Iroh that the captain is eating like a king while passengers get scraps
Jet exclaims that this is unfair and asks Zuko to help "liberate" some food
Zuko agrees to assist Jet's group in getting food
ticketmaster refuses to allow the cabbage merchant to bring on any vegetables due to possible parasites
Aang approaches the ticketmaster and requests four tickets
ticketmaster refuses Aang's request due to lack of passport, Momo being an animal, and disbelief that Aang is the Avatar
Aang backs away
Toph claims she will take care of it and approaches with her Beifong family document
ticketmaster gushes her loyalties to the Beifong family and accommodates Toph by granting passage to the four, allowing Momo as her seeing-eye-lemur
Sokka starts celebrating their acquisition of the tickets
Sokka is grabbed at the neck by a guard asking for their tickets and passports
Suki greets Sokka by kissing him on the cheek
Sokka is surprised by a clean-faced Suki
Sokka, Suki, and the others catch up on old times and tell Suki about Appa's disappearance
Refugees call up to Team Avatar in desperation, saying their tickets and passports were stolen
Aang appeals to the ticketmaster to allow the trio onto the ferry
The ticketmaster refuses to allow the trio onto the ferry
Aang decides he will lead them all through the Serpent's Pass
The group heads to the Serpent's Pass
Suki joins the group
Sokka has misgivings but does not voice them
The group arrives at the Serpent's Pass
Katara reads the inscription on the post at the entrance
The inscription states that they should abandon hope
The group heads along the pass
The group sees a Fire Nation ship in the lake
The rock path gives way underneath one of the refugees
Toph saves the refugee
The rock fall attracts the attention of the Fire Nation ship
The Fire Nation ship begins firing at the group
Aang deflects fireballs from the group
One fireball manages to make a hit
Sokka pushes Suki out of the way of rock debris from a Fire Nation blast
Sokka is in the path of falling debris
Toph earthbends a barrier to deflect the debris
Sokka focuses on getting Suki out of harm's way instead of thanking Toph
Toph is annoyed but quickly follows the group
As night falls, Sokka acts overprotective of Suki, which baffles her
Zuko, Jet, and Smellerbee sneak into the captain's private food storeroom
Jet "liberates" some food
Longshot shoots up a line for them to escape
The group escapes back to the lower deck
The escape line is pulled out, disguising the escape route
Aang looks out over the water
Katara lets Aang know it is all right to miss Appa
Katara asks Aang why he is keeping his emotions in check
Aang replies that he did not like how his anger changed him in the desert
Katara encourages Aang to not give up on hope
Katara attempts to give Aang a hug
Aang bows and thanks Katara for her concern
Sokka looks at the moon
Suki approaches Sokka and comments on the moon's beauty
Suki asks Sokka why he is being so over-protective
Sokka tries to explain he was unable to protect someone at the North Pole and does not want that to happen again
Suki tells Sokka, in a roundabout way, that she has missed him
Sokka and Suki almost kiss
Sokka claims he cannot kiss Suki
Suki apologizes
Sokka says it is not her fault
The ferry continues its passage to Ba Sing Se
Jet hands out food to the other passengers
Iroh comments that Smellerbee is an unusual name for a young man
Smellerbee says, "Maybe it's because I'm not a man; I'm a girl" and storms off
Longshot calms Smellerbee down through silent gestures
Jet sits with Iroh and Zuko and thinks about seeing the wall of the great city
Iroh says the wall is a sight to behold, recalling he saw it once when he was a different man
Jet says he is going to Ba Sing Se for a new beginning
Iroh, appearing to direct his attention at Zuko, says people deserve a second chance
The group reaches a section of the Serpent's Pass that is completely submerged beneath the lake
The group is initially discouraged
Katara tells everyone to get in a single file line
Katara parts the water for the group to walk the path
Aang helps Katara and they create a bubble for the group to walk in
Momo jumps into the water, looking to catch some fish
Something large swims by in the water
The bubble is burst by the creature
Toph earthbends the floor of the path high enough to get the group above the waterline
The creature circles the group and reveals itself
Sokka states that he realizes why it is called the Serpent's Pass
Aang tells Katara to get the others to safety as he distracts the serpent
Katara makes an ice bridge for the others to cross
Katara heads out on top of the water to assist Aang
Sokka encourages Toph to cross the ice
Toph prefers to stay where she can see
Toph changes her mind when her miniature island is destroyed
Toph crosses the ice bridge
The serpent smashes the bridge, sending Toph into the water
Toph calls for help, unable to swim
Sokka prepares to go help Toph
Suki jumps in and rescues Toph
Sokka calls out to Toph that he is coming for her
Toph kisses Suki, thinking she is Sokka
Toph realizes her error when Suki speaks
Toph tells Suki, "You can go ahead and let me drown now," embarrassed
Aang and Katara create a large maelstrom, spinning the serpent
Aang and Katara knock out the serpent as it hits one of the walls
The group completes its trek to the other side of the pass
The group sees the wall of Ba Sing Se
Ying goes into labor
Katara takes charge and designates jobs to everyone to help deliver the baby
Jet tells Zuko that from the scar he knows who he is
Zuko becomes defensive
Jet says he is an outcast like Zuko and they need to have each other's back
Zuko states he has realized that being on one's own is not always the best path
Katara helps deliver the baby
The others go in to see the baby, except for Aang
Katara tells Aang that he has to see the newborn
Aang heads in to see the newborn
Aang’s expression changes from discouraged to encouraged
The couple thinks of a name for their baby
Aang tears up and says, "I've been going through a really hard time lately, but you've made me... hopeful again."
Ying says she knows what they should name their daughter – Hope
Than agrees with the name
Aang tells Katara he thought he was being strong, but was really running away from his feelings
Aang says seeing the family full of love reminded him of how he feels about Appa and about Katara
Aang and Katara embrace as the others walk toward them
The others walk toward Aang and Katara
Aang says he is going to try to find Appa
The others wish Aang luck
Suki tells Sokka she needs to get back to her warriors
Sokka is surprised to realize Suki came to protect him
Suki apologizes for the previous night
Sokka stops Suki with a kiss
Aang reaches the wall
Aang ascends to the top of the wall
Aang is shocked to see a huge mechanical drill with the Fire Nation emblem
The huge mechanical drill with the Fire Nation emblem is present
Aang turns to Momo and speaks
Aang says, "Sorry Momo, Appa is going to have to wait."

The_Drill.action: Fire Nation tundra tanks rumble across the rugged terrain toward the Outer Wall of Ba Sing Se
The enormous Fire Nation drill follows close behind the tundra tanks
Several segments of the drill extend forward, elongating the machine
Steam is released from the drill's pipes
Metal spikes from the drill lodge into the surrounding land
The command module for the drill ascends from the top, containing Mai, Ty Lee, Azula, War Minister Qin, and several Fire Nation soldiers
War Minister Qin boasts about the drill's ingenuity and its capability of breaching the walls of Ba Sing Se
Ty Lee questions whether the earthbenders guarding the Outer Wall can halt the drill
Qin objects, stating the drill's metal shell is impervious to earthbending, and strikes the periscope Ty Lee was looking through
Azula concurs with Qin's claims, but orders Mai and Ty Lee to take out the earthbenders just to be safe
Mai, who was twirling a dagger out of boredom, immediately sheathes her dagger and moves to execute Azula's order
Mai muses that she finally has something to do
Team Avatar approaches Ba Sing Se with Than, Ying, and their newborn baby, Hope
Aang flies down on his glider, followed by Momo, and lands in front of the group
Aang warns the group of an oncoming danger approaching Ba Sing Se
Toph and Aang use earthbending to transport everyone up the Outer Wall
Toph and Aang use earthbending to transport everyone up the Outer Wall
Sokka asks, "Now what's so big that Appa has to wait?"
Aang indicates the advancing drill upon arrival at the top of the wall
Ying hopelessly states that they had reached Ba Sing Se and still were not safe
Earthbender soldiers approach and question the group’s reasons for coming to the Outer Wall
Aang reveals himself as the Avatar and requests to be taken to their leader
General Sung says the Avatar’s help is unneeded
General Sung assures that the wall’s impenetrability prevents any enemy invasion of Ba Sing Se
Toph reminds General Sung that Iroh had once infiltrated the city
General Sung says Iroh was quickly removed and humorously explains that Ba Sing Se means "impenetrable city"
Toph alerts General Sung of the oncoming drill
General Sung sends the Terra Team to stop the drill
Sokka expresses his favor of the Terra Team’s name
The Terra Team launches a Fire Nation tank into the air and bends earth columns upward against the drill's metal shell
The drill crushes the earth columns when it extends forward again
The soldiers defend themselves as a flurry of daggers are hurled at them
Mai and Ty Lee skid down the side of the drill, prepared to battle the earthbenders
Ty Lee swings off a rope onto the ground
Ty Lee takes out all the earthbenders in seconds by striking their pressure points
Ty Lee stands triumphantly over the defeated warriors after blocking their chi
General Sung exclaims that they are doomed and begins to flail his arms in panic
Sokka slaps General Sung and tells him to get a hold of himself
General Sung apologizes for his behavior
Toph asks if General Sung needs their assistance
General Sung turns to Aang to humbly ask for his help
Katara, Toph, and Aang look to Sokka for a plan, noting he is the "idea guy"
Sokka complains that he always has to come up with the plans
Katara mentions that Sokka is also the "complaining guy"
Sokka agrees with the "complaining guy" moniker
Iroh and Zuko arrive at the ticket gate in the Ba Sing Se monorail station
The ticket woman accidentally pronounces Iroh and Zuko's names incorrectly and casts them suspicious looks
Iroh corrects the ticket woman on the pronunciation of their names, angering her
Iroh quickly compliments the ticket woman, comparing her beauty to that of a flower in full bloom
The ticket woman is flattered, compliments Iroh back, stamps their tickets, and welcomes them to Ba Sing Se
Zuko snatches his ticket out of Iroh's hand and walks away, disgusted
Jet and Smellerbee discuss the possibility of Lee (Zuko) joining the new Freedom Fighters
Smellerbee opposes the idea, highlighting their unfamiliarity with Lee
Jet notes that Lee did not get his scar from a waterbender
Jet asks Longshot for his input
Longshot gives Jet a blank stare
Jet responds that he "can respect that"
Katara uses her healing abilities on an earthbender attacked by Mai and Ty Lee
The general mentions the earthbender did not appear to be injured
Katara informs the general that the earthbender's chi is blocked
The earthbender provides a description of the person who ambushed him
Katara identifies the ambusher as Ty Lee
Katara remarks that Ty Lee does not look threatening, but "it's like she takes you down from the inside"
Sokka's face lights up and he states they should dismantle the drill in a similar manner
Toph suggests hitting the drill's pressure points
Aang agrees with the plan
The group starts their descent from the wall toward the drill
Toph creates a large dust cloud to use as cover to approach the drill
Toph instructs the others to follow close behind her as only she will be able to "see"
Ty Lee examines the dust cloud through the periscope and remarks that it was "poofy"
Azula looks suspiciously at War Minister Qin
War Minister Qin reassures Azula that everything is in order
The group dashes through the cloud close to the drill
Toph creates a tunnel underground
Toph tells everyone to jump in before closing it up behind them
Sokka remarks that it is so dark he cannot see
Toph sarcastically responds that it is a nightmare
Sokka apologizes
Toph keeps tunneling until they emerge underneath the drill
Sokka sees an open area up into the interior
Aang boosts Sokka and Katara inside
Toph refuses to go, explaining she would not be able to bend or see inside the metal drill
Toph stays outside to aid their efforts by wedging a large earth column against the drill to try to slow it down
Sokka says he needs the drill's schematics
Sokka slices off the wheel of a nearby pipe, releasing steam rapidly
Aang and Katara grow worried that someone will hear them
Aang and Katara grow worried that someone will hear them
Sokka explains that engineers will come by with schematics to fix problems
a firebender engineer walks out of the mist and approaches the source of the incident
the engineer turns around sharply
Katara freezes the steam, entrapping the engineer
Sokka takes the schematics from the engineer
the team runs up the stairway
the team runs up the stairway
the team runs up the stairway
Sokka examines the schematics and explains they need to cut through the braces connecting the inner mechanism and the outer shell
Sokka explains that this will result in the collapse of the machine
Iroh and Zuko seat themselves on a bench at the monorail station
Jet comes over and sits next to Zuko, inquiring about his plans once inside the city
a tea seller walks by, advertising his wares
Iroh asks for jasmine tea
Iroh spits out the tea in disgust, complaining it is cold
Jet pulls Zuko aside and tries to convince him to join the Freedom Fighters
Zuko declines Jet's offer
Jet suspiciously eyes Iroh drinking the now hot, steamy tea and realizes they are firebenders
Zuko knocks the tea out of Iroh's hand and scolds his uncle for firebending his tea in public
Iroh remarks, "I know you're not supposed to cry over spilled tea, but it's just so sad."
Sokka, Katara, and Aang arrive at the location of the massive steel braces and carry out their scheme
Aang and Katara begin slicing through one brace using waterbending to shoot Katara's water skin supply back and forth
Aang and Katara grow fatigued after getting halfway through the brace
Sokka attempts to encourage Aang and Katara to continue
Katara threatens to harm Sokka with her waterbending, causing him to shut up
Aang and Katara finish cutting through the brace, but the two ends only slightly shift out of alignment
A loud rumbling sound reverberates and the entire drill shudders
The team prepares to evacuate, believing they have destroyed the drill
War Minister Qin comes over the announcement system and explains the rumbling was due to the drill making contact with the wall
Sokka pushes against the brace with all his might but fails to budge it
The team cuts through one brace with minimal damage after exerting a lot of time and energy
Aang recalls his earthbending lessons and points out they only need to slice halfway through the braces to weaken them
Aang explains that he will deliver a final blow to shear the braces
The team agrees with Aang’s plan
Aang reminds the team that everyone in Ba Sing Se is depending on them
Aang resumes work cutting away at the braces
Katara resumes work cutting away at the braces
Toph continues to hinder the drill’s progress from outside
earthbenders atop the wall hurl large boulders in an attempt to damage the drill
the boulders fail to damage the drill
A soldier informs the War Minister that an engineer has been ambushed
Another soldier tells the War Minister a brace was cut clean through
Azula senses sabotage
Azula beckons Ty Lee and Mai to investigate with her
Aang and Katara continue cutting through the braces
Sokka persistently cheers Aang and Katara on
a blue fireball comes hurtling toward Sokka, causing him to duck and shout in alarm
Katara and Aang look up to find Azula, Mai, and Ty Lee standing on an overhead beam
Ty Lee states her excitement to see the Avatar "and friends" while shooting Sokka a flirtatious look
Sokka responds with a wave and a drawled "Hey"
Katara pulls Sokka away
Ty Lee dives toward Aang
Aang airbends Ty Lee away
They split up at an intersection
Katara tosses her water skin to Aang, saying he would need it
Mai and Ty Lee follow Katara and Sokka
Azula follows Aang
Sokka and Katara come to a hatch marked "slurry pipeline"
A rock and water mixture channels through the pipeline
Sokka explains it is a convenient escape route
Sokka and Katara jump into the muddy water to avoid Mai's thrown knives
Mai throws knives at Sokka and Katara
Sokka and Katara are washed away through the pipeline
Ty Lee peers down into the pipe and tries to persuade Mai to accompany her
Mai refuses to go "in that wall sludge juice"
Ty Lee shrugs and jumps in
Mai closes the hatch after Ty Lee
Aang speeds past numerous engineers and control rooms with Momo perched on his back
Momo perches on Aang's back
Aang climbs a ladder to the top of the drill
Aang plans to deliver the final strike
Katara and Sokka exit outside the rear of the drill covered in slurry
Ty Lee pursues Katara and Sokka in hot pursuit
Katara uses waterbending to create a plug, imprisoning Ty Lee
Sokka urges Katara to continue plugging the drain
Sokka explains that when Aang delivers the final blow, the pipes will be ready to burst from the built-up pressure
Aang chooses a spot on the top of the drill to deliver the final blow
Several boulders suddenly fall from overhead, one nearly hitting Aang
Aang shouts up to the general to stop hurling rocks
Sung, still paralyzed with fear, orders his men to keep hurling rocks
Aang uses waterbending to slowly cut an "X" into the hull to create a weak spot
Katara holds back the slurry to build up pressure
Sokka continues to "coach" Katara, commenting on her technique and reminding her to breathe
Katara angrily snaps at Sokka, calling him a "chattering hog monkey"
Sokka orders Katara to "Just bend the slurry, woman!"
Katara bends a wave of slurry at Sokka's face to shut him up
Toph approaches Katara
Toph teams up with Katara to help plug up the drain by earthbending the slurry
Katara and Toph force the slurry back further, exerting more pressure on the pipeline
The pipeline sections bulge outward from the increased pressure
Aang stops working on his "X" and wishes he was a metalbender
Momo shrieks, alerting Aang
Aang turns around and catches sight of a blue fire blast coming toward them
Aang dodges the blue fire blast
Aang sends Momo away
Aang prepares for a duel with Azula
Azula shoots several fireballs at Aang
Aang avoids all of Azula's fireballs
Aang blasts a gust of air at Azula
Aang uses water whips to deter Azula's blasts off-target
Azula shoots two fierce blasts at Aang
Aang attempts to deflect the blasts with a water shield
The water shield evaporates and Aang is sent back several feet
More boulders are hurled downward
Aang throws a boulder at Azula
Azula ducks and shoots another fireball
Aang creates a wall of rubble
Aang hurls components of the wall at Azula
Azula kicks the boulders away and almost strikes Aang with a kick of flames
Azula somersaults in the air and delivers a powerful fire blast
The fire blast destroys the makeshift wall
Aang is hurtled into the Outer Wall and falls unconscious onto the surface of the drill
The drill extends again
Azula walks over to Aang with sinister intent
Azula hoists Aang up and holds him against the wall
Aang’s body slides between the wall and the drill
Azula readies a blue flame in her hand
Aang slowly awakens and sees the oncoming attack
Aang makes an earth gauntlet from the wall and blocks Azula’s attack, pushing her back several feet
Aang readies for battle
Slurry spews from the cracks in the wall, coating the drill with a slick layer of liquid and dirt
Azula recovers from the incident and rockets toward Aang using firebending
Azula slides across the mud instead
Azula’s momentum flings her into Aang while windmilling to regain control
Aang and Azula slam into the wall and both begin sliding down opposite sides of the cylindrical drill
Azula manages to turn over and stop her descent by using her hands and the soles of her shoes
Momo arrives on the scene and latches onto Aang’s back, helping him up
Aang uses airbending to climb back to the top
Aang thanks Momo
Aang runs to the weakened spot
A recently hurled boulder lands next to Aang
Aang moves the boulder over and cuts the four lower sides off with earthbending to craft a spike
Aang fits the spike into the "X" he carved
Aang rides an air scooter straight up the wall
Aang turns around at the apex and races down on foot
Azula spots Aang and sends a powerful blue fire blast at him at close range
Aang speeds past the blast, missing it by a fraction of a second
Aang slams down with an air impact on the rock spike, driving it deep
The impact causes the compromised braces to snap and the excess slurry bursts out of the machine
The drill is left jammed partially in the Outer Wall
Azula, thrown back by the final blow, slides down the drill to join Ty Lee
Ty Lee sits in the slurry, having been freed from her predicament once the influx of slurry stopped
Mai emerges from one of the hatches completely dry and announces to the two that they lost
Smellerbee, Longshot, and Jet, eyes wild with hatred, board the same monorail as Zuko and Iroh to Ba Sing Se
Iroh leans over and tickles an infant, who is Ying's baby, Hope
Sokka brainstorms clever nicknames for the group, including "The Aang Gang", "The Boomeraang Squad", and "Fearsome Foursome"
The team prepares to enter Ba Sing Se
The team is saved from Fire Nation war tactics once again

City_of_Walls_and_Secrets.action: Aang, Katara, Sokka, and Toph are allowed into Ba Sing Se and given a house in the Upper Ring as a reward for protecting the city
The group feels optimistic about reaching the city, except for Toph, who is not thrilled about "life in the big city"
Aang and his friends are met by Joo Dee, a woman assigned to guide them around the city
Sokka attempts to get Joo Dee to talk about the War and a possible audience with the Earth King, but is ignored
Joo Dee ignores Sokka's questions about the War and the Earth King
Team Avatar observes the segregation of rich and poor people in Ba Sing Se and dislikes it
Aang recalls that he did not visit Ba Sing Se before because the Air Nomads taught equality for all
It is revealed that an audience with the Earth King will require at least a month of waiting
No one in the city wants to talk or think about the War
Aang tries to find Appa, but no one is willing to give information due to Joo Dee's meaningful glances
A neighbor warns the team that talking about the War is forbidden and to avoid the Dai Li, the secret police force
Zuko and Iroh find jobs in a tea house
Iroh becomes popular for his superior tea brews
Jet constantly spies on Zuko and Iroh, waiting for signs of firebending
Smellerbee tells Jet that all he saw was an old man with a cup of hot tea, which did not prove anything
Smellerbee points out that they and Longshot agreed to start over in Ba Sing Se and change their ways
Jet counters that they will start over after he exposes Zuko and Iroh's true identity
Jet steals Iroh's spark rocks to tempt him into firebending
Iroh borrows a neighbor's spark rocks instead of using firebending
a notice arrives of a party being held in the palace for the King's pet bear
Katara decides it is a good way to reach the Earth King
Toph says the gang would be spotted right away if they went to the event as commoners
Toph and Katara dress up in formal attire and head for the palace
Toph and Katara dress up in formal attire and head for the palace
Aang and Sokka wait
Aang and Sokka wait
Aang looks at Katara, blushing and complimenting her on how beautiful she is
The girls are initially refused entry by a guard
The guard rejects Toph's family seal
The girls manage to go inside with the help of an official named Long Feng
Long Feng introduces himself as the culture minister and insists on accompanying them
The girls give the guard a raspberry on their way out
Aang and Sokka arrive at the palace and enter the side entrance as servants
All of the kids are discovered by Joo Dee, who appears frightened
Joo Dee tries to push Sokka out
Joo Dee knocks Sokka into Aang
Aang spills his drink holder on a guest
Aang is exposed as the Avatar, causing a commotion
Sokka, Katara, Toph, and Momo are gathered gently but forcefully by Dai Li agents
Aang is confronted by Long Feng, who reveals himself as Grand Secretariat and head of the Dai Li
Aang is taken to a room where the others are waiting
Jet charges into the tea house while Iroh and Zuko are working
Jet accuses Iroh and Zuko of being firebenders, stating he saw Iroh heating his tea
Customers tell Jet that does not prove anything since Iroh works in a tea house
Jet insists Iroh is a firebender
A customer sternly tells Jet to drop his swords "nice and easy"
Jet draws his swords and advances on Iroh
Zuko grabs a pair of dual broadswords from the customer and engages in a duel with Jet
Zuko holds his own against Jet without using firebending
Iroh urges Jet to stop
Jet taunts Zuko about firebending
Zuko disarms Jet of one sword at the cost of one of his own blades
Zuko states Jet is the one who needs help and swings his sword at Jet, who dodges
Jet claims the Fire Nation is trying to silence him
Pao and customers become convinced Jet is insane
The Dai Li arrive and order Zuko and Jet to drop their weapons
Zuko complies and drops his weapons
Jet urges the Dai Li to arrest Zuko and Iroh, restating his claim
Iroh calmly states Jet is confused and that he and Zuko are refugees
Pao complains that Jet destroyed his shop and assaulted his employees
The customer whose swords Zuko took corroborates the story
The Dai Li order Jet to come with them
Jet attacks the Dai Li
The Dai Li quickly arrest Jet and lead him away
Smellerbee and Longshot watch from the crowd as Jet is taken away
Jet is later subjected to brainwashing in a darkened room
Aang and the rest try to talk about the coming solar eclipse inside Long Feng's chamber
Long Feng abruptly cuts off Aang and the rest
Long Feng forbids any mention of the War to preserve Ba Sing Se's culture and utopian life
It is revealed that Long Feng, the Earth King's secretariat, is in charge of day-to-day affairs and military matters in the city
It is revealed that the Earth King is merely a figurehead and beloved icon
Aang threatens to talk and tell everyone about the War
Long Feng threatens Aang and the rest with expulsion from the city
Long Feng warns that if Aang persists, he may never see Appa again
Long Feng calls in Joo Dee to accompany the group
Aang stares in disbelief
The group realizes that a different woman has taken the place of Joo Dee

The_Tales_of_Ba_Sing_Se.action: Team Avatar cleans themselves up for the day at their Upper Ring house in Ba Sing Se
Aang shaves his head
Sokka shaves his mustache
Katara does her hair
Toph sleeps in
Katara wakes Toph
Toph presents herself with messy hair and dust-covered body, calling it a "healthy coating of earth"
Katara suggests they have a "girls' day out" and takes Toph to the Fancy Lady Day Spa
Toph reluctantly agrees, warning the attendants not to touch her feet
Katara and Toph walk into the spa
Toph is forced into a foot massage that turns into a foot scrubbing
Toph earthbends an attendant through a wall
Katara and Toph take a mud bath
Toph uses earthbending to make creepy faces with the mud and scare away the attendant
Katara and Toph relax in a sauna, using their bending to toss hot rocks and water into a central pit to make steam
The girls leave the spa with heavy make-up on their faces
Toph comments that she does not usually do fancy things, but does not dislike it
Three older girls, led by Star, make fun of Toph's makeup
Toph becomes visibly upset by the remarks
Katara urges Toph to ignore the older girls
Toph forces a laugh back at the girls
Toph earthbends a hole in the bridge, sending the older girls into the water below
Katara washes the girls downstream with a large wave
Katara tries to console Toph as they continue walking
Toph claims that, because she is blind, she does not have to worry about personal appearance or the approval of others
Toph is still hurt by the girls' words and sheds a few tears
Katara tells Toph she admires her confidence, self-assurance, and strength
Katara acknowledges that Toph will never know what she looks like, but says she thinks Toph is very pretty
Toph responds that she would like to return the favor, but does not know what Katara looks like
Katara laughs at Toph's response
Toph gives Katara a friendly, yet hard, punch on the arm
Iroh strolls through a market
Iroh buys a few things at a street stand, such as a picnic basket
Shopkeeper recommends a lavender basket for a romantic picnic
Iroh says it is not for a romantic picnic but for a special occasion, and selects the original basket
Iroh helps the shopkeeper by moving a moon flower into the shade, explaining that moon flowers like partial shade
Iroh continues his walk and sees a small boy crying and his mother trying to calm him
Iroh borrows a liuqin from a nearby shop
Iroh sings a song to the weeping child about a young soldier boy marching home from war
The boy stops crying as Iroh sings to him
The boy thanks Iroh by pulling his beard and laughing
Iroh watches some boys playing earthbending ball in a small street alley
The ball ricochets off a rock and crashes through a window
Iroh tells the boys it is always best to admit mistakes to restore honor
The massive owner appears in the window and threatens the boys
Iroh retracts his comments and tells the boys to run
Iroh and the boys run down an alley
Iroh is threatened at knife-point by a mugger
Iroh, unconcerned, tells the mugger his stance makes him weak to attacks
Iroh knocks the mugger down and steals his dagger
Iroh corrects the mugger’s stance and tells him he does not look like a criminal
The mugger admits he is confused with his life and has turned to crime
Iroh and the mugger share tea
Iroh supports the mugger’s dream to become a masseur
The mugger comments that no one has ever believed in him
Iroh responds that help from others can be a great blessing, the same wisdom he offered Toph
Iroh comes to rest upon a hill with a large tree
Iroh sets up some rocks
Iroh pulls out materials from the basket he purchased earlier
Iroh prepares for a memorial for Lu Ten's birthday, his only son lost in the Siege of Ba Sing Se
Iroh places a cloth out upon the ground along with a picture of Lu Ten
Iroh lights two incense sticks using firebending
Iroh places the incense sticks in a holder
Iroh wishes happy birthday to his son
Iroh confesses he wished he could have helped Lu Ten, as he helped others along his way, and that Lu Ten's death helped him become a better person
Iroh starts singing "Leaves from the Vine," his song for the crying boy, but is broken up by tears as he mourns Lu Ten's death
The episode ends with a dedication to Mako Iwamatsu, Iroh's voice actor
Mako Iwamatsu passes away on July 21, 2006, after a long battle with esophageal cancer
Aang flies over Ba Sing Se and lands at a small zoo, looking for Appa
Aang looks around and sees a wide variety of miserable animals in small cages
Animals are partially starved in their cages
Zookeeper explains to Aang that the zoo is no longer receiving funding from the Dai Li because it is not popular with children
Zoo is filthy, with cages containing piles of feces
Zookeeper expresses desire to let the animals run wild in open spaces
Aang suggests moving the animals to an open area just outside the city
The animals prove difficult to control and run wild over the city, terrorizing the citizens
Hog monkeys destroy a shop
Various animals attack the citizens
The cabbage merchant has his cabbages eaten by a rabaroo
Aang tries to restore order
Aang pulls out his bison whistle and blows a huge burst of air through it using airbending
The creatures are attracted by the sound of the whistle
Aang hops on an air scooter
The animals run after Aang
The zookeeper frantically tries to get the guards to open the gate
The guards refuse to open the gate until they see the oncoming stampede
The guards open the gates
Aang reaches the other side and hops on his air scooter again
Aang uses earthbending to create a wall around the animals
Aang continues to earthbend paths, secluded areas, and habitat accessories
Children and their families flock to the new zoo
The zookeeper thanks Aang for his help and tells him he should have a job with animals
Domestic cats, dogs, and cat-dogs are also inside the animal pens, having followed the sound of the whistle
The zookeeper thanks Aang again but advises him to stick to saving people rather than animals
Sokka walks aimlessly through the streets, his boomerang now a toy
Sokka stumbles across the Five-Seven-Five Society, a haiku class full of beautiful girls
Sokka peeks through the window, enjoying the show dreamily
Sokka is shoved from behind by an ostrich horse and falls in through the window
Sokka explains the incident to the girls, accidentally rhyming in haiku
Madame Macmu-Ling becomes upset with the intrusion and giggling of the class
Madame Macmu-Ling presents the rules of haiku to Sokka in a formal tone
Sokka gets into a poetry duel with Madame Macmu-Ling
The girls in the class giggle after each of Sokka’s verses
Sokka comically wins several bouts in the poetry duel
Sokka mistakenly adds an extra syllable to the final line of his encore
The class becomes silent and hard-faced
Sokka counts the syllables and realizes his mistake
A guard kicks Sokka out of the class and back onto the street
Sokka changes his mind about liking poetry
Zuko worries that a young girl has deduced he and Iroh are from the Fire Nation
Zuko informs Iroh of his suspicions
Iroh comments that the girl appeared quite often around their establishment
Iroh infers that the girl, Jin, simply has a crush on Zuko
Jin comes up to the counter and asks Zuko out after paying for her tea
Iroh quickly accepts on Zuko's behalf
Zuko and Jin meet after sundown outside the shop
Zuko leaves the shop, dressed in nice clothes and with slicked back hair styled by Iroh
Iroh styles Zuko's hair, taking ten minutes according to Zuko
Jin messes up Zuko's hair before they leave
Zuko and Jin go out to eat
Jin asks Zuko how he feels about the city and his hobbies
Zuko replies with simple one word answers
The waiter asks if Zuko and his girlfriend would care for dessert
Zuko loudly replies that Jin is not his girlfriend
Zuko feels nervous and makes a few missteps during the date
Jin takes Zuko's nervousness in stride
Zuko awkwardly comments on Jin's appetite
Jin asks Zuko about his life
Zuko makes up a story about being in a traveling circus with his uncle
Jin asks Zuko what he performed as, then stops him to guess herself
Jin guesses "juggling"
Zuko goes along with Jin's guess
Jin encourages Zuko to juggle
Zuko attempts to juggle and gets himself covered in food, claiming lack of practice
Jin pulls Zuko to the Firelight Fountain, one of her favorite parts of the city
At night, the fountain is usually lit by lanterns, causing the water to sparkle
When they arrive, the lanterns are all dark and unlit
Zuko senses Jin’s disappointment
Zuko tells Jin to close her eyes
Zuko makes sure no one else is around to watch
Zuko quietly lights all the lanterns with his firebending
Jin and Zuko stare into the fountain
Jin reaches out and holds Zuko’s hand
Jin slyly tries to give Zuko a kiss
Zuko holds up a coupon for a free tea between them and gives it to Jin
Jin, nonplussed, tells Zuko to close his eyes so she can present her gift
Jin kisses Zuko lightly and briefly
Zuko gives Jin a brief kiss in return, but quickly breaks away and leaves
Jin asks Zuko the reason
Zuko says "it's complicated" and heads back to the teahouse
Iroh asks Zuko about the night
Zuko answers by slamming the doors to his room
Zuko opens the door slightly afterward and tells Iroh it was nice
Zuko gently slides the door closed
Iroh smiles
Momo dreams that he and Appa are eating moon peaches from a tree so high it reaches over the clouds
Momo is awoken by a clap of thunder
Momo instinctively hides in Sokka's bag
Momo emerges from the bag and finds a tuft of Appa's hair on his head
Momo smells the fur and realizes it is Appa's
Momo sees an Appa-like shadow on the ground
Momo wraps the fur around his wrist and takes off after the shadow
Momo finds only a lone cloud
Momo has a similar sighting that turns out to be a cherry tree
Momo decides to continue searching the city for Appa
Momo draws the attention of a trio of pygmy pumas
The pygmy pumas see Momo as a potential meal
Momo tries to escape from the pygmy pumas
The pygmy pumas work together to try to bring Momo down
The pygmy pumas momentarily trap Momo in a box
Momo seizes an opportunity to escape from the box
Momo lands among a crowd of people watching a street artist with dancing monkeys
The street artist seizes Momo, outfits him with a hat, and places him with the monkeys
The street artist mistakes Momo for a monkey due to his shortsightedness
The three cats chase Momo out of the performers' circle and pin him to the ground
An animal control officer suddenly captures Momo and the three cats
The four animals are brought to a butcher
The man who captured the animals begins haggling with the owner
Momo frees himself by using his opposable thumbs to remove the skull pin that locks his cage
Momo starts to run off
Momo sees the three mournful pumas and feels sympathetic
Momo frees the three pumas from their cages
The men find the cages empty
All four animals run off on the rooftops
The four animals sit on a rooftop snuggling
One of the pumas removes Appa's fur from Momo's wrist
The three pumas run off down an alley, making sure Momo is following
The cats stop and place the fur in a large three-toed footprint left in exposed earth
Momo notices the print as he lands in it
The pumas sit together nearby
Momo curls on top of the tuft of fur and falls asleep, thinking of his beloved companion
Rain starts to fall

Appa%27s_Lost_Days.action: Appa is captured by the sandbenders outside of Wan Shi Tong's Library
Appa has adventures after being captured
Appa reaches Ba Sing Se
Sandbenders attack Appa with intent of capturing him
Sandbenders capture Appa
Toph's seismic sense is hampered by the sand
Toph is unable to help due to being divided by the need to prevent the library from sinking
The sandbenders drag Appa away
Ghashiun orders Appa's saddle to be ransacked for valuables
The sandbenders search Appa's saddle but find nothing more valuable than Sokka's club
Appa tries to fight back, destroying one of the sand-sailers
Ghashiun is unfazed because the destroyed sailer was stolen from the Hami Tribe
Ghashiun tosses the supplies from the saddle away
Ghashiun continues his plan to sell Appa to beetle-headed merchants
The merchants decide to buy Appa, giving the sandbenders a small chest of money
The merchants begin dragging Appa into position to secure him onto a caged sled
Appa is fastened into the sled
Aang blows his whistle from afar during his search for Appa
Appa hears the whistle and begins to struggle, lifting the entire sled off the ground
The merchants shoot Appa with shirshu-spit darts fired from a blowgun
The darts pierce Appa's foot and he falls quickly to the ground
Appa sees the giant cloud created by a frustrated Aang before falling unconscious
The merchants discuss selling Appa for parts
Appa wakes up to find himself inside a small cage
Appa notices several depressed-looking animals next to him
A firebender talks to Appa and seems sympathetic
The firebender reveals himself as an animal tamer who intends to break Appa to his will
The animal tamer feeds the other animals except for Appa
The animal tamer lets out a lion vulture and demonstrates how Appa will earn his food by sending the lion vulture through hoops
The animal tamer performs tricks with his back to Appa
Appa slyly sucks in air, pulling cabbages from the cart into his mouth
The animal tamer turns around and becomes furious
The animal tamer threatens Appa with fire
Appa unsuccessfully attempts to pull a bale of hay closer to him to eat
a small boy disobeys his father's orders not to move
the boy helps Appa by moving the hay close enough for him to eat
the boy stays with Appa, only leaving when the animal tamer comes back
the animal tamer speaks to Shuzumu, insisting that Appa is not prepared to perform and demands more time to train him
Shuzumu tells the tamer that a circus is "the home of fear and danger" and that Appa will perform that night
the tamer warns Appa that if he does not behave tonight, he will regret it
the boy watches the tamer warn Appa
Appa is introduced as "The Wind Buffalo" and dressed in a garish costume and makeup
The crowd laughs at the sight of Appa
The animal tamer attempts to get Appa to fly by commanding, "Up"
Appa spins around
The crowd laughs more
The animal tamer is infuriated and strikes Appa with a whip of flame
Appa flies up, goes through a burning hoop, stands on a high platform, and flies through another hoop
Appa's costume catches on fire
Appa slaps the hoop to the ground, striking the animal tamer on the head and knocking him down
The crowd laughs again
Appa lands and sees the boy who helped him earlier, and is reminded of Aang
The boy urges Appa to escape
Appa begins to flee
The animal tamer rises and sends a fire blast at Appa
The animal tamer attempts to strike Appa with a fire whip
Appa slips through an airhole at the top of the tent and escapes, leaving behind his costume
The small boy watches and smiles at Appa's escape
Appa flies back to the spot where the library was, only to find it completely buried
Appa tries to dig out the buried library
Appa decides it is a futile effort and flies off
Appa lands to find food
Appa starts to move toward a giant purple egg
a gilacorn runs off with the egg held above its head just as Appa is about to eat it
Appa turns over and goes to eat a cactus, scaring away a jackalope in the process
Appa eats the whole cactus and gets a mouth full of thorns
Appa lifts off again and finds himself in the buzzard wasp nest
Appa quickly earns the ire of the buzzard wasps
Appa manages to fight off the buzzard wasps
Appa collapses in the sand, trying to get at the yellow, sap-like, gooey substance on his feet
Appa wanders into a farm
Appa goes into an open barn and eats some hay and water
Appa collapses, exhausted, on the ground
Appa dreams of his past
An airbending nun prepares several children to bond with young flying bison at the Eastern Air Temple
The nun gives each child an apple and tells them to choose a bison carefully
Aang finds Appa and offers him the fruit
Appa devours the fruit, pushes Aang over, and licks his face
Aang laughs and says they will be together forever
The scene switches to Aang, sleeping during "The Serpent's Pass," having the same dream as Appa
Appa is startled awake by a farmer attacking him with a pitchfork
The farmer attacks Appa with a pitchfork, frightened of Appa's size
The farmer's wife, drawn by his cries, comes to the barn with a torch
Appa, frightened of fire, rears up and backs into the barn, damaging the wall
Appa flies out straight through the roof and flies off
Appa's path takes him directly over the Serpent's Pass
Iroh sees Appa in the sky
Zuko, awakened by Iroh's reaction, asks what is going on
Iroh decides not to tell Zuko about Appa
Appa continues to fly and lands in the middle of a forest
Appa lands in front of an overgrown doorway
Appa tries to stand up
a boar-q-pine charges out of the passage and tackles Appa
Appa and the boar-q-pine tumble down the hillside
Appa is pricked with several quills
Appa fights off the boar-q-pine
Appa claims the ruin as his own
The Kyoshi Warriors pick berries
One Kyoshi Warrior notices the damage caused by the fight and several clumps of white fur
Suki recognizes the fur as Appa's
Suki finds Appa still sleeping
Appa is frightened of people and is reluctant to let Suki get closer, despite her gift of fruit
Suki gathers the rest of the warriors
Suki calms Appa down by telling him she wants to help him and return him to Aang
The Kyoshi Warriors remove the quills left from the boar, remove Appa's shackles, and clean his fur
A blast of blue flames knocks a tree over
Azula, Mai, and Ty Lee arrive on their mongoose lizards, still pursuing the Avatar
The Kyoshi Warriors defend Appa
Azula and her friends gain the upper hand against the Warriors
Appa turns back to help
Suki grabs a burning branch and uses it to scare Appa away, convincing him to find Aang
Suki assures Appa that they would be okay
Suki turns to engage Azula in combat
Appa flies far away, passing over a group of Water Tribe ships
Hakoda sees Appa flying above the ships
Appa makes his way back to the Eastern Air Temple
Appa remembers his early days, playing with his siblings as a calf
Appa walks around the temple
Appa stumbles upon an old man he thinks is Aang
Appa tackles and licks the old man happily
Appa realizes the man is not Aang, backs away, and growls
The old man introduces himself as Guru Pathik and understands Appa’s confusion
Guru Pathik lies back, looking at Appa occasionally and receiving a growl in return
Night settles in
Appa falls asleep
Guru Pathik reads Appa’s energy, remarking on his love, trust, and fear
Guru Pathik tells of his vision of Aang and Appa years prior and his decision to wait at the temple
Guru Pathik leaves Appa to rest, advising him to let the clouds in his mind part and be at peace
Appa finds some fruit which the guru left for him to eat
Appa finds the guru meditating with a group of animals surrounding him
Appa decides to leave the guru alone
Appa flies around a bit
Appa comes to rest next to the guru at the top of the temple
The guru gives Appa a note, which he ties to Appa's horn
The guru tells Appa about how much his energy and Aang's are intertwined
The guru puts his hand on Appa's forehead and tells him where to find Aang
Appa flies off, heading to Ba Sing Se
Appa flies into Ba Sing Se
Two Dai Li agents spy from the rooftops
Appa continues to fly around the city
Appa hears something
Three pygmy pumas watch Appa
Appa lands in the middle of the city
Appa sees a man blowing a whistle in the shadows
The man is revealed to be Long Feng
Long Feng uses earthbending to capture Appa underground
Long Feng leaves only a single footprint in the mud
The pygmy pumas later lead Momo to the footprint

Lake_Laogai_(episode).action: The episode begins at Team Avatar's house in the Upper Ring of Ba Sing Se
Sokka attempts to draw pictures of Appa for posters
Aang and Katara burst in with a professionally-made poster featuring a better picture of Appa
Katara nearly bursts out laughing at Sokka's crude drawings
Sokka's spirits are briefly lifted when Toph sarcastically remarks that his drawing looks just like Appa to her
Toph sarcastically remarks that Sokka's drawing looks just like Appa to her
Sokka quickly remembers that Toph cannot see it
The group plans on spreading the flyers all over the city, hoping for information
Iroh's tea-making skills earn him renown throughout the city
Quon offers Iroh his own tea shop in the Upper Ring of the city
Pao is dismayed by Quon's offer to Iroh
Iroh is delighted by the opportunity to have his own tea shop
Zuko is much less enthusiastic about the tea shop
Zuko goes outside to sulk
One of Aang's flyers falls into Zuko's hand
Zuko is faced with another opportunity to capture Aang
Aang returns home, impatient to see if the flyers have had any success
Katara tells Aang to be patient
There is a knock on the door
Aang excitedly thinks it is someone with information on Appa
Joo Dee appears at the door
Joo Dee explains her disappearance as a vacation to Lake Laogai
Joo Dee informs the group that dropping flyers without clearance is against the rules and tells them to cease all activities
Aang angrily declares they do not care about the rules and will not ask for permission
Aang screams that they will find Appa on their own and tells Joo Dee to stay out of their way, then slams the door in her face
Sokka remarks that Aang's outburst will cause trouble later
Aang, still furious, dismisses Sokka's concern and announces they will find Appa by any means necessary
Toph expresses her joy at breaking the rules by destroying the corner wall of their house with earthbending
The group heads out
Two Dai Li agents secretly watch their every move
Long Feng is disappointed in Joo Dee's second failure to control the Avatar and his friends
Joo Dee worries that the group no longer trusts her
Long Feng says, "The Earth King has invited you to Lake Laogai"
Joo Dee's pupils dilate and she calmly accepts the invitation
Long Feng tells Joo Dee to await further orders
Joo Dee leaves
Long Feng reveals he is worried the Avatar may disrupt his control over the city and the king
A Dai Li agent asks if they should take care of the Avatar
Long Feng says no, stating a direct confrontation would be too risky
Long Feng intends to see if their "problem" can still be dealt with quietly
Iroh excitedly mulls over names for his new tea shop
Zuko informs Iroh that the Avatar is in the city and searching for his bison
Iroh warns Zuko that pursuing the Avatar in Ba Sing Se could jeopardize their attempts at rebuilding their lives
Zuko remains set on reclaiming his believed destiny
Iroh tells Zuko to take time to think about what his destiny means
The gang sets up more posters in the city
Sokka suggests the group split up to cover more ground and that Toph come with him
Toph calls Sokka out for thinking she can't set up posters by herself
Toph grabs a poster and rapidly glues it to a wall face-side down
Toph realizes the poster is upside down and agrees to go with Sokka
Jet approaches Katara and offers to help
Katara becomes furious with Jet and attacks him
Jet deflects Katara's attacks and drops his hooked swords to prove his honesty
The others arrive, drawn by the noise, and find Jet pinned to the wall
Toph reads Jet's heartbeat and breathing patterns and pronounces he is telling the truth
Katara remains distrustful but agrees to let Jet help in the search
Jet leads the group to a large empty warehouse where he says Appa may have been held
Toph finds a tuft of Appa's fur, confirming Jet's claim
An old man enters, glad he no longer has to clean up after Appa's droppings
The old man says Appa was sent to Whaletail Island, bought by a wealthy noble for a zoo
Aang immediately wants to set off for Whaletail Island
Jet offers to come along
Katara forcefully objects but is overruled
The group is sidetracked when Smellerbee and Longshot come across them
Katara turns on Jet, as he had claimed to have left his gang behind
Jet insists he has been living a peaceful life in the city
Smellerbee says Jet was arrested by the Dai Li a few weeks beforehand
Toph says both Jet and Smellerbee are telling the truth, even though their stories do not match
Sokka realizes Toph can tell neither of them is lying because they both think they are telling the truth
Jet has been brainwashed to get the Avatar and his friends to leave Ba Sing Se without talking to the Earth King about the War, as part of Long Feng's plan
A Dai Li agent walks alone down a street at night
The Blue Spirit (Zuko in disguise) bumps the Dai Li agent aside
The Dai Li agent chases the Blue Spirit down an alley
The Dai Li agent attacks the Blue Spirit, knocking the head off a decoy
Zuko quickly captures the Dai Li agent
Zuko demands that the agent tells him what he needs to know
Zuko implies he will cut off the agent's head if denied
The group, Longshot, and Smellerbee hold Jet
Katara guesses that Jet and the old man were used to try to lead them away from the city on a false lead
Aang realizes that Appa may be held in the same place they brainwashed Jet
Aang tries to get Jet to remember where he was taken
Jet tries to remember the night his home was attacked, but it proves too painful
Katara uses her healing to try to help Jet remember
Jet remembers that he was taken to a secret facility beneath the water of a lake
Sokka remembers what Joo Dee had said about her "vacation" to Lake Laogai
Sokka jogs more of Jet's memory
The base is revealed to be under Lake Laogai
The group makes their way to the lake the next day
Toph quickly finds and uncovers an underwater tunnel
The group goes inside the tunnel, quietly making their way through the dark corridors
In one room, a group of women is being "conditioned" to be "Joo Dee"s
Jet leads the group to a cell he thinks will be big enough to hold Appa
Jet opens the cell
Inside his cell, Appa gets up as his cell door is opened
The Blue Spirit, not Jet, opens Appa's cell door
The gang are confronted by Long Feng and several Dai Li agents after entering another cell
A battle quickly breaks out
Long Feng escapes the room
Aang and Jet pursue Long Feng
Long Feng tries to bargain with Aang one last time
Aang and Jet refuse Long Feng's bargain
Long Feng extends the same invitation to Jet that he did to Joo Dee
Jet's pupils dilate
Jet attacks Aang
Zuko, disguised as the Blue Spirit, is confronted by Iroh in Appa's cell
Iroh asks who could be behind the mask, implying he knows it's Zuko
Iroh scolds Zuko for not planning ahead, referencing the North Pole incident
Iroh pleads with Zuko to look inside himself and find his own destiny, not the one forced by his father
Zuko throws his mask and swords to the ground in anger
Aang tries to get Jet to snap out of his trance while avoiding Jet's attacks
Aang reminds Jet of his past as a Freedom Fighter
Jet recovers from his trance
Long Feng tells Jet to finish Aang
Jet hurls one of his hooked swords at Long Feng's head
Long Feng dodges and retaliates with a crushing blow
Long Feng escapes as the others enter the chamber
Katara tries to heal Jet and realizes how badly he was injured
Smellerbee asks the group to go on ahead, saying they will be fine
Katara tells them they cannot just leave Jet and Smellerbee there
Longshot tells Aang and his group to go find Appa and leave Jet to Smellerbee and himself
Jet tries to assure Katara that he will be all right
Toph tells Sokka that Jet was lying
Smellerbee starts crying
Longshot is shown notching an arrow
They reach Appa's cell and find the chains broken and Appa gone
They think the Dai Li have taken Appa
They rush back to the surface, trying to escape on foot
Long Feng and Dai Li agents surround them and create two earth walls to prevent escape
Momo takes off into the sky
Appa comes down and breaks through the two earth walls, opening an escape route
Toph and Aang take out the remaining Dai Li on the middle wall and fling them into the lake
Long Feng tries to attack Appa
Appa catches Long Feng's foot in his mouth and tosses him into the lake, skipping across the surface
Aang and the others joyfully reunite with Appa
They make their escape from the island on Appa
Katara stares into the water below with teary eyes
Aang senses Katara's pain and comforts her, including her in a group hug
They sail off for a nearby island, supporting one another
Zuko and Iroh leave the base, barely missing the fighting
Zuko acknowledges his uncle's words
Zuko drops the Blue Spirit mask into the water
Zuko stands looking at the mask as it sinks into the darkness
The Blue Spirit mask sinks into the darkness

The_Earth_King.action: Aang and his friends relocate to a small island after escaping from Lake Laogai
The group recovers and discusses what to do next
Katara wishes to leave Ba Sing Se since they have found Appa
Toph wishes to leave Ba Sing Se since they have found Appa
Sokka wants to stay to inform the Earth King of the conspiracy and the approaching solar eclipse
Sokka argues that informing the Earth King was their reason for heading to Ba Sing Se
Aang agrees with Sokka
The group spots boats likely to be commandeered by Dai Li
The others agree to remain in Ba Sing Se
All four ride bareback on Appa to Ba Sing Se's Upper Ring
The group breaks into the palace, tearing through the defense of Royal Earthbender Guards
Iroh and Zuko arrive at their apartment after escaping from Lake Laogai
Iroh happily reassures Zuko that he did the right thing by letting the Avatar's bison go free
Zuko dazedly states that he does not feel right
Zuko suddenly collapses on the floor
Iroh, shocked and worried, rushes to Zuko's aid
