Aang and his friends reach the Earth King's throne room
They encounter Long Feng and numerous Dai Li agents standing before the Earth King as a last defense
Aang and his friends attempt to inform the Earth King about the Hundred Year War and Long Feng's conspiracy
The Earth King is oblivious to the war and trusts his chief adviser, making it difficult for Aang and his friends to convince him
Aang and his friends drop their weapons to gain the emperor's trust
Long Feng attempts to arrest Aang and his friends
The Earth King learns that Aang is the Avatar
Bosco, the Earth King's pet bear, acts friendly toward Aang
The Earth King decides to hear Aang and the others out after seeing Bosco's reaction
Aang explains to the Earth King about the war outside Ba Sing Se's walls, Long Feng and the Dai Li's conspiracy, the imprisonment of Appa, and the brainwashing of Jet and others
Long Feng denies all the charges brought against him
Long Feng claims he has never encountered a flying bison
The Earth King ponders over the allegations
Long Feng whispers to the Earth King that Aang and his friends are part of an "anarchist cell" attempting to overthrow him
The Dai Li bind Aang and his friends with earthbending
The Earth King orders that the group be detained
Sokka has an epiphany and, with Aang's airbending, exposes a bite mark on Long Feng from Appa
Aang uses airbending to help expose the bite mark
Long Feng claims the bite mark is a birth mark
Aang brings Appa in to compare the mark to the bison's teeth
The Earth King, though unconvinced of the larger conspiracy, agrees to investigate further
Long Feng frowns and shuffles off with his Dai Li agents
Iroh watches over Zuko, whose condition appears to be worsening
Zuko is feverish and incredibly thirsty
Team Avatar and the Earth King ride the monorail to Lake Laogai
The Earth King states that he has lived in isolation and has never been outside the palace before
Team Avatar and the Earth King arrive at Lake Laogai
They find that the entrance to the subterranean facility has collapsed or been destroyed to prevent further investigation
They decide to show the Earth King the Outer Wall, which was earlier penetrated by the Fire Nation's drill
The Earth King refuses and decides to return to the palace
Aang convinces the Earth King to continue the investigation by letting him ride on Appa
Zuko dreams he is the Fire Lord with a hairstyle similar to Ozai's and no scars
Two dragons, one blue and one red, coil around Zuko's throne and whisper advice to him
The blue dragon, in Azula's voice, tries to persuade Fire Lord Zuko to "rest"
The red dragon, in Iroh's voice, tells Zuko not to listen to the blue dragon and to "get out of there"
The scene crumbles around Zuko
The blue dragon attacks Zuko
The scene fades to darkness as Zuko sees an image of Ursa calling out for help
The Earth King arrives at the Outer Wall and is shocked to see the remains of the Fire Nation's drill
Long Feng attempts to pass the drill off as part of a construction project
Katara asks why there is a fire insignia on the drill
Long Feng claims the drill was imported
The Earth King is unconvinced and orders Long Feng to be arrested
The two Dai Li agents share a conspiratorial glance and bind Long Feng's arms with metal manacles
The group returns to the palace
The Earth King thanks them for opening his eyes and admits he felt like a fool for not knowing about the war
After persuasion, the Earth King agrees to support Sokka's plan to invade the Fire Nation on the day of the solar eclipse
Sokka dubs the day of the invasion "the Day of Black Sun," when the Fire Nation will be helpless
The Council of Five raid Long Feng's office
The Council of Five discover files about every person in Ba Sing Se, including Aang and his friends
The Council of Five find the note from Guru Pathik that was attached to Appa's horn, informing Aang he can teach him how to control the Avatar State
The Council of Five find a letter from Toph's mother, saying she is in the city and wants to meet her daughter
The Council of Five find an intelligence report giving the location of Southern Water Tribe warriors led by Hakoda
Iroh informs Zuko that he is going through a spiritual metamorphosis because releasing Appa went against his self-image
Iroh tells Zuko that the transformation will not be pleasant
Iroh assures Zuko that he will emerge as the "beautiful prince he was always meant to be"
Aang and his friends try to decide what actions to take after receiving news
Katara suggests, albeit reluctantly, that the group split up
Aang initially objects to splitting up
Aang and the others agree to split up, finding no other reasonable course
Sokka sadly states he will stay in the palace to guide the Earth King
Katara quickly cuts Sokka off, stating she will stay with the Earth King instead
Katara recognizes Sokka's desire to meet their father is greater than her own
Aang decides to take Sokka to where Hakoda is stationed before proceeding to the Eastern Air Temple on Appa
Toph decides to go meet her mother
Katara decides to stay behind to assist the Earth King
Aang and Sokka prepare for their journey
Aang attempts to tell Katara his true feelings
Sokka interrupts Aang at the most untimely moment
The Earth King bids farewell to Aang and Sokka
The Earth King receives word that a group of Kyoshi Warriors will be arriving
Sokka realizes the group must include Suki and falls off Appa in excitement
Sokka quickly recovers and vouches that the Kyoshi Warriors are strong and trustworthy allies
The Earth King responds that he will welcome the Kyoshi Warriors with open arms
Katara hugs Aang and kisses him on the cheek in farewell, causing Aang to blush
Toph expresses a rare moment of compassion for her friends
Aang, Katara, Sokka, and Toph engage in a group hug, with Sokka reluctantly participating
The boys take off
Zuko awakens from his feverish nightmares
Zuko sees that Iroh is asleep
Zuko goes to the bathroom to wash his face
Zuko looks up at the mirror
Zuko is shocked to see himself without his scar, instead with a shaved head and blue arrow tattoos like an airbender, resembling Aang
Zuko awakens in reality, realizing it was a nightmare
Zuko lets out a loud scream
Zuko touches his scar, reassuring himself he is actually awake
Aang and Sokka travel over the plains surrounding Ba Sing Se on Appa, feeling optimistic
Aang and Sokka travel over the plains surrounding Ba Sing Se on Appa, feeling optimistic
A Dai Li agent delivers Long Feng his meal in prison and reassures him of the Dai Li's loyalty
Toph goes to meet her mother in an Upper Ring building
Toph enters the empty building, suspects trickery too late, and is captured in a metal coffin
Master Yu and Xin Fu emerge from the shadows and prepare to return Toph to the Beifong family for a reward
The Kyoshi Warriors arrive before the Earth King, who formally welcomes them
Azula, Mai, and Ty Lee are revealed to be disguised as the Kyoshi Warriors

The_Guru.action: Iroh prepares a cauldron of jook in the apartment
Zuko enters the room and comments on the smell of the soup
Iroh warns Zuko that he probably would not like the soup
Zuko smells a ladle-full and says he would love to have some
Iroh observes Zuko's emotional change since recovering from his fever
Zuko smiles and announces optimistically that it is a new day, Iroh's tea shop is opening, they have a wonderful new apartment, and things are looking up
Aang and Sokka land at the mouth of Chameleon Bay, the alleged location of Hakoda and the Southern Water Tribe warriors
Sokka grows very nervous
Aang calms Sokka down
Sokka questions Aang about his feelings regarding meeting Guru Pathik to master the Avatar State
Aang smiles and replies that he is not nervous
Aang takes off on Appa, shouting back to Sokka that they will reunite in a week
Sokka walks into the base
warriors of the Southern Water Tribe greet Sokka with warm and welcome arms
warriors exchange brief greetings with Sokka
warriors guide Sokka to a large tent
Sokka enters the tent
several more warriors, including Bato and Hakoda, study a map inside the tent
Hakoda looks up and rises to embrace Sokka happily
The Earth King entertains Azula, Mai, and Ty Lee, who are disguised as Kyoshi Warriors
The Earth King tells Azula, Mai, and Ty Lee about Long Feng's manipulation of the city
Azula expresses false interest in the story
The Earth King reveals the plans for the invasion of the Fire Nation during the Day of Black Sun
Azula grows surprised but suppresses her shock, expressing only interest in the plan
The Council of Five reviews the invasion plan with Katara, who is accompanied by Momo
General How explains that General Fong's fortress will be the staging point of the attack and demonstrates by earthbending stone pieces across a world map
Momo darts across the board and knocks the pillars over
Katara laughs at Momo's actions and attempts to joke with the five generals
The five generals are not amused
General How asks Katara to deliver a scroll detailing the invasion plans for the Earth King's seal
Aang arrives at the Eastern Air Temple and finds Guru Pathik meditating atop a tower
Guru Pathik introduces himself as a spiritual brother to the Air Nomads and a close friend of Monk Gyatso
Aang asks Guru Pathik how he plans on teaching him to gain control of the Avatar State
Guru Pathik replies that Aang must first bring balance to himself before he can bring balance to the world
Guru Pathik asks Aang to drink a liquid
Aang drinks the liquid
Aang spits out the liquid because it is a strange combination of onion and banana juice
An ostrich horse pulls a cart carrying Master Yu, Xin Fu, and Toph, who is detained within a huge metal box
Master Yu and Xin Fu argue about which road leads to the Beifong estate
Toph commands Master Yu and Xin Fu to stop arguing and let her out to go to the bathroom
Master Yu prepares to release Toph
Xin Fu sharply pulls Master Yu back, noticing the obvious trick
Toph pounds against the cage, infuriating Xin Fu
Xin Fu tells Toph she cannot bend metal, no matter how good an earthbender she is
Toph begins to focus her hand on the cage
Azula, Mai, and Ty Lee remove their disguises in Ba Sing Se
Azula presents an idea to Mai and Ty Lee
Azula states they have the perfect opportunity to conquer the Earth Kingdom from the inside
Azula proposes they stage a coup to overthrow the Earth King
Azula establishes that controlling the Dai Li is the key to accomplishing their goal
Guru Pathik begins Aang's instruction of the chakras at the Eastern Air Temple
Pathik tells Aang he must open the seven chakras to master the Avatar State
Pathik determines that Aang has no knowledge of chakras
Pathik provides a visual representation of chakras to Aang
Pathik uses a pond clogged with algae to explain the concept of chakras
Pathik explains that removing clogs between pools allows water to flow, just as opening chakras allows energy to flow
Pathik explains there are seven chakras, each with a purpose and a different blockage, and that opening them all causes an intense sensation
Pathik warns Aang that the process of opening chakras cannot be halted once begun
Aang accepts the hazard of opening the chakras
Aang and Pathik begin the process, traveling to different areas in the Eastern Air Temple and learning about the first six chakras
Aang has flashbacks and opens each chakra fairly easily
Aang meditates and sees his fears laid out before him
The Blue Spirit charges at Aang with dual swords in his vision
Katara is dragged underground by General Fong in Aang's vision
Sozin's Comet arrives in Aang's vision
Aang sees himself in the Avatar State destroying his surroundings
Aang sees himself surrounded by fire, sitting before a man who appears to be Fire Lord Ozai
Aang clears his thoughts and the images disappear
Aang breathes a sigh of relief as he comes out of meditation
Guru Pathik congratulates Aang and announces that the first chakra has been opened
Guru Pathik explains the water chakra and asks Aang what he blames himself for
Aang reminisces about fleeing the Southern Air Temple upon learning he was the Avatar
Aang remembers hurting Earth Kingdom soldiers at General Fong's fortress while in the Avatar State
Guru Pathik tells Aang to accept these things and urges him to forgive himself
Aang forgives himself and successfully opens the second chakra
Sokka, his father, and the rest of the warriors prepare tangle mines at Chameleon Bay
Hakoda explains that tangle mines are comprised of a buoyant casing around an explosive mine, filled with skunk fish and seaweed
Upon detonation, the seaweed entangles itself in the propellers of Fire Nation ships, immobilizing them
The stench of the skunk fish forces the soldiers aboard to abandon ship
Hakoda jokes that he has dubbed the plan "The Stink and Sink"
Sokka explodes with laughter at Hakoda's joke
Bato remarks dryly on the similarities between father and son
a scouting party returns and informs the warriors of the presence of four Fire Nation ships
Hakoda quickly tells Bato to finish preparing the mines
Hakoda tells the rest of the warriors to prepare for battle
the other warriors prepare for battle
Sokka hesitates as the other warriors prepare for battle
Sokka asks Hakoda what he should do
Hakoda, in a slightly irritated yet joking tone, repeats that he wants the rest of the men to get ready for battle
Sokka smiles at his father's confirmation of his coming-of-age
Sokka quickly prepares himself for the encounter ahead
The Jasmine Dragon opens with Iroh and Zuko standing proudly as customers arrive at the tea shop
Iroh expresses disbelief at having accomplished his dream of running his own shop
Iroh advises Zuko to follow his own dreams
Zuko congratulates Iroh
Iroh expresses pride in Zuko for choosing to be with him
Zuko enthusiastically hugs Iroh
Zuko runs off to start preparing tea
Iroh happily joins Zuko
Mai and Ty Lee, disguised as Kyoshi Warriors, lounge on the front steps of the Earth King's palace
Mai complains about her outfit and the tasks involved in being the Earth King's audience
Ty Lee advises Mai to be patient and repeats Azula's plan aloud
Mai snaps at Ty Lee to be quiet, warning her not to reveal they are from the Fire Nation
A small group of Dai Li agents, spying from their hiding spots on pillars, quickly and stealthily crawl off
Azula emerges after the Dai Li agents leave and compliments her friends on their acting job
Azula notes that Long Feng will surely be informed of their plans now
Aang and Pathik arrive at the fire chakra, located in the stomach
Pathik explains the fire chakra deals with willpower and is blocked by shame
Pathik asks Aang what he is ashamed of and why he is disappointed in himself
Aang has a vision of when he first attempted firebending and severely burned Katara's hands
Aang tells Pathik he would never firebend again
Pathik tells Aang he must accept all aspects of his life to obtain balance
Pathik reminds Aang that as the Avatar, he is a firebender
Aang accepts his duty and successfully opens the third chakra
Pathik introduces the next chakra: air, located in the heart, dealing with love, blocked by grief
Aang lays his grief before him and sees all the Air Nomads eliminated by the Fire Nation, with Gyatso first among them
The Air Nomads fade away and become smoke
Pathik explains that love is a form of energy that swirls around people
Pathik states the Air Nomads' love for Aang still exists in his heart and is reborn as new love for Katara
Aang lets his pain flow away with his tears
Aang asks for more banana-onion juice
Pathik introduces the sound chakra, located in the throat, dealing with truth, blocked by lies
Aang remembers concealing his true identity from Sokka and Katara
Aang states he did not tell them he was the Avatar because he never wanted to be
Pathik tells Aang he cannot lie about his own nature and must accept his destiny as the Avatar
Aang accepts this idea with a sigh and a vision, successfully opening the sound chakra
Pathik congratulates Aang
Pathik begins to explain the light chakra: located in the forehead, dealing with insight, blocked by illusion
Pathik says the greatest illusion is the one of separation
Aang connects this concept with the four nations
Pathik tells Aang that everyone is part of one collective people, yet lives as if divided
Aang realizes Pathik is saying everyone is connected, making divisions meaningless
Pathik explains that even the separation of the four elements is an illusion
Pathik states that even metal is simply refined and purified earth
Toph uses her ability to sense the tiny fragments of earth within the metal cage
Toph learns the unique sub-skill of bending metal
Toph punches a huge dent through the cage doors twice
Toph escapes her cell
Katara and Momo decide to stop at the new tea house before going to see the Earth King
Katara enters the tea house
Katara hears and sees Zuko reciting an order for tea to Iroh in the back of the shop
Zuko recites an order for tea to Iroh
Katara gasps in surprise
Katara runs off to warn the Earth King
Pathik congratulates Aang and explains that with the final chakra's opening, Aang will be able to willfully enter and exit the Avatar State and control his actions within it
Pathik explains that the thought chakra, located at the crown of the head, deals with cosmic energy and is blocked by earthly attachment
Pathik asks Aang to meditate on what he is attached to
Images of Katara flood Aang's mind
Aang focuses on his attachment to Katara
Pathik instructs Aang to let his attachments go
Aang becomes angry, finding the idea of letting go of Katara ludicrous because he loves her
Aang questions why he should not allow himself to feel love for Katara, referencing Pathik's earlier statement that it was beneficial
Pathik narrows his eyes, remains calm, and states that Aang must let go of all earthly attachments, including Katara, to allow pure cosmic energy to enter him
Katara rushes into the palace
Katara tells Azula (whom she believes is Suki) that Zuko and Iroh have infiltrated the city
Katara urges Azula to inform the Earth King
Azula calmly replies that it will not be necessary
Katara realizes her mistake upon recognizing the golden hue of Azula’s eyes
Ty Lee hurtles into the air and disables Katara
Azula and her friends stand over Katara’s prone form
Azula remarks that it is time she and Zuko encounter each other again
Pathik tells Aang he must open all the chakras to master the Avatar State
Aang decides to try to open all the chakras
Aang visualizes Katara disappearing into space and himself being taken high above the planet
Aang walks along a purple stream of cosmic energy radiating from a gigantic image of himself in the Avatar State
The stream surrounds Aang in a sphere of energy radiating from its palms
Aang is surprised when an image of Katara being captured appears behind him
Aang abandons the Avatar State-induced figure and runs toward the image of Katara
The stream of cosmic energy dissipates, causing Aang to plummet to the Earth
Aang jolts awake from his meditation and says Katara is in trouble and he must go back to Ba Sing Se
Pathik tries to stop Aang, warning that the last chakra is blocked and he will not be able to trigger the Avatar State
Aang hesitates, but continues on without looking back or responding
Pathik is dismayed by Aang's decision
Master Yu and Xin Fu are startled by a tearing sound from the back of the wagon
Master Yu and Xin Fu go to investigate the sound
Master Yu and Xin Fu discover a massive hole in the back of Toph's prison, which is empty
Toph emerges from several feet away
Toph uses earthbending to thrust Xin Fu and Yu into the chamber
Toph basks in her triumph and states "I am the greatest earthbender in the world, don't you two dunderheads ever forget it."
Toph rapidly journeys toward Ba Sing Se by surfing along a self-made wave of earth
Xin Fu and Yu are left locked uncomfortably within the coffin
Xin Fu is frustrated at having to spend time with his companion
Yu claims that he has to go to the bathroom
Sokka and the rest of Hakoda's warriors finish preparation of their ships for attack
Sokka assures his father that he will make him proud and prove his skill as a warrior
Hakoda smiles and replies that he has always known his son to be a great warrior
Aang and Appa suddenly appear, cutting the heartfelt moment short
Sokka witnesses the worried expression on Aang's face and realizes the urgency of the situation
Sokka bids farewell to his father
A group of Dai Li agents escorts a restrained and complaining Azula to Long Feng's cell
Azula demands to know why she has been brought to the prison in the middle of the night
Long Feng smiles craftily and reveals his knowledge of Azula's true identity
Long Feng states he wants to make a deal with Azula to regain control of the city
Long Feng explains the Earth King's trust in Azula is key to the scheme's success
Long Feng promises the Avatar will be delivered to Azula in return for her help
Azula smirks before the proposal continues
Iroh and Zuko are surprised when a messenger delivers wonderful news
a messenger delivers news that they have been invited to serve tea to the Earth King
Zuko looks extremely content and happy for his uncle's good fortune
Zuko continues working in the shop
Azula is escorted back to her apartment by the Dai Li
Azula smirks

The_Crossroads_of_Destiny.action: Sokka and Aang hurry back to Ba Sing Se on Appa
Sokka and Aang spot Toph earthsurfing toward the Royal Palace
Toph earthsurfs toward the Royal Palace as quickly as she can
Sokka and Aang pick up Toph
Sokka and Aang fly to Ba Sing Se, trying to get there quickly to see whether Katara is in danger
Aang had a vision in the previous episode that Katara was in trouble
Aang lies to the others, saying he has mastered the Avatar State
Azula, having taken control of the Dai Li from Long Feng for the planned coup, works quickly to establish her leadership and instill fear and obedience
Azula tells the Dai Li that the Earth King will soon lock them away, just as he did to Long Feng
Azula inspires fear in the Dai Li agents
One of the Dai Li agents visits Long Feng
Long Feng asks the agent about Azula
The agent says Azula is both terrifying and inspirational
Iroh and Zuko arrive at the Earth King's palace by invitation to serve him tea
Iroh and Zuko wait for the king and become apprehensive
A group of Dai Li agents surrounds Iroh and Zuko
It is revealed to be a plot by Azula
Zuko takes a defensive stance
Iroh calmly asks Azula if she knows why he is called "Dragon of the West"
Azula waves Iroh off, saying she has no time for anecdotes
Iroh sips his tea and breathes fire, attacking the Dai Li agents
Iroh and Zuko begin their escape, dodging earth projectiles from the Dai Li
Iroh destroys a wall with a blast of lightning and leaps out of the palace
Iroh lands on the ground and urges Zuko to escape with him
Zuko decides to stop running and turns to fight Azula
Iroh is displeased with Zuko's decision but flees the scene
Zuko challenges Azula to an Agni Kai, which she rejects
Zuko attempts to attack Azula
Dai Li agents capture and arrest Zuko
The gang arrives at the palace and confronts the Earth King
The Earth King assures the gang that nothing is amiss and that Katara is fine
The Earth King explains that after the meeting with the Council of Five, Katara decided to go with the Kyoshi Warriors
Azula, Ty Lee, and Mai disguise themselves as the Kyoshi Warriors
The gang goes to their house and finds it empty
The gang realizes Katara is indeed in danger
The gang searches the empty apartment
Toph senses someone at the door
Toph identifies the person as an old friend, Iroh, and invites him in
Aang and Sokka are surprised by Iroh's arrival
Iroh tells them that Azula has captured Zuko and pleads for help to rescue him
Sokka grows defensive
Aang puts the whole image into perspective
Sokka changes his mind
The gang heads outside
The gang traps the captured Dai Li agent in a wedge of rocks
Sokka questions the Dai Li agent
The gang learns that Katara is being held in the Crystal Catacombs
Katara stands in a dark, crystal-lit cave
a light shines down from above
the entrance opens and Zuko is thrown into the cave
Zuko comes to
Katara takes a defensive stance
Katara is initially enraged to see Zuko
Katara tells Zuko that all he cares about is capturing the Avatar, and that spreading war and hatred is in his blood
Katara says Zuko does not know the pain of the Fire Nation killing her mother
Zuko apologizes
Zuko says that losing a mother is something they both have in common
Katara is slowly softened by Zuko’s words
The gang discovers a huge underground city outside the palace
The gang decides that Toph and Sokka should go warn the Earth King about the coup
The gang decides that Iroh and Aang should go rescue Katara and Zuko
Aang and Iroh travel down a tunnel to the prison
Aang mentions that Toph says Iroh gives great advice and tea
Aang tells Iroh about meeting Guru Pathik
Aang questions his choice and doubts his abilities without the Avatar State
Iroh suggests that Aang was wise to choose happiness and love over power and perfection
Sokka and Toph see General How outside the palace
Dai Li place General How under house arrest
Every general in the Council of Five is arrested
Sokka and Toph make it into the Throne Room to warn the Earth King about the coup
Ty Lee starts to flirt with Sokka upon seeing him
Sokka says he is involved with Suki
Ty Lee inadvertently gives away her Kyoshi Warrior disguise by asking "Who?"
Toph recognizes Ty Lee and throws her aside with rocks
Mai throws a hail of ornate shurikens at Toph
Toph blocks the shurikens with a wall of earth
Ty Lee begins to fight Sokka, commenting it is like they are dancing as he dodges her strikes
Sokka dodges Ty Lee's strikes
Azula holds the king at flamepoint
Sokka and Toph surrender
Ty Lee disables Sokka and Toph
Long Feng appears and immediately orders the Dai Li to arrest Azula
Dai Li do not comply with Long Feng's order
Azula explains to Long Feng that the Dai Li do not know who is more powerful and who will take the throne
Azula guesses that Long Feng is lower class by birth and gained power through work, conniving, and trickery
Azula declares she has "divine right to rule"
Azula confidently takes the Earth King's throne
Long Feng surrenders and bows to Azula, admitting she beat him at his own game
Azula smugly replies that Long Feng was not even a player
Katara apologizes to Zuko for yelling at him
Katara tells Zuko that for a long time, whenever she saw the face of the enemy, she saw his face
Zuko turns away and touches his scar, assuming she was referencing it
Katara offers to heal Zuko's scar using water from the Spirit Oasis
Zuko allows Katara to touch his scar
Aang and Iroh burst through the prison wall
Katara rushes over to Aang and wraps her arms around him
Iroh makes his way over to Zuko and embraces him
Zuko and Aang exchange glares
Zuko grows furious that Iroh was with the Avatar
Iroh sends Katara and Aang off to help Sokka and Toph
Katara glances despondently at Zuko
Katara rushes off with Aang
Iroh explains to Zuko that he is a changed man, stronger and wiser
Iroh tells Zuko it is time to choose the path of good
A mass of crystals springs up and immobilizes Iroh
Azula and Dai Li agents emerge from the shadows
Azula says she expected treachery from Iroh, but not from Zuko
Azula tries to persuade Zuko to join her by promising his father's love and honor
Iroh begs Zuko not to listen to Azula
Azula continues to plot and tells Zuko they will only win if he joins her
Iroh begs Zuko to look deep inside his heart and see what he truly wants
Azula sends the Dai Li away
Azula tells Zuko he can choose whatever he wants and walks away
Zuko is left to ponder his options
Azula catches up to Aang and Katara as they rush to save Toph and Sokka
Azula shoots a bolt of fire at Aang and Katara
A battle ensues between Aang, Katara, and Azula
Aang and Katara corner Azula
Aang and Katara corner Azula
Zuko shows up
Zuko begins fighting on Azula's side, to everyone's amazement
The two pairs switch off in the midst of battle
Zuko fights Katara, each using element whips on opposite sides of a river
Katara fights Zuko, each using element whips on opposite sides of a river
Katara expresses her anger and disappointment in Zuko, saying she thought he had changed
Zuko replies inscrutably, "I have changed."
Aang and Azula charge at one another
Aang and Azula charge at one another
Azula sends a blast of fire, sending Aang soaring into the air
Toph, Sokka, and the Earth King escape from their cell through Toph's metalbending
Ty Lee and Mai are tasked with guarding the king's pet bear at the palace
Mai lounges, bored, on the steps to the throne
Ty Lee amuses herself by trying to teach Bosco to walk on his front legs
Toph uses earthbending to bind Ty Lee's hands and feet to the floor
Toph and Sokka prepare to take on Mai
Mai, nonchalant, tells them to take the bear
The king reunites happily with his pet bear
Katara takes on both Zuko and Azula
Katara is knocked out when a blast from Azula slams her into a wall of crystal
Azula blasts Katara into a wall of crystal
Aang stands back up after being thrown earlier
Aang slams into the ground, creating a tidal wave of earth toward Azula and Zuko
Aang is stopped by the Dai Li
Dozens of Dai Li agents join the fight
Katara assumes the octopus stance, shaping water like octopus tentacles
Aang creates a quick crystal shelter with earthbending
Aang meditates and tries to release his attachment to Katara to enter the Avatar State
Aang rises into the air
Azula shoots Aang in the back with a lightning bolt, killing him
The Avatar Spirit falls from the plane of existence
Aang's attachment to Katara remains intact
Aang falls back to the ground
Katara creates a huge wave through waterbending, scattering the Dai Li, Zuko, and Azula
Katara catches Aang before he hits the ground
Katara holds Aang in tears, defenseless
Iroh appears suddenly
Iroh tells Katara and Aang to escape and that he will hold off Azula and the others
Katara and Aang escape
Iroh surrenders himself to the Dai Li
Iroh glances at Zuko and turns his head in shame
The entire group escapes and flies away on Appa
Katara uses her Spirit Oasis water to heal Aang, who initially appears dead
A flash of light occurs, but nothing happens
Katara begins to cry and holds Aang close
Aang's tattoos glow momentarily
Aang opens his eyes and smiles weakly at Katara
Katara, overwhelmed and relieved, hugs Aang closer
Zuko frets over betraying Iroh
Azula reassures Zuko by telling him that it was actually Iroh who betrayed them
Zuko asks Azula about returning to the Fire Nation and expresses doubts about being accepted and having his honor restored
Azula reassures Zuko by saying he has restored his own honor
Zuko still seems unsure
Azula is firmly in control of the city
The Earth King, Bosco, Momo, and Team Avatar fly atop Appa's back over the Outer Wall and out of the city
The Earth King looks back and sadly proclaims, "The Earth Kingdom ... has fallen."

The_Awakening.action: Aang lies on a bed in a dimly lit ship, bandaged
Aang opens his eyes and groans in pain
Aang sits up, groaning and clutching his side
Aang shakes his head, disoriented, and wonders aloud what happened
Aang notices Fire Nation tapestries and begins to panic, believing he has been captured
Aang flees his room, taking his airbending staff to help him walk
Aang runs into two guards in Fire Nation uniform
Aang airbends at the two guards to distract them and staggers away
Two guards chase after Aang, calling out to him to stop
Aang makes it to the upper deck but stumbles and falls due to his wounds
Aang looks up and sees Momo, Bato, and Hakoda in Fire Nation uniforms and cloaks
Momo greets Aang affectionately
Aang discovers Toph and Katara are present as well
The two guards are revealed to be The Duke and Pipsqueak
Aang asks his friends if he is dreaming
Katara pulls Aang into a hug
Sokka, in Fire Nation armor, greets Aang
Aang, overwhelmed and weak, faints
Appa shows up with a new saddle
Zuko watches the moon
Mai approaches Zuko
Mai asks Zuko if he is cold
Zuko reveals his conflicted feelings about his internal changes and imminent return to the Fire Nation
Mai tells Zuko not to worry and kisses him before leaving
Katara informs Aang that he has hair now by complimenting him on it
Aang responds with surprise
Aang begins to feel pain and returns to his room with Katara to undergo healing
Katara heals Aang’s back
Aang recalls the battle in Ba Sing Se and feels he has failed everyone as the Avatar
Aang realizes his spirit was separated from his body by Azula’s lightning attack and that Katara saved his life
Katara says she used the spirit water on Aang, not knowing exactly what she did
Aang says Katara saved him
They return outside as daylight begins to break
Sokka informs Aang about returning to Chameleon Bay after Aang was injured
Sokka tells Aang they found his father and later captured a Fire Nation ship
Hakoda greets Aang
Hakoda tells Aang that despite the Fire Nation capturing Ba Sing Se, they will proceed with the invasion plan on the Day of Black Sun
Katara angrily corrects Hakoda that it is Sokka's plan
Sokka explains that the Earth King and his bear, Bosco, decided to travel the world in disguise
Sokka mentions they recently got through the Serpent's Pass
Sokka says their forces are outnumbered but have the advantage that the world believes the Avatar is dead
Aang does not take the revelation well, feeling he has failed
Lo and Li announce the capture of Ba Sing Se, the return of Prince Zuko, and the death of the Avatar to the assembled armies
Lo and Li explain how Azula instructed the Dai Li to bring down a section of the wall for the Fire Nation Army to enter unopposed
soldiers march through the streets
platoons of tundra tanks roll toward the Earth Kingdom Royal Palace
Earth Kingdom citizens, including Iroh, Pao, Jin, Ying, and Than with their baby Hope, are shown near Pao's teahouse, scared from the events
Hope cries, scared from the events
Azula and Zuko appear before the crowd in the Fire Nation
Azula and Zuko are welcomed by rising cheers from the assembled armies
Aang and the others run into a Fire Nation ship
The others hide
Hakoda and Bato try to talk their way out of trouble
The Fire Nation crew finds holes in Hakoda and Bato's story
The Fire Nation crew believes the ship is captured
The captain decides to let them think they got away, but orders the ship to be sunk once clear
Toph overhears the captain's order
Toph drops the captain and his guards into the water using metalbending
Katara puts space between the two ships with a huge waterbending attack
The enemy ship is nearly capsized
The heroes try to escape
The heroes' ship takes heavy damage from the pursuing Fire Nation ship
The serpent of Serpent's Pass appears
The serpent is struck by a stray fireball
The heroes get the chance to escape as the enraged serpent attacks the Fire Nation ship
Zuko feeds turtle ducks at the palace, as he once did with his mother
Azula steps in and asks Zuko why he looks depressed, scaring the ducks away
Zuko tells Azula he does not want to see his father yet because he did not capture the Avatar
Azula reassures Zuko that the Fire Lord should not care because the Avatar is supposed to be dead
Azula asks Zuko suspiciously if he thinks the Avatar is still alive
Zuko recalls the Spirit Oasis water Katara showed him and its healing abilities, but tells Azula the Avatar could not have survived
Azula gives Zuko a suspicious look, but eventually walks away
Fire Lord Ozai congratulates Zuko on a job well done and says he is proud Zuko killed the Avatar
Zuko asks Ozai what he heard about this
Ozai responds that Azula told him Zuko had slain the Avatar
Later that night, Zuko confronts Azula about why she said he killed the Avatar instead of telling the truth
Azula answers that she wanted to make Zuko feel better and let him have some glory, so he would have nothing to worry about
Azula implies that if the Avatar is found alive, whoever failed to kill him will face Ozai’s wrath, making Zuko her scapegoat
Aang confesses to Katara that he feels ashamed everyone thinks he is dead and that he failed to stop the infiltration at Ba Sing Se
Aang gives into a burst of rage and asks Katara to leave him alone
Katara reluctantly leaves Aang to gather supplies in port
Aang flees the ship while his friends are away, hoping to infiltrate the Fire Nation alone
Aang passes a blockade of Fire Nation ships
a storm picks up
Aang uses a piece of driftwood and his glider to make a sail-surfer
Katara returns to Aang's room with food and finds it vacant
Katara realizes what has happened and rushes out of the room
Katara runs to her father, telling him in tears that Aang has run away to fight alone
Hakoda believes Aang is being brave
Katara counters, saying Aang is being selfish and stupid, and wonders why he thinks he is the only one who can save the world and leaves them behind
Hakoda realizes Katara may be talking about him as well
Katara pours her heart out to her father about the war and how she has missed him
Hakoda comforts Katara, telling her he missed her and Sokka every day since he left and thought of them constantly
The storm becomes even more intense
Aang loses his glider
Aang feels like a failure, thinking he will not make it out of the storm
Avatar Roku appears to Aang in a vision, apologizing for not stopping the war before it began and encouraging Aang not to give up
Princess Yue appears in a beam of moonlight, calming the ocean and offering encouragement
Aang stands on the driftwood with Yue's help
Aang makes a huge tidal wave and uses waterbending to power himself forward
Aang washes up on Crescent Island, where the Crescent Island Fire Temple once stood
His friends find Aang lying on the shoreline after splitting up with the Water Tribe warriors
His friends tell Aang they intend to travel with him to help him continue his earthbending training
Toph finds Aang’s broken glider floating in the water and returns it to him
Aang decides that the world must think he is dead to maintain the element of surprise
Aang impales the glider into the molten rock, leaving it to burn to flames

The_Headband.action: Zuko tosses and turns in his sleep
Zuko awakens and arises from his bed
Zuko makes his way toward a Fire Nation prison
Zuko looks distantly toward the prison
Zuko stands near the entrance of the prison
a guard spies Zuko from the roof and demands to know his identity
Zuko walks away without revealing his identity
Aang and the others travel through the Fire Nation, using waterbending and airbending to hide Appa in a cloud
They take temporary shelter in a cave
They seek out new clothes to blend in with Fire Nation citizens
They find an unattended clothesline and steal some outfits
Toph modifies her shoes by removing the soles
Aang blushes shyly when Katara appears in her new outfit
Aang indicates Katara has to remove her mother's necklace to stay hidden
Katara removes her mother's necklace
They venture into a bazaar to purchase accessories, including a new necklace for Katara
The group attempts to blend in and act natural in town
Aang claims to be an expert of Fire Nation customs
Aang calls people on the street "hotman" and uses out-of-date expressions
The others go to buy food from a meat stand
Aang abstains from buying meat due to his vegetarianism and goes off on his own
Aang is caught by a group of soldiers
Soldiers escort Aang into a classroom
Aang’s stolen clothing is identified as a local school uniform
Soldiers assume Aang is a truant
Teacher assumes Aang is a new student from the Fire Nation colonies in the Earth Kingdom
Aang calls himself Kuzon and attempts to blend in at the school
Aang fails to do a proper greeting to the teacher
Aang fixes his greeting with hints from a nearby girl
Teacher informs Aang about the rules against headwear
Aang claims his headwear is to hide an embarrassing scar
After dismissal, Aang meets a friendly girl named On Ji
Aang meets On Ji’s apparent boyfriend, Hide, a popular and aggressive student
Aang has a brief confrontation with Hide
Aang makes friends and plays "hide and explode" with Shoji and other children from the school
Aang, covered in soot, returns to the cave at nightfall
Katara, Sokka, and Toph are relieved by Aang's return
Katara, Sokka, and Toph were worried by Aang's long absence
Aang's friends are shocked to learn he attended a Fire Nation school all day, especially Sokka
Aang claims to have enjoyed the experience and wishes to return to the school the next day
Sokka strongly objects to Aang returning to the school
Aang argues he is obtaining valuable knowledge about the Fire Nation
Aang convinces Sokka to allow him to continue attending the school
Aang joyfully exclaims, "Flamey-O, hotman!" to Sokka
Sokka becomes utterly confused by Aang's statement
Zuko returns to the prison
Zuko aggressively threatens the guard from the previous night not to tell anyone about his visit
Zuko enters Iroh's cell
Iroh turns away from Zuko, showing great disappointment in his nephew
Zuko blames Iroh for Iroh's circumstances and says they could have returned to the Fire Nation as heroes
Iroh refuses to speak to Zuko
Zuko goes into a fit of rage and leaves
Aang gets in trouble for not reciting the Fire Nation Oath correctly
Students think Aang’s bumbling through the oath is a funny joke
Teacher punishes Aang’s disruption by calling for a pop quiz on Fire Nation history
Teacher asks the first question about the year Fire Lord Sozin defeated the Air Nation Army
Aang, confused, asks if it was a trick question and points out the Air Nomads had no formal military and were defeated by an ambush
Class is astonished by Aang’s answer
Teacher rejects Aang’s claim, stating it is not in the Fire Nation’s history texts and that he could not know that unless he had been there
Aang responds that he will just write down his best guess
Aang tries to play the tsungi horn in band class
band teacher chides Aang for dancing, which is not considered proper in the Fire Nation
On Ji is amused by Aang's dance
Aang offers to show On Ji the dance during recess
Hide becomes angry and attempts to hit Aang
Aang skillfully evades every attempted blow with his hands behind his back
Aang causes Hide to stumble and fall over without bending or touching him
Aang gets in trouble for "roughing up" a favored student of the Headmaster
parent-teacher conference is held
Sokka and Katara attend the conference acting as Aang's parents, "Mr. Wang Fire" and "Mrs. Sapphire Fire"
Headmaster warns that any more disruption will result in "Kuzon" being sent to reform school
Sokka and Katara succeed in fooling the teacher
Sokka tells Aang he can't return to school
Aang tells Sokka this is his first chance to be a normal kid since learning he was the Avatar
Aang insists they need to do something for the children in the school to allow freedom of expression
Aang plans to hold a secret dance party in the cave for everyone in the school
Sokka, still wearing his beard, responds with an irritated "Go to your room!"
Zuko has a romantic picnic with Mai
Zuko and Mai share a kiss
Azula interrupts Zuko and Mai
Azula tries to get Zuko to talk to her
Zuko refuses to talk to Azula
Azula sends Mai away by saying Ty Lee needs help untangling her braid
Mai complies politely but gives Azula an angry look behind her back
Azula tricks Zuko into admitting he was visiting Iroh
Azula warns Zuko not to visit Iroh, saying it would make others suspicious
Zuko visits Iroh in prison again
Iroh faces away from Zuko
Zuko brings Iroh food as a gift
Zuko begs Iroh for advice, explaining his confusion about the Avatar and his need for guidance
Iroh remains silent
Zuko flies into a rage, berates Iroh, and leaves
Iroh continues to remain silent, but a tear runs down his face
The children arrive in the cave and the school band begins to play
The children are stunned by nervousness, not knowing how to dance and afraid of getting in trouble
Aang demonstrates some dance forms
Aang asks On Ji to dance
On Ji dances with Aang, encouraging the rest of the students to dance
Sokka states that Aang and On Ji look good together
Katara gives a slightly terse response to Sokka
The students gradually start to freestyle dance
Toph, Sokka, and Katara do not join in the dancing
Aang asks Katara to dance
Katara hesitates, then takes Aang’s hand and dances with him
Aang and Katara perform a combination of Bagua and Northern Shaolin in synchronization
The students are awed by Aang and Katara’s dance
Katara and Aang share a moment of mutual attraction at the end of the dance
The Headmaster and several soldiers appear, having been told of the dance by Hide
The Headmaster and soldiers try to catch Aang
Aang hides within the crowd of kids
The soldiers are thrown off when they discover several other students wearing the same headband as Aang
Every student in the cave puts on a headband, delaying the Headmaster’s hunt for Aang
Team Avatar slips away through the opening behind the cave
Aang stops and exchanges smiles with Shoji before running off
Aang closes the opening behind him with earthbending
Shoji is left shocked and speechless
Some of the soldiers accept defeat and dance
The Headmaster is chagrined by the soldiers' dancing
The group flies off on Appa
Toph compliments Aang on his efforts, believing he made a serious impact on the students
Katara agrees with Toph
Katara thanks Aang for dancing with her
Katara kisses Aang on the cheek
Aang blushes
Sokka refuses to take his fake beard off
Sokka claims the beard is permanently glued to his skin
Zuko walks into a dark alley at night
Zuko meets a mysterious man with a metal arm and leg, and a tattooed third eye
Zuko states his purpose for contacting the man: he has heard the man is capable of doing his job effectively and secretly, and he wants him to track down and eliminate the Avatar

The_Painted_Lady.action: The group floats downstream on Appa in the middle of a sludge-contaminated river
The group has little chance of catching any food
The group decides to buy food in the village of Jang Hui
This decision conflicts with Sokka's "master schedule"
Sokka's master schedule details every minute up to the day of the invasion
The village is located in the middle of the river, which has been polluted by a nearby military factory
Dock ferries them to the village
The village is in a deep depression with many villagers having fallen ill and a lack of food due to the polluted water
Katara wishes to help the villagers
Sokka argues that the importance of their overall mission overrides all other situations
They purchase food from Xu, a shopkeeper who resembles Dock except for his hat
Katara gives one of the fish they bought to a small child
The small child gives the fish to his sick mother
Katara wishes she could do more
Sokka attempts to rearrange their schedule
Sokka mentions that the duration of the solar eclipse is only about eight minutes
Appa is found lying on his side, looking rather sickly
Appa’s tongue becomes purple, causing concern among the group
Katara suggests that the village might have herbs to provide a remedy for Appa
They arrive at the village and find the residents in much better spirits
Xu explains that the legendary river spirit, the Painted Lady, came overnight and supplied the villagers with food
The factory uses all the medicine, resulting in a lack of medicine for the villagers
Katara suggests letting Appa rest for another day
A thick fog shrouds the village
The Painted Lady arrives and heals several of the sick
The child Katara helped earlier thanks the Painted Lady
The Painted Lady leaves across the water
Appa still appears sick the next day
They return to the village
The town is in even higher spirits
Villagers erect a large statue of the Painted Lady in gratitude for healing their sick
Xu/Dock suggests that perhaps the Painted Lady could heal Appa
Sokka skeptically notes that the Painted Lady's work will be all for nothing unless she continually helps them
Sokka says that as long as the factory is still there, the village is doomed
Sokka jokingly suggests that the Painted Lady use her "spirit magic" to blow up the factory
Katara is revealed to be the Painted Lady
Katara assumes her disguise as the Painted Lady again the next night
Momo wakes up and rouses Aang
Aang sees the Painted Lady and tries to appeal to her for help
Katara (as the Painted Lady) runs away
Aang chases Katara (as the Painted Lady) all the way to the village
Katara halts, thinking she has lost Aang
Aang suddenly appears out of nowhere and tells the "spirit" that he is the Avatar
Aang compliments the "spirit" on her appearance, saying other spirits he has met are not very attractive
Katara, speaking with an adult voice, tries to excuse herself
Aang begins to recognize the "spirit"
Aang airbends the hat off Katara's head
Aang discovers Katara's secret about Appa's "illness"
Katara confesses and apologizes for delaying their travels to help the village
Aang praises Katara as a secret hero
Aang and Katara infiltrate the factory and wreck it, stopping the output of pollutants
Aang and Katara infiltrate the factory and wreck it, stopping the output of pollutants
The two arrive back at the camp in the morning
Sokka and Toph wait for them, having found out the truth themselves
The group prepares to leave
They see a squad of Fire Nation troops heading toward the town on jet skis
They realize the army must believe the villagers had sabotaged the factory
Katara is determined to stop the Fire Nation troops
Sokka readily agrees to help Katara upon realizing she is set on helping
General Mung accuses the villagers of destroying the factory and stealing food and medicine
The soldiers begin to attack the village
Gusts of wind extinguish the fires the soldiers ignite
A large mist envelops the town and emits spooky sounds, generated by Appa, Toph, and Sokka
The young boy realizes the Painted Lady is coming
General Mung denies the Painted Lady is coming
Katara, disguised as the Painted Lady, arrives
The soldiers attempt to fight Katara
Katara scares the soldiers off with blasts of air caused by Aang and launches their boats into the cliffs
Aang, hiding below the docks, creates blasts of air to help Katara
Mung is left to face Katara
Aang creates blasts of air to help Katara dodge Mung's attacks and knock him into the river
Katara demands that Mung leaves and never comes back
Mung leaves in defeat
The villagers praise the Painted Lady
Dock notices the Painted Lady's smudged face paint and recognizes her as Katara
Dock realizes Katara is a waterbender
The villagers become outraged by Katara's trickery
Sokka comes to Katara's defense, stating they should thank her for destroying the factory and getting rid of the Fire Nation
Katara calms Sokka and apologizes to the villagers for deceiving them, justifying her actions as necessary to help
Katara tells the villagers they must help themselves instead of waiting for someone else
Dock agrees with Katara but is unsure how to proceed
Toph suggests cleaning the river
Dock immediately jumps on the idea along with the other villagers
Dock thanks Katara for her help, but annoys Sokka by saying his "brother" Xu might tell Katara's secret
Katara asks Dock if he will help clean the river
Dock declines, but directs them to his "brother" Bushi, who loves cleaning rivers
Dock switches his hat and introduces himself as Bushi
Aang announces he knew all along that Dock, Xu, and Bushi were the same person
Bushi (Dock) tells Aang that his "other brother" Dock is crazy, irritating Aang
All the villagers set out in boats to clean the river
Team Avatar helps clean the river
Katara lifts the water with her bending
Aang uses earthbending to extract dirt and metal from the water
Toph uses earthbending to extract dirt and metal from the water
Team Avatar returns to their camp on the shore after the river is cleaned
At night, Katara goes to the river to freshen up
A thick mist appears
The real Painted Lady appears to Katara
The Painted Lady smiles and thanks Katara before disappearing in the fog
Katara is left slightly baffled but pleased

Sokka%27s_Master.action: Aang, Katara, and Sokka watch a meteor shower
Toph chooses to "watch" the meteor shower with the group despite being unable to see
One meteoroid falls to Earth and impacts nearby, starting a forest fire near a village
Aang and Toph build trenches around the fire
Katara flies on Appa to a nearby creek
Katara waterbends from the creek to dispose of the fire
Sokka is placed in charge of Momo and does nothing to contribute
Momo helps out by snuffing more ember fires than Sokka
Sokka is dismayed by his lack of contribution
Warden Poon goes to give Iroh his food in prison
The guard heavily mocks a disheveled Iroh as he scrabbles for the gruel
Iroh scrabbles for the gruel
Iroh scoops what had fallen onto the ground into his mouth
The guard leaves
Iroh becomes more collected, wipes the sweaty hair out of his face, and calmly finishes his meal in peace, only eating out of the bowl
Sokka expresses how he feels useless for not being able to bend or fight as well as the others, stating he cannot "fly around" like the rest of them
The group tries to console Sokka
The group takes Sokka shopping at a weapons store
Katara tries on leather gloves
Aang tries on intimidating but heavy armor
Sokka tries many weapons and injures himself a few times
Sokka sees a priceless sword and falls in love with it
The shop owner commends Sokka's eye for quality and informs him the sword was forged by Piandao, a renowned Fire Nation swordsmaster and swordsmith
The shop owner tells Sokka that Piandao lives in a nearby castle
Aang suggests Sokka seek the tutelage of the master
Sokka decides to seek Piandao's tutelage because every other group member was formally trained by a real master
Iroh lies on the ground deep in thought
Iroh's eyes widen suddenly
Iroh begins explosively doing bent-knee sit ups
camera cuts away to the group
Sokka travels to Piandao's home
Fat cautions Sokka at the front gate that Piandao turns away almost everyone
Fat asks Sokka what he brought to prove his worth
Sokka cannot come up with anything to prove his worth
Fat shakes his head and lets Sokka in to "get it over with"
Sokka is taken to where Piandao is doing calligraphy
Sokka introduces himself to Piandao
Piandao comments that Sokka's name is unusual
Sokka awkwardly claims his name is normal in the Fire Nation colonies
Piandao, without turning around, skeptically guesses Sokka's story
Sokka corrects Piandao, saying he has been all over the world and has a lot to learn
Piandao notes Sokka is not "selling" himself well
Sokka agrees he is not selling himself well
Sokka admits he is not sure of his own worthiness
Piandao decides to take Sokka under his wing because of this honesty
Piandao gives Sokka an outfit similar to his own as his formal student
Piandao demonstrates weapon form outside in his back garden area
Piandao explains that the weapon is an extension of oneself and must be thought of as another part of one's body
Piandao describes the sword as a simple weapon that becomes versatile in the hands of a master with imagination and creativity
Piandao charges Sokka with tasks such as landscape painting and calligraphy
Piandao has Sokka practice against Fat
The guard mocks Iroh
The group misses Sokka and tries to make jokes in his absence
Katara tries to make a joke
Aang says, "I guess the jokes don't run in the family."
Iroh trains in the guards' absence, doing straight-legged inverted sit-ups and clapping push-ups
Iroh keeps up the facade of loneliness and desperation in front of the guard to hide his exercises
Sokka's swordsmanship greatly improves, and he finally beats Fat by taking a new approach to the challenges
Piandao believes Sokka is worthy to choose a steel to make his own sword
Sokka is unsatisfied with the samples of metal before him
Sokka remembers the meteorite and, with Piandao's approval, returns to camp for help
Piandao gives pleased approval for Sokka to source his own raw material
Aang and Katara are overjoyed to see Sokka again
Katara is overjoyed to see Sokka again
Toph claims she did not care and turns away to hide her blush
They haul the meteorite up the road to the castle with Toph's earthbending aid
Piandao addresses them at the gate, asking about Aang, Katara, and Toph
Sokka claims Aang, Katara, and Toph are friends and fellow Fire Nation citizens
Sokka asks about using the meteorite as a potential material
The blade smith proclaims they will craft a sword like no other
Iroh is seen training whenever not under watch
Iroh performs difficult exercises including inverted crunches, inverted push-ups, and one handed pull-ups
Iroh seems to regain much of his strength by this time
The sword master spends a full night forging a sword in traditional Chinese fashion: melting, pouring into a mold, shaping, and quenching
The sword master presents Sokka with a unique, all black sword, praising his creativity and imagination
Sokka refuses the sword and confesses to being from the Southern Water Tribe
The sword master attacks Sokka
Sokka and the sword master engage in a duel
Aang and the others try to intervene
Sokka tells Aang and the others to stand back, wanting to fight the battle on his own
guard comes to drop off food for Iroh
guard mocks Iroh, comparing his former status as general and "Dragon of the West" to his current jailed condition
guard drops the food on the floor, except for an apple that Iroh reaches for
Iroh reaches for an apple
guard leaves
Iroh secretly reconditions his body to be more formidable than ever before, keeping his jailer unaware
Sokka and Piandao move their duel outside
Sokka uses the techniques he has learned, along with his youth and agility, to keep up with Piandao
Piandao points out Sokka’s progress in approval several times
Piandao finally gains the upper hand in the duel
Katara, Aang, and Toph jump down into the courtyard to help Sokka
Sokka stands down and sheaths his sword by catching the thrown scabbard from Fat onto the blade
Piandao reveals he knew Sokka was from the Water Tribe all along from his name
Piandao notes that Aang is the Avatar
Piandao suggests Sokka use the cover name Lee instead
Piandao claims the art of the sword does not belong to any one nation and gives Sokka his sword back
Piandao tells Sokka he must continue to train on his own
Piandao expresses confidence that Sokka will become an even greater master one day
Sokka and Piandao bow to each other
Fat runs after Sokka and gives him a small bag with the swordsmaster's gift
Sokka receives the small bag from Fat
Sokka discovers a Pai Sho tile: the white lotus, inside the bag
Fat closes the doors of the front gate
They notice the symbol of the white lotus surrounding that of the Fire Nation on the gate
Sokka gives Toph a piece of meteorite he saved for her, calling it "space earth"
Toph bends the meteoric iron into various shapes: a spiral, a star, and a "splat"
Katara contradicts Sokka, claiming there is no such thing as earth from space
Sokka calls Katara out for ruining everything
Katara states she cannot believe she missed Sokka

The_Beach.action: Zuko, Azula, Mai, and Ty Lee are sent on vacation to Lo and Li's beach house on Ember Island while Fire Lord Ozai holds restricted meetings with his advisers
Ty Lee is excited about the vacation
Zuko expresses his displeasure at being sent away on a forced vacation, stating he feels like he is being treated as a child
The boat pulls into the small port
Lo and Li greet the teenagers
All of them, except for Ty Lee, express their disdain at the beach house
Lo and Li urge the teenagers to give Ember Island a chance, saying the beach has a special way of smoothing even the most jagged edges and revealing one's true self
Aang and friends relax in secluded hot springs, believing they are safe from the Fire Nation
Aang plays in the water
Two bored Fire Nation soldiers see Aang unknowingly
The Fire Nation soldiers recognize Aang
The Fire Nation soldiers send a messenger hawk to Fire Lord Ozai, reporting the Avatar is still alive
The message is intercepted by a raven eagle owned by Combustion Man
Combustion Man, a firebender assassin hired by Zuko, receives the intercepted message
Zuko, Azula, Mai, and Ty Lee go down to the beach to have fun
Azula nastily crushes a kid's sandcastle
Ty Lee is approached by several boys
Ty Lee is shaded and fanned by the boys
Zuko makes several unsuccessful attempts at impressing Mai with pretty seashells and ice cream
Azula pulls the group into a game of kuai ball with other teens
Azula plots their strategy as if plotting a battle
Zuko, Azula, Mai, and Ty Lee use their various skills to easily overwhelm their opponents
Azula gets carried away, sets the net on fire, and sinisterly gloats as if she has crushed an enemy
The game arouses the attention of Chan and Ruon-Jian
Chan and Ruon-Jian invite Ty Lee and Mai to a party
Chan and Ruon-Jian reluctantly invite Zuko and Azula after some persuasion
Azula makes a point of not revealing her or Zuko's royal identity
Azula explains she wants to see what it is like to be a normal person for a change
The group arrives at Chan's house early for the party
Chan notes the group's early arrival
Azula notes that they are the perfect party guests and very punctual
Chan warns the group that his father does not know about the party and not to mess anything up
Chan awkwardly thanks Azula
Azula tries to compliment Chan's outfit, saying it is "sharp," but gets carried away and makes an extreme comment
Zuko gets jealous of Ruon-Jian, thinking Mai is interested in him
Ty Lee is flanked by a crowd of adoring boys and is forced to render them unconscious to escape
Azula calls Ty Lee a tease and says none of the boys really care about her
Ty Lee starts crying
Azula hastily apologizes and confides she was just jealous, then asks Ty Lee for tips
Ty Lee tells Azula to smile and laugh at anything a boy tells her
Ty Lee pretends to approach Azula with a generic guy impression to help her practice
Azula laughs way too loudly, drawing everyone's attention
Mai tells Zuko that she is bored and hungry
Zuko goes to find Mai some food, much to his chagrin
Azula asks Chan for a tour of the house
Chan gives Azula a tour of the house
Azula and Chan go to the veranda and flirt
Azula and Chan share a kiss
Azula loses control of the situation and maniacally exclaims they could be the world's strongest couple and dominate the Earth
Azula creates blue fire in both her hands
Chan freaks out at Azula's sudden change in behavior and leaves in a hurry
Azula is left disappointed
Zuko fetches food for Mai
Zuko finds Ruon-Jian talking to Mai
Zuko violently shoves Ruon-Jian into a large pot, smashing it
Mai angrily tells Zuko that his temper is out of control
Zuko tells Mai she does not feel anything and is a "big blah"
Mai, hurt and confused, tells Zuko that their relationship is over
Chan hears the sound of the shattering vase
Ruon-Jian accuses Zuko of breaking the vase
Chan ejects Zuko from the party
Zuko heads to his family's abandoned vacation house
Zuko reminisces about memories of himself as a child with his mother, father, and uncle
Zuko spots a stone imprinted with his own baby hand print
Zuko's eyes are drawn to an old family portrait hanging on the wall
Combustion Man arrives near Team Avatar's campsite
Toph wakes up, sensing the enemy's approach
Toph comments that a "metal man" is coming
Combustion Man attacks, projecting rays of energy from his forehead tattoo that explode upon impact
Combustion Man's bolts counter anything the team throws at him, forcing them to take cover
Aang tells his friends to get on Appa and get ready to escape
Aang tries to lead the enemy away
Aang uses airbending and earthbending to fend off Combustion Man's attacks
Aang eventually escapes and rejoins the group on Appa
Toph comments on the randomness of the encounter
Katara says that she feels this new enemy seems to know exactly who they are
Azula finds Zuko and tells him to come to the beach with her
Zuko tries to reconcile with Mai
Mai is still cold toward Zuko
Zuko burns his family's painting to help kindle fire to keep them warm
Ty Lee wonders out loud why Zuko would burn something so precious
Zuko snaps at Ty Lee to mind her own business and says she does not understand him
Mai comes to Ty Lee's defense and orders Zuko to leave her alone
Zuko mocks Ty Lee and calls her a circus freak
Ty Lee bursts out into tears
Azula laughs at Ty Lee
Ty Lee tearfully reveals she has six sisters exactly like her and joined the circus to be unique
Ty Lee snaps back that being called a circus freak is a compliment
Mai says Ty Lee's lack of attention growing up is why she wants so much of it now
Ty Lee turns on Mai, remarking that despite being an only child and receiving attention, Mai still has a "dingy, pasty, gray" aura
Mai says she does not believe in auras
Zuko retorts that Mai does not believe in anything
Mai sarcastically apologizes for not being as high-strung and crazy as the rest of them
Zuko states that Mai does not keep all of her emotions bottled up
Mai talks about her parents giving her everything she wanted as long as she behaved exactly as told
Mai explains her father's political career kept her under a spotlight and unable to live freely
Azula deduces that Mai stifles her true feelings and cannot express herself
Azula elicits a sharp and emotional outburst from Mai
Mai has a sharp and emotional outburst
Zuko says he likes it when Mai expresses herself
Mai refuses to let Zuko touch her, saying she is still angry
Zuko and Mai begin to argue
Ty Lee claims that negative energy is bad for the skin
Zuko states he does not have the luxury, referencing his father's scar on his face
Zuko rants about his own life, admitting he is always angry but unsure why
The girls pressure Zuko into giving an answer
Zuko screams that he is angry at himself and shoots fire in the air because he does not know the difference between right and wrong anymore
Mai returns to Zuko’s side, says the one thing she cares about is him, and kisses him
Zuko receives Mai’s affection
Azula reluctantly delves into her past and says her mother thought she was a monster
Azula admits her mother was correct, but that it still hurt to hear
The heaviness between the four vanishes as all their secrets are out in the open
Ty Lee says she will remember this night forever and that Lo and Li were right about the beach’s ability to reveal people’s true selves
Ty Lee states that she feels all 'smoothed'
Azula decides to end the night by returning to Chan's party to wreak havoc
The four head back to Chan's party
Zuko tells Chan that the party is over when the door is answered
The four begin trashing the house, each in their own unique way
Chan is left sobbing
The four forge their own memory of the place, uniting as friends for the first time in years

The_Avatar_and_the_Fire_Lord.action: Aang is visited in a dream by Roku
Roku tells Aang it is time to learn about his past life's history with Fire Lord Sozin
Roku instructs Aang to travel to Roku's old home, a deserted volcanic island, during the summer solstice
Aang and the gang arrive at the volcanic island
The gang is puzzled not to see anything on the island
Toph senses there is an entire village buried beneath the volcanic ash of the island's shore
As the solstice begins, Aang meditates
Aang meets Roku in the Spirit World
Zuko is awoken by the noise of someone outside his bedroom door
Zuko rushes out to see who it is
Zuko finds a scroll sitting on the floor
The scroll tells Zuko he must find out about his great-grandfather's death to understand his own destiny
Zuko visits a portrait gallery of the past Fire Lords
Azula greets Zuko
Zuko asks Azula if she knows how their great-grandfather Fire Lord Sozin died
Azula reminds Zuko what they learned in school: Sozin died a very old man, peacefully in his sleep
Zuko mulls over the mysterious message to himself that night in his room, unsure of what it means
Zuko tosses the unrolled scroll back to the table in frustration
The scroll drapes over the lit lamp on the table
The lamp's heat reveals a hidden message in invisible ink, telling Zuko to go to the Dragonbone Catacombs to find the secret history kept by the Fire Sages
Zuko eludes the Fire Sages
Zuko infiltrates the catacombs
Zuko finds in Sozin's section "The Final Testament of Fire Lord Sozin", an autobiographical record of his life
Zuko reads the record
Roku shows Aang a flashback of his youth
Roku practices firebending with Crown Prince Sozin
Roku and Sozin celebrate their shared birthday together
On their sixteenth birthday, Roku's identity as the Avatar is revealed by the Fire Sages
Roku and Sozin are forced to separate as Roku leaves the Fire Nation to master the other three elements
Sozin visits Roku before he is supposed to leave and finds him dispirited
Roku starts packing in preparation to leave
Fire Sages tell Roku he will not need any worldly possessions
Sozin gives Roku a gift: the hair ornament customarily worn by the Crown Prince of the Fire Nation
Roku wears the hair ornament ever since
Roku begins his journey at the Southern Air Temple, training to master airbending
Roku becomes friends with young Gyatso
Aang is surprised and delighted to learn about Roku and Gyatso's friendship
Gyatso shows Roku a new trick: flipping his airbender staff around and standing on it during a gliding lesson
Aang realizes Gyatso was airsurfing and marvels that he never thought of it himself
Roku tells Aang that some friendships are so strong they can transcend lifetimes
Roku masters airbending over several years
Roku travels to the Northern Water Tribe to learn waterbending from his master
Roku finds waterbending challenging but masters it after several years
Roku journeys to the Earth Kingdom and masters earthbending with an earthbender named Sud
Roku becomes a fully realized Avatar
Roku returns to the Fire Nation after twelve years of training
Sozin, now Fire Lord, greets Roku warmly
Roku marries his childhood sweetheart, Ta Min, with Sozin as his best man
Sozin takes Roku aside during the wedding and discusses the Fire Nation's prosperity and his vision for an empire
Roku is greatly disturbed and tells Sozin that the four nations must remain separate and to abandon such thoughts
Roku and his dragon, Fang, discover that Sozin has invaded the Earth Kingdom and established several colonies
Roku confronts Sozin at the Royal Palace and demands he cease the expansion
Sozin declares that Roku's first loyalty should be to the Fire Nation and considers anything less as treason
Roku warns Sozin not to challenge him and declares the invasion over
Sozin, enraged, attacks Roku from behind with a powerful display of firebending
Roku quickly disables Sozin
Roku uses the Avatar State to destroy the throne room, completely leveling it
Roku warns Sozin that he is sparing his life due to their past friendship, but threatens to kill him if he oversteps again
Roku's island is consumed in a massive volcanic eruption in the middle of the night
Roku helps Ta Min and his fellow villagers escape safely
Roku tries to contain the eruption, but without success
So massive is the eruption that Sozin can see and feel it from a hundred miles away in the capital
Sozin flies to the island on his blue dragon to assist his old friend
The island's secondary volcano begins erupting
Sozin arrives on the scene and works with Roku to try and quell the second peak
A powerful clap of thunder from volcanic lightning within the plume causes the ground Sozin is standing on to crumble away
Roku saves Sozin with earthbending
The volcano releases another plume of smoke and ash
Roku and Sozin flee down the slope
A sudden venting of gas overwhelms Roku, causing him to collapse
Roku calls to Sozin for help
Sozin realizes the elimination of the Avatar would benefit his plans for the Fire Nation
Sozin coldly flies away on his dragon, leaving Roku to die
A pyroclastic flow rolls down the mountainside
Fang refuses to leave Roku behind, flies down, and curls up around him
The superheated flow consumes both Roku and Fang
Roku exhales his last breath
Avatar Aang is born to the Air Nomads
Zuko finishes reading Sozin's testament
Sozin explains his attack on the Air Nomads to eliminate the next Avatar
The Avatar manages to escape Sozin's attack
Sozin spends the rest of his life searching fruitlessly for "The Last Airbender"
Zuko is disappointed that the record ends here
Zuko checks the underside of the scroll for the mystery he is supposed to find
Zuko does not understand how the record concerns him
Zuko goes to the Fire Nation jail tower and bursts angrily into Iroh's cell
Zuko accuses Iroh of sending him the message and demands the point
Iroh explains the message referred to Zuko's mother's grandfather, Avatar Roku, not Sozin
Zuko is alarmed by Iroh's explanation
Iroh explains that Zuko's conflicted destiny is due to their combined heritage
Iroh removes a brick from the back wall of his cell, revealing a hidden space
Iroh retrieves a small wrapped bundle from the hidden space
Iroh unwraps the cloth, revealing the lost Crown Prince's ornamental hairpiece
Iroh gives the hairpiece to Zuko and tells him he alone can redeem their family and the Fire Nation by restoring balance to the world
Aang awakens from his vision with Roku
Aang tells his friends the story
Aang reflects on Roku and Sozin’s lives and actions, concluding that any person is capable of great good or evil and deserves a chance at redemption
Toph wonders if friendships can really transcend lifetimes
Aang and Katara hold Toph’s hand to assure her
Katara holds Toph’s hand to assure her
Sokka expresses skepticism, saying there is no real scientific proof
Katara, annoyed, demands Sokka just hold hands with the rest of them
Sokka quickly holds hands with the rest of them

The_Runaway.action: Toph hurriedly runs toward a statue in Fire Fountain City, trying to evade someone
Several guards capture Toph, ensnaring her in a net
Toph yells at Katara, claiming Katara betrayed her
Katara replies that Toph brought this upon herself and left her no other choice
Toph is dragged away by the guards while glaring at Katara
Katara stands firmly by the statue
Katara and Toph train Aang, who is blindfolded and must use seismic sense to detect their attacks
Katara and Toph train Aang, who is blindfolded and must use seismic sense to detect their attacks
Katara and Toph train Aang, who is blindfolded and must use seismic sense to detect their attacks
Toph accidentally hits Katara in the chest with a rock
Katara and Toph start fighting each other
Katara and Toph start fighting each other
Aang, still blindfolded, tries to figure out what is going on from a distance
Sokka suddenly charges from behind a rock, loudly screaming "Sneak attack!", and makes a failed attempt to strike Aang
Aang comments that it is not a sneak attack if it is yelled out loud
Toph and Katara wrestle in mud
Aang interrupts the fight
Toph and Katara stop fighting
Katara goes off to clean up
Toph suggests to the boys that they go into town and have some fun
Aang, Sokka, and Toph agree to go into town
Aang, Sokka, and Toph venture to the city with only one silver piece
Toph suggests they earn more money from a gambling game
Toph detects through earthbending that the dealer is cheating
Toph uses her bending to cheat and wins the game
The gambler wagers Sokka’s sword against his twenty silver pieces
Toph raises the wager to forty silver pieces and wins again
Toph, Aang, and Sokka earn forty silver pieces
They arrive back at camp
Katara advises them against gambling
Aang makes an Avatar promise not to gamble again
Toph, Aang, and Sokka do not listen to Katara or the promise
Toph cheats in a dice-like game and a test-your-strength game, winning more money
Toph pretends to get run over by a noble’s carriage
Sokka, dressed as a Fire Nation soldier, scams the noble by accepting money for silence
Katara warns that these scams are dangerous and immoral
Toph tells Katara to quit acting like the mother of the group
Katara suggests Toph is making a fuss because she secretly misses her own parents
Toph angrily storms off
Sokka goes into town to buy a messenger hawk, which he names Hawky
Sokka finds a wanted poster for Toph, dubbed "The Runaway"
Sokka is horrified and tears down the poster
Sokka later shows the wanted poster to Toph
Toph is delighted at the nickname and the reward money offered for her return
Toph convinces Sokka to keep the wanted poster a secret from Katara by giving him funds for Appa's armor and expensive atlases
Katara rummages through Toph's belongings and discovers the wanted poster
Katara confronts Toph about the wanted poster
Toph angrily replies that Katara is not her mother and should not give her orders
Katara and Toph refuse to speak to one another
Katara and Toph refuse to speak to one another
Sokka tries to write an apology message by posing as Toph and using Hawky to send it to Katara
Katara sees through Sokka's ruse due to Toph's blindness and illiteracy
Sokka talks to Toph to try to mend the rift between her and Katara
Sokka and Toph discuss how Katara's motherly instincts can be supportive and nurturing
Sokka and Toph discuss how Katara's motherly instincts can be supportive and nurturing
Katara listens to Sokka and Toph while bathing in the water beneath the cliff
Sokka reveals how Katara took on the role of their mother after her death, eventually replacing the face of their mother for him
Toph admits she appreciates Katara's motherly nature and believes Katara cares for her more than her real mother ever did
Katara approaches Toph and suggests they work together to collect the bounty she has accumulated as the "ultimate scam"
The group expresses surprise
Aang faints while foaming at the mouth
Sokka faints while foaming at the mouth
Katara insists she can show her fun side
Katara pretends to turn in Toph to the authorities
The scenario is revealed to be a trap set by Combustion Man
Katara and Toph are imprisoned in a wooden cell
Aang and Sokka battle Combustion Man
Aang and Sokka battle Combustion Man
Katara and Toph try to devise a way to escape the cage
Katara and Toph try to devise a way to escape the cage
Katara suggests Toph use her meteorite bracelet to make a saw
Toph answers that she left her meteorite bracelet at camp
Katara wipes her forehead and discovers it covered in sweat
Katara jumps up and starts to run in place, building up more sweat
Toph asks Katara what she is doing
Katara replies, "I'm making my own water!"
Katara waterbends her own sweat to slice through the wooden cell and escape
Toph praises Katara
Katara and Toph race to help Aang
Katara and Toph race to help Aang
Katara and Toph arrive just in time to save Aang from Combustion Man
Katara and Toph arrive just in time to save Aang from Combustion Man
Toph accidentally strikes Combustion Man's third eye tattoo with a pebble, knocking him back
Combustion Man rises and attempts to fire another explosion at the group
Combustion Man finds his chi-blocked; the air around him explodes
The group escapes
While running away, Sokka names their opponent Combustion Man
Toph comes to the conclusion that she does not truly hate her parents
Katara acts as a scribe for Toph
Toph writes down a heartfelt message for her parents
Toph sends Hawky to deliver the message
Sokka inquires as to where Hawky is, not knowing he had been sent off

The_Puppetmaster.action: Team Avatar camps out in the woods for the night
Team Avatar sits around a campfire, telling ghost stories
Sokka tells a horror story about a haunted sword that fails to scare the others
