Katara tells a frightening story about a little girl named Nini, a friend of her mother's who disappeared during a harsh winter storm in the Southern Water Tribe
Katara recalls that despite Nini's vanishing, the villagers kept seeing smoke coming out of her chimney
The story leaves the entire group jumpy
Toph claims she can hear people screaming from under the mountain, frightening everyone
Hama appears out of the darkness behind the group and terrifies everyone
Hama warmly offers the group a safe place to stay for the night
The group gratefully accepts Hama's offer
At Hama's inn, the group enjoys spiced tea
Hama warns the group that people in the woods have recently been mysteriously disappearing during full moons
The group is concerned by Hama's warning
Hama reassures the group calmly that they will be safe with her
They all go out shopping in the marketplace for food
Hama and Katara become fast friends
Hama comments that she and Katara are "going to get along swimmingly"
Sokka overhears locals talking about strange disappearances of villagers during the full moon
The market clerk claims he has lost at least one delivery boy in the past full moons
Sokka, Toph, and Aang agree to scope the village to discover what could have angered the spirits
Sokka, Toph, and Aang agree to scope the village to discover what could have angered the spirits
Hama sends her guests back to the inn, saying she has errands to attend
Sokka remarks to Hama how mysterious her town is, becoming increasingly suspicious
Hama grins oddly and tells Sokka, "Mysterious town for mysterious children."
Sokka decides to investigate the house for anything suspicious while Hama is absent
Katara protests, saying it is rude
Sokka searches the house and uncovers a cupboard filled with wooden puppets in a hallway
Sokka finds a locked door in the attic
Sokka peers through the keyhole and sees a box in the room beyond
Sokka uses his sword to pick the lock
Sokka breaks into the room
Toph earthbends her meteorite bracelet into a key for the box
Hama walks in and catches them in the act
Hama opens the box for them and pulls out a whale tooth comb
Hama reveals she is from the Southern Water Tribe
Hama prepares a traditional Water Tribe dinner for the group
Hama explains she knows they are from the Southern Water Tribe because she heard them talking in the woods
Hama reveals she is a waterbender by bending their soup into their bowls
Katara is delighted by Hama’s waterbending
The rest of the group is curious about how Hama ended up in the Fire Nation
Hama tells the story of how she was taken from the South Pole into the Fire Nation
The Fire Nation captures all of the waterbenders during raids of Hama's tribe, reducing their numbers over time
Hama is eventually captured by the Fire Nation
Hama is the only one among those taken who manages to escape alive
Katara is deeply moved by Hama's tragic story
Katara expresses her delight at having found another Southern waterbender
Hama offers to teach Katara what she knows to carry on the tradition of the Southern waterbenders
Katara is overjoyed and consents, wishing to learn more about her tribe's cultural heritage
Hama tutors Katara, explaining how water exists in all forms even in the air
Hama draws water from the air
Hama freezes the water and shoots it at a nearby tree
Katara is fascinated by Hama's demonstration
Hama leads Katara to a field of fire lilies
Hama demonstrates that water can be drawn from anything alive
Hama pulls water from the flowers, leaving them dried and dead
Katara is amazed but becomes sad upon realizing the lilies were killed
Hama dismisses Katara's concerns, reminding her that they were simply flowers and that survival is most important
Hama tells Katara she has one more technique to teach, usable only during the full moon
Katara expresses hesitance due to rumors of disappearances
Hama reassures Katara, stating that two master waterbenders under a full moon should be able to handle anything
Aang, Sokka, and Toph believe the disappearances to be the work of an angry spirit and carry out an investigation
Aang briefly explores the area
Aang concludes the town is surrounded by natural beauty and the townsfolk have not angered the spirits
The group questions one of the locals, leading them to Old Man Ding
Old Man Ding tells the three he never actually saw a spirit but felt possessed and compelled to walk toward the mountain
Old Man Ding almost walks into a cave but regains control when the sun rises
Toph realizes she had heard screaming coming from under the mountain
Toph concludes the captured villagers must be imprisoned there
Katara and Hama arrive in the forest with the full moon in the night sky
Hama remarks sinisterly she has never felt so alive
Aang, Sokka, and Toph discover an underground prison in a cave
Toph metalbends the door open
Toph crafts her bracelet into a key to free the villagers from their shackles
The villagers tell Aang, Sokka, and Toph it was not a spirit that captured them, but the innkeeper Hama
Sokka and Aang go to stop Hama
Toph leads the villagers out
Hama reveals to Katara the story of how she escaped
Hama and the other waterbenders are imprisoned and separated from all water
guards pump dry air into the cells
guards tie the prisoners' arms and legs before giving them water to drink
Hama realizes that water exists within all living things
Hama begins to manipulate water in the bodies of rats that crawl into her cage
Hama secretly masters the technique over years
Hama takes control of one of the guards using bloodbending
Hama forces the guard to unlock her cage
Hama walks free after decades of imprisonment
Hama explains that bloodbending can only be used with the influence of the full moon
Katara has doubts about learning bloodbending, horrified by the idea of controlling another person's body
Hama declares the choice is not Katara's and insists they must enact vengeance on the Fire Nation
Hama says Katara must avenge her mother's death
Katara realizes Hama has been causing the villagers to disappear
Hama wickedly states the people of the Fire Nation deserve the same treatment they gave to the Southern waterbenders and insists Katara must learn bloodbending
Katara refuses to learn bloodbending and commands Hama to stop terrorizing the town
Hama, enraged at being denied, uses bloodbending to temporarily force Katara into submission
Katara regains control of her body
Katara reminds Hama that she also draws power from the moon and can overcome Hama's technique
Katara and Hama begin to battle, each drawing water from the grass and trees around them
Katara and Hama begin to battle, each drawing water from the grass and trees around them
It becomes obvious that Katara is the superior combatant
Katara appears on the cusp of victory
Aang and Sokka arrive to assist Katara
Aang and Sokka arrive to assist Katara
Hama uses bloodbending to control Aang and Sokka like puppets to attack Katara
Katara evades Aang and Sokka's attacks
Katara freezes Aang and Sokka to two separate trees to stop them
Hama frees Aang and Sokka
Hama sends Aang and Sokka flying at each other, with Sokka's sword pointing at Aang
Katara uses bloodbending to subdue Hama and protect Aang
Toph arrives with the other villagers
Villagers handcuff Hama and take her away, vowing to imprison her forever
Hama cackles in wicked glee as she is dragged off, saying imprisonment no longer matters
Hama sarcastically congratulates Katara on becoming a real bloodbender
Katara breaks down in tears
Aang and Sokka attempt to comfort Katara
Aang and Sokka attempt to comfort Katara

Nightmares_and_Daydreams.action: The gang arrives ahead of schedule at the Black Cliffs, the intended meeting place for the invasion force
Aang is stressed about the invasion being only four days away
Aang has a nightmare at night
Aang comes face to face with Fire Lord Ozai in his nightmare
Fire Lord Ozai tells Aang he forgot to wear his pants in the nightmare
Zuko enjoys his life of royalty
Zuko makes the most of his royal life
Zuko's relationship with Mai is going well
Aang stays awake training for twenty-four hours
Aang tries to get some sleep
Aang has a nightmare about forgetting to study for a math test administered by Ozai
Aang wakes up and starts walking around some koala sheep
Katara gets up and asks Aang to try to get some sleep
Mai tells Zuko that there is a war meeting coming up
Zuko says he was not invited to the war meeting
Zuko thinks his father does not want him there
Aang has a nightmare in which Ozai tells him he has overslept and missed the invasion, riding a flying, fire-breathing hippo cow
Aang wakes up and starts going crazy from lack of sleep, saying he is unraveling
Katara, Toph, and Sokka try to help Aang using different methods
Katara tries yoga with Aang in an artificial hot spring
Katara asks Aang how he feels
Aang says he feels hot, as if he is in the Fire Lord's palace and Ozai is shooting fireballs at him, making him panic
Katara suggests Aang should talk his problems out
Sokka dons his beard and mustache from "The Headband" and tries to be a psychiatrist, talking to Aang about his feelings on confronting Ozai
Aang asks how to calm himself
Sokka suggests Aang should scream into a koala sheep, which he says is a pillow
Aang tries Sokka's suggestion, but it does not make him feel better
Zuko confronts Azula, who is getting her hair done, about the war meeting and asks why she got invited and he did not
Azula tells Zuko he did not get an invitation because it is obvious he is supposed to be there, and tells him to stop acting like a paranoid child and go to the meeting
Zuko says he will not go to the meeting
Toph gives Aang a brutal massage using log-shaped pieces of earth and earthbending to create hard vibrations
Toph offers acupuncture with a boar-q-pine
Aang, petrified of the idea, runs away screaming
The gang asks Aang if he is okay
Aang answers that he may feel a bit better
The gang goes to sleep
Aang has a frightening nightmare, a jumbled montage expressing his fear and stress over the upcoming battle
Aang wakes up and decides not to sleep until after the invasion due to stress
Aang, now very tired from lack of sleep, daydreams about professing his love for Katara and kissing her, calling her his "forever girl"
Aang snaps out of the illusion
Aang lies to Katara, saying he was daydreaming about living underwater
Aang spars against the noodle Ozai he made in "The Headband"
Aang begins to hallucinate
Zuko is told by a servant that everyone is waiting for him at the meeting
Zuko realizes that his father does want him at the meeting
Aang tries to train
Aang starts hearing Momo and Appa talk
Aang hallucinates Momo and Appa fighting samurai-style
Aang hallucinates koala sheep cheering on Momo and Appa
Aang sees a six-armed Guru Pathik floating on a cloud with two bowls of onion and banana juice, singing about chakras
Aang sees rocks surrounding Guru Pathik and forming columns
Zuko tells Mai that his father treated him like his right-hand man
Zuko confesses that although he acted like the perfect prince, he was not being himself
Aang, completely exhausted, wanders around the campsite
Aang stumbles across what he thinks is a bed of clouds
Aang dismisses the bed as another hallucination
Aang’s friends inform him that it is a bed of koala sheep wool and is real
Aang’s friends explain they spent hours working on the bed so he could sleep
Aang insists he is not ready
Katara and the rest of the gang encourage Aang
Katara tells Aang he has been training for this since the day they met and that he is ready
Aang calms down and falls asleep
Aang has a dream in which he appears before the Fire Lord
Ozai says Aang forgot his pants
Aang smiles and says it is the Fire Lord who has forgotten his pants
Ozai covers himself, embarrassed that his "royal parts are showing"
Aang’s nerves are gone and he can now rest for the upcoming invasion

The_Day_of_Black_Sun,_Part_1:_The_Invasi.action: Aang cheerfully greets everyone on the morning of the invasion
Katara notes that Aang seems well rested
Aang confidently believes he can face the Fire Lord
Toph asks Aang if he is going to use the Avatar State against the Fire Lord
Aang explains that his seventh chakra was locked when Azula struck him with lightning, so he cannot use the Avatar State
A fleet of Southern Water Tribe ships appears on the horizon under a cloak of mist
Aang and Toph create makeshift docks in the cove
Sokka greets his father, Hakoda
Katara greets her father, Hakoda
Sokka asks Hakoda if he brought the people requested for the invasion
Hakoda replies that he did, but expresses uncertainty about some of them, especially the Foggy Swamp Tribe waterbenders
Bato wishes the Foggy Swamp Tribe waterbenders would put some pants on
Huu emerges from one of the boats and mystically declares that pants are an illusion, as is death
Due believes a rock in the water is a Fire Nation trap
Haru greets Katara, sporting a newly grown mustache
Katara hugs Haru
Tyro thanks Katara for helping them find their courage when they were imprisoned by the Fire Nation
Toph senses The Hippo and The Boulder approaching
Toph prepares to fight The Hippo and The Boulder
The Hippo and The Boulder tell Toph they are done with the earthbending tournament and want to help fight for the Earth Kingdom
There is a small explosion on one of the ships
Everyone rushes to see what happened
The mechanist, Teo, Pipsqueak, and The Duke emerge
The mechanist was working on a new type of bomb using peanut sauce, but it proved to be a failure
Pipsqueak and The Duke remark the bombs are destructive and delicious
Teo presents Aang with a gift, a brand new glider staff that contains a secret snack compartment
Warden Poon continues to taunt the imprisoned Iroh
Ming appears with Iroh's breakfast
Ming sneaks in some white jade tea for Iroh
The other guard leaves
Iroh expresses his gratitude for Ming's kindness
Everyone gathers to hear the invasion plan
Sokka attempts to brief the force about the invasion
Sokka becomes nervous and loses track of his speech, telling the story of how he met Aang
Hakoda takes over for Sokka and confidently rallies the small army
Hakoda explains the plan to storm the beach and reach the palace before the eclipse
Hakoda explains that during the eclipse, they can take on the firebenders and Aang can defeat the Fire Lord
Hakoda explains that after defeating the Fire Lord, they will control the Fire Nation Capital and end the Hundred Year War
Everyone cheers and prepares to depart
Appa wears armor
Aang shaves his head
Sokka is embarrassed by his earlier behavior
Aang reassures Sokka that he will have a chance to prove himself on the battlefield
Zuko begins writing a farewell note to Mai in his bedroom
The invasion force heads to the Great Gates of Azulon
The invasion force approaches the gates under the cover of mist
An alarm goes off as the invasion force approaches
A large net rises from both sides of the gate, blocking the path
The net catches fire
Groups of firebenders infiltrate the ships
Firebenders are shocked to discover that the ships are all empty
The invasion force passes the gate in submarine-like ships propelled by waterbending
The subs have a limited air supply
The invasion force resurfaces before reaching the beach
Ming visits Iroh with his supper, sneaking in extra rice
Iroh reveals to Ming that her gestures of kindness have made his imprisonment bearable
Iroh comments that Ming looks ill and insists she should go home
Ming insists that she is fine
Iroh explains that it would be better if Ming were not present that afternoon
Ming understands the subtext of Iroh's warning
The gang gets together one final time to wish each other good luck before parting ways
Aang and Katara say goodbye in private
Katara reveals she is proud of how much Aang has grown since they first met
Aang realizes how big this day is and that he may not survive
Aang decides to make his move and kisses Katara
Aang snaps out his glider and flies away to face the Fire Lord
Katara blushes and looks sadly toward the horizon
Katara hops on Appa
Katara creates a bubble around Appa's head so they can breathe underwater
Appa dives into the water as they commence their attack
Zuko mentally apologizes to Mai
Zuko leaves a note in Mai's bedroom
Zuko sits in front of a painting of his mother
Zuko confesses his earlier mistakes aloud
Zuko promises to do what is right
Zuko gets ready to leave
The subs evade capture at a gate armed with harpoon gun-type weapons
The subs arrive at the beach near the capital
Forces storm the plaza
New caterpillar-like tanks powered by earthbenders are released from the subs
The earthbender tanks face off against tundra tanks
Ground fighting ensues, followed by other warriors, benders, and eight supply trucks
The earthbender tanks easily defeat the Fire Nation guards
Huu uses his plantbending to create a giant monster form out of seaweed
Katara, Sokka, and Hakoda notice a threat from towers shooting missiles
Katara, Sokka, and Hakoda attack the towers with Appa
Katara and Sokka take out one tower
Hakoda destroys another tower but is injured in the process
Katara and Sokka take their father a short distance from the battle
Katara attempts to heal Hakoda
Hakoda is still unable to join the battle
Sokka pledges to his father and sister that he will lead the battle in his father's place
Sokka flies off on Appa
Sokka lands among the troops, which have been split up in mild disarray
Sokka reorganizes the troops into wedge formation
Sokka orders the troops to charge up to the palace
The invasion breaches the wall of the capital city
The situation appears to be going well
Aang enters the capital city
Aang finds the city completely deserted of people
Aang reaches the Fire Lord's throne room in the palace
Aang finds Ozai is nowhere to be found
Aang falls to his knees in anguish and yells, "Fire Lord Ozai, where are you?"

The_Day_of_Black_Sun,_Part_2:_The_Eclips.action: Aang rejoins the invasion force
Aang reveals that the whole city has been abandoned
The gang is surprised by the revelation
Sokka concludes that the Fire Lord must have expected the invasion and is likely hiding in a secret underground bunker
The group deliberates about what to do next
They agree to continue with the invasion
The invasion force continues toward the palace
Aang, Toph, and Sokka search for Fire Lord Ozai
Toph searches for Fire Lord Ozai
Sokka searches for Fire Lord Ozai
Toph locates the secret bunker inside a nearby volcano using her ability to see underground
They pass through tunnels of gas and magma
Toph opens the metal bunker using her metalbending ability
They corner a Fire Nation noble
The Fire Nation noble tells them where the Fire Lord's chamber is
The total eclipse begins
Aang blasts open the door of the chamber
They find Azula waiting for them instead of the Fire Lord
Azula reveals she has known about the invasion for months and is not surprised to find Aang alive
Zuko enters the chamber where his father is hiding
Zuko approaches Ozai and explains he is there to tell the truth
Ozai is amused and impressed that Zuko chose the moment of an eclipse to face him
Ozai dismisses his guards
Zuko explains that it was Azula who supposedly killed the Avatar in Ba Sing Se, not him
Ozai wonders why Azula would lie
Zuko reveals the Avatar is still alive and probably leading the invasion
Ozai becomes enraged upon hearing this news
Ozai orders Zuko to leave
Zuko refuses to leave and disregards his father's orders
Ozai threatens Zuko for his disobedience
Zuko pulls out his swords and demands that his powerless father sit and listen
Azula refuses to tell Aang, Sokka, and Toph her father's location
Toph's lie-detecting ability proves ineffective on Azula
Azula evades Toph's lie-detecting because she is an expert liar
Two Dai Li agents drop from the ceiling and use earthbending to protect Azula
Aang and Toph attack Azula
Aang and Toph attack Azula
The invasion forces start to take the capital city
All the firebenders surrender at the loss of their power
Zuko tells Ozai that all he ever wanted was for Ozai to love him
Zuko says he spent his exile trying to regain what he believed was his honor, but was really trying to please Ozai
Zuko angrily berates Ozai for cruelly challenging him to a duel as a child and banishing him for speaking out of turn
Ozai claims the act was to teach Zuko to respect his superiors
Zuko retorts that it was cruel and wrong
Zuko reveals his belief that the Fire Nation is doing wrong in the world and lies to itself about being benefactors
Zuko explains the Fire Nation has a responsibility to bring about an era of peace
Ozai laughs and mocks Zuko, thinking kindness is weak and realizing Zuko's older brother influenced him
Zuko proudly confirms his brother's influence, aggravating Ozai
Aang, Sokka, and Toph fight Azula and her Dai Li agents
Aang, Sokka, and Toph incapacitate Azula and her Dai Li agents
Azula evades capture, proving too agile for Aang and the others to take down
Sokka stops Aang and Toph from pursuing Azula further
Sokka realizes Azula is baiting them into wasting time
Azula taunts Sokka by hinting she has captured, imprisoned, and possibly tortured Suki
Sokka becomes enraged and rushes toward Azula
Azula prepares to stab Sokka with a concealed dagger
Toph senses Azula’s movement, disarms her, and pins her against the wall with a rock
Sokka seizes Azula and demands she tell him where Suki is
Azula refuses to comply
Zuko confesses that his uncle Iroh has been more of a father to him than Ozai
Zuko plans to set Iroh free from prison and beg for his forgiveness
Ozai continues to mock Zuko and Iroh
Zuko states his intention to join Team Avatar and help Aang stop the Fire Nation
Ozai wonders why Zuko would go through such trouble and tries to provoke a fight
Zuko puts his swords away and states he knows his own destiny and that it is Aang's destiny to defeat Ozai
Ozai calls Zuko a coward for approaching him only when he is powerless
Zuko departs the chamber
Ozai tries to bait Zuko into staying longer with knowledge about his mother
Zuko demands to know what happened
Ozai reveals the circumstances regarding the disappearance of Princess Ursa
Ozai mocks the death of Lu Ten, Iroh's son
Fire Lord Azulon orders Ozai to kill Zuko as punishment
Ozai admits he planned to follow through with Azulon's request
Ursa discovers Ozai's intentions and swears to protect Zuko at any cost
Ozai implies that Ursa arranged Azulon's death so Zuko would be spared and Ozai would become Fire Lord
Ursa is banished for her treason instead of being killed
Zuko realizes his mother may still be alive
Ozai states that banishment is far too light a punishment for treason and that Zuko's punishment will be far worse
Ozai senses the eclipse ending outside
Ozai shoots a powerful bolt of lightning at Zuko
Zuko catches the lightning
Zuko redirects the lightning back toward Ozai
An explosion blasts Ozai backward into the wall
Zuko quickly escapes the chamber
Ozai seethes with rage
Azula realizes she has regained her firebending
Azula escapes from Sokka and the others
Azula reveals her father's location, believing he could defeat them now that the eclipse is over
Sokka feels ashamed for wasting their time
Toph tells Sokka they had no chance against Azula since she knew about the invasion from the beginning
Aang thinks he could still face the Fire Lord without the eclipse
Sokka and Toph doubt this is a good idea and convince Aang to retreat
Sokka and Toph doubt this is a good idea and convince Aang to retreat
Aang, Sokka, and Toph return to the battle site and inform everyone of the Fire Nation's awareness of the invasion
fleets of hot air balloons and zeppelins emerge from behind the palace and move to attack the invasion force
Zuko goes to free his uncle from prison
Zuko discovers that Iroh has already busted out of his cell and is long gone
Iroh leaves behind dazed and unconscious guards
The leader of the guards tells Zuko he has never seen anything like it, calling Iroh a "one man army"
Aang and the troops attempt to stop the fleet of airships
Aang and the troops realize they are outmatched
The airships obliterate the submarines, making the invasion team's escape impossible
Hakoda tells Aang and the younger fighters to flee for their lives
The adults remain behind and surrender to be taken as prisoners
The younger fighters say their goodbyes to the adults
The rest of the gang, along with Haru, Teo, and The Duke, get onto Appa and fly away
Aang states that the Western Air Temple will be a safe place for them to stay
Azula watches from one of the airships as the group escapes
Azula decides not to pursue the group, confident they will have to return someday
Zuko follows Appa on a hot air balloon
Zuko has a look of strong determination on his face

The_Western_Air_Temple.action: elders of the invasion force are captured and become prisoners after the defeat in the Fire Nation on the Day of Black Sun
the gang travels to the Western Air Temple for refuge
Appa becomes tired from carrying so many people
the gang walks on foot
the group reaches a cliff that descends into a misty canyon below
Toph exclaims that they have arrived at the temple, though no one else sees it
Aang realizes they have arrived, as the temple is built on the underside of the cliff
Toph is amazed at the temple, despite previously remaining impassive
Zuko follows the group from the invasion in his war balloon
Zuko arrives first and descends into the temple
Zuko remembers the last time he had been to the Western Air Temple, a week after his banishment
In a flashback, Zuko, freshly burned and bandaged, proclaims he will scour the world for the Avatar for the rest of his life
Zuko calls his uncle Iroh lazy when Iroh suggests that he rest
Zuko feels ashamed of the memory
Zuko’s lament is cut short as he sees Appa fly overhead
Appa flies overhead
Zuko runs off to hide
Zuko makes camp near the temple in a wooded area
Zuko practices his speech to a badgerfrog to convince Team Avatar to accept him as Aang’s firebending teacher
Teo, Haru, and The Duke explore the temple
The gang discusses the next course of action after the invasion failed
Sokka suggests reverting to the old plan of Aang mastering all four elements and facing Fire Lord Ozai before Sozin's Comet
Aang expresses reluctance, remarking they do not know where to find a firebending teacher
Katara suggests looking for Jeong Jeong
Aang doubts they will ever see Jeong Jeong again
Aang, thinking it is hopeless, uses this as an excuse to run off and fly around the temple
Sokka and Katara are left to ponder the problem
Sokka and Katara are left to ponder the problem
Zuko practices what to say to the gang to convince them he is now on their side, with an impassive badgerfrog as his audience
Zuko realizes practicing with the badgerfrog will get him nowhere
Zuko wonders what Iroh would do
Zuko mimics his uncle's hoarse voice and rambles about "looking within himself to save himself"
Zuko finds himself unable to understand his mystical uncle even when pretending to be him
Zuko switches to mimicking Azula
Zuko accepts that threatening to do something unspeakably horrible to Aang and his friends will not get him far
Aang explores the temple on his glider
Katara and Sokka follow Aang on Appa, trying to convince him to stop and continue the discussion
Katara and Sokka follow Aang on Appa, trying to convince him to stop and continue the discussion
Katara and Sokka land with Appa
Katara and Sokka land with Appa
Zuko appears before them
Zuko tries to introduce himself politely
The group prepares to attack Zuko and refuses to trust him
Appa happily greets Zuko by licking his face
Aang is surprised by Appa's reaction
Zuko tries to explain that he has changed and wants to teach Aang firebending
Sokka and Katara point out Zuko's past wrongdoings, such as attacking their village and stealing Katara's necklace
Sokka and Katara point out Zuko's past wrongdoings, such as attacking their village and stealing Katara's necklace
Zuko apologizes anxiously
Zuko reveals he freed Appa from imprisonment in Ba Sing Se to get on their good side
No one except Toph believes Zuko
Zuko continues to apologize and accidentally reveals he hired Combustion Man to kill them
The gang resolutely refuses to allow Zuko into their group and threatens to attack if he does not leave
Zuko tries to offer himself as a prisoner
Katara attacks Zuko with waterbending and tells him never to come back
Zuko laments over how terribly things went at his camp
Sokka and Katara ponder Zuko's motives at the temple
Sokka and Katara decide that Zuko cannot be trusted
Aang has trouble supporting their conclusion
Aang recalls the time Zuko, as the Blue Spirit, rescued him from Zhao
Katara refuses to believe Zuko saved Aang out of compassion, insisting it was for his own benefit
Toph interjects, saying she sensed Zuko was sincere and honest
Toph believes the others are letting their hurt feelings affect their judgment
The others dismiss Toph's statement, noting she does not know what it was like to be chased by Zuko
Toph acknowledges that fact but points out Aang needs a firebending teacher
Aang adamantly refuses to accept Zuko as his teacher
Toph, frustrated at the group's prejudice, leaves the temple
Toph says she wonders who is really the blind one in the team before leaving
Zuko hears someone coming through the bushes while sleeping in his tent
Toph approaches Zuko’s tent
Zuko, unable to see in the darkness, immediately firebends at the intruder
Toph tries to defend herself
Toph’s feet are burned as she steps back
Zuko realizes it is Toph and apologizes, pleading with her to come back
Toph, completely blind, scurries away on her hands and knees in fear
Zuko tries to help Toph
Toph pushes Zuko away with earthbending
Toph departs
Zuko shouts in frustration, "Why am I so bad at being good?!"
The group wonders about Toph's whereabouts
Toph earthbends through the wall of the cliff and falls to the ground
Toph admits she went to visit Zuko the previous night, resulting in his burning her
Katara begins to heal Toph's feet
Toph explains it was sort of an accident
The gang ignores Toph's explanation and calls Zuko a serious threat
Combustion Man appears on a higher ledge of the temple and prepares to attack the group
Zuko arrives and prevents Combustion Man from getting a clear shot at the gang
Zuko redirects Combustion Man's attack, alarming the others below
The group finds cover
Zuko tries to prevent Combustion Man from attacking them further
Zuko pleads to call off the hit on the Avatar
Aang takes back his refusal of Zuko
Combustion Man turns on Zuko and blasts him over the edge of the temple
Zuko grabs hold of a vine below just in time
Aang and Katara use their bending to counterattack Combustion Man
Aang and Katara are at a severe disadvantage due to their lower position
Combustion Man changes tactics and begins targeting the pillars attaching the structure to the cliff
Toph realizes Combustion Man intends to blast the entire building off the cliffside
Katara notes she cannot attack without exposing herself or getting a good angle from the low ground
Sokka estimates Combustion Man's position based on the angles of the next two straight-line attacks
Sokka throws his boomerang, curving it around a corner
Boomerang hits Combustion Man square on his forehead tattoo and knocks him off his feet
Combustion Man turns toward the sound, blocking his chi and leaving him disoriented
Sokka is disappointed when the assassin manages to get back up
Sokka and others hurry back to cover
Combustion Man tries another attack, but his blocked chi causes the air around him to explode violently
Explosion causes the lower half of the structure Combustion Man was standing on to detach and plummet into the canyon
Combustion Man's metal arm falls spinning into the depths
Zuko reconvenes with Team Avatar
Aang thanks Zuko for his help
Zuko tries to reconcile with the group, admitting his hardships have changed him
Zuko affirms he wants to help end the Hundred Year War and restore balance
Zuko apologizes to Toph for burning her feet and admits he needs to better control fire
Aang is reminded of his own firebending accident with Katara
Aang accepts Zuko as his firebending teacher and asks the group to allow Zuko entry
Toph agrees to let Zuko join, wanting revenge for her burned feet
Sokka accepts Zuko to help with their plan to defeat the Fire Lord
Katara reluctantly agrees to let Zuko join because Aang believes it is right
Zuko promises not to let the group down
Sokka leads Zuko to his bedroom
Zuko unpacks his belongings
Sokka leaves and comments to Aang about how weird he finds Zuko's presence
Zuko takes out a picture of Iroh and remembers his uncle's advice about destiny
Katara appears in the doorway and confronts Zuko in cold fury
Katara reminds Zuko that he has struggled with doing the right thing before
Katara threatens Zuko with death if he betrays the team or hurts Aang
Katara leaves the room, slamming the door behind her
Zuko is left shocked and ponders

The_Firebending_Masters.action: Zuko notes Aang's nervousness and reminds him not to fear firebending
Aang is relieved to hear Zuko's reassurance
Zuko warns Aang about the dangers of firebending
Zuko commences Aang's firebending training at the Western Air Temple
Zuko tells Aang to show how much fire he can produce
Aang produces very little fire
Aang suggests a demonstration from Zuko would help
Zuko attempts to firebend but finds his firebending is very weak
Zuko tries to firebend at lower altitudes, hoping the height is the problem, but fails
Zuko apologizes to everyone, saying he has lost his firebending
Katara laughs out of spite and coldly jokes about how convenient it would have been if Zuko's firebending had failed months before
Aang hypothesizes that Zuko must not have enough rage to create fire like he used to
Sokka suggests they just need to make Zuko angry and starts poking him with his sword sheath
Zuko says he no longer wants to rely on anger and hatred
Toph encourages Zuko to learn from the original source of firebending, like how she learned earthbending from the badgermoles
Toph tells a story about running away, hiding in a cave, meeting the badgermoles, and learning earthbending from them
Aang claims the Air Nomads taught him that flying bison were the original airbenders and points out Appa
Zuko says that dragons were the original firebenders, but they have been extinct for some time
Aang comments that there used to be many dragons and Avatar Roku had a dragon, but Zuko does not find it helpful
Zuko tells Aang about the Sun Warriors, an ancient civilization who learned from the dragons, hoping their ruins could provide a new source for firebending
Zuko and Aang fly to the Sun Warrior ruins on Appa
Zuko and Aang land and start exploring the ruins
Aang nearly falls into a trap
Zuko is surprised to see the trap still works after centuries
Zuko bypasses the trap by running across the adjoining wall
Aang is hesitant to continue
Zuko encourages Aang to continue
Zuko and Aang find a massive stone carving depicting dragons and a Sun Warrior
Two of the dragons in the mural breathe fire onto the Sun Warrior
Aang is surprised because he thought the dragons were friends with the Sun Warriors
Zuko remarks that the dragons had a funny way of showing friendship
Aang asks Zuko what happened to the dragons
Zuko reveals that the dragons became extinct because Fire Lord Sozin started hunting them for glory and the title of 'Dragon'
Zuko tells Aang that his uncle Iroh killed the last living dragon
Aang and Zuko come across a locked door with a red sunstone on top while exploring the ruins
Zuko sees that a celestial calendar is used to open the door specifically on the summer solstice
Zuko uses his sword to reflect sunlight onto the jewel, causing the door to open
Aang compliments Zuko's intelligence, telling him he "doesn't care what anyone else thinks"
Aang and Zuko find various statues inside, which depict a firebending form called the Dancing Dragon
Aang imitates the first statue and discovers there are tile switches linked to the dance positions
Aang persuades Zuko to perform the dance with him
Zuko performs the dance with Aang
They activate an ancient key mechanism by completing the dance
A pedestal with a huge gold, egg-shaped gem rises in the middle of the room
Zuko picks up the gem despite Aang's warnings
Zuko inadvertently triggers a trap
The chamber doors close and viscous slime fills the room
Zuko and Aang find themselves trapped by the liquid in a barred skylight
Zuko and Aang are captured by the Sun Warrior tribe
The Sun Warrior tribe accuses them of attempting to steal Sun Warrior treasures
Zuko and Aang explain that Aang is the Avatar and they desire to learn the true form of firebending
Zuko and Aang are put through a ritual to carry a portion of the first fire up a mountain and present it to the firebending masters, Ran and Shaw
Aang is scared to try to hold the flame
The Sun Warrior chief comforts Aang by saying that fire is not just destructive, but it is life
Aang is nervous about his fire
Zuko gives Aang tips to remain steady and in control
Aang remains steady and in control
Aang and Zuko reach the lair of the masters
Aang and Zuko are sent to the top of a stairway
Aang and Zuko see two caves at the top
Aang and Zuko are warned of the master's judgement
Zuko’s lineage is blamed for wiping out the dragons
Aang is blamed for disappearing as the Avatar, allowing the dragons to go extinct
Aang becomes nervous again
Zuko convinces Aang they will see it through to the end and can handle anything
Aang and Zuko present their fires and each face one of the two caves
Aang loses his portion of the fire due to being distracted and scared
Aang tries to take a piece of Zuko’s fire, but accidentally knocks Zuko’s hands down, extinguishing his flame as well
Two dragons emerge from the two caves, to Zuko and Aang’s shock, and start flying in circles around them
Zuko and Aang earn the respect of the dragons by performing the firebending technique they learned from the statues, dancing with the dragons
The dragons breathe beautiful, multicolored flames around Zuko and Aang, revealing the true and harmonious way of firebending
Aang and Zuko realize that fire is not destruction, but life and energy like the sun
The dragons return to their caves
Zuko understands the reason he could not firebend was because he had lost his "inner fire"
Zuko recalls he used to draw his fire from rage, anger, and his desire to capture Aang
Zuko realizes he can no longer draw fire from rage after joining the group
Zuko learns from watching the dragons that he can draw fire from his desire to help bring balance to the world
Zuko describes his new feeling as "the sun but inside of you"
Aang realizes that fire is not destruction, but energy and life, and is able to firebend
Zuko mentions what Uncle Iroh told him
The chief explains that Iroh was the last outsider to visit the masters and deemed worthy to learn the secret
Zuko figures out that Iroh lied to keep the dragons alive so they could continue on
The chief explains he has no choice but to imprison Aang and Zuko forever to keep the dragons' existence a secret, which astonishes them both
The chief reveals he is joking, but is very serious that they do not tell anyone
Zuko and Aang show everyone the firebending moves they learned
Sokka pokes fun at Zuko and Aang by saying they would "tap-dance" their way to victory over the Fire Lord
Zuko tries to explain how serious and ancient the technique is
Everyone cannot look past the name of the form, the "Dancing Dragon," and do not take it seriously

The_Boiling_Rock,_Part_1.action: Zuko serves freshly brewed tea to his new friends while they hide out from the Fire Nation in the Western Air Temple
Zuko tries to tell his friends a tea joke his uncle once told him
Zuko only remembers the punch-line of the joke
No one finds the joke funny
Sokka serves tea
Sokka asks to talk to Zuko alone
Sokka asks Zuko where Fire Nation criminals and war prisoners would be imprisoned
Zuko refuses to tell Sokka, saying knowing will only make Sokka feel worse
Sokka states he is concerned for what happened to Hakoda after the failed invasion
Zuko hesitantly answers that Hakoda would most likely be held at the Boiling Rock, a prison in the center of a volcano in the ocean
Zuko is suspicious of why Sokka would ask such a thing so randomly
Sokka promises Zuko he is not planning anything
Sokka tries to sneak out on Appa in the middle of the night, assuming everyone else is asleep
Sokka climbs onto Appa’s saddle and finds Zuko waiting
Zuko berates Sokka for his poorly thought-out plan to infiltrate Boiling Rock prison on Appa
Sokka explains that the invasion plan was his idea and that it failed, resulting in his father and others being captured
Sokka informs Zuko of his intention to infiltrate the prison and rescue his father
Zuko decides to go along with Sokka
Zuko suggests they use the war balloon he stole to escape from the Fire Nation capital instead of Appa
The next morning, the gang finds a note written by Zuko stating that he and Sokka had gone fishing together
Zuko leaves Aang with "homework" to continue his firebending practice, annoying Aang
Aang is annoyed by the "homework"
Sokka and Zuko sit in awkward silence while flying in the war balloon
Sokka tries to break the ice by commenting on the "fluffy clouds"
Sokka tries to break the ice by whistling
Sokka and Zuko eventually talk about girls
Zuko says he misses his ex-girlfriend Mai, whom he left behind in the Fire Nation
Sokka says his first girlfriend turned into the moon
Zuko replies, "That's rough, buddy."
They spot the prison below and descend in the balloon toward it
Large clouds of steam emit from the boiling volcanic lake surrounding the prison
The steam makes the air outside the balloon warmer than the air inside it
The balloon crash-lands on the beach outside the prison walls
Sokka dumps the remains of the balloon into the boiling lake so it will not be found
Zuko believes Sokka does not have any real concrete plan and worries about getting caught
Sokka assures Zuko he will think of something so they can get off the Boiling Rock safely
They infiltrate the prison unnoticed
They steal Fire Nation prison guard uniforms and helmets
They impersonate guards and are ordered to oversee a quarrel
Chit Sang is harassed by a guard for not bowing down
Chit Sang declares bowing is not a real rule and walks off
The guard makes a whip of fire and lashes out at Chit Sang's back
Chit Sang firebends to defend himself
Chit Sang is sentenced to "the cooler" for firebending
The guard forces Sokka to come with him to put Chit Sang in the cooler
Sokka accompanies the guard to put Chit Sang in the cooler
Zuko is left in the prison yard
Zuko asks other guards where dangerous criminals such as prisoners of war are confined
Other guards respond to Zuko's question
Zuko and Sokka meet up later on
Zuko tells Sokka that Water Tribe prisoners are not located in this prison
Sokka believes he has failed yet again
Zuko tries to cheer Sokka up by imitating Iroh's wise sayings
Sokka cheers up, not from Zuko's attempt, but at the sight of Suki returning to her prison cell
Suki returns to her prison cell
Sokka locates Suki's cell and enters while wearing his helmet
Sokka attempts to kiss Suki
Suki, believing Sokka is a Fire Nation guard, pushes him down and knocks his helmet off
Suki realizes it is Sokka and is overwhelmed with happiness, driven to tears of joy
Suki reveals to Sokka that she knew he would come, contrary to Azula's claim
Suki and Sokka kiss
Zuko spots a guard coming and warns Sokka with knocks on the cell door
Zuko tries to keep the guard out, first with an excuse, then with physical force
Sokka comes out in his guard disguise and helps the guard arrest Zuko to prevent suspicion
Sokka whispers to Zuko that he will figure something out
Zuko allows himself to get captured
Zuko is interrogated by the prison's warden, who knows Zuko because the warden is Mai's uncle
The warden states that Zuko broke Mai's heart after leaving the Fire Nation and intends to turn him in to his father for a reward
Zuko and Suki do mopping duty together in the prison
Suki recognizes Zuko as the person who almost burned down her village and becomes agitated
Zuko awkwardly apologizes to Suki and says, "It's nice to see you again," then moves away
Sokka, still disguised, gathers Zuko and Suki for a plan
Chit Sang overhears the plan and invites himself in
They plan to use a cooler to ride their way out like a boat
Zuko volunteers to unscrew a cooler cell free by pretending to mop and bumping into Chit Sang
Zuko and Chit Sang fight, and Zuko firebends
Zuko is sent to a cooler cell
Zuko uses his breath of fire technique to keep warm and successfully unscrews the cooler free
As guards pass by, Zuko and Sokka hide
Zuko and Sokka overhear that war prisoners are coming the next day
Sokka thinks there is a chance his father is among the prisoners
They bring the loose cooler cell
Chit Sang brings his girlfriend and best friend to the escape
Sokka decides against escaping and stays at the prison to break his father out
Suki agrees to stay
Zuko agrees to stay
Chit Sang leaves with his friends
At dawn, Chit Sang and his friends sweat in the cell-boat
Chit Sang breaks a piece of metal to use as an oar
Chit Sang is burned by the steaming water and yells out in pain
Fire Nation guards become aware of an escape attempt
Fire Nation guards catch the cooler cell drifting away
Sokka, Suki, and Zuko hide and see prisoners come out of the gondola
Sokka, Suki, and Zuko hide and see prisoners come out of the gondola
Sokka, Suki, and Zuko hide and see prisoners come out of the gondola
Sokka loses hope of seeing his father
Hakoda is called out as the last person

The_Boiling_Rock,_Part_2.action: new prisoners arrive at the Boiling Rock and are lined up to be introduced to the warden
Sokka, disguised as a prison guard, is glad to see his father Hakoda among the new arrivals
The warden explains the history of the Boiling Rock, mentioning that no one has ever escaped
Hakoda defies the warden's orders to look him in the eyes and trips him
Sokka snickers at Hakoda's action
The warden angrily orders the new arrivals to be confined to their cells
Sokka enters his father's cell, still disguised as a guard
Hakoda almost attacks Sokka, not recognizing him
Hakoda recognizes Sokka and is overjoyed
The warden interrogates the recaptured Chit Sang and grills him to find out who was helping with the escape plan
The warden believes Chit Sang or his friends are not smart enough to think of such an intricate plan by themselves
Chit Sang refuses to talk, saying he is not "a squealer"
The warden orders his guards to torture Chit Sang by holding him upside down in his chair until he reveals the identity of the person
Sokka and his father devise a plan for Suki, Zuko, and themselves to escape the prison
Sokka makes his way to Zuko's prison cell to share the plan
Sokka is interrupted by two guards who arrive to take Zuko away
Sokka feigns brutalizing Zuko alone inside his cell to reveal when they should next meet
Zuko is taken into another cell by the guards
Zuko is confronted by his ex-girlfriend, Mai, in the new cell
The warden returns to see if Chit Sang will talk
Chit Sang reveals that somebody helped him, only indicating it was someone disguised as a prison guard
Mai, hurt that Zuko broke up with her, berates him for leaving only a farewell letter as he left to join the Avatar
Zuko tries to explain he had to leave in order to save the Fire Nation
Mai counters that Zuko is betraying the Fire Nation
Zuko firmly tells Mai he does not see it that way
Mai crosses her arms and looks to the side
Sokka goes to see Suki in her cell to fill her in on the escape plan
Sokka explains the plan to escape on the prison's gondola using the warden as a hostage
Sokka tries to leave the cell casually
Two guards find Sokka and tell him the warden wants to see him
Sokka is lined up with several other prison guards for inspection by Chit Sang
Chit Sang inspects the guards to point out who was devising the escape plan
Sokka is visually terrified, but neither the warden nor most other guards notice
Chit Sang points to the guard that bullied him the previous day
The warden believes the false information and has the accused guard taken away for interrogation
The accused guard is interrogated by the warden
Princess Azula and Ty Lee interrupt the interrogation
The warden is initially furious at the interruption, but bows and apologizes after seeing Azula
Azula asks the warden what he is doing
The warden explains he foiled an escape attempt allegedly plotted by the guard
Azula glances at the guard and declares he is not the traitor
Azula explains to the stunned warden that she knows because she is "a people person"
Sokka tricks a guard into unlocking all of the cells during lockdown
Guard unlocks all of the cells, letting the prisoners out into the yard
Sokka meets up with Suki and Hakoda outside
Hakoda tries pushing a big, tough-looking prisoner to start a riot
The tough-looking prisoner tells Hakoda he has been working on anger management
Chit Sang appears and surprises Sokka
Chit Sang tells Sokka he did not expose him and wants in on the escape plan
Sokka tells Chit Sang they are trying to escape right now and asks if he knows how to start a riot
Chit Sang picks up a bystander and yells "Hey, RIOT!", starting a fight among the inmates
Every inmate starts fighting spontaneously
The prisoner Hakoda tried to provoke decides to stop controlling his anger and joins the riot
The warden appears and angrily demands to know how all the prisoners were released into the yard during lockdown
The guard who let the prisoners out sneaks away guiltily
Mai interrogates Zuko
a guard under orders of Mai's uncle appears to protect Mai during the riot
Zuko firebends at the guard's feet and rushes out of the room
The guard attempts to shield Mai from the fire, preventing her from reacting
Zuko locks Mai and the guard in the cell
Zuko escapes to the yard and meets up with the others
Sokka and Zuko argue about how to capture the warden
Suki takes the initiative and goes after the warden on her own
Suki runs on the heads of the rioting prisoners and guards and scales the walls of the yard with acrobatics
Suki disables the guards protecting the warden, ties him up, and gags him
The group meets up with the warden as their hostage and makes their way to the gondola
The group makes it onto the gondola
Zuko breaks the lever so the guards cannot stop them
The group begins to ascend
Hakoda spots Azula and Ty Lee and asks Zuko who they are
Zuko responds that they are a problem
Ty Lee runs onto the gondola's cable
Azula propels herself with firebending toward the gondola
Suki fights Ty Lee
Sokka and Zuko fight Azula on top of the gondola
Sokka and Zuko fight Azula on top of the gondola
The warden breaks free of his binding and yells at the guards to cut the wire
Chit Sang restrains the warden
The warden's orders are relayed to the guards at the prison
The guards begin to cut the wire after some initial hesitation
Azula and Ty Lee propel themselves onto another oncoming gondola on a separate cable
Azula tells her brother goodbye, assuming he will fall to his death
guards are about to finish cutting the cable
Mai disables the guards, causing them to drop the saw
Mai pins the guards with her daggers, angering Azula
a guard asks Mai what she is doing
Mai replies, "Saving the jerk who dumped me."
Mai frees the line, allowing the group's gondola to start moving again
The group makes it out of the volcano, leaving the warden behind in the gondola
Hakoda tells the warden that the non-escape record is finally broken
Suki wonders how they are going to leave
Zuko stops running, figures Azula must have come to the Boiling Rock somehow, and spots an unmanned Fire Nation airship below the cliff
The group uses the airship to escape off the island
Mai is detained by guards and brought before Azula
Azula asks the guards to leave her and Mai alone
Ty Lee watches from the sidelines fearfully
Azula demands to know why Mai saved the escapees, knowing the consequences
Mai tells Azula that she does not know people as well as she thinks
Mai tells Azula that she loves Zuko more than she fears Azula
Azula retorts that Mai is the one who miscalculated
Azula and Mai get in position to battle
Ty Lee intervenes by blocking Azula’s chi
Mai and Azula are surprised by Ty Lee’s action
Azula slumps onto the concrete
Ty Lee and Mai try to escape
Ty Lee and Mai are quickly captured
Azula, being dragged up by her guards, calls them fools
Azula orders the guards to let Ty Lee and Mai rot in prison so she will never have to see their faces again
Sokka and Zuko exit their newly captured airship
Aang and the others remark that Sokka and Zuko's fishing trip must have been crazy

Hakoda, Suki, and Chit Sang exit the ship and present themselves to the others
Katara is overjoyed to see her father and embraces him and Sokka
Zuko happily watches the reunion

The sun rises

Aang airbends the large temple doors closed to protect the others

Aang notices Zuko looking out at the airships

Zuko runs toward the ships

The temple continues to be bombarded

Aang and the others fly away on Appa
Zuko rises out of the mist on another airship

Aang guides Appa downward

Zuko thanks Sokka
Sokka pushes Zuko impatiently out of the tent
Sokka rubs his hands together eagerly
Sokka peeks outside and beckons Suki to come in
Sokka finds that Zuko had only taken a few steps away from his tent

Zuko and Katara approach Aang to borrow Appa
Zuko and Katara approach Aang to borrow Appa
Aang sarcastically asks if it is Katara's turn to "take a little field-trip with Zuko"

Katara tells Aang her intentions
Aang tries to dissuade Katara from going, saying revenge is never the answer and comparing her to Jet
Katara refuses to listen and points out that Jet had attacked innocent people
Sokka agrees with Aang
Katara claims that Sokka did not love their mother like she did
Aang tells Katara a metaphor told to him by monks, comparing revenge to a two-headed snake
Aang tries to convince Katara to choose forgiveness rather than vengeance
Katara says that forgiveness for the man who killed her mother is an impossibility
Katara and Zuko prepare to take Appa without Aang's consent
Aang catches Katara and Zuko in the act and confronts them
Aang allows Katara and Zuko to take Appa, believing it is a journey Katara needs to take
Aang pleads with Katara to choose forgiveness rather than revenge

Katara and Zuko depart

Katara and Zuko infiltrate the base
Katara and Zuko find their way to a room containing the information they need

Katara and Zuko discover that the Southern Raiders are stationed at Whaletail Island
Katara recalls her personal experiences when she lost her mother
Eight-year-old Katara spots black snowfall and goes to look for her mother, Kya, at their home

Kya comforts Katara, telling her everything will be all right

Katara holds her mother's necklace and agrees

Zuko interrogates the captain
Katara realizes by appearance that the captain is not the man who killed her mother

Yon Rha is sent by his grumpy mother to get fresh tomato-carrots from the marketplace
Yon Rha goes to the marketplace
It begins to rain on Yon Rha's way back
Yon Rha senses he is being stalked
Yon Rha swivels around and shoots a blast of fire at a nearby tree
Yon Rha unsuccessfully attempts to uncover the suspects
Yon Rha continues on his way
Zuko ambushes and subdues Yon Rha

Yon Rha realizes Katara's identity and recalls the events of that fateful day

Yon Rha commences an expedition to eliminate the remaining bender

Yon Rha pleads for his life, offering his own mother to even the situation

Yon Rha sobs in the rain
Katara waits on a dock on Ember Island for the rest of the group to arrive on Appa

Zuko asks Aang what he is going to do when he faces Ozai
Aang looks away with uncertainty

Aang is disappointed

The actress playing Katara gives a long-winded speech about hope

Sokka and Katara are disappointed by their portrayals

The real Katara is annoyed by the actress's line

The real Aang and Zuko are greatly embarrassed by the scene

Katara is overly infatuated with Jet
Sokka and Princess Yue kiss at the North Pole

The real Sokka sheds tears over the scene

The rest of Team Avatar groans in annoyance over the display

The audience is shocked
Toph wears a big smile in delight of her character portrayal
Zuko and Iroh go their separate ways because Iroh thinks Zuko's hair is too long
Iroh leaves Zuko due to Zuko's long hair
Everyone corners Princess Azula

Azula and Aang battle atop the drill

Sokka replies to Zuko, saying "Y'know, it was very unclear."
Zuko and Katara are shown imprisoned in the cave
Katara actress flirts with Zuko actor, making real Zuko and Katara uncomfortable
Real Zuko and Katara slide away from each other
Katara actress tells Zuko actor she does not think of the Avatar romantically
Katara actress and Zuko actor embrace
Real Aang storms off, unable to deal with the scene
Zuko (in the play) sides with Azula and pushes over Iroh, telling him he hates him for all time

Aang (in the play) enters the Avatar State in the Earth Kingdom palace

Azula (in the play) poses with Mai and Ty Lee actresses

The team looks on in disbelief
Katara meets up with Aang outside during the intermission
Aang is greatly upset by the play
Sokka asks Suki to sneak him backstage so he can give the Sokka actor some of his jokes

Toph cheers Zuko up
Toph gives Zuko an affectionate punch

Toph laughs at Zuko
Sokka reads some of his lines to the Sokka actor backstage

Aang tells Katara he hoped they would be together after the invasion

Sokka's actor asks Toph if she and Aang have a "rocky relationship"
Sokka squeals with joy at the fact that his actor used his line

Ozai and Aang battle

Aang screams "No-o-o-o-o-o!" as he dies, waving a huge red piece of cloth around him

Ozai gives a speech about how the world is now his
The crowd gives Ozai a standing ovation
The team looks on aghast
The group walks back to the beach house
The group talks about how the play was absolutely terrible
Sokka comments that at least "the effects were decent"

Sozin%27s_Comet,_Part_1:_The_Phoenix_Kin.action: Aang practices his firebending at the Fire Lord's beach house
Zuko watches Aang and demands more ferocity from him
Aang makes a feeble attempt at a particular firebending move

Aang’s friends declare they will fight alongside him to have a better shot at defeating Ozai
Aang’s friends declare they will fight alongside him to have a better shot at defeating Ozai
Aang’s friends declare they will fight alongside him to have a better shot at defeating Ozai
Team Avatar joins together in a group hug, including a reluctant Zuko
Team Avatar joins together in a group hug, including a reluctant Zuko
Team Avatar joins together in a group hug, including a reluctant Zuko
Team Avatar joins together in a group hug, including a reluctant Zuko
Zuko teaches Aang the technique of lightning redirection

Katara finds a picture of a baby in the attic and shows it to everybody, thinking it is Zuko
