Aang and Momo fall asleep
A strange small island suddenly appears off shore
Aang is unconsciously drawn toward the island
Momo flies after Aang
The others prepare to leave the beach house
The others cannot find Aang anywhere
The others check the beach and find Aang's footprints in the sand, leading into the ocean
The others deduce Aang did not run away, was not captured, and is not on a Spirit World journey
The group decides to split up to search Ember Island
Toph states she wants a life-changing field trip with Zuko and goes with him
Suki and Katara find a crowd shouting Aang's name, but realize it is the Ember Island actor
Toph tells Zuko her life story
Zuko tells Toph they should focus on looking for Aang
Toph calls this "the worst field trip ever."
The search is futile; no one finds Aang
The group realizes Momo is gone as well
Sokka believes Appa has eaten Momo and climbs into Appa's mouth
Everyone looks to Zuko as a leader and expert on tracking Aang
The group flies on Appa toward the Earth Kingdom
Fire Lord Ozai prepares for departure with a grand ceremony at the Royal Plaza
Ozai orders Azula to stay in the Fire Nation
Azula begins to panic, claiming it is not fair and not to be treated like Zuko
Ozai explains that Azula has an important job to watch over the home field and appoints her the new Fire Lord
Ozai rewards Azula for her loyalty
Azula concedes that the title of "Fire Lord Azula" seems appropriate
Ozai declares himself the Phoenix King, ruler of the world
Ozai departs for the airship base
Zuko leads the group into a tavern
The group finds June, a bounty hunter, who has a shirshu that can track Aang
Momo wakes Aang up
Aang sees with great surprise that he is on a mysterious island in the middle of the sea

Sozin%27s_Comet,_Part_2:_The_Old_Masters.action: Zuko convinces June to use her shirshu, Nyla, to track Aang using the scent of his staff
June attempts to track Aang with Nyla, but the shirshu is unable to track him
June explains that Aang is "gone"
Sokka asks if Aang is dead
June clarifies that Aang is not dead, he just does not exist
Aang and Momo awaken on a strange island in the middle of the sea
Aang and Momo wonder where they are
Aang deduces they are not in the Spirit World because he can still bend
Aang decides to climb to the top of the island to see if they can discover anything
Zuko realizes the group cannot track down Aang and concludes Iroh is the only one who could possibly defeat Ozai
Zuko brings back Iroh's rancid sandal as a smell sample for Nyla to track
Nyla runs off to find the source
Team Avatar follows Nyla on Appa
The group is led to the breached Outer Wall of Ba Sing Se after a day-long journey
The group decides to camp out for the night and begin again at dawn
The group is woken up by fire surrounding their encampment
Jeong Jeong, Piandao, Pakku, and Bumi show themselves and greet the group
Aang wanders around the island
Aang finds a large hexagonal symbol on the ground
Aang tries to bend the symbol but is unable to
Aang deduces the symbol is not made of rock or normal earth
Aang decides to contact Roku for help
Roku confesses he does not know where they are
Roku sees Aang is frustrated about having to kill the Fire Lord
Roku confesses his disciplined outlook as Avatar led to the War, as sparing Fire Lord Sozin cost him his life
Roku tells Aang he must be decisive
Roku disappears
The gang and the Order embrace
Pakku reveals he is engaged to Sokka and Katara's grandmother
Toph wonders why they are surrounded by old people
The Order reveals they are members of the Order of the White Lotus, a secret organization focused on beauty and philosophy, and were summoned to Ba Sing Se by Iroh, the Grand Lotus
The gang exclaims they are looking for Iroh
The masters agree to lead the gang to Iroh
Bumi notices someone important is missing from the group and inquires about Momo's whereabouts
Sokka responds that he and Aang are missing
Bumi states that Aang and Momo will be all right as long as they have each other
Bumi leads the others to the White Lotus camp to find Iroh
Aang summons Avatar Kyoshi for wisdom
Kyoshi recalls her encounter with Chin the Conqueror, severing Kyoshi Island from the mainland to stop Chin, inadvertently causing his death
Aang reasons that Kyoshi technically did not kill Chin
Kyoshi tells Aang she sees no difference and would have killed Chin herself to keep the world in balance
Kyoshi tells Aang only justice will bring peace and disappears
Aang becomes agitated by Kyoshi's response and wishes he never asked her for wisdom
Bumi reveals that he single-handedly liberated the city of Omashu during the Day of Black Sun
Bumi frees himself from his cage with earthbending
Bumi casts out Fire Nation buildings from Omashu
Bumi laughs and eats rock candy as he launches Ozai's gigantic statue out of the city
Fire Nation soldiers, powerless due to the eclipse, helplessly flee
Bumi asks the others if they did anything interesting on the day of the eclipse
Zuko and Sokka look awkwardly at each other
Zuko and Sokka claim that they did not do anything interesting
Aang looks deep within himself and summons Avatar Kuruk for wisdom
Kuruk explains that during his time as Avatar he was laid back and let the four nations work their own problems out
Koh the Face Stealer punishes Kuruk for his laziness by dragging his love Ummi into the Spirit World
Kuruk blames himself for what happened to Ummi
Kuruk tells Aang he must actively shape his own destiny and the destiny of the world
Kuruk disappears
Aang despairs that three of his past lives are telling him to do what he does not want to do
The gang arrives at the White Lotus encampment
Zuko is shown to Iroh's tent
Zuko sits in front of the tent, ashamed and worried that Iroh hates him for his betrayal
Katara sits and comforts Zuko, believing Iroh would forgive him if he was truly sorry
Zuko enters the tent and finds his uncle asleep, snoring loudly
Zuko sits down to wait for Iroh to wake, with a wry, affectionate smile
Aang interacts with Momo and realizes the three past lives he asked were not Air Nomads and cannot understand his position
Aang summons the previous Air Nomad Avatar, Yangchen, to ask for wisdom
Aang and Yangchen agree that all life is sacred
Yangchen insists that, as the Avatar, Aang has a duty to protect the world
Aang argues that the monks taught him to detach himself from the world for spiritual enlightenment
Yangchen counters that the Avatar cannot detach from the world and insists Aang must do whatever it takes to protect it
Yangchen disappears
Aang laments to Momo that he has no other choice but to kill Ozai
Iroh wakes up to find Zuko waiting for him
Zuko tearfully apologizes for all he has done
Iroh grabs and hugs Zuko, surprising him
Iroh states he was never angry at Zuko, but was afraid Zuko had lost his way
Zuko admits that he had lost his way
Iroh establishes that Zuko found his way again and did so by himself
Iroh mentions he is very happy that Zuko found his way to the encampment
Zuko jokes that it was not that hard as Iroh "has a pretty strong scent"
Aang wakes as a new day begins
Aang sees mountains in the distance moving closer
Aang realizes the island itself is moving
Aang swims around the side of the island
Aang sees a giant arm paddling
Aang understands the island is a gigantic sea animal
Aang starts swimming toward the animal's face
Team Avatar has a meal with Iroh at the camp
Zuko asks Iroh to stop the Fire Lord since Aang is missing
Iroh says he is not sure he could defeat Ozai
Iroh explains it would not be the right way for the War to end, as history would see it as senseless violence and a brother killing a brother for power
Iroh says only the Avatar should defeat the Fire Lord
Iroh rejects Zuko’s claim to the throne and tells Zuko he must take Ozai’s place as Fire Lord to stop the War
Iroh believes Zuko has restored his own honor and that his destiny is to restore the honor of the Fire Nation
Iroh says his own destiny is to reclaim Ba Sing Se in the name of the Earth Kingdom
Iroh tells Zuko he must face Azula before claiming the throne and insists he cannot do it alone
Zuko asks Katara to come to the Fire Nation with him
Katara gladly accepts Zuko’s request
Sokka, Suki, and Toph plan to intercept the Fire Nation air fleets and help Aang if needed
Iroh and the White Lotus members bid farewell to Zuko and Katara on Appa, and Sokka, Suki, and Toph on an eel hound
Zuko asks Iroh what he will do after reconquering Ba Sing Se
Iroh responds that he will reopen his tea shop, the Jasmine Dragon, and play Pai Sho every day
Aang finds that the swimming island is a lion turtle
lion turtle stops swimming and lifts Aang onto its paw
Aang asks the lion turtle for wisdom on how to stop the Fire Lord without taking his life
lion turtle answers Aang with words of wisdom
lion turtle brings up a paw, touching Aang's chest and forehead with two claws, causing a green light to illuminate
Aang does not understand the turtle's words, but is comforted by the wisdom
lion turtle lets Aang on land
Aang waits for the arrival of Fire Lord Ozai
The air fleets of the Fire Nation led by Ozai prepare to depart
The air fleets watch as Sozin's Comet enters the Earth's atmosphere
The sky turns orange-red
Aang watches in anticipation
Aang prepares to save the world

Sozin%27s_Comet,_Part_3:_Into_the_Infern.action: Sozin's Comet flies through the Earth's atmosphere
Zuko and Katara continue on their journey to the Fire Nation
Zuko expresses his worry that Aang will lose against Ozai
Katara is also worried
Katara assures Zuko that Aang is going to come back
Azula is cared for and cleaned by her servants before her coronation as Fire Lord
a servant offers Azula a cherry
Azula spits out a cherry pit and believes it to be an assassination attempt
the servant pleads for mercy
Azula spares the servant’s life but orders her to leave the castle
Azula starts bossing the other servants around
Sokka, Suki, and Toph make it to the airship fleet
Sokka, Suki, and Toph believe they are too late as the ships begin to depart for the Earth Kingdom
The ships begin to depart for the Earth Kingdom
Toph earthbends Sokka, Suki, and herself aboard one of the ships
The leader of the Dai Li and six other Dai Li agents arrive before Azula in the Fire Lord's throne room
Azula berates the Dai Li for being late, noting it is enough time for her to be assassinated
The leader of the Dai Li states that they would not betray Azula
Azula states that the Dai Li probably told Long Feng the same thing before siding with her
Azula banishes the Dai Li from the Fire Nation for disloyalty, surprising them
The Dai Li leave the throne room
Azula tells the Dai Li to send the next group in on their way out
Toph knocks on the control room door
Toph uses metalbending to incapacitate the soldiers and captain
Sokka has an idea to get rid of the rest of the ship's crew
Sokka poses as the ship's captain on a loudspeaker, ordering all crew members to go to the bomb bay to celebrate a crew member's birthday
A crew member is overjoyed and pleasantly surprised, believing the captain remembered his birthday
Sokka releases the platform underneath the bomb bay, causing all of the ship's crew to fall screaming into the ocean
The ship is at a low enough altitude so that the entire crew survives with minimal injury
Sokka increases the ship's speed to catch up to Ozai's flagship
Lo and Li approach Azula and question her about banishing so many people
Azula states that none of them could be trusted and that they would all eventually betray her, like Mai and Ty Lee
Lo and Li suggest postponing the coronation
Azula becomes more paranoid and orders Lo and Li to duel each other in an Agni Kai
Lo and Li lament that neither of them are firebenders
Azula banishes Lo while pointing at Li
Azula tells Li that she can stay
Lo and Li are confused as to who is really banished
The Order of the White Lotus prepares for battle outside Ba Sing Se's walls
Iroh feels the power of the comet
Iroh releases a powerful blast of fire that breaks through the wall
The rest of the Order join in and invade the city
The Order begins to take down the comet-enhanced Fire Nation soldiers
Azula looks in a mirror and tries to do her hair by herself, resulting in a very messy style
Azula, agitated, haphazardly cuts her bangs
Azula looks into the mirror and sees her mother Ursa reflected behind her
Ursa tells Azula she would not miss her own daughter's coronation as Fire Lord
Ursa says Azula's use of fear to control people has left her confused
Azula replies that she had no other choice and could not trust anyone
Azula takes pride in her power of fear and points out that even Ursa feared her
Ursa denies fearing Azula and says she really loves her
Azula, enraged, throws her brush into her mother's reflection
Azula discovers Ursa was never really there at all
Azula begins to sob to herself
The ship Sokka and the others took control of cannot catch up to Ozai's flagship, and they are afraid they are too late
Ozai uses the comet's power to begin to scorch the Earth Kingdom below with an amazing display of firebending
Momo and Aang watch in the distance as Ozai's torrent of fire rains down upon the land
Aang has Momo leave him
Aang destroys the flagship's engine using earthbending and firebending, catching Ozai's attention
Ozai and Aang stare each other down for a moment
Ozai removes his Phoenix King garments and propels himself toward Aang to fight
The flagship lands on the earth pillars below
Sokka and the others wonder what happened and see to their happiness that Aang has returned
Ozai boasts that the Avatar has at last been offered up to him by the universe as an act of providence
Aang says their fight is not necessary and that Ozai has the power to stop destroying the Earth Kingdom
Ozai declares he has all the power in the world and unleashes a fury of comet-enhanced firebending, beginning the battle
Ozai and Aang's firebending attacks collide, creating a massive explosion in midair
Sokka cheers Aang on while watching the fight
Suki wonders if they should help Aang
Sokka believes they should not interfere and should concentrate on stopping the other ships from destroying the Earth Kingdom
The fleet begins to rain fire down upon the land
Sokka crashes their stolen airship into the other ships
The airship falls short of crashing into all of the other ships
Sokka and Toph are separated from Suki as their ship begins to break apart
The ship begins to break apart
Sokka and Toph are forced to continue without Suki
Azula's coronation begins at the palace
Zuko and Katara arrive on Appa, interrupting the coronation
Azula suggests settling ownership of the crown in an Agni Kai
Katara warns Zuko that Azula is trying to take them down separately
Zuko acknowledges Katara's warning and senses something "off" about Azula, deciding to face her alone
Zuko and Azula begin their fight with intense firebending powered by the comet
Aang and Ozai are evenly matched in battle
Aang counters Ozai's fierce firebending
Aang is unable to land any successful hits, even with all four elements
Ozai proves too powerful and quick
Ozai, tired of Aang evading, unleashes a relentless barrage of lightning
Aang dodges the lightning attacks
Aang catches the last lightning bolt, surprising Ozai, and prepares to redirect it
Aang, reluctant to kill Ozai, redirects the lightning into the sky
Ozai takes advantage of Aang's mercy and sends a blast at him while he recovers
Aang stops most of the attack with earthbending
Aang is blasted off the top of the rock and falls helplessly toward a shallow lake
Aang uses waterbending to save himself from a fatal fall
Aang looks up and sees Ozai swiftly approaching, eager to resume the fight
Zuko continues his duel with Azula
Katara watches from the sidelines
Zuko and Azula's attacks set fire to the surrounding buildings
Zuko counters all of Azula's attacks skillfully
Zuko takes the offensive and grounds Azula with a spinning kick of fire
Zuko taunts Azula for not using lightning and boasts he will redirect it
Azula becomes infuriated and prepares a lightning attack
Azula sends the lightning at Katara instead of Zuko
Zuko crosses the lightning's path, shielding Katara from the bolt
Zuko is left critically wounded, with a large red and purple wound near his heart, and is incapable of fighting
Azula begins to attack Katara furiously, laughing maniacally, before Katara can reach Zuko
Aang is overwhelmed and cornered by Ozai
Aang earthbends a shield around himself
Ozai taunts Aang for being weak
Ozai insults the Air Nomads, saying they did not deserve to exist
Ozai mocks Aang and tells him to prepare to die
Ozai attacks Aang

Sozin%27s_Comet,_Part_4:_Avatar_Aang.action: Ozai continuously blasts away at Aang's earth shield and taunts him
Toph metalbends the rudder to make the airship spiral into the others
A Fire Nation soldier spots Toph and Sokka and tries to blast them off using firebending
Toph and Sokka evade the attack but fall off the side of the airship
Sokka tries to break the fall using his sword, but lands on a platform and breaks his leg
Toph is left dangling off the edge, holding Sokka's hand
Two firebenders approach to attack Sokka and Toph on either side
Sokka uses his boomerang and sword to ward off the firebenders, losing both weapons in the process
More firebenders come, and Sokka and Toph are seemingly doomed
Suki pilots an incoming airship, saving Sokka and Toph, who land on it
Ozai overwhelms Aang's stone sphere with a charged fire bomb
Aang nearly loses consciousness from the heat
Ozai increases his assault and charges up an attack powerful enough to destroy the sphere completely
The impact throws Aang backward with his back against a jagged stone wall
Aang's scar aligns with a protruding piece of rock
Aang's thought chakra is unblocked upon collision
Aang receives a vision of his Avatar predecessors in the Avatar State
Ozai approaches the pile of rocks underneath which Aang is buried
Ozai taunts Aang to come out
Ozai is surprised as Aang grabs him by the beard
Aang emerges from the rubble, his tattoos glowing from the restored Avatar State
Ozai strikes for Aang's head
Aang effortlessly pushes Ozai's hand out of the way, directing his fire harmlessly into the sky
Aang counters with a powerful air blast to Ozai's chest, sending him tumbling backward
Ozai slams to a halt by connecting with a stone pillar and sags onto his knees
Ozai glances up and sees an enraged Avatar floating through the sky in a sphere of air
Aang roars
Enhanced fire whips shoot out of Aang's mouth and all his limbs
Aang rises higher into the sky
Aang uses fire whips to destroy several earth pillars
Aang summons loose rocks toward him
Aang simultaneously draws water from the nearby ocean
Aang concentrates the bulk of his flames
Aang condenses the rocks
Aang tightens the water to all form circles around him
The Order of the White Lotus successfully liberates Ba Sing Se
Pakku and Piandao work together to get past some Fire Nation soldiers
Bumi takes down several Fire Nation tundra tanks
Jeong Jeong takes down several Fire Nation tundra tanks
Iroh burns down the Fire Nation flag hanging from the Earth Kingdom Royal Palace
Aang, possessed by the Avatar State, charges at Ozai and knocks him into the sky
Ozai recovers before Aang can get to him again
Ozai flees by using firebending to propel himself at great speeds
Aang chases after Ozai
Aang earthbends small fragments of rock and fires them at Ozai like bullets
Ozai barely manages to evade the rock fragments and continues fleeing
Azula relentlessly shoots fire and lightning at Katara, driving her away from any source of water
Azula taunts Prince Zuko, who is collapsed on the ground
Katara finds water to bend
Azula is too quick for Katara’s attacks, forcing Katara to flee from her flames
Katara discovers a grate with water underneath and some chains nearby
Katara lures Azula above the grate
Katara bends the water around herself and Azula just before Azula can attack at point-blank range, freezing them both in place
Azula looks on helplessly
Katara unfreezes the water around herself, swims in the thawed water, and binds Azula’s hands to the grate with chains
Katara melts the remaining water and runs to Zuko to heal him
Zuko weakly thanks Katara
Katara responds tearfully that she should be thanking Zuko
Azula sees Zuko rise, still alive, and her mind shatters completely
Azula firebends from her mouth wildly before rolling on the ground, screaming and crying uncontrollably
Zuko and Katara silently look upon Azula with pity
Zuko and Katara silently look upon Azula with pity
Aang, controlled by the Avatar State, pursues the evading Phoenix King
Aang tries to crush Ozai between two stone pillars with earthbending
Ozai manages to avoid the collision in time
Ozai tries to counterattack
Ozai’s attempts fail against the power of all the past Avatars
Aang catches Ozai with waterbending
Aang subdues Ozai by earthbending shackles onto his arms and legs
Aang, hovering over Ozai, speaks with the voices of his predecessors, declaring Ozai must pay the ultimate price
Aang, possessed by the Avatar State, prepares to deliver the final blow, combining all four elements into one attack
Aang’s peaceful nature breaks through the control of the Avatar State
Aang exits the Avatar State, cancelling his attack at the last instant
Aang releases Ozai from his bonds
Aang says he is not going to end the fight that way
Ozai mocks Aang, saying he is still weak despite being the most powerful being in the world
Ozai tries to strike Aang with firebending as Aang's back is turned
Aang senses Ozai's movement using Toph's earthbending technique
Aang quickly disables Ozai with earthen shackles
Aang remembers what the lion turtle taught him
Aang uses the technique to bend Ozai's inner energies, risking being destroyed himself
Aang's blue energy is nearly consumed by Ozai's orange energy, but he manages to overcome it
A pillar of blue light shoots into the sky
The pillar of blue light is similar to the beam that appeared when Aang was freed from the iceberg
Ozai, exhausted, tries to attack Aang again but cannot firebend
Aang explains he removed Ozai's firebending powers so Ozai cannot hurt anyone anymore
Aang demonstrates control over the Avatar State
Aang waterbends the tide to rise and douse the burning land
Momo flies back to Aang and lands on his shoulder
Sokka, Toph, and Suki meet up with Aang as Sozin's Comet departs Earth
Sokka, Toph, and Suki congratulate Aang
Suki wonders if Ozai, collapsed on the ground, is dead
Ozai snarls that he is still alive
Aang explains that he took away Ozai's firebending with an ability he learned from a lion turtle
Toph comments on Aang's strange adventures
Sokka taunts Ozai and calls him "The Loser Lord"
Ozai weakly tries to defend his title as the Phoenix King, but passes out again from exhaustion
Toph insults Ozai's title by calling him the "Phoenix King of Getting His Butt Whooped"
Suki tries, "King of the Guys Who Don't Win?"
Toph advises Suki to leave the name-calling to them
The sky fades from bright red to the dark of night as the comet fades into space
Zuko tries to dress himself for his coronation as Fire Lord, but has trouble due to his injuries
Mai arrives and helps Zuko dress
Zuko is glad to see Mai was released from prison
Mai and Zuko kiss
Mai warns Zuko not to break up with her ever again
Everyone is reunited in the Fire Nation capital city
Friends who were imprisoned are set free
Katara and Sokka find their father Hakoda
Hakoda tells Katara and Sokka he is the proudest father in the world and that their mother would be proud of them
Sokka is distressed to see Ty Lee dressed in uniform among the Kyoshi Warriors
Sokka assumes Ty Lee is disguising herself again
Suki tells Sokka that Ty Lee officially joined the Kyoshi Warriors
Ty Lee explains she bonded with the warriors in prison and after a few chi-blocking lessons, she was allowed to join and be "best friends forever"
Zuko, wearing the robes of the Fire Lord, approaches Aang
Aang dons the attire of an airbending master
Zuko reminisces about how different his life was a year prior, noting his change from being obsessed with capturing the Avatar to being Aang's friend
Aang expresses disbelief that he was still frozen in a block of ice the year prior
Zuko comments on how different the world is and comforts Aang by saying the world will be even more different once they rebuild it together
Zuko and Aang hug
Zuko and Aang hug
The coronation begins in the plaza
Zuko addresses the crowd, announces the end of the War, and gives Aang credit for saving the world as everyone cheers
Zuko is crowned Fire Lord
Zuko declares the start of a new era of love and peace
Zuko pays his imprisoned father Ozai a visit
Ozai sarcastically remarks that the new Fire Lord has graced him with his presence
Zuko remarks that Ozai should be glad Aang let him live
Zuko tells Ozai that banishment was the best thing that ever happened to him, as it put him on the right path
Zuko expresses hope that being in prison will give Ozai a chance for redemption
Ozai sees through Zuko’s purpose and asks why he is really visiting
Zuko gravely demands Ozai tell him where his mother Ursa is
Children use the stack of Fire Nation tanks Bumi destroyed for earthbending games
Zuko helps serve tea to the gang, including Mai and Iroh, at the Jasmine Dragon
The gang, including Mai and Iroh, relax together at the Jasmine Dragon
Sokka paints a crudely drawn group portrait to remember the good times
Katara complains that her oversize hair loopies make her look like Momo in the drawing
Zuko grumbles that his hair was not that spiky as portrayed in the drawing
Mai comments that she looks like a man in the drawing
Suki comments that she is shown firebending in the drawing
Momo climbs on the table and makes noises to Sokka, which Sokka interprets as a complaint
Iroh complains about being depicted with a big stomach in the drawing, claiming he has trimmed down his weight
Toph tells them she thinks they all look perfect, making a joke about her blindness
Everyone laughs at Toph’s joke
Aang smiles
Aang steps outside the tea shop to pet Appa
Katara follows Aang outside
Aang and Katara warmly embrace
Aang and Katara share a passionate kiss
